夕刻過ぎに降り出した雨は、日没と同時に激しさを増し、屋根をばちばちと叩く音が静か: The rain that began falling in the late evening intensified as the sun dipped below the horizon, the rhythmic drumming against the roof echoing through the otherwise silent bar.
電球の仄かな明かりが棚に並ぶ洋酒の瓶を琥珀色に浮き上がらせる。: The faint glow of electric bulbs cast an amber light upon the rows of Western liquor bottles lining the shelves.
牛込区神楽坂の路地裏でひっそりと店を構えているバー、《ＣＡＤＥＮＺＡ》。: Tucked away in a back alley of Kagurazaka, Ushigome District, sat the quiet establishment known as “CADENZA.”
最近――昭和が二桁になったばかりのバーやカフェと言えば、女給によるいかがわしい接: Lately—now that we’ve entered the second decade of the Showa era—most bars and cafes have taken to selling lewd service from waitresses as their main attraction.
だがここはマスターである護堂弦一郎が、純粋にジャズ・ミュージックを愉しむ場として: But this place was managed by the master, Godou Genichirou, as a sanctuary for the pure enjoyment of jazz music.
雨の所為か店を開けたばかりの所為か、がらがらの店内には俺――玖藤奏介以外、カウン: Perhaps due to the rain, or perhaps because the doors had only just opened, the place was empty save for me—Kudou Sousuke—and the Master, who was busy polishing glasses behind the counter.
グラスの曇りを慎重に拭いながらマスターが呟いた。: The Master muttered the words as he meticulously wiped a smudge from a glass.
奏介「雨なんてのは大抵が嫌なモンだろ」: Sousuke: “Rain’s usually a nasty business regardless.”
俺は空になったグラスを持ち上げてマスターに見せる。: I raised my empty glass, signaling the Master.
マスター「恵みの雨という言葉もある」: Master: “Some call it a blessing from the heavens.”
マスターは口元に笑みを浮かべると、俺のグラスにウィスキーを注いだ。角が取れ丸みを: A faint smile played on the Master’s lips as he poured whiskey into my glass. The ice, its sharp edges worn smooth and round, let out a soft clink.
奏介「この程度で酔うかよ」: Sousuke: “As if this much could get me drunk.”
グラスをあおる。独特の香気が鼻から抜け、まろやかな液体が喉を通り過ぎる。: I drained the glass. The distinct aroma wafted through my senses as the mellow liquid burned down my throat.
マスター「演奏できなくなったら金は払わないぞ」: Master: “If you get too wasted to play, you aren’t getting paid.”
店の奥に置かれているグランドピアノ。狭い店内で不釣り合いな程場所を占めているそれ: The grand piano sat in the back of the shop. Occupying a disproportionate amount of space in the cramped interior, it was the tool of my trade.
銀座のバーでピアノ弾きをしていた俺は、この店が開店すると同時に相方共々移ってきた: I used to be a piano player at a bar in Ginza, but I moved here along with my partner the moment this place opened.
銀座の堅苦しさに嫌気がさしていたというのもある。俺は単に酒が呑めてピアノが弾けれ: I’d grown sick of the stifling formality of Ginza. All I wanted was a place where I could drink and play the piano in peace.
この店はいい。自由に弾けるし、そして何より――客が少ない。: This shop was perfect. I could play freely, and best of all—there were hardly any customers.
あまり他人と関わり合いになりたくない俺にとっては、まるで天国のような場所だ。: For someone like me, who preferred to avoid unnecessary entanglements with others, it was paradise.
マスター「もしくは――酒ばかり呑んでいるピアノ弾きの所為だな」: Master: “Or perhaps—it’s the fault of the piano player who does nothing but drink.”
マスターは俺の貌を見てにやりと笑った。: The Master looked at me and flashed a wry grin.
鼻を鳴らし、俺はマスターから視線を外した。バーの入口が目に留まる。風で扉がぎしぎ: Snorting, I looked away from him. My eyes fell on the entrance to the bar. The door was creaking and rattling in the wind.
今年――昭和十一年は大雪で年明けを迎えた。その所為か、夏に入っても例年のうだるよ: This year—Showa 11—began with heavy snowfall. Perhaps because of that, even now that summer had arrived, the usual sweltering heat was nowhere to be found.
それはそれでいいのかもしれない。ただ――暑さを感じない理由が、背筋も凍るような事: That might have been a good thing. If only—if only the reason we didn’t feel the heat wasn’t because of the string of blood-curdling incidents that kept occurring.
今年の五月に起こった阿部定事件を出すまでもなく、最近の大東京市では猟奇的な事件が: Even without mentioning the Abe Sada incident from this past May, Greater Tokyo has been plagued by a series of bizarre and gruesome crimes lately.
政府要人の惨殺、婦女の誘拐、陵辱。さらには突発的に殺人を犯すと云う物騒なものまで: The brutal murders of government officials, the kidnapping and violation of women. Even senseless, impulsive killings.
まるで探偵小説の中身のような、真実とも噂話ともとれぬような荒唐無稽な話が世間を賑: Absurd stories that blurred the line between truth and rumor, reading like something out of a detective novel, were the talk of the town.
それもこれも、数年前の金融恐慌以降の社会不安が巻き起こしたものだろうか。: Was it all a byproduct of the social unrest following the financial crisis a few years back?
まあ――都会の片隅でひっそりと生きている俺にはあまり関係のない話だが。: Well—it didn't much matter to a man like me, living quietly in a corner of the city.
からん、と扉につけられた鐘が鳴った。: The bell attached to the door gave a sharp ring.
コウモリ傘の水を払っているその人物を見て、俺は決まり悪く視線を逸らした。: As I saw the person shaking the water off their umbrella, I awkwardly averted my gaze.
そいつは俺の真横のスツールに腰掛けた。: The newcomer took a seat on the stool right next to mine.
マスターが後ろを向き、リキュールの瓶を手に取る。: The Master turned around and reached for a bottle of liqueur.
奏介「……よくそんな甘いモンが飲めるな」: Sousuke: “...I don't know how you can drink such sweet swill.”
？？？「何よ、文句でもあるの？」: ???: “What’s that supposed to mean? You got a problem?”
口を尖らせ、俺に食ってかかるこの女は橘美華夏。俺とはガキの頃からの付き合いがある: The woman snapping back at me with a pout was Tachibana Mikage. She and I had been close since we were brats.
赤坂に豪邸を構える橘家には、俺も随分と世話になった。: I owed a great deal to the Tachibana family and their mansion in Akasaka.
俺にピアノ弾きとしての道を示してくれたのも、音楽家である美華夏の父だ。: It was Mikage’s father, a musician himself, who had set me on the path of a piano player.
美華夏「奏介が飲むわけじゃないんだからいいじゃない」: Mikana: “It’s not like you’re the one drinking it, so what does it matter?”
俺が飲むわけではないのだから、関係のない話だ。: I wasn't the one drinking it, so it was none of my business.
俺は美華夏から視線を外し、グラスに口を付けた。: I looked away from Mikage and took a sip from my glass.
マスター「ファジー・ネーブルおまちどうさま」: Master: “One Fuzzy Navel, sorry for the wait.”
オールドファッションド・グラスを傾け、薄い口唇を付ける美華夏。: Mikage tilted the Old Fashioned glass, pressing it against her thin lips.
美華夏「みんなでちょっと集まるのよ。琢磨と柚芭ちゃんだけどね」: Mikana: “We’re having a little get-together. Just Takuma and Yuzuha-chan, though.”
奏介「琢磨はともかく……柚芭もかよ」: Sousuke: “Takuma is one thing... but Yuzuha too?”
柚芭とは俺の妹だ。故あって橘家に預けられていたが、最近また一緒に暮らすようになっ: Yuzuha was my younger sister. For certain reasons, she’d been staying with the Tachibana family, but we’d recently started living together again.
ふたりで暮らす江戸川橋のアパートはここからも近く、夜に繁華街まで出歩くのにも危険: The apartment we shared in Edogawabashi wasn't far from here, so walking out to the entertainment district at night wasn't too dangerous.
そしてそれにくわえ、菱谷琢磨は警視庁の刑事だ。気にくわない奴だが、世間的な信用は: Besides, Nirasaki Takuma was a detective with the Metropolitan Police. He was an annoying bastard, but he was highly respected by society.
美華夏「琢磨とはまだ喧嘩してるの？」: Mikana: “Are you and Takuma still fighting?”
奏介「知らん。向こうが勝手に突っかかってくるだけだ」: Sousuke: “Who knows. He’s the one who keeps picking fights with me.”
美華夏「はぁっ……ホント子供じゃないんだから、そういう意地の張り合いはやめたらど: Mikana: “Sigh... honestly, you’re not children. Why can’t you stop this petty bickering?”
ふて腐れたように頬をふくらませると、美華夏は前を向いてしまった。: Mikage puffed out her cheeks in annoyance and turned away.
俺が何をどう思おうが、他人にあれこれ言われる筋合いはない。: No matter what I thought or felt, I didn't need anyone else sticking their nose into it.
入口の鐘が鳴った。: The bell at the entrance rang.
耳慣れた声。妹の柚芭だ。: A familiar voice. My sister, Yuzuha.
ため息混じりに軽く柚芭を睨む。いくら馴染みとはいえ、年若い柚芭がおいそれと足を踏: I gave Yuzuha a light, exasperated glare. No matter how familiar the place was, a bar wasn't somewhere a young girl like Yuzuha should be stepping into so casually.
柚芭「だって……お兄ちゃんのピアノ聴きたかったんだもん」: Yuzuha: “But... I wanted to hear you play the piano, big brother.”
そんなに良い物でもないだろうにと思うが、口には出さない。ピアノ弾きとして少しばか: I thought to myself that it wasn't even that impressive, but I didn't say it out loud. As a piano player, I did have at least a shred of pride.
俺はポケットの小銭をまさぐり、十銭硬貨が何枚かあるのを確認すると柚芭に手渡した。: I rummaged through the change in my pocket, confirmed I had a few ten-sen coins, and handed them to Yuzuha.
奏介「酒以外なら頼んでもいいぞ」: Sousuke: “You can order anything as long as it isn't alcohol.”
満面の笑みを浮かべ、柚芭はカウンタ席に腰掛けた。: With a radiant smile, Yuzuha took a seat at the counter.
美華夏「それにしても……貴方たち、少しは喋りなさいよ」: Mikana: “Honestly... you two should try talking to each other for once.”
？？？「俺は別にこいつと話に来たわけじゃない」: ???: “I didn't come here to talk to this guy.”
奥のスツールに座った男――琢磨が吐き捨てるように呟く。: The man sitting on the far stool—Takuma—muttered with a look of disdain.
柚芭のすぐ後に店に着いたのだろう、俺は全く気付かなかったが。: He must have arrived right after Yuzuha; I hadn't noticed him at all.
さすがに勤務時間外だからか、背広は着ていない。: Since he was off-duty, he wasn't wearing his suit.
奏介「――ハッ、酒がまずくなる」: Sousuke: “—Hah, the alcohol’s starting to taste like shit.”
俺はグラスを持ったまま立ち上がった。: I stood up, glass still in hand.
琢磨も立ち上がり、俺の前に立ちふさがる。: Takuma stood up as well, blocking my path.
琢磨「お前のしけた面眺めてると酒がまずくなるんだよ」: Takuma: “Looking at your miserable face makes my drink taste like shit.”
奏介「男に見つめられる趣味はねえぞ」: Sousuke: “I don't have a thing for men staring at me.”
美華夏「ちょっと……やめてよ、ふたりとも」: Mikana: “Hey... stop it, both of you.”
柚芭が服の裾を引く。: Yuzuha tugged at the hem of my shirt.
マスター「――お前ら、ここが何処か忘れたのか？」: Master: “—Have you boys forgotten where you are?”
マスターの低い声に俺たちは動きを止めた。: The Master’s low voice brought us to a halt.
マスター「喧嘩なら外でやってくれ」: Master: “If you want to fight, take it outside.”
琢磨から離れ、マスターに向かってグラスを差し出す。: I moved away from Takuma and held out my glass to the Master.
そういいながらもマスターはウィスキーを注ぐ。: Even as he said it, the Master refilled my whiskey.
軽く口唇を湿らせ、店の奥に据えられたグランド・ピアノの前へ進む。: After wetting my lips, I walked toward the grand piano in the back of the shop.
店の裏から出てきた女給姿の少女とぶつかりそうになる。: I nearly collided with a young girl in a waitress uniform emerging from the back.
高梨千花、このバーに住み込みで働いている少女だ。: Takanashi Chika, a girl who lived and worked at the bar.
頬を染め、後じさる千花。照れているわけではない。俺と遭遇したときはいつもこうだ。: Chika flushed red and backed away. She wasn't being shy. She was always like this when we crossed paths. She acted as if she were perpetually terrified of me.
妙にしつこく絡まれるよりは、こちらの方がいい。俺は美華夏の方をちらりと盗み見てひ: Better this than someone being annoyingly persistent, I thought to myself, stealing a glance toward Mikage.
勢いよく頭を下げ、千花はカウンタ席の方へ行ってしまった。: With a sharp bow, Chika scurried off toward the counter.
カウンタ内でマスターが人の悪そうな笑みを浮かべているのを無視し、ピアノの鍵盤に触: Ignoring the Master’s mischievous grin from behind the counter, I touched the piano keys.
馴染みのある澄んだ音が店内に響いた。: A familiar, clear tone echoed through the room.
譜面台の横にグラスを置き、椅子に腰を下ろす。: I set my glass down next to the music stand and took a seat.
マスター「裏で休憩しているはずだが……千花、すまないが呼んできてくれるか？」: Master: “She should be resting in the back... Chika, sorry, but could you go call her?”
千花が店の裏へ入っていく。: Chika disappeared into the back of the shop.
その間、俺は演奏の準備を進める。: In the meantime, I prepared for the performance.
と言っても、特に何もすることはない。精々椅子の位置を動かして弾きやすいようにする: Not that there was much to do. At most, I just adjusted the position of the chair to make it easier to play.
譜面台に楽譜は置いてあるものの、あまり意味はない。曲目を覚えていると云う訳でもな: There was sheet music on the stand, but it didn't mean much. It wasn't like I had the repertoire memorized. I always just played whatever I felt like.
お陰でファッツやサッチモのようなジャズの定番を聴きたがる客にはすこぶる評判が悪い: As a result, I had a terrible reputation among customers who wanted to hear jazz staples like Fats or Satchmo.
それでもマスターは俺を雇い続けてくれているのだから感謝しなくてはならないだろう。: Still, the Master kept me on, so I supposed I should be grateful.
まあ――俺が雇われているのはあくまで《おまけ》でしかないのかもしれないが。: Well—perhaps my being hired was merely a "bonus" of sorts.
口元を隠してあくびをしながら、ステージ衣装に身を包んだ女が裏から出てきた。: A woman in stage attire emerged from the back, covering a yawn with her hand.
韮崎璃宝、上海出身の歌手だ。数年前、上海事変により父方の親戚が住む日本へ渡ってき: Nirasaki Riho, a singer from Shanghai. Apparently, she’d fled to Japan to live with relatives on her father’s side after the Shanghai Incident a few years ago.
俺とはその後暫く経ってから銀座で出会った。: I met her in Ginza some time after that.
当時の俺はまだ修行中の身で、歌手として名を馳せていた彼女に半ば拾われるような形で: Back then, I was still an apprentice, and she, already a renowned singer, had practically taken me under her wing.
お互いべたべたとした人間関係を嫌うからこそ、つかず離れずで今まで続いてきているの: Perhaps we’d lasted this long without getting too close or drifting too far apart precisely because we both loathed clingy relationships.
璃宝「ちょいと一杯引っかけたら気持ちよくなっちゃってねぇ。雨だれが子守歌代わりに: Riho: “I had a little drink and it just felt too good, you know? The sound of the rain was like a lullaby.”
悪びれもせず璃宝は言うと、眠たげな目を細めて笑った。: Riho spoke without a hint of remorse, her sleepy eyes narrowing as she smiled.
璃宝「あら、奏介がやる気だなんて珍しいわね……ふぅーん……」: Riho: “Oh? Sousuke actually being motivated? How rare... hmm...”
カウンタ席を眺め、璃宝がひとりほくそ笑む。: Looking over at the counter, Riho chuckled to herself.
璃宝「妹さんが来てるからかしら？ それともあっちのお嬢さん？」: Riho: “Is it because your sister is here? Or maybe that young lady over there?”
璃宝「いつでもいいわよ。今晩はどんな曲？」: Riho: “Anytime. What are we playing tonight?”
一瞬の静寂の後、ぱちぱちとまばらに拍手が鳴る。: After a moment of silence, a smattering of applause broke out.
一番熱心に拍手を送っていた柚芭が駆け寄ってくる。: Yuzuha, the most enthusiastic of the group, came running over.
璃宝「あらあら、ホント妬けちゃうくらい仲の良い兄妹ね」: Riho: “My, my, what a pair of siblings. It almost makes me jealous how well you get along.”
柚芭「韮崎さんにだって、お兄ちゃんは渡しませんよっ」: Yuzuha: “I’m not letting you have him, Nirasaki-san!”
璃宝「安心しなさいな、アタシは奏介を盗るつもりなんてないからさ」: Riho: “Don’t you worry, I have no intention of stealing Sousuke away.”
氷の溶けてしまったグラスを手に取り、カウンタへ戻る。: I picked up my glass, the ice now completely melted, and headed back to the counter.
ねぎらいの言葉をかけられ、返事代わりに空のグラスを差し出した。: In response to his words of appreciation, I simply handed him my empty glass.
苦笑いを浮かべながら、マスターはウィスキーを注ぎ足す。: With a wry smile, the Master poured me more whiskey.
氷がないことに少し不満を抱く。いくら店に電気冷蔵庫があるとはいえ、そう数は作れな: I was a bit annoyed by the lack of ice. No matter how much the shop had an electric refrigerator, it couldn't make that much, so I suppose it couldn't be helped.
横の美華夏が小さな籠を俺の方に差し出してきた。中を覗くと胡桃がいくつか、殻をつけ: Mikage, sitting next to me, held out a small basket. Looking inside, I saw several walnuts still in their shells.
柚芭「お兄ちゃんも、寄り道しないで早く帰ってきてね」: Yuzuha: “Don’t take any detours and come home soon, big brother.”
奏介「判ってる。……ひとりで帰るのか？」: Sousuke: “I know. ...Are you going back alone?”
柚芭「ううん、美華夏ちゃんたちに送ってもらうから大丈夫だよ」: Yuzuha: “No, Mikage-chan and the others are walking me back, so it’s fine.”
気に食わない。物騒な時代故に刑事の護衛があるのは正直ありがたいが、それがよりにも: I didn't like it. In these dangerous times, having a detective as an escort was honestly something to be grateful for, but of all people, it had to be Takuma.
琢磨「お前に心配されなくとも、俺がちゃんと送り届けてやるよ」: Takuma: “I’ll get them home safe without any help from you.”
さも呆れた、と言わんばかりに美華夏が溜息を吐く。: Mikage let out a sigh that practically screamed how fed up she was.
琢磨が背を向け店を出て行く。: Takuma turned his back and walked out of the shop.
柚芭「……それじゃまた後でね、お兄ちゃん」: Yuzuha: “...I’ll see you later then, big brother.”
柚芭と美華夏も店を出て行った。: Yuzuha and Mikage followed him out.
嵐が去った後のような静けさ。: A silence like the aftermath of a storm settled over the bar.
美華夏が残していった籠から胡桃を手に取り、しばらく手の中で弄ぶ。: I took a walnut from the basket Mikage had left behind and toyed with it in my hand for a while.
硬い殻がぶつかりあい、カリカリと音を立てる。: The hard shells knocked against each other with a dry clicking sound.
呆けていたのを誤解されたようだ。: He seemed to have misinterpreted my zoning out.
ポケットからナイフを取り出す。: I pulled a knife from my pocket.
ぱきっ、と軽い音を立てて胡桃が割れ、中から実が転がり落ちる。: With a crisp crack, the walnut split open, and the nut fell out from within.
色こそ違えど、脳髄のような形。それは嫌が応にも過去の記憶を呼び覚ます。: Though the color was different, the shape was like a brain. It couldn't help but stir up memories of the past.
忘れもしない大震災。目の前に広がっていたのは地獄の光景だった。: The Great Kanto Earthquake, a day I could never forget. What had unfolded before my eyes was a vision of hell.
つぶれた家屋。立ち上る炎と黒煙。苦悶の呻き。怨嗟の声。: Crushed houses. Rising flames and black smoke. Groans of agony. Cries of resentment.
子を探す親の声。親を求める子の声。: Parents calling for their children. Children screaming for their parents.
白昼に起こった悲劇のただ中に、俺と柚芭はぽつんと取り残されていた。: In the midst of that midday tragedy, Yuzuha and I were left all alone.
目の前には崩壊した建物。数瞬前まではここで昼飯を待っていたのに。: In front of me was a collapsed building. Only moments ago, we’d been waiting for lunch there.
目の前の地面は黒く濡れている。水ではなく、流れ出た血で。: The ground before me was wet and black. Not with water, but with flowing blood.
瓦礫から飛び出した手。僅かに動いているのは気のせいだったろうか？: A hand sticking out from the rubble. Was it just my imagination that it moved slightly?
微笑みすら浮かべなくなった母親の貌。頭部は無惨にえぐり取られ、『胡桃のような』脳: My mother’s face, no longer even able to form a smile. Her head had been brutally gouged open, revealing a ‘walnut-like’ brain.
ぴしっ、と手の中の殻が音を立て、破片が食い込む痛みに我に返った。: The shell in my hand cracked with a sharp snap, and the pain of the fragments digging into my skin snapped me back to reality.
胡桃の実は相変わらずその脳髄のような形を保っている。: The walnut meat still maintained that brain-like shape.
奏介「少し……飲み過ぎたようだ」: Sousuke: “A little... think I drank too much.”
胡桃を口へ放り込む。: I tossed the walnut into my mouth.
璃宝「……無理しない方がいいわよ。辛いのだったら――」: Riho: “...You shouldn't push yourself. If it’s too much for you—”
璃宝が俺の貌を覗き込み、嫣然と微笑む。: Riho peered into my face and flashed a seductive smile.
璃宝「――ウチで休んでいく？」: Riho: “—Why don't you come rest at my place?”
奏介「悪いが、そんな気力もない」: Sousuke: “Sorry, but I don't have the energy for that.”
拗ねたような璃宝。これまでも幾度となく肌を重ねてきた彼女だが、こういう仕草は実に: Riho looked pouted. Even though she and I had shared a bed countless times, she still looked remarkably childlike when she made faces like that.
璃宝「妹さんが待ってるものね」: Riho: “Because your sister is waiting, I suppose?”
早く帰ると約束したのは確かだが、別に柚芭のためという訳でもない。: It was true that I’d promised to come home early, but it wasn't necessarily for Yuzuha’s sake.
単に今日は璃宝を抱く気がしない、それだけだ。: I simply didn't feel like holding Riho tonight, that was all.
璃宝「つまらない男ねぇ。千花、こんな男には引っかからないように気をつけないとダメ: Riho: “What a boring man. Chika, you have to be careful not to get caught by a man like this, okay?”
マスター「親御さんから大事な娘を預かってるんだ。当然だろう」: Master: “I’m looking after a precious daughter for her parents. Of course I am.”
璃宝「まぁ千花がいないと、このお店もあっという間につぶれちゃうかもしれないしね」: Riho: “Well, without Chika, this shop might go under in the blink of an eye.”
璃宝「アタシは歌うだけ。マスターはお酒の蘊蓄たれるだけ。奏介は……まぁ、何もしな: Riho: “I just sing. The Master just rambles about alcohol. And Sousuke... well, he doesn't really do anything.”
ストレートのウィスキーを飲み干すと、一気に酔いがまわったように感じた。: As I drained the straight whiskey, I felt the intoxication hit me all at once.
これ以上は呑まない方が良さそうだ。: It was probably best not to drink any more.
空のグラスをカウンタに残し、俺は立ち上がる。: Leaving the empty glass on the counter, I stood up.
璃宝「酔って川に落ちるんじゃないわよ」: Riho: “Don’t go falling into the river because you’re drunk.”
軽く片手を揺らめかし、俺は店を出た。: With a light wave of my hand, I left the shop.
表に出た途端、強い横殴りの風雨に身体が流された。アルコールがまわっている所為か、: The moment I stepped outside, the fierce, driving wind and rain nearly knocked me off my feet. Perhaps because the alcohol was hitting me, my footing felt unsteady.
傘を開く。ばさばさと布地がはためき、骨がきしむ。家に帰り着くまで保つだろうか。: I opened my umbrella. The fabric snapped and billowed, and the ribs groaned. I wondered if it would hold out until I reached home.
まあ――壊れたら壊れたでそのまま濡れて帰ればいい。: Well—if it broke, it broke, and I’d just have to walk home in the rain.
多くの娼館やバーが軒を連ねる神楽坂とはいえ、この雨ではさほど人通りがないようだ。: Even in Kagurazaka, with its many brothels and bars, there were few people out in this weather.
坂を上り、少しくだり、また上る。: Up the hill, down a bit, and up again.
少しくらい酔っていようが、何度も通っている路を間違えたりはしない。: No matter how drunk I might be, I wouldn't lose my way on a path I’d walked a thousand times.
雨は一際激しさを増し、降り続いている。: The rain only grew more intense, continuing its relentless downpour.
何度目かの角を曲がると、いつも通る道は荷車でふさがれていたので、すぐ右の細い路地: As I turned yet another corner, I found my usual path blocked by a cart, so I stepped into a narrow alleyway immediately to the right.
道の端、申し訳程度に張り出した軒の下で人が蹲っている。: At the edge of the path, a person was crouching under a meager overhang.
派手な着物は雨に濡れ、結い上げられた髪もほつれている。花街の芸妓なのだろうか。: The gaudy kimono was drenched, and the styled hair was coming undone. Was she a geisha from the pleasure quarters?
酔った勢いか、俺は女の側にしゃがみ込み声をかけた。: Driven by the alcohol, I knelt down beside the woman and called out to her.
奏介「大丈夫か？ どこか雨のしのげる所に――」: Sousuke: “Are you okay? Somewhere out of the rain—”
俺が近づいた事に気付いたのか、女が貌を上げて立ち上がった。: Noticing my approach, the woman raised her head and stood up.
女「――すいません、急に眩暈がして――」: Woman: “—I’m sorry, I suddenly felt dizzy—”
壁にもたれかかり、足元もおぼつかない女。: The woman leaned against the wall, her footing unsteady.
傘を女の頭上にかざしてやる。濡れてしまってもう意味を成さないかもしれないが、気分: I held my umbrella over the woman’s head. It might have been pointless since she was already soaked, but it was the thought that counted.
奏介「……他に何もできねえからな」: Sousuke: “...It’s the least I can do.”
医者でもない俺は、彼女の容態を看る事すらできない。: I wasn't a doctor; I couldn't even tell what was wrong with her.
女の熱い吐息が俺の手に当たる。: The woman’s hot breath brushed against my hand.
そうしてしばらくの間、俺と彼女は雨の中黙って突っ立っていた。: And so, for a while, she and I stood there in the rain in silence.
女「――もう、大丈夫です。ご迷惑をおかけしました」: Woman: “—I’m alright now. I’m sorry for the trouble.”
やがて女が貌を上げた。憂いのある表情だったが、充分に艶のある顔立ちだ。: At last, the woman raised her head. Her expression was melancholy, but her face was undeniably alluring.
奏介「いや――なに、俺は何もしてねえよ」: Sousuke: “No—it was nothing. I didn't really do anything.”
彼女の手に傘の柄を握らせる。: I pressed the handle of the umbrella into her hand.
奏介「……俺の家はすぐ其処だからな。やるよ」: Sousuke: “...My place is just right there. You can have it.”
女は小さく首を傾げて笑みを浮かべた。: The woman tilted her head slightly and offered a smile.
女「はい。貴方もお気をつけて」: Woman: “Yes. You take care as well.”
女に背を向ける。: I turned my back to the woman.
雨はしのげなくなったが、まあいい。: I was no longer shielded from the rain, but that was fine.
走って帰ろうと脚を踏み出したときだった。: I was just about to start running home when it happened.
嘔吐感に堪らず膝をついた。: Unable to withstand the nausea, I fell to my knees.
服に雨水が染み込んでくる。: The rainwater soaked into my clothes.
ここまで泥酔するほど呑んだ覚えはない。: I didn't remember drinking enough to get this wasted.
だが身体は酩酊状態のようにぐらぐらと揺れている。: But my body was swaying as if I were in a state of deep intoxication.
何とか立ち上がり、前へ進もうとする。: I tried my best to stand up and move forward.
靴の中まで濡れている。: Even my shoes were filled with water.
背後から女のうめき声が聞こえた。: A groan from the woman came from behind me.
壁に手をつき振り返る。: I leaned a hand against the wall and looked back.
俺もヤバいが、女の様子も尋常ではない。: I was in bad shape, but the woman’s condition was clearly abnormal.
女は濡れた地面に膝をつき、苦しげに呻っている。: The woman fell to her knees on the wet ground, groaning in agony.
癪――などではない。もしそうならば、俺まで苦しむ道理はない。: This wasn't just—some simple ailment. If it were, there was no reason for me to be suffering too.
割れるように痛む頭を押さえつつ、女の元へ這い寄る。: Pressing a hand to my splitting head, I crawled over to her.
肩に手を掛けようとしたとき、女と目があった。: Just as I was about to place a hand on her shoulder, our eyes met.
――爛々と、禍々しい光が宿っていた。: —They were wide, glowing with an ominous, sinister light.
頬に鋭い痛み。: A sharp pain in my cheek.
頭突きを受け、鼻を押さえる。どうやら鼻血は出ていないようだが、眉間のあたりがずき: I caught a headbutt and clutched my nose. It didn't seem to be bleeding, but the area around my brow throbbed with pain.
ただの狂人ならともかく、女離れした――人間離れした膂力を持っている。: Even setting aside her madness, she possessed a strength that was unwomanly—no, inhuman.
俺だってそんなにやわじゃない。それでもこの女と組み合ったとして、勝てる気がしない: I wasn't exactly a weakling myself. Still, I didn't feel like I could win in a struggle with this woman.
ずきん、とこめかみが疼き、視界が霞む。: A sharp throb in my temple, and my vision grew hazy.
堪らず膝をついた。: I fell to my knees, unable to help it.
女が地を這うような動きで近づいてくるのが視界の端に映った。: From the corner of my eye, I saw the woman approaching with a crawling, beast-like movement.
間近に迫る女の狂気に彩られた表情。歪んだ笑みの形で固まったような口元からはだらだ: The woman’s face, painted with madness, was inches from mine. Saliva overflowed from her mouth, which seemed frozen in a distorted grin.
かなり薄れている意識の中でそんなことを考える。: I thought as much in my fading consciousness.
女の表情が霞む。これは――これでいいかもしれない。: The woman’s expression blurred. This—perhaps this was for the best.
思い切り腕を突き伸ばす。: I thrust my arm out with all my might.
野獣のような唸り声を発する女。尋常ではない。: The woman let out a low growl like a wild animal. This was anything but normal.
アヘンか何かの薬物を投与されているような、異常な衝動に駆られているようだ。: It was as if she were being driven by an abnormal impulse, perhaps under the influence of opium or some other drug.
話の通じない奴に対抗する術は――こちらから先に仕掛けるしかない。: When dealing with someone who couldn't be reasoned with—there was no choice but to strike first.
もう相手が女だとか、そんな甘いことはいってられない状況だ。: I couldn't afford to be soft just because my opponent was a woman anymore.
奏介「ハッ――喧嘩ならこっちの方が慣れて……」: Sousuke: “Hah—if it’s a fight you want, I’m more than used to it...”
女と距離を置く。切り裂かれた左腕がじんじんと痛む。おさえた右手になま暖かい血の感: I put some distance between myself and the woman. My slashed left arm throbbed with pain. My right hand, clutching it, felt the sensation of warm blood.
女は奇妙な笑みを浮かべる。血を見て興奮しているのか、口の端から涎をまき散らしてい: The woman wore a strange smile. Perhaps she was excited by the sight of blood, saliva splattering from the corner of her mouth.
いよいよやばい。出血の痛みでさらに意識が朦朧としてくる。: This was getting really bad. The pain from the bleeding made my consciousness even more hazy.
奏介「……容赦しねえぞ、コラ」: Sousuke: “...I’m not holding back now, you bitch.”
右足を僅かに引き、女に対峙する。雨粒が左腕の傷に染み込む。: I pulled my right foot back slightly and squared off against her. Rainwater seeped into the wound on my left arm.
奏介「ハッ――言っただろ？ 容赦しねえってな」: Sousuke: “Hah—I told you, didn't I? I’m not holding back.”
泥水の中に倒れた女に向かって吐き捨てる。: I spat the words at the woman, who had collapsed into the muddy water.
鼻ぐらいは折ったかもしれない。それでも自業自得だ。: I might have broken her nose. Still, she’d brought it on herself.
奏介「なんのつもりだか知らねえが――」: Sousuke: “I don't know what you were planning, but—”
自分が今何をしているのか、判らなくなってくる。: I started to lose track of what I was even doing.
目の前の女は？: Who was the woman in front of me?
女がゆらりと立ち上がる。全身ずぶ濡れで、顔面からはだらだらと血をまき散らしている: The woman stood up slowly. She was drenched from head to toe, and blood was splattering from her face.
俺は足を引き、拳を構えた。: I pulled my leg back and raised my fists.
それなら、徹底的に叩き潰してやる。: If that was the case, I’d crush her once and for all.
数年前まで、暴力が日常だった。: Until a few years ago, violence had been my daily life.
震災後、荒れ地となった東京で暴れた記憶がよみがえる。: Memories of running wild in the wasteland of Tokyo after the earthquake flooded back.
生きるため、というよりはただ闇雲に暴れたかっただけの少年時代。: A childhood spent not so much fighting to survive, but simply because I wanted to lash out.
元号が大正から昭和に代わり、震災復興が徐々に進み、人々が元の生活を取り戻しても、: Even as the era changed from Taisho to Showa, and the city’s reconstruction progressed, we continued to live our decadent lives with a delinquent mindset.
そのお陰か――喧嘩だけは強くなった。: Thanks to that—I’d become a damn good fighter.
こんな不意の襲撃にも、自らを護る術くらいは心得ている。: Even against a sudden attack like this, I knew how to defend myself.
目の前の狂人に向けて手招きする。: I beckoned the madwoman toward me.
奏介「さっきまでの威勢はどうしたんだよ！！」: Sousuke: “What happened to all that spirit from before!?”
蹴りを喰らわせ、距離を置く。: I delivered a kick and put some distance between us.
奴は蹲ったまま動かない。: She lay huddled on the ground, motionless.
奏介「喧嘩を売るなら相手をよく見ろってんだ」: Sousuke: “If you’re gonna pick a fight, you’d better pick your opponent carefully.”
汗だか雨だかよく判らない額の水を拭う。: I wiped the water—whether it was sweat or rain, I couldn't tell—from my brow.
倒れたまま、そいつが嗤った。: Even as she lay there, she laughed.
疼痛を振り払うように腕を薙ぐと、奴は蛙が潰れたような声をあげた。: As I swung my arm as if to brush away the pain, she let out a sound like a crushed frog.
脚を掴まれ、引き倒された。: My leg was grabbed, and I was pulled down.
手のひらに小粒の砂利が食い込む。: The sensation of small pebbles digging into my palm.
目の前に奴の歪んだ笑み。: The woman’s distorted grin was right in front of me.
首に手がかかる。: Her hands reached for my throat.
地面を掻く右手に、何か硬い物が触れた。: My right hand, clawing at the ground, felt something hard.
それを掴み、そいつの腹へ叩き込む。: I grabbed it and drove it into her stomach.
繰り返し腹を打つ。: I repeatedly struck her in the stomach.
首を絞める手がゆるむ。: The grip on my throat loosened.
左腕でそいつの身体を引きはがして逃れる。: I pushed her body away with my left arm and escaped.
新鮮な空気を吸い込む。雨の所為で温く湿ってはいたが、それでも充分うまい。: I sucked in the fresh air. It was warm and damp because of the rain, but it still tasted wonderful.
奴は倒れたまま動かない。時折ぴくぴくと身体が痙攣する程度だ。: The woman lay motionless. At most, her body would occasionally twitch or convulse.
女の指が地面の濡れた砂を掻く。: Her fingers clawed at the wet sand on the ground.
二・二六事件以降、戒厳令やら何やらで街中にも陸軍の憲兵が出張って来ている。: Since the 2.26 Incident, the Army’s military police have been out in force even in the city due to martial law and such.
憲兵に何のかんのと因縁をつけられて収監された奴の話もよく耳にする。: I’d heard plenty of stories about people being thrown in jail on some flimsy pretext by the MPs.
俺は重い身体を引きずるようにして、警笛から遠ざかるように移動し始めた。: I began to drag my heavy body away, moving away from the sound of the whistles.
先程から度々襲ってきていた眩暈はもうしない。: The dizziness that had been recurring earlier was gone.
それでも相変わらず警笛の音は響いている。: Still, the sound of the whistles continued to echo.
心なしか数が増えたようだ。: It felt as if their numbers had increased.
今の俺の格好を見られれば、すぐに捕まってしまうだろう。服は泥水で汚れて酷い有様だ: If I were seen looking like this, I’d be arrested in an instant. My clothes were a mess, stained with mud, and I was covered in injuries.
柚芭がまた怒りそうだ。: Yuzuha would probably be angry again.
ここからなら自宅までさほどかからないだろう。幸い、頭痛も消えている。: It wouldn't take long to get home from here. Fortunately, the headache was gone as well.
警笛の音が近くなってきた。考えている暇はなさそうだ。: The sound of the whistles was getting closer. There was no time to think.
俺は人通りのない路地を駆けだした。: I started running through the deserted back alleys.
自宅まで後少しというところで、前方から聞こえてきた笛の音に足を止めた。: Just as I was nearly home, I heard a whistle from up ahead and stopped in my tracks.
周辺の地図を脳裏に思い浮かべる。自宅の江戸川アパートの方へ延びる道がひとつ、神楽: I visualized the map of the area in my mind. One road led toward my home in the Edogawa apartment, and another led back toward Kagurazaka.
早稲田の方へ抜ける道もあるが、それではあまりにも遠回りだ。: There was also a path that came out toward Waseda, but that would be far too much of a detour.
神田川へ出るか――: Should I head for the Kanda River—
家屋と家屋の間、猫くらいしか通れないような細い路地に連れ込まれそうになり、慌てて: I was nearly pulled into a narrow alley between houses—one so thin only a cat could pass through—and I tried to pull away in a panic.
すれ違いざま、どんっ、と背中を押される。: As we passed each other, someone shoved me in the back.
数歩たたらを踏み、壁に手をつく。振り返ると洋傘を差した女が路地を塞ぐように、こち: I stumbled a few steps and leaned against the wall. Looking back, I saw a woman with a Western umbrella blocking the alleyway, her back to me.
表の路地を、制服で身を包んだ数人の憲兵が走り抜けていく。: Several military police in uniform ran past the alley.
こちらに気付いた様子はない。: They didn't seem to have noticed us.
安堵の吐息。連中は容赦がない。捕らえられればどうなるか。身に覚えのない罪を着せら: A sigh of relief. Those men were merciless. If I were caught, who knows what would happen. I could be framed for a crime I didn't commit, or processed as a political criminal.
？？？「どうして、って……追われてるみたいでしたから」: ???: “Why? Well... you looked like you were being chased.”
奏介「憲兵に追われていたのを見たろう？ 犯罪者かもしれないんだぞ？」: Sousuke: “You saw the MPs chasing me, didn't you? I could be a criminal.”
？？？「いまどき特高や憲兵隊に追われているのが兇悪犯だなんて思っている人はいませ: ???: “Nobody thinks people being chased by the Special Higher Police or the MPs are hardened criminals these days.”
？？？「政治犯や思想犯、どれも一般市民には無害ですから」: ???: “Political or ideological prisoners aren't a threat to ordinary citizens.”
奏介「俺がそんな危険思想の持ち主だとでも？」: Sousuke: “You think I have such dangerous ideas?”
？？？「でも、兇悪犯にも見えませんよ」: ???: “But you don't look like a hardened criminal either.”
大仰に両手を広げてみせる。泥まみれで傷だらけ、これで善良な一般市民だと言い張る奴: I held my arms out wide. Covered in mud and injuries—I’d like to see the person who could claim I was just a law-abiding citizen.
突然女が素っ頓狂な声を上げ、俺の左腕を指さした。: Suddenly, the woman let out a startled cry and pointed to my left arm.
痛みがほとんど麻痺していた。女に簪で斬られたのを思い出す。: The pain had been mostly numb. I remembered the woman slashing me with her hairpin.
シャツに血が滲み、雨水で広がっている。: Blood was seeping through my shirt, spreading with the rainwater.
？？？「ダメじゃないですか！！ 大切な商売道具でしょう！？」: ???: “You can't let that be!! Those are your precious tools, aren't they!?”
女が真白いハンカチを取り出し、傷口に当ててくる。: The woman pulled out a pristine white handkerchief and pressed it against the wound.
外からの圧迫に、忘れていた痛みがぶり返す。: The pressure from the outside made the forgotten pain flare up again.
女の貌を窺う。: I peered into the woman’s face.
女の身体が、一瞬動きを止めた。: The woman’s body froze for a moment.
？？？「……そうですよね。覚えてる訳ないですよね……」: ???: “...Of course. There’s no way you’d remember...”
アルコールの残る頭で必死に記憶を掘り起こそうとする。: I tried desperately to dig through my memories with my alcohol-soaked brain.
？？？「神楽坂のバーで一度だけ玖藤さんの演奏を聴いただけですから……」: ???: “I only heard you play once at that bar in Kagurazaka...”
奏介「店に一度だけ来た客の事なんざ覚えてる訳ねえだろうが」: Sousuke: “There’s no way I’d remember a customer who only came to the shop once.”
奏介「アンタが俺に自己紹介でもしたってんなら別だがな」: Sousuke: “Unless you happened to introduce yourself to me.”
ぺこり、と女――綾音はお辞儀をした。傘の先が当たりそうになり少し避ける。: The woman—Ayane—gave a small bow. I moved back slightly to avoid the tip of her umbrella.
綾音「玖藤さんの演奏は先日聴かせていただきました。なんというか――すごく良かった: Ayane: “I heard you play the other day. It was—it was truly wonderful.”
綾音「ちょうどあの頃、いろんな事で悩んでいて……玖藤さんの飾らない、自由な演奏を: Ayane: “I was struggling with so many things back then... and then I heard your honest, free performance.”
綾音「玖藤さんにとっては練習だったのかもしれませんけど、すごく周囲に解け合ってい: Ayane: “Perhaps it was just practice for you, but it felt so natural, as if it were blending into everything around it.”
俺は練習をしない。本番が適当だからだ。おそらく彼女が聴いたのは、璃宝がいないとき: I didn't practice. My actual performances were haphazard enough as it was. She’d probably heard me idling away on the keys when Riho wasn't around.
綾音「それで、わたしの悩みも解決できたんです」: Ayane: “And because of that, I was able to solve my problems.”
俺は何もしちゃいない。どんな悩みだったか知らないが、それを解決したのは彼女自身の: I hadn't done a thing. I didn't know what her problems were, but she was the one who had solved them.
俺には――関係ない。: It had—nothing to do with me.
まったく……変な女だ。俺を助けるわ、怪我の手当はするわ、勝手に悩みを解決するわ。: Honestly... what a strange woman. Helping me, tending to my injuries, and solving her own problems all on her own.
軽く左腕を振り上げる。ちくり、と痛みが奔ったが気にしてもいられない。: I raised my left arm slightly. A sharp pain shot through it, but I couldn't afford to worry about that.
まだその辺りに憲兵がいるかもしれない。: There might still be MPs in the area.
さっさと自宅に帰るか、しばらくここでやり過ごすか。: Should I head straight home, or wait here for a while?
目の前の綾音を見る。そう悪くはない容姿。少しばかり胸の隆起に乏しいが、それは望む: I looked at Ayane in front of me. She wasn't bad-looking. Her chest was a bit lacking, but I suppose one couldn't have everything.
奏介「ん……もう少し時間を潰そうと思ってな」: Sousuke: “Hnn... just thought I’d kill a little more time.”
奏介「暗がりで男女がすることと云えばひとつだろ？」: Sousuke: “There’s only one thing for a man and a woman to do in the dark, right?”
狭い路地に綾音の声が響く。: Ayane’s voice echoed through the narrow alley.
綾音「こんなことする人だなんて思ってもいませんでしたっ！」: Ayane: “I never thought you were this kind of person!!”
ふっ、と貌を逸らし、綾音が路地を出て行く。: Averting her face, Ayane ran out of the alley.
からかいすぎたか。: Maybe I teased her too much.
左頬を殴られなかっただけマシだろう。: At least she hadn't hit my left cheek.
濡れ鼠はとっとと退散するに限る。: A drowned rat had better make itself scarce.
俺は辺りを警戒しながら、少しずつ自宅のアパートの方角へ歩き出した。: Keeping a watchful eye on my surroundings, I began walking toward my apartment.
幸いにも、自宅の方向に憲兵はいなかった。: Fortunately, there were no military police in the direction of my home.
それどころか誰ともすれ違うことすらなかったので、今の俺の有様にも訝しがられること: In fact, I didn't cross paths with a single soul, allowing me to reach my room without anyone casting a suspicious glance at my current state.
ドアを開け、中へ。: I opened the door and stepped inside.
独身者用の集合住宅のため、すぐに狭い和室が来る。卓袱台の前に座っていた柚芭が驚い: Since it was a collective housing complex for bachelors, the cramped Japanese-style room appeared immediately. Yuzuha, who was sitting at the low table, looked up at me with a startled expression.
柚芭「ただいまじゃないよ、もうっ！！ どうしたの、そんなずぶ濡れでっ！？」: Yuzuha: “Don't you ‘I'm home’ me!! Honestly! What happened to you, getting so soaking wet!?”
奏介「……帰りにちょっと足を滑らせてな、転けた」: Sousuke: “...I just slipped a bit on the way back and took a tumble.”
柚芭「そんなっ……転んだだけでそんな怪我する訳ないでしょう！？ 早くシャツ脱いで: Yuzuha: “That's a lie... you couldn't possibly get hurt like that just from falling!? Hurry and take off your shirt, I have to treat it...”
左袖の血の滲みを見て、柚芭は切羽詰まった声をあげた。: Seeing the blood seeping through my left sleeve, Yuzuha’s voice took on a desperate edge.
奏介「血はもう止まってるから、そんなに心配すんな」: Sousuke: “The bleeding’s already stopped, so don't worry so much.”
柚芭「ダメだよっ！！ 傷口からバイ菌が入ったらどうするのよっ！！」: Yuzuha: “I have to worry!! What if germs get into the wound!?”
柚芭「それにずぶ濡れなんだから、風邪ひいちゃうよっ……」: Yuzuha: “Besides, you're drenched, you'll catch a cold...”
柚芭の声に涙が混じってきた。: Yuzuha’s voice began to tremble with tears.
まあ――確かに、着替えてアパートの地下にある共同浴場で湯に浸かりたい気分ではある: Well—it was true that I felt like changing and soaking in the hot water of the communal bath in the apartment basement.
さすがに人の目が在りそうなところで、この汚れた格好で注目を浴びたくはない。: I certainly didn't want to draw attention to myself in this filthy state anywhere people might be watching.
奏介「――着替え出してくれるか？」: Sousuke: “—Can you get me a change of clothes?”
柚芭が押入に向かう。その間にシャツを脱ぐ。下着まで雨で濡れているようだ。: Yuzuha headed for the closet. In the meantime, I stripped off my shirt. It seemed even my underwear was soaked through from the rain.
柚芭「きゃっ！？ お、お兄ちゃんっ！！ 部屋の中裸でうろうろしないでって言ってる: Yuzuha: “Eek!? B-big brother!! I told you not to wander around the room naked!!”
柚芭「とにかく服着てよっ、もうっ！！」: Yuzuha: “Just put some clothes on already, geez!!”
そっぽを向いたまま、俺の着替えを投げて寄越す柚芭。: Yuzuha threw my change of clothes toward me while keeping her face turned away.
まったく、面倒な年頃だ。: Good grief, she's at a difficult age.
俺の返事にようやく柚芭がこちらを向く。: At my reply, Yuzuha finally turned back toward me.
奏介「それじゃ、風呂に行ってくる」: Sousuke: “Well then, I’m heading to the bath.”
遅い時間だからか、アパートの地下にある共同浴場には誰もいなかった。: Perhaps because it was late, there was no one in the communal bath in the basement.
身体の汚れを落とし、ぬるくなった湯船に浸かる。: I washed the grime from my body and sank into the lukewarm tub.
妙な日だった。: It had been a strange day.
いくら世相が不穏な時期であろうと、自らにあんな災難が降りかかってくるとは。: No matter how turbulent the times were, I never expected such a disaster to befall me personally.
あの女の身に、何が起こっていたのだろうか。: I wondered what had happened to that woman.
奏介「……ま、俺には関係ないな」: Sousuke: “...Well, it’s none of my business.”
正直、どうでもいい。: To be honest, I didn't care.
俺にさえ関わらなければ、誰が何処で何をしていようと関係ない。: As long as it didn't involve me, I didn't care who was doing what or where.
部屋に戻ると、柚芭が布団を二組並べて敷いていた。: When I returned to the room, Yuzuha had laid out two sets of futons side by side.
柚芭「お兄ちゃん、どう？ 気持ちよかった？」: Yuzuha: “How was it, big brother? Did it feel good?”
訳も解らず一方的に襲われた場合、果たして喧嘩と呼んでいいものだろうか。: When you're attacked unilaterally without knowing why, does that really qualify as a fight?
ただの喧嘩と、襲撃されたのとでは言葉の――事態の重みが違う。: A simple fight and being ambushed carried a different weight—both in words and in the severity of the situation.
柚芭「もうっ！ 相手の人は大丈夫なの！？」: Yuzuha: “Honestly! Is the other person okay!?”
本当は知らない。: The truth was, I didn't know.
だが、真実を話すわけにもいかないだろう。: But I couldn't exactly tell her the truth either.
ましてや、女を殴り飛ばしたなどとは。: Much less the fact that I’d struck a woman.
まだ柚芭は疑り深そうな眼差しでこちらを窺っている。: Yuzuha was still eyeing me with a suspicious gaze.
柚芭「ダメだからね、あんまり喧嘩しちゃ」: Yuzuha: “You can't do that, you know, getting into so many fights.”
満足げに柚芭が頷いた。: Yuzuha nodded with a satisfied air.
柚芭「もう夜も遅いし、寝ちゃう？」: Yuzuha: “It’s late already, shall we sleep?”
言うなり布団に横になる。: I lay down on the futon as I spoke.
狂人に襲われるわ、変な女には叩かれるわ。: Attacked by a madwoman, struck by a strange girl.
かすかな呟きを耳聡く聞き付けたのか、柚芭がこちらを向く。: Yuzuha turned toward me, her sharp ears catching my faint murmur.
傷口に当てられていたハンカチも、返せる縁があるかどうか。: I wondered if I’d ever have the chance to return the handkerchief she’d pressed against my wound.
周囲が暗くなると、急に外の雨音が気になるようになってくる。: As the surroundings grew dark, the sound of the rain outside suddenly began to weigh on my mind.
息を吐き、身体の力を抜く。: I exhaled and let the tension leave my body.
ごそごそと、隣の布団に寝間着に着替えた柚芭が潜り込む。部屋が狭いため、別の布団と: With a rustle, Yuzuha, now changed into her nightgown, crawled into the adjacent futon. Because the room was cramped, our bodies felt as if they might touch despite being on separate futons.
身体を動かし、柚芭の方を向く。: I shifted my body and turned toward Yuzuha.
柚芭「今日のピアノ……良かったよ」: Yuzuha: “Your piano today... it was really good.”
柚芭「うん。帰りに美華夏ちゃんとも話してたんだけど、なんかね、音が透明になったと: Yuzuha: “Yeah. I was talking about it with Mikage-chan on the way back, and it felt like... like the sound became transparent, or like the harshness was gone...”
手まで動かして説明しようとする柚芭。: Yuzuha tried to explain, even moving her hands for emphasis.
柚芭「とにかく……良かったよ。お兄ちゃんはあまり店に来るなって言うけど、あたしは: Yuzuha: “Anyway... it was great. You always tell me not to come to the shop so much, but I want to hear you play more.”
柚芭にバー通いはまだ早いと思う。とはいえ、同年代の千花が働いているので、あまり強: I thought it was too early for Yuzuha to be frequenting bars. That said, Chika—who was around the same age—worked there, so I couldn't be too strict about it.
極稀に訪れる酔客に絡まれたりしたら困る。俺は一応用心棒としても雇われているが、酔: I’d be in trouble if she got involved with the rare drunk customer who decided to be troublesome. I was technically hired as a bodyguard too, but even if they were drunk, I couldn't very well go around hitting customers.
少し頬を膨らませ、不機嫌そうに訊いてくる柚芭。: Yuzuha asked with a sulky puff of her cheeks.
柚芭がおとなしくなる。それでも目は開かれたまま、俺の方を見つめている。: Yuzuha grew quiet. Yet her eyes remained open, staring toward me.
居心地悪く、天井に目を向けた。: Feeling awkward, I turned my gaze toward the ceiling.
真新しい木目。築二年という新しい建物であり、室内で煙草を吸うのを柚芭が快く思わな: Fresh wood grain. It was a new building, only two years old, and since Yuzuha didn't approve of me smoking indoors, there was no staining yet.
雨はまだ降り続いている。明日の朝には止むだろうか。: The rain continued to fall. I wondered if it would stop by morning.
明日は何もなければいいが。: I hoped nothing would happen tomorrow.
今日みたいな災難はもうこりごりだ。: I’d had quite enough of disasters like today.
寝てしまったようだ。: She seemed to have fallen asleep.
しばらく柚芭の寝顔を観察する。: I watched Yuzuha’s sleeping face for a while.
柚芭と暮らすようになったのは、このアパートに越してきた直後、一年と少し前の事だっ: We’d started living together a little over a year ago, right after moving into this apartment.
震災以後、俺たちは美華夏の家――橘家に身を寄せていた。亡父と美華夏の父が旧友だっ: Since the earthquake, we’d been staying at Mikage’s house—the Tachibana residence. My late father and Mikage’s father had been old friends.
まだ幼かった柚芭は、両親の貌をほとんど覚えてはいないだろう。だから――柚芭にとっ: Yuzuha, who was still very young then, probably hardly remembered our parents’ faces. So—for Yuzuha, home might mean the Tachibana family.
橘家に近寄ることもほとんどなく、従って柚芭ともほとんど貌を合わせていなかった。: I hardly ever approached the Tachibana house, and consequently, I rarely saw Yuzuha’s face.
そして突然――成長した柚芭がひょっこりと俺の前に姿を現した。: Then suddenly—the grown-up Yuzuha had appeared before me out of the blue.
正直、扱いに困る。どう接すればいいのか判らない。: To be honest, I didn't know how to handle her. I didn't know how to act.
俺も、家族のことなど何も判っちゃいなかったようだ。: It seemed I didn't understand anything about family after all.
柚芭は俺のことを《優しい兄》とよく言う。: Yuzuha often called me a “kind brother.”
だが――違う。優しい訳じゃない。: But—she was wrong. I wasn't kind.
接し方が判らないから、腫れ物に触れるように、慎重に扱っているだけだ。: I just didn't know how to act around her, so I treated her with caution, as if handling something fragile.
思わず笑みをこぼす。: A smile escaped me despite myself.
柚芭に心配をかけさせない為にも、喧嘩は控えなくては。: To avoid making Yuzuha worry, I’d have to cut back on the fighting.
今日のような事がもうないようにと祈りつつ、俺は目を閉じた。: Praying that nothing like today would happen again, I closed my eyes.
窓から差し込む陽射しの眩しさに目を醒ました。: I woke to the dazzling sunlight streaming through the window.
少し頭が痛い。宿酔する程は呑まなかったはずだが。: My head hurt a bit. I shouldn't have drunk enough to have a hangover.
柚芭「あ、お兄ちゃん起きた？ おはよっ」: Yuzuha: “Ah, you’re awake, big brother? Good morning!”
布団の上で身体を起こす。: I sat up on the futon.
柚芭「だって、あたし出かけるんだもん」: Yuzuha: “Well, I have to go out.”
制服を着ているのはその為か。: Is that why she was already in her school uniform?
柚芭「だからほらっ、一緒にご飯食べよっ、ねっ？」: Yuzuha: “So come on, let’s eat breakfast together, okay?”
立ち上がり、布団を片づける。出来た空間に卓袱台を広げ、柚芭が朝食を運んでくる。: I stood up and put away the futon. In the space I’d cleared, I set up the low table, and Yuzuha brought over the breakfast.
米飯に味噌汁、コロッケと漬け物。ごく普通の朝食だ。: Rice, miso soup, croquettes, and pickles. A perfectly ordinary breakfast.
柚芭の号令に俺も箸を取る。: At Yuzuha’s signal, I picked up my chopsticks.
柚芭「お兄ちゃん、『いただきます』は？」: Yuzuha: “Big brother, where’s your ‘thank you for the food’?”
どうも、こういう空気は苦手だ。: I was never very good with this kind of atmosphere.
柚芭「それじゃお兄ちゃん、行ってくるね」: Yuzuha: “Well then, big brother, I’m off.”
柚芭「夕方には帰ってくるけど、お兄ちゃんは今日も仕事なんだよね？」: Yuzuha: “I’ll be back in the evening, but you have work again today, right?”
奏介「ああ。晩飯は食っちまって構わないぞ」: Sousuke: “Yeah. You can go ahead and eat dinner without me.”
ひとりになり、畳の上に寝転ぶ。昨晩の雨が嘘のように晴れ渡った空が窓の外に広がって: Now alone, I sprawled out on the tatami mats. Outside the window, the sky was perfectly clear, as if last night’s rain had been a lie.
バーに貌を出すのは、せめて昼過ぎだ。: I wouldn't show my face at the bar until after noon at the earliest.
早くに起こした柚芭を少し恨みつつ、目を閉じる。: Feeling a slight resentment toward Yuzuha for waking me so early, I closed my eyes.
蝉が喧しく鳴いているようだ。: The cicadas seemed to be buzzing noisily.
今日は暑くなるだろうか。: I wondered if it would get hot today.
そろそろ暑くなって貰わないと、ビールの売り上げが落ちるとマスターがぼやきかねない: The Master would probably start complaining about beer sales dropping if it didn't warm up soon.
少し眠っていたようだ。: I seemed to have dozed off for a bit.
身体を起こし、時計を見る。正午を少し過ぎていた。: I sat up and checked the clock. It was just past noon.
肩を鳴らす。布団もなしで畳の上に眠っていた所為で身体が少し痛む。: My shoulders popped. My body ached a little from sleeping on the tatami without a futon.
この時間ならばバーも開いているだろう。勿論開店している訳ではなく、バーの上の階に: The bar should be open by this time. Of course, it wasn't open for business yet; Chika, who lived on the floor above the bar, was just getting things ready.
服を着替え、鏡を見ながら軽く髪を整える。: I changed clothes and lightly straightened my hair in the mirror.
今日は客が来るだろうか。昨日のような常連――腐れ縁の知り合いばかりというのはごめ: I wondered if any customers would come today. I hoped it wouldn't just be regulars—those acquaintances I was stuck with—like yesterday.
昼過ぎの神楽坂は、雑多な賑わいを見せていた。: Kagurazaka in the early afternoon was a scene of bustling activity.
商店は活気に満ち、毘沙門天には露店が並んでいる。: The shops were full of life, and stalls lined the area around Bishamonten.
花街の方が賑わうのはまだ後の時間だろう。: The pleasure quarters wouldn't start getting busy until later.
坂の両脇には和洋中の料理店から甘味処、カフェーや飲み屋が建ち並んでいる。それだけ: Both sides of the slope were lined with Japanese, Western, and Chinese restaurants, sweet shops, cafes, and bars. Not only that, but venerable old shops that had existed since the Edo period stood side-by-side.
その為裏通りにある店にあまり客が来ないのも仕方がない事だろう。普通の客ならば表通: Under the circumstances, it was perhaps inevitable that not many customers came to a shop on a back street. Any ordinary customer would choose the flashy shops on the main thoroughfare.
曲がり角をいくつか折れ、《ＣＡＤＥＮＺＡ》の前へ出る。: After a few turns, I arrived in front of “CADENZA.”
千花が店の前を箒で掃いていた。: Chika was sweeping the front of the shop with a broom.
予想通りというか何というか。千花は俺の姿を見るや悲鳴を上げて飛び退いた。: Exactly as I’d expected. The moment she saw me, Chika let out a scream and jumped back.
その様子に苦笑する。不意に訪れたとはいえ、そこまで嫌われてるとは思わなかった。: I gave a wry smile at the sight. Even if I’d appeared unexpectedly, I didn't think I was *that* disliked.

千花「何が、って……知らないはずないと思いますけど……」: Chika: “What do you mean, ‘what happened’... I don't think there’s any way you wouldn't know...”
入口に『準備中』という札を掛けながら千花がまくし立てる。こんな彼女も珍しい。: Chika rambled as she hung the ‘Closed’ sign on the entrance. Seeing her like this was rare.
激昂しながらも後退っているのが千花らしいといえばらしいのだが。: It was very like Chika to be backing away even while she was worked up.
マスターの声に振り向く。: I turned at the Master’s voice.
珍しくカウンタを出ているマスターが、苦虫を噛み潰したような表情でこちらを見ていた: The Master, who rarely stepped away from the counter, was looking at me with a sour expression.
マスター「今朝ここに警察が来た。お前を捜しているらしい」: Master: “The police were here this morning. Apparently, they’re looking for you.”
昨日の事だろうか。確かに夢中でボコボコにしたとはいえ、あれは正当防衛だろう。まあ: Was it about yesterday? True, I’d lost myself in the moment and beaten her to a pulp, but surely it was self-defense. Well—I suppose I couldn't exactly make a plea since my opponent was a woman.
マスター「――心当たりはあるようだな」: Master: “—So you do have an idea.”
心なしかマスターの視線が厳しくなる。: The Master’s gaze seemed to grow colder.
マスター「まったく……とんでもない事をしてくれたな」: Master: “Honestly... you’ve done something truly unthinkable.”
マスター「だからといって、殺す事はないだろう」: Master: “Even so, there was no need to kill her.”
俺は……女を叩きのめしはしたが、決して殺してはいない。: I... I’d beaten the woman down, but I certainly hadn't killed her.
殺してはいない――筈だ。: I hadn't killed her—I was sure of it.
マスター「警官の話では……ここから少し北に行った新小川町の路上で女の死体が発見さ: Master: “According to the police... a woman’s body was found on the street in Shin-ogawamachi, a little north of here.”
マスター「そこから走り去る――お前によく似た男が通行人によって目撃されているとの: Master: “A man who looked just like you was reportedly seen running away from the scene by a passerby. They said he was covered in blood, his shirt stained red.”
奏介「ま……待てよ。あれは俺が怪我をして――」: Sousuke: “W-wait a minute. That was because I was injured—”
左の袖をまくる。慌てている所為で傷の上を少し引っ掻き、痛みが奔った。: I rolled up my left sleeve. In my panic, I scratched the wound slightly, and a jolt of pain shot through me.
奏介「……その女にやられた傷だ」: Sousuke: “...The woman did this to me.”
マスター「やられたから殺したのか？」: Master: “So you killed her because she attacked you?”
奏介「……まさか。ついカッとなってボコボコにしたが、決して殺しちゃいない」: Sousuke: “...No way. I lost my temper and beat her up, but I definitely didn't kill her.”
そのあたりの加減は知っている。人はそう簡単に死にはしない。: I knew my own strength. People don't die that easily.
マスター「……女に手を挙げるのも感心せんがな」: Master: “...Raising a hand to a woman isn't exactly admirable either.”
奏介「あれが女かよ。……下手すりゃ俺の方が殺されていたかもしれないんだぜ？」: Sousuke: “As if she were a woman. ...If I hadn't, I might have been the one killed, you know?”
マスター「……聞けば聞くほどお前がやったとしか思えんのだがな……奏介よ、本当に殺: Master: “...The more I hear, the more I can only think you did it... Sousuke, you really didn't kill her?”
奏介「だからさっきから何度も――」: Sousuke: “I’ve told you a dozen times—”
カウンタを拳で叩き、立ち上がる。千花がびくっと身体を竦ませた。: I slammed my fist on the counter and stood up. Chika shrank back in fear.
俺の言う事など何も聞いちゃいない。: He wasn't listening to a word I said.
奏介「これが落ち着いてられるか！？ 濡れ衣着せられて仕事も干されてよおっ！！」: Sousuke: “How am I supposed to be calm!? I’m being framed and now I’ve lost my job!!”
一瞬、脳髄が揺れた。顎が外れたかのような痛み。: For a moment, my brain rattled. A pain as if my jaw had been dislocated.
乱暴にスツールへ腰を下ろす。: I sat down roughly on a stool.
マスター「ここに警官が来たと言ったろう。お前がのこのことピアノを弾いていればすぐ: Master: “I told you the police were here. If you’re just sitting here casually playing the piano, you’ll be caught in an instant.”
マスター「幸い警官はお前の素性を知らなかったようだから何とかごまかしたが……ばれ: Master: “Fortunately, the police didn't seem to know your identity, so I managed to deflect them somehow... but it’s only a matter of time before they find out.”
だったらなぜ、俺が此処にいると判ったのだろうか。: Then why had they known I would be here?
マスター「うむ……目撃者と云うのが、店に何度か足を運んでいた人間らしくてな、お前: Master: “Hm... it seems the witness is someone who’s been to the shop a few times and recognized you.”
マスター「……身を隠した方がいい。今の時勢、冤罪であっても投獄は免れんだろうから: Master: “...You should go into hiding. In these times, you won't escape imprisonment even if it’s a false accusation.”
面倒な事になった。身を隠す？ どこに？ 未だバラックが立ち並び、文明都市の暗部が: What a mess. Go into hiding? Where? Some place where barracks still stood and the dark underbelly of the civilized city spread out—
根岸か浅草か葵町か――それとも一気に川を越えて川崎か千葉の方角か――: Negishi, Asakusa, or Aoicho—or perhaps cross the river all at once toward Kawasaki or Chiba—
ともあれ、自宅には戻れない。柚芭には悪いが、巻き込む訳にもいくまい。: At any rate, I couldn't go home. I felt bad for Yuzuha, but I couldn't involve her.
奏介「柚芭に心配するなと伝えてくれるか？」: Sousuke: “Can you tell Yuzuha not to worry?”
マスター「ふむ。……千花、頼まれてくれるか？」: Master: “Hmph. ...Chika, can you do that for him?”
マスター「これからどうするつもりだ？」: Master: “What do you plan to do now?”
奏介「……さあな。何も判らねえよ」: Sousuke: “...Who knows. I haven't a clue.”
マスターはカウンタ内に戻ると、封筒を投げて寄越してきた。: The Master returned behind the counter and tossed an envelope toward me.
マスター「着の身着のままでは辛いだろう」: Master: “It’ll be hard with just the clothes on your back.”
封筒の中には札束が詰まっていた。一円札で百枚近くはありそうだ。五円や十円札も混じ: The envelope was stuffed with a wad of cash. It looked like nearly a hundred one-yen bills. There were five and ten-yen bills mixed in too.
普段の数ヶ月分はあるだろうか。: It was probably enough to cover several months’ pay.
マスター「たまには貌を出せ。店が開いている時は困るが、閉店後になら、何時来ても構: Master: “Show your face once in a while. I can't have you here while the shop’s open, but you’re welcome anytime after we close. ...Just make sure no one sees you.”
マスターが一旦言葉を切った。: The Master paused for a moment.
マスター「……本当に、殺してないんだな？」: Master: “...You really didn't kill her, did you?”
立ち上がり、店の外へ出ようとする。: I stood up and prepared to leave the shop.
千花は俺を止めると、扉から貌を出して外の様子を窺った。: Chika stopped me, then peeked out the door to check the surroundings.
千花「……今なら大丈夫です。……あ、あとっ……その、頑張ってください。気をつけて: Chika: “...It’s okay now. ...A-and... um, do your best. Be careful...”
軽く片手を挙げ、俺は午後の雑踏に紛れ込んだ。: Raising a hand slightly, I vanished into the afternoon crowd.
あまり目立つ振る舞いは出来ない。: I couldn't afford to draw attention to myself.
かと云って、自宅に戻る訳にもいかない。: On the other hand, I couldn't go back home.
さらに、この辺りは現場にも近く、捜査中の警官と鉢合わせしてしまう可能性もあった。: Furthermore, this area was close to the scene, and there was a chance I’d run into police officers conducting their investigation.
上野へ向かうか、浅草まで出るか。: Should I head for Ueno, or maybe Asakusa?
鉄道で万世橋まで行くか。それとも七銭払って市電に乗り、都合の良さそうな所で降りる: Take the train to Manseibashi? Or pay seven sen to ride the streetcar and get off wherever seemed convenient?
いや――駅舎のような人の集まる場所には警官が張り込んでいそうだ。遠方への逃走を防: No—the police were likely staked out at places where people gathered, like train stations. To prevent someone from escaping to a far-off place, waiting at the station was the best bet.
ならば徒歩か。外堀に沿って歩けばさほどの距離でもないが、やはり目立つ。: In that case, I’d have to go on foot. If I walked along the outer moat, it wouldn't be that far, but it would still be conspicuous.
小石川から本郷の裏道を抜けていくしかなさそうだ。: I’d have to take the back roads from Koishikawa through Hongo.
色眼鏡をかける。陽射しが遮られ辺りが薄暗くなる。これでも変装のつもりだが、もしか: I put on my tinted glasses. The sunlight was blocked, and the surroundings grew dim. It was meant to be a disguise, but it might just make me stand out even more.
まあ――俺だと気付かれなければ問題ないだろう。: Well—as long as no one recognized me, it shouldn't be a problem.
指名手配されるのがこれほどまでに面倒な事だったとは。: I never realized being wanted by the police would be such a hassle.
大通りを避け、建物の間を縫うように延びる細道を進む。次第に方向感覚が狂いだし、ど: I avoided the main streets and took the narrow paths that wound between the buildings. Gradually, my sense of direction began to falter, and I lost track of which way I was heading.
小石川区を抜けて本郷区へ入ったあたりだろうか。それともすでに下谷区まで来てしまっ: I must have passed through Koishikawa and entered Hongo. Or perhaps I’d already reached Shitaya.
帝大が近くにあるはずなのだが見あたらない。まあ確たる目標があって歩いているわけで: The Imperial University should have been nearby, but I couldn't see it. Well, I wasn't walking with a specific goal in mind, so anywhere I could hide was fine.
――治安はあまり良くないようだ。先程からずっと数人の男に後ろを付けられている。こ: —The public order didn't seem to be very good around here. Several men had been following me for a while now. Some local thugs, perhaps?
昨日怪我をした左腕の調子を確認する。傷痕が残っているため痛みがあるが、腕を振るう: I checked the condition of my left arm, which I’d injured yesterday. It was still painful because of the scar, but it seemed there was no problem with swinging it.
奏介「まあ――俺もちんぴら、か」: Sousuke: “Well—I suppose I’m just a punk myself.”
苦笑する。今の俺だって後ろの連中と大した変わりがない。世間に顔向けできないのは同: I gave a wry smile. I wasn't much different from the guys behind me right now. Neither of us could show our faces in polite society.
その江戸時代から変わっていないような呼び止め方に思わず吹き出しそうになる。: The way he called out to me, as if nothing had changed since the Edo period, almost made me burst out laughing.
振り返り改めて男たちを見ると、なるほど如何にもちんぴらといった風貌を漂わせていた: I turned around and looked at the men; sure enough, they had the unmistakable look of low-life thugs.
気障ったらしい妙な男がにやにやと嗤いながら近づいてきた。: A strange, affected man approached with a leering grin.
男「なぁ……アンタ、ここらじゃ見ねぇ面だ――」: Man: “Say... you’ve got a face we don't see around these parts—”
最後まで聞かず、鳩尾につま先をめり込ませる。: I didn't wait to hear the rest before burying my toe in his solar plexus.
腹を抱えて蹲った男に変わり、大柄な男が前に出てきた。: As the man curled up, clutching his stomach, a large man stepped forward.
側頭部に蹴り。男はその体格通りに転がりながら塀にぶつかった。: A kick to the side of the head. The man went tumbling into a fence, his large frame echoing the impact.
昨日の夜の事を思い返す。こんな攻撃じゃ人は死なないはずだと確認する。: I thought back to last night. I confirmed for myself that people didn't die from attacks like this.
もっと殴っていたか。ピアノ弾きという立場も忘れ、昨日は拳を使ってしまった。: Had I hit her more than this? I’d forgotten my position as a piano player and used my fists yesterday.
今は左腕の怪我もあって無理はできない。: Right now, I couldn't push myself too hard because of the injury to my left arm.
転がる男を蹴り続ける。先程までの威勢は何処へ行ったのか、ちんぴらは手足を丸め、少: I continued to kick the man as he rolled on the ground. Where had all that bluster from before gone? The thug curled up his limbs, making a futile attempt to minimize the pain.
男「このくらいで勘弁してくれ。殺される訳には――」: Man: “T-that’s enough, have mercy. I can't afford to be killed—”
俺の肩を揺さぶってきた男を薙ぎ払う。: I swiped at a man who tried to grab my shoulder.
奏介「この程度で死ぬかよ――」: Sousuke: “As if this could kill you—”
まだ昨日の半分の力も出していない。: I hadn't even used half the strength I’d used yesterday.
俺が素手で人を殺せる訳もない。: There was no way I could kill someone with my bare hands.
男の呻き声が徐々に小さくなっていく。: The man’s groans gradually grew quieter.
昨日の女はまだ動いていた筈だ。: The woman yesterday had still been moving.
では何故この男はこうも容易く蹲っているのか。: Then why was this man crouching so easily?
男「ひっ……！ よ、弱いから勘弁してくれっ……」: Man: “H-eek...! I-I’m weak, so please spare me...”
俺がふと洩らした呟きにも反論しようとしない卑屈な男の姿に、俺は不意に憐れみを感じ: Seeing the servile man who wouldn't even argue with my casual murmur, I suddenly felt a pang of pity.
蹴り続けていた足も怠くなってきた。: My leg was getting tired from all the kicking.
這々の体で男が逃げ出す。: The man scrambled away as fast as he could.
奏介「……何してんだ、俺は」: Sousuke: “...What the hell am I doing?”
目立ってはならないと自重していたはずなのに、気付けば路上で喧嘩をしている。いや―: I was supposed to be keeping a low profile, yet here I was, fighting in the street. No—it couldn't even be called a fight; it was just one-sided violence.
勿論、俺自身の事だ。: I was referring to myself, of course.
奏介「――さて、どうしたモンか……」: Sousuke: “—Well now, what should I do...?”
気付けば神田川を越え、九段下の辺りまでやって来ていた。: Before I knew it, I’d crossed the Kanda River and reached the Kudanshita area.
上野方面へ向かおうと考えていたのに、いい加減なモンだ。: I’d intended to head toward Ueno, but I was being careless.
大きな建物が並び、通りに人が溢れている合間を縫うように乗合自動車が走っている。: Large buildings lined the streets, and buses wove through the throngs of people.
主要な交差点には派出所があり、警官が周囲に目を配っているようだが、俺に気付いた様: There were police boxes at the major intersections, and the officers were keeping a watchful eye on their surroundings, but they didn't seem to have noticed me.
それもそうだ――こんなに賑わっている街で、ひとりひとりの貌を判別し、それが犯罪者: Of course they hadn't—in a city this bustling, no one could possibly identify every single face and recognize a criminal.
怪しげなそぶりさえ見せなければ大丈夫だろう。派出所の前を通る際、露骨に貌を伏せた: As long as I didn't act suspicious, I should be fine. As long as I didn't conspicuously lower my head when passing a police box.
そう考えると、少しだけ余裕が出てきた。: When I thought about it that way, I started to feel a bit more relaxed.
さっきのような喧嘩沙汰は避けなければ――: I had to avoid brawls like the one just now—
……そう、厄介な事は極力避けなければならない。: ...Yes, I had to avoid trouble as much as possible.
じっとこちらを見ている怪しげな男と目が合ってしまった。異様に鋭い視線。もしかする: I’d made eye contact with a suspicious man who was staring intently at me. An unnervingly sharp gaze. Perhaps he was a spy for the Special Higher Police.
何気なさを装って場所を移動するも、男の目は俺をずっと追ってきている。多くの人々が: I moved away as if nothing were wrong, but the man’s eyes followed me relentlessly, even through the crowd.
こんな場所――二・二六事件の際は戒厳司令部が置かれた軍人会館の目の前――で屋台を: Operating a stall in a place like this—right in front of the Army Hall where the Martial Law Headquarters had been located during the 2.26 Incident—was suspicious in itself.
特高ではなく陸軍憲兵隊の手の者か。それでも充分に厄介だ。: Was he a member of the Army’s military police rather than the Special Higher Police? Either way, it was a major pain.
関わり合いにならない方がいい。: It was better not to get involved.
……心なしか、怪しげな男が哀しげな貌をしたような気がした。: ...For some reason, I thought the suspicious man looked sad.
馬鹿げている――そう思いつつも、俺はその屋台へ近づいていった。: It was ridiculous—yet despite my thoughts, I approached the stall.
奏介「――おい、親父、何を売ってるんだ？」: Sousuke: “—Hey, old man, what are you selling?”
親父は無言で傍らの値札を指さす。: The old man silently pointed to a price tag nearby.
奏介「……いや、値段の前に何を売ってるか――」: Sousuke: “...No, before the price, what are you selling—”
辛気くさい声で親父は呟いた。: The old man muttered in a gloomy voice.
親父の手元を見れば、丸いくぼみがいくつもある黒い鉄板が敷かれている。: Looking at the old man’s hands, I saw a black iron plate with several round indentations.
その上に、小麦粉で出来た生地を広げている。: He was spreading a batter made of flour over it.
親父「昔は《ちょぼ焼き》とか《ラジオ焼き》とか言われてたもんだ……。誰が考えたん: Old Man: “They used to call it ‘Choboyaki’ or ‘Radioyaki’... I don't know who thought of it, but they went and put octopus in it out of nowhere...”
親父「厭になるぜ……まったくよぉ……」: Old Man: “It’s enough to make you sick... honestly...”
ぶつぶつと愚痴を洩らす親父。: The old man grumbled and complained under his breath.
こいつは――売る気があるのか？: Was this guy—even interested in selling anything?
親父「偉いさんってのはいつもそうだ。テメェの都合でばかり物事を考えやがって……迷: Old Man: “The big shots are always like that. Only thinking about things for their own convenience... and it’s always us vermin who have to suffer for it... heh-heh...”
親父「――で、兄さん……買うのか？」: Old Man: “—So, young man... you buying?”
見つめてくる目が怖い。: The way he stared at me was terrifying.
ここで買わないと言ってしまうと、取り返しの付かないことになりそうだ。: If I said I wasn't buying, I felt like something irreversible would happen.
親父は鼻で嗤うと、錐のような道具で焼き色の付いた生地をひっくり返し始めた。: The old man gave a derisive snort and began flipping the browned batter with a tool that looked like an awl.
面白いように、生地が丸くなる。: It was interesting to watch the batter turn into round balls.
その固まりが十三個――包みに入れられた。: Thirteen of those balls—were placed into a package.
正直、あまり有難みを感じない。: To be honest, I didn't feel particularly grateful.
屋台から離れ、人気のないところで包みを開ける。: I moved away from the stall and opened the package in a deserted spot.
十三個並んだタコ焼きと云う物体。: Thirteen takoyaki arranged in a row.
湯気が上がり、確かに香ばしい匂いはしているが――: Steam was rising, and they certainly smelled savory, but—
奏介「……まあ、曲がりなりにも売り物だから喰えないと云う事はねえだろ」: Sousuke: “...Well, it’s a commercial product, so I suppose it won't be inedible.”
覚悟を決め、楊枝でひとつ摘んで口の中へ放り込む。醤油だろうか、香ばしい匂いが口の: Steeling myself, I picked one up with a toothpick and popped it into my mouth. Was it soy sauce? The savory aroma spread through my mouth—
味なんか解らない。: I couldn't even tell what it tasted like.
だが吐き出すわけにもいかない。: But I couldn't very well spit it out.
ようやく飲み込む。: I finally managed to swallow it.
落ち着いて食えば旨いのかもしれない。: If I ate them calmly, they might be delicious.
包みの中は残り十二個。: There were twelve left in the package.
おまけの分は絶対余計だと、俺は心底後悔していた。: I deeply regretted that bonus piece.
俺は踵を返し、その場を後にした。: I turned on my heel and left the area.
ずっと背中に妙な男が放つ射るような視線を感じていた。: I could feel the man’s piercing gaze on my back the entire time.
結局神楽坂の方まで戻ってきてしまった。: In the end, I found myself drifting back toward Kagurazaka.
やはり、俺はこの辺りの方が落ち着くようだ。: I suppose this neighborhood is where I feel most at ease, after all.
狭い路地を縫い、大通りを横切り三業地の方へ。: I wove through the narrow back alleys, crossing the main thoroughfare toward the licensed quarters.
花街に入り浸って捜査の目を眩ますのも悪くはない。幸いにして金はある。: Hiding away in the pleasure district to blind the eyes of the law wasn't a half-bad idea. Fortunately, I had the cash to burn.
どこの芸妓屋に入ろうかと散策していた時だった。: I was wandering about, trying to decide which geisha house to duck into, when it happened.
はじめは芸者の呼び込みかと思った。だがすぐに間違いだと気付く。: At first, I thought it was just a geisha soliciting a customer. But I realized my mistake almost immediately.
何というか――雰囲気が全く違う。男に媚びるような仕草はなく、どこか男を従属させる: How should I put it—the atmosphere was entirely different. There was no trace of the usual flattery meant to entice men; instead, she radiated a quiet authority that seemed to demand submission.
事実、その女の背後には何人かの男が付き従っているようだった。如何にも破落戸といっ: In fact, several men were lingering behind her. A rough-looking bunch of thugs with faces that screamed ‘trouble.’
びくん、と背筋に寒気が奔る。: A sudden, icy chill shot down my spine.
そういえば目撃者がいたとマスターが言っていた。: The Master had mentioned a witness.
この女が一部始終を見ていた――？: Did this woman see the whole damn thing—?
逃げるか。……いや、それよりもあの時の事を聞き出すべきか。: Should I run? No, it might be better to squeeze the truth out of her first.
俺が殺していないと云う証明が出来れば全ては解決するのだが。: If I can prove I didn't kill anyone, all of this is over.
――いや、それも無理か。まず俺が殺していない事を納得させる必要がある。: —No, that’s a pipe dream. First, I’d have to convince her I’m innocent.
なにせ彼女は俺が殺したと思いこんでいるからこそ、警察に通報したのだろうから。: After all, if she’s already convinced I’m a murderer, she’s probably the one who called the cops in the first place.
？？？「――何を考えているのかしら？」: ???: “—I wonder what’s going through that head of yours?”
女が口元に薄く笑みを浮かべる。: The woman’s lips curled into a thin, knowing smirk.
クソッ――何を考えているのか判らないのはお互い様だろうに。: Damn it—neither of us has a clue what the other is thinking.
強請る気か。それとも警察に連れて行く気か。後者である予感があまりしないのは、女の: Is she planning to blackmail me? Or is she going to hand me over to the police? The only reason I don't feel the latter is likely is because of the sheer, intoxicating danger she exudes.
女が軽く片手を挙げる。と、背後に控えていた男たちが動き、俺を逃がさないかのように: The woman gave a slight wave of her hand. At that, the men behind her moved as one, surrounding me to cut off any hope of escape.
退路を断たれた。女を睨み付けるが、彼女は全くと言っていいほど反応を見せない。: They’d blocked my exit. I glared at her, but she didn't show so much as a flicker of a reaction.
奏介「……手前等をのして帰れるくらいには、な」: Sousuke: “...Strong enough to drop these bastards and walk away.”
足元に転がっている小石をつま先で払いのけ、女に対して半身の姿勢を取る。当然周りの: I kicked aside a pebble with the toe of my boot and shifted into a fighting stance, keeping half my body toward the woman. I kept a sharp eye on the thugs around me too.
危険な匂いがする女の笑み。: There was a predatory edge to her smile that set my nerves on edge.
喧嘩みたいな目立つことはするまいと先程誓ったばかりなのに――: I’d just sworn to myself not to do anything that would draw attention—like getting into another brawl—
？？？「――さっき破落戸を叩きのめしたのを見かけたわよ」: ???: “—I saw you take down those punks earlier.”
……ああ、そう云う事か。この女は先程の喧嘩を見ていたようだ。: ...Ah, I see. This woman must have been watching that fight.
其処から尾行していたと云う訳か。: Which meant she’d been tailing me ever since.
馬鹿馬鹿しい。ひとりで勝手に最悪の事態を妄想していたようだ。: How pathetic. I’d been working myself into a panic over the worst-case scenario all on my own.
？？？「――それ程でもない、と言いたそうね。結構な自信じゃない」: ???: “—‘It was nothing,’ is that what you’re thinking? You’ve certainly got plenty of confidence.”
俺が鼻で笑ったのを誤解したのか、女は口元をほんの僅か歪める。: Misinterpreting my derisive snort, the woman’s mouth twisted just a fraction.
奏介「いや――何、少し安心しただけさ。気にするな」: Sousuke: “No—it’s nothing. I’m just a little relieved, that’s all. Don't worry about it.”
そう言いながらも、女は舐めるように俺の頭から足先までを見てくる。蛇に身体を這われ: Even as she said it, her eyes traveled over me from head to toe, as if she were tasting me with her gaze. It felt like a snake slithering across my skin.
奏介「先刻から何なんだ？」: Sousuke: “What’s with you? You’ve been at this for a while now.”
品定めされているようで居心地が悪い。: Being sized up like a piece of meat made my skin crawl.

葵「名乗らなくてもいいわ」: Aoi: “There’s no need to introduce yourself.”

葵はきつい一瞥をくれると部屋を出て行った。: With a sharp glance, Aoi left the room.

あの身体を抱かなかったのは少々勿体ないような気もしたが、危険であることが判ってい: It felt like a bit of a waste not to have taken that body, but there was no need to invite trouble when I already knew how dangerous she was.

葵の爪先が動き、俺の股間を刺激する。: Aoi’s toes moved, stimulating my groin.
