柚芭には済まないが、ひとりでいるのも悪くない。: I felt bad for Yuzuha, but being alone wasn't so bad.
特に、今のような状況にあっては。: Especially in a situation like this.
右手を見る。: I looked at my right hand.
殺して――いないはずだ。: I hadn't—at least, I shouldn't have.
あの時は殴って蹴っただけだ。: I’d only hit and kicked her.
お陰で指が少し痛んでしまった。: And as a result, my fingers were a little sore.
ピアノ弾きとして、これは失敗だった。: As a piano player, that was a failure.
だが――もうピアノ弾きの仕事は来ない。: But—I wouldn't be getting any more work as a piano player.
だったら――構わないんじゃないか？: In that case—it didn't really matter, did it?
呆と室内を眺める。部屋の片隅――壁際にある黒い箱。: I stared vacantly around the room. In the corner—against the wall—was a black box.
奏介「――染みついた習性は直せそうもないな」: Sousuke: “—I guess I can't break old habits.”
立ち上がり、ピアノの元へ。蓋を開け、純白の鍵盤に触れる。: I stood up and walked over to the piano. Opening the lid, I touched the pristine white keys.
沈黙した部屋に静かに鳴る音。: The sound rang out quietly in the silent room.
昨日だって弾いたはずなのに。: Even though I’d played just yesterday.
椅子に座り、両手を鍵盤へ。: Sitting on the bench, I placed both hands on the keys.
勝手に指が動き、慣れ親しんだ音色を奏でる。: My fingers moved of their own accord, playing a familiar melody.
弾いている間は何もかも忘れられる。: While I was playing, I could forget everything else.
何もなかった事になれば――: If only everything could just disappear—
見てもいない女の死体が脳裏に浮かんでくる。: The image of a dead woman I hadn't even seen floated into my mind.
いや――本当に見ていたとしたら？: Wait—what if I really *did* see her?
奏介「……ンな訳ねえだろ」: Sousuke: “...As if that could be true.”
女を殴ったのは事実。: I hit the woman; that was a fact.
だが殺すまではやっていない。: But I didn't go so far as to kill her.
汗が頬を伝い、ぽたぽたと鍵盤の上に落ちる。: Sweat trailed down my cheek and dripped onto the keys.
今日はもう寝てしまおう。: I’ll just go to sleep for today.
明日になれば、また何かが変わるかもしれない。: Maybe something will change by tomorrow morning.
――それが少しでも良い方向であればいいのだが。: —I only hope it’s a change for the better.
柔らかい布団の中で目を醒ました。: I woke up enveloped in the softness of the futon.
一瞬ここがどこだか判らなかった。: For a split second, I didn't even know where I was.
上体を起こし、室内を見回して、改めて此処が隠れ家であったことを思い出す。: I sat up, scanning the room, before the realization hit me again—this was my hideout.
目覚めは悪くない。: Waking up didn't feel half bad.
高級そうな布団のお陰だろうか。: It was probably thanks to this high-end bedding.
自宅の硬い布団で眠っているだろう柚芭に少し申し訳なく思う。: I felt a twinge of guilt for Yuzuha, who was likely sleeping on the hard futon back at our apartment.
突然の声に驚き振り向くと、葵が椅子に腰掛けていた。: Startled by the sudden voice, I spun around to find Aoi perched in a chair.
葵「フフ……可愛い寝顔だったわよ。襲っちゃいたくなるくらい」: Aoi: “Heh... you have such a cute sleeping face. Almost made me want to pounce on you.”
朝からそんな話はげんなりする。: I wasn't in the mood for that kind of talk first thing in the morning.
葵「ええ。簡単で単純な仕事。初めての貴方にはちょうどいいでしょう？」: Aoi: “Yes. A quick, simple job. For a novice like you, it’s a perfect start, don't you think?”
葵「フフ……それは始める前に言うわ」: Aoi: “Heh... I’ll tell you before we begin.”
まあ、それは最初から覚悟していた事なのだが。: Well, I’d been prepared for that from the start.
葵「報酬は……そうね、五十円もあれば充分かしら？」: Aoi: “The pay... let’s see, fifty yen should be plenty, right?”
五十円――公務員の月収とほぼ同じ額だ。: Fifty yen—that was nearly a month’s salary for a civil servant.
それだけの金額を一度の仕事で払うとは、ある程度は危険な仕事なのだろう。それも法に: Paying that much for a single job meant there was a certain level of risk involved. Likely something that flirted with breaking the law.
葵「納得したのなら行くわよ」: Aoi: “If you’re satisfied, let’s move out.”
決して納得した訳ではないのだが、俺は大人しく葵について部屋を出た。: I wasn't exactly satisfied, but I followed Aoi out of the room without a word.
アパートから暫く北へ歩き、根津の込み入った小径に入ったところで葵が足を止めた。: We walked north from the apartment for a while, stopping only when we reached the cramped alleys of Nezu.
そう言い残し、バラックのような建物の中に入っていく。: With those parting words, she disappeared into a shack-like building.
予想通りというか何というか、碌でもない仕事のようだ。: Just as I’d suspected—this was going to be a nasty piece of work.
ふと考え、すぐに首を振る。: The thought crossed my mind, but I shook my head immediately.
――無駄だ。この場だけ対処できたとしても、連中は必ず俺を見つけ出すだろう。: —Useless. Even if I escaped this moment, they’d track me down.
見つけられなくなるほど周到に隠れたとしても――次に狙われるのは柚芭だ。: And even if I hid well enough to stay buried—Yuzuha would be their next target.
なんで俺がこんな目に――: How the hell did I end up in this mess—
葵が出てきた。手に何やら四角い紙包みを持っている。: Aoi emerged, holding a rectangular paper-wrapped bundle.
葵「今日はこれをある所へ届けてもらうわ」: Aoi: “Today, I need you to deliver this to a certain location.”
受け取ると、それほど大きい訳ではないのだが、ずっしりとした重みがあった。: I took it from her; it wasn't particularly large, but it had a substantial weight to it.
葵「中を確認してちょうだい」: Aoi: “Why don't you check the contents for yourself?”
笑みを浮かべる葵。厭な予感がする。: Aoi flashed a smirk. I had a bad feeling about this.
袋の口を少し開け――俺は厭な物を見てしまった。: I pried the opening of the bag a bit—and saw exactly what I’d dreaded.
ぎっしりと隙間なく詰め込まれた札束。二十円紙幣でざっと一千枚――それ以上はあるだ: Stacks of bills, packed tight with no room to spare. At least a thousand twenty-yen notes—maybe more.
葵「三万円あるわ」: Aoi: “There’s thirty thousand yen in there.”
奏介「……土地でも買ってこいと？」: Sousuke: “...You want me to go buy a plot of land or something?”
葵「それを持って戸山ヶ原へ行ってちょうだい。そこである男が接触してくるでしょうか: Aoi: “Take that to Toyamagahara. A man will contact you there; just hand it over to him.”
新宿の近く、山手線内にあるのにもかかわらず寂寥とした地。陸軍の学校や練兵場もある: A desolate place near Shinjuku, despite being inside the Yamanote line. It housed Army schools and drill grounds, but most of it was just an empty field of pampas grass.
葵「怪しげな取引にはうってつけの場所でしょう？」: Aoi: “A perfect spot for a shady deal, wouldn't you say?”
奏介「手前ェで怪しげとか言うなよ」: Sousuke: “Don't call your own business shady.”
話にならない。: There was no arguing with her.
葵「それと引き替えにある物を渡されるでしょうから、今度はそれを事務所へ届けてちょ: Aoi: “He’ll give you something in exchange, so bring that back to the office.”
まったく――これからはこんな仕事ばかりなのだろうか。: Honestly—was my life just going to be jobs like this from now on?
こんな仕事、受ける方がおかしい。: You’d have to be insane to accept a job like this.
奏介「誰か他の奴に頼んでくれ。俺が三万円の運び屋なんて――荷が重すぎる」: Sousuke: “Ask someone else. Me, a courier for thirty thousand yen—the weight’s too much to carry.”
葵「断る権利があると思ってるのかしら？」: Aoi: “Do you really think you have the right to refuse?”
俺の頬を張った体勢のまま、葵はにこやかに問いかけてきた。: Aoi asked with a pleasant smile, her hand already connecting with my cheek in a sharp slap.
葵「あたしはお願いしてるんじゃないの。命令してるのよ」: Aoi: “I’m not asking you a favor. I’m giving you an order.”
奏介「――判ったよ、行けばいいんだろ？」: Sousuke: “—Fine, I get it. I just have to go, right?”
葵「聞き分けの良い男は好きよ」: Aoi: “I like a man who knows how to listen.”
こうなれば仕方ない。毒を食らわばという奴だ。: I had no choice. In for a penny, in for a pound.
葵「くれぐれも落としたりしないでちょうだいね」: Aoi: “Whatever you do, don't go dropping it.”
葵「あと、逃げたりしたら……判ってるわね？」: Aoi: “And if you think about running... you know what happens, right?”
これだけの金を持たされたら逃げたくもなる。: Carrying this much cash, the urge to run was definitely there.
葵「――それじゃ、よろしく頼むわね」: Aoi: “—Well then, I’m counting on you.”
葵と別れ、戸山ヶ原へ向かう。: I parted ways with Aoi and headed for Toyamagahara.
このまま汽車に飛び乗ってどこかへ逃げようかという考えがふと脳裏をよぎったが、どう: The thought of hopping on a train and disappearing somewhere crossed my mind, but they were likely keeping me under surveillance.
下手な事をして危害を加えられるのなら、素直に仕事を終わらせてしまった方がいい。: If doing something stupid meant putting myself in harm's way, it was better to just get the job over with.
人の波を掻き分けながら、神楽坂を上っていく。: I wove through the crowds, heading up Kagurazaka.
スリに遭わないよう、紙包みはしっかりと抱える。: I clutched the paper bundle tight to make sure no pickpockets got lucky.
辺りに怪しい奴がいないかと目を配っていたのだが――: I kept a sharp eye out for anyone suspicious—
肩がぶつかった少女を見て、俺は動きを止めた。: I stopped dead when I saw the girl I’d bumped shoulders with.
千花「く、玖藤さん……っ！？ どっ、どうしてこんな処にっ……！？」: Chika: “K-Kudou-san...!? W-what are you doing in a place like this...!?”
奏介「仕事中だ。ピアノ弾きを干されちまったからな」: Sousuke: “Working. Since I got fired from my piano gig.”
千花「あ、あのっ、そう云う事じゃなく……っ、誰かに見つかったら……」: Chika: “O-oh, that’s not what I meant... i-if someone finds you...”
大変申し訳ないが、千花の仕草が一番人目を惹いている。: I felt terrible, but Chika’s behavior was drawing the most attention.
外に出る仕事が来たという事は、すでに官憲の捜査対象から外されていると考えていいだ: The fact that I’d been given a job that required me to be out in public meant I was likely already off the authorities' immediate radar.
それでも目立たないように行動した方がいいのは当然だろうが。: Though, obviously, it was still better to keep a low profile.
千花「わたしがぼうっとしてて……ぶつかっちゃって、その……玖藤さん、逃げなきゃい: Chika: “I was just spacing out... and I bumped into you, and... and Kudou-san, you’re supposed to be hiding...”
奏介「少なくとも、千花が心配するようなことじゃない」: Sousuke: “At the very least, it’s not something you need to worry about, Chika.”
苦手とされている所為か、やたらとやりにくい。: Because she was so timid, she was incredibly difficult to talk to.
千花「いえっ、あの……珈琲豆を買いに……」: Chika: “No, um... I was out to buy coffee beans...”
千花「玖藤さん……今、どんなお仕事をされているのですか？」: Chika: “What... what kind of work are you doing now?”
正直に言う訳にもいくまい。: I couldn't exactly tell her the truth.
何とか誤魔化せればいいのだが……。: I just had to find a way to dodge the question...
千花「あっ……す、すいませんっ！ 出しゃばったこと言っちゃって……」: Chika: “Ah... I-I’m sorry! I shouldn't have been so forward...”
千花「わたしなんかが訊いても答えてなんてくれないですよね……」: Chika: “There’s no way you’d answer someone like me...”
正直、扱いに困る。: Honestly, I didn't know how to handle her.
奏介「おおっぴらに言えるような仕事じゃないんだ。勘弁してくれ」: Sousuke: “It’s not the kind of work I can talk about openly. Give me a break.”
悪いのは俺だ。: I was the one to blame.
奏介「マスターによろしく言っておいてくれ」: Sousuke: “Give my regards to the Master.”
俺は彼女に軽く片手を挙げ、その場をあとにした。: I gave her a light wave and left the scene.
戸山ヶ原にある箱根山に登ると、眼下に大東京を一望できる。: Climbing Mt. Hakone in Toyamagahara offered a panoramic view of Greater Tokyo.
遠くには東京湾も見える。: In the distance, I could even see Tokyo Bay.
辺りに人影はない。: There wasn't a soul in sight.
確かに取引には最適の場所かもしれないが……。: It was certainly an ideal spot for a trade...
汗を拭う。辺りに陽射しを遮る物がないため、夏の日光が直接降りかかってくる。: I wiped the sweat from my brow. With nothing to block the sun, the summer heat beat down on me directly.
時間にはまだ余裕がある。: I still had time to spare.
周囲を薄ぼんやりと眺めていると、南の方向から男が山を登ってくるのが見えた。: As I scanned the surroundings, I saw a man climbing the hill from the south.
どう見てもちんぴら風の怪しげな男だ。: He looked like a suspicious thug, no matter how you sliced it.
男「アンタ、《清鳳会》の使いかい？」: Man: “You the messenger from the Seihoukai?”
男は俺の近くまで来ると、辺りをはばかるような小声で問いかけてきた。: The man approached me, asking in a low voice that suggested he was wary of his surroundings.
男「例の物は持ってきた。アンタも用意はいいか？」: Man: “I brought the goods. You ready on your end?”
三万円と釣り合いのとれる物とは一体なんなのだろうか。見たところ男は手ぶらで、何も: I wondered what kind of item could possibly be worth thirty thousand yen. From what I could see, the man was empty-handed.
考えていても仕方がない。俺は男に金の入った包みを手渡した。: There was no point in overthinking it. I handed the bundle of cash to the man.
早速男は紙包みの中を覗き込み、口笛を吹いた。: The man immediately peered into the paper bundle and let out a whistle.
男「――確かにあるようだな。……ほら、持ってけ」: Man: “—Looks like it’s all here. ...Here, take it.”
男が懐から封筒を出してきた。: The man pulled an envelope from his coat.
受け取ってみると、宛名も何もない茶封筒で、蝋で厳重に封がされているので中身は判ら: I took it; it was a plain brown envelope with no address, sealed tight with wax so the contents were hidden.
蝋に捺された印章も見た事のないものだ。: The seal stamped into the wax was one I’d never seen before.
奏介「判ってる。子供の使いじゃないんだ」: Sousuke: “I know. I’m not a child running an errand.”
男は元来た道をそそくさと立ち去っていった。: The man scurried off the way he’d come.
これであとは、この封筒を葵の元へ持ち帰るだけだ。: Now all that was left was to bring this envelope back to Aoi.
三万円という重圧こそあったが、これではやはり子供の使いと変わらない。: Despite the pressure of carrying thirty thousand yen, this really was no different from a child’s errand.
まあ――さほど危険がないのはありがたいが。: Well—I was grateful there hadn't been much danger.
牛込柳町の電車通りを歩いて神楽坂まで戻る。: I walked along the streetcar tracks of Ushigome Yanagicho and returned to Kagurazaka.
夕刻が近い所為か、路のあちこちに気の早い酔客がふらついていた。: Since evening was approaching, early drunkards were already stumbling about here and there.
そんな街を横目で眺めつつ《清鳳会》の事務所へ向かう。: I glanced at the streets as I headed for the Seihoukai office.
事務所にはいると、椅子に座っていた葵が腰を上げた。: When I entered, Aoi, who had been sitting in a chair, stood up.
奏介「これでいいんだろ？」: Sousuke: “This is what you wanted, right?”
葵がナイフで封を切り、中の書類を確認する。: Aoi sliced the seal with a knife and checked the documents inside.
奏介「そんな紙切れ一枚に三万円か……」: Sousuke: “Thirty thousand yen for a single scrap of paper...”
葵「あら。そんな事言っていいのかしら？」: Aoi: “Oh? Do you really think you should be saying that?”
葵は書類を俺に向けて寄越した。: Aoi held the document out toward me.
奏介「俺が見たって仕方のない物だろ」: Sousuke: “What’s the point of me looking at it?”
葵はそう言うと、書類を封筒に戻す。: With that, Aoi tucked the document back into the envelope.
葵「これ以降、貴方を憲兵隊が追うことはないわ。警察もほぼ同様ね。交渉がうまくいっ: Aoi: “From now on, the military police won't be chasing you. Same goes for the regular police. It seems the negotiations were a success.”
奏介「その事が書いてあったのか……？」: Sousuke: “That’s what was written in there...?”
葵「ええ、そうよ。……実際、貴方に三万ものお金をつぎ込む意味があるのかは疑問だけ: Aoi: “Yes, exactly. ...Though I have to wonder if it was really worth dropping thirty thousand on a man like you.”
何でもない俺のために三万もの大金を出すとは。: Dropping a fortune for a nobody like me.
葵「――これで貴方、ますます逃げられなくなったわねぇ？」: Aoi: “—Which means you’re even less likely to escape us now, doesn't it?”
葵「――まぁ、真面目なお巡りさんには賄賂も効かないでしょうから、注意するのは今ま: Aoi: “—Though bribes won't work on the truly earnest officers, so you’ll have to be just as careful as before.”
随分と無駄な金を使ったもんだ。: A hell of a lot of money wasted.
葵「また何かあったらよろしくね」: Aoi: “I’ll be counting on you if anything else comes up.”
面倒な処に入ってしまったかもしれない。: I might have stumbled into a real mess.
今年は厄年だったろうか。: Was this a bad luck year for me?
そのうち厄払いにでも行った方が良さそうだ。: Maybe I should go get an exorcism or something.
この街はこれからが本番だ。夜店が並び、料亭が賑わい、芸妓が客を引く。: This town was just getting started. Stalls lined the streets, restaurants were bustling, and geisha were enticing customers.
ここには俺の貌を知っている奴もいる。あまり目立った振る舞いも出来ず、貌を伏せて道: There were people here who knew my face. I couldn't afford to stand out, so I walked with my head down.
奏介「本当に――犯罪者みてえだな」: Sousuke: “I really do feel like a criminal.”
裏通りを行くと、いつの間にかバーの前に出ていた。: I took the back alleys and found myself in front of the bar before I knew it.
厚い扉は閉ざされている。すでに営業を終了したのだろうか。: The heavy door was closed. Had they already finished for the day?
マスター『閉店後になら、何時来ても構わないぞ』: Master: “You’re welcome anytime after we close.”
人寂しいのだろうか。自分でもよくわからない。: Was I lonely? I didn't even know.
あれ以降――碌に人と話していない。: Since all this started—I hadn't really talked to anyone properly.
普段は邪魔だと感じている周囲の人間。: Usually, I found the people around me to be a nuisance.
誰かに愚痴でも聞かせたいのだろうか。: Maybe I just wanted someone to listen to my griping.
俺はゆっくりとバーの扉を開けた。: I slowly pushed open the door to the bar.
千花「申し訳ありません。本日はもう営業を――」: Chika: “I’m so sorry. We’ve already closed for the—”
床を箒で掃いていた千花が振り返り、言葉を止める。: Chika, who was sweeping the floor, spun around and went silent.
カウンタ席に腰掛ける。: I took a seat at the counter.
千花「何か飲まれますか？ ……お酒はお代をいただくようにとマスターから言われてま: Chika: “Would you like something to drink? ...The Master told me to make sure you pay for any alcohol...”
千花がカウンタに入り、ウィスキーのボトルとグラスを出してくる。: Chika went behind the counter and brought out a bottle of whiskey and a glass.
千花はカウンタ内の奥にある冷蔵庫へと向かった。: Chika headed toward the refrigerator in the back.
ここの冷蔵庫は珍しく電気で動いている。しかもマスターの知り合いが改造をしたらしく: The refrigerator here was rare in that it ran on electricity. Apparently, an acquaintance of the Master had modified it so it could drop to temperatures you wouldn't normally expect.
氷を作る事が出来ると云うのは、それだけでも充分話題になるはずなのだが、マスターは: The fact that it could make ice should have been a major talking point, but the Master rarely boasted about it.
千花「お待たせしました。……七十銭です」: Chika: “Sorry for the wait. ...That’ll be seventy sen.”
律儀な千花に思わず苦笑を洩らす。: I couldn't help but let out a wry smile at her earnestness.
千花「……判りました。マスターに伝えておきます」: Chika: “...Understood. I’ll let the Master know.”
からん、と氷が揺れた。: The ice clinked as it settled.
千花「……それでは、わたしはこれで」: Chika: “...Well then, I’ll take my leave.”
千花「いえ……マスターに言いつかっていましたから。……あ、あと」: Chika: “No... I was told to look after things. ...Oh, also.”
千花がカウンタの影から鍵を取り出した。: Chika pulled a key from the shadows of the counter.
千花「合鍵……渡しておきますね」: Chika: “I’ll give you a spare key.”
真鍮製の小さな鍵。振り返り重厚な扉を見る。こんな小さな鍵で、外の世界と簡単に遮ら: A small brass key. I looked back at the heavy door. Something so small could so easily shut out the rest of the world.
千花「何かあったら呼んでください。……玖藤さんなら大丈夫かと思いますけど」: Chika: “Please call me if anything happens. ...Though I’m sure you’ll be fine, Kudou-san.”
言外に呼ぶ必要はないことを臭わせ、千花は店の奥へ入っていった。二階の自室に戻るの: Implicitly suggesting there was no need to call her, Chika headed into the back of the shop. Likely returning to her room on the second floor.
嫌われたもんだ。それでも今回は少し長く話したような気がする。マスターや璃宝がいな: I guess she really does dislike me. Still, it felt like we talked longer than usual this time. Probably because the Master and Riho weren't around.
グラスを傾ける。ほんの少し甘みがあった。砂糖を入れたのだろう、オールド・ファッシ: I tilted my glass. There was a faint sweetness to it. She must have added sugar; it tasted like an Old Fashioned.
ひとりで飲んでいると、厭でも店内の静寂が耳につく。: Drinking alone, the silence of the bar began to grate on my ears.
ピアノの前へ。塵ひとつ付いていない黒い表面を撫で、グラスを置く。: I walked over to the piano. I stroked the dust-free black surface and set my glass down.
適当に鍵盤を叩く。: I idly tapped at the keys.
あくまで静かに。千花が上の階にいる。: Quietly, of course. Chika was upstairs.
沈黙を嫌いはしたが、騒々しいのも厭だ。: I hated the silence, but I didn't want noise either.
ただ何も考えずに弾く。: I just played, thinking of nothing.
全てがどうでも良くなってくる。: Everything was starting to feel insignificant—
何が起ころうと――何を起こそうと――俺には関係なく――: Whatever happened—whatever I did—it had nothing to do with me—
全て忘れてしまえれば――: If only I could just forget it all—
演奏を終えた途端、拍手の音が店内に響いた。: The moment I finished playing, the sound of applause echoed through the shop.
千花が降りてきたのかと思ったが、違った。: I thought Chika had come back down, but I was wrong.
何時の間にか、店内に人が入って来ていた。スツールに腰掛けてこちらを見ているのは、: Someone had entered the shop without me noticing. The person sitting on a stool watching me looked like a girl who hadn't even reached adulthood yet.
この場の雰囲気にそぐわない少女は、その外見には似付かわしくない大人びた眼差しをこ: Looking out of place in this atmosphere, the girl fixed me with a mature gaze that belied her youthful appearance.
口元に薄く微笑みを浮かべ、少女が一礼する。: The girl gave a polite bow, a faint smile on her lips.
奏介「……ピアノがいいんだろうさ」: Sousuke: “...It’s a good piano, that’s all.”
鍵盤を撫でながら答える。: I answered while stroking the keys.
少女はこちらの質問を敢えて無視したようだ。答えたくないのか。それならそれでいい。: The girl seemed to have deliberately ignored my question. Maybe she didn't want to answer. Fine by me. I wasn't interested anyway.
少女は立ち上がると、ゆっくりとこちらへ歩いてくる。: The girl stood up and walked slowly toward me.
少女が指さした先に、確かに会社の名前が刻まれていた。: Where she pointed, the company name was indeed engraved.
？？？「単純な事の積み重ねで世界は出来ています」: ???: “The world is built upon an accumulation of simple things.”
単純ならば――俺がこんな妙な事に巻き込まれたりはしていないはずだ。: If it were—I wouldn't have been dragged into all this nonsense.
？？？「ラプラスの悪魔というものがあります」: ???: “There is a concept known as Laplace’s Demon.”
奏介「悪ぃが、神様や悪魔なんて信じない質でね」: Sousuke: “Sorry, but I’m not the type to believe in gods or demons.”
俺の言葉に彼女は静かに首を振った。: At my words, she shook her head slowly.
？？？「古典物理学の一理論です。ある一瞬において、世界中全ての原子の位置及びベク: ???: “It is a theory of classical physics. It suggests that if one knew the position and vector of every atom in the universe at a single moment, the future could be determined.”
？？？「つまり――世界というものは原子の動きのみで予測されてしまえるほど単純なの: ???: “In other words—the world is simple enough to be predicted solely by the movement of atoms.”
奏介「俺は莫迦だからよく解らねえよ」: Sousuke: “I’m just a fool, I don't really understand that stuff.”
それきり黙る少女。何なんだろうか。: With that, the girl went silent. What was her deal?
頭は良さそうだが、訳が判らない。: She seemed smart, but she made no sense.
少女――久遠はじっと俺の目を見ている。: The girl—Qwon—stared intently into my eyes.
久遠「奏介、何か弾いてください」: Kuon: “Sousuke, please play something for me.”
慇懃無礼という程ではないが、断る事を赦さないような口調。: Her tone wasn't exactly impolite, but it carried an authority that seemed to brook no refusal.
まあいい。どうせ暇なのだ。: Whatever. I had nothing better to do.
彼女の返事に思わず苦笑を洩らす。正直なのも考えものだ。: I couldn't help but let out a wry smile at her reply. Being that honest was its own kind of problem.
椅子に座り直す。久遠はピアノの向こう側、音響板の影に隠れるような場所に座ったよう: I sat back down. Qwon took a seat on the other side of the piano, half-hidden by the soundboard.
先程と同じ、静かな調べ。: The same quiet melody as before.
観客がひとりであれば、音を抑えていても充分店内に響き通る。: With only one listener, even a muted sound was enough to fill the shop.
無心で指を動かす。: I moved my fingers without thought.
俺は何をしているんだ。: What the hell am I doing?
官憲に追われる身でありながら、こんな所で女相手にピアノを弾いている。: Wanted by the law, and here I am playing the piano for a girl.
のんびりしたもんだ。: I was certainly taking my sweet time.
バーの扉のすぐ向こうで警官が待ちかまえているかもしれないのに。: Even though a policeman might be waiting right outside the door.
久遠の呟きが耳についた。: Qwon’s murmur grated on my ears.
鍵盤にフェルト布を掛けながら訊ねる。: I asked as I laid the felt cloth over the keys.
久遠「貴方が満足いく演奏をされたのなら、私も満足しているでしょうね」: Kuon: “If you were satisfied with your performance, then I suppose I am as well.”
久遠「比喩も暗喩もなく、言葉通りの意味です。奏介は今の演奏に満足していないようで: Kuon: “I mean exactly what I said, without metaphor or allusion. It seems you aren't satisfied with your performance just now, Sousuke.”
久遠「貴方の曲は演奏時の感情が直接伝わって来ます。不安、焦燥、恐怖……ない交ぜに: Kuon: “Your music conveys your emotions directly as you play. Anxiety, irritation, fear... a jumble of negative emotions directed straight at the listener.”
奏介「よく――言う」: Sousuke: “You’ve got a lot to say.”
欠点――演奏だけではなく自分自身の――を指摘されているのに、不思議と腹は立たなか: I was being called out on my flaws—not just in my playing, but in myself—yet strangely, I didn't feel angry.
その指摘が的を射ているからかもしれない。: Perhaps it was because her assessment was so spot on.
久遠「聴いていると――貴方と同じ感情に囚われてしまうかもしれませんね」: Kuon: “Listening to it—one might find themselves trapped by the same emotions as you.”
それは音楽家にとって望むべき事なのではないか。聴衆に自分の思いを伝えられるのなら: Wasn't that exactly what a musician was supposed to want? If you can convey your thoughts to an audience, there’s no greater success.
問題は、俺の鬱屈した負の感情という処か。: The problem was the sheer weight of my own gloomy, negative emotions.
久遠「彼のベートーヴェンも、病に冒された晩年に残した作品の方が崇高であったと聞き: Kuon: “I’ve heard that even Beethoven’s works from his later years, when he was plagued by illness, were more sublime.”
ほんの少し、久遠の口元に笑み。: A tiny smile played on Qwon’s lips.
久遠がゆっくりと扉の方へ歩いていく。: Qwon walked slowly toward the door.
久遠「それではまた――ここで逢いましょう、奏介」: Kuon: “Until we meet again—let’s see each other here, Sousuke.”
一礼し、久遠は店を出て行った。: With a bow, Qwon left the shop.
俺はしばし、ピアノの前で呆けたように座っていた。: I sat at the piano for a while, stunned.
見た目よりはずっと大人びている。いや――大人びていると云うよりは何か達観している: She was far more mature than she looked. No—it was more like she had a certain sense of detachment.
捉え処がないと云うか、とにかく不可思議な少女。: I couldn't get a handle on her; a truly mysterious girl.
どうも薄気味が悪い。どこからか見張られているような気分がする。: I was starting to feel creeped out. It felt like I was being watched from somewhere.
先程の少女の澄んだ視線に今でも見つめられているような気がした。: I felt as if her clear gaze were still fixed on me even now.
奏介「悪ぃがそんな気分じゃない」: Sousuke: “Sorry, but I’m not in the mood.”
久遠は淡々と、非難めいた事すら言わずに頷いた。: Qwon nodded calmly, without a hint of reproach.
久遠「でしたら、少しお話に付き合ってください」: Kuon: “In that case, please talk with me for a bit.”
この少女と共通する話題があるとは思えなかったが、グラスを取り、カウンタ席へ移動す: I didn't think I had anything in common to talk about with this girl, but I picked up my glass and moved to the counter.
氷はすでに溶け、ウィスキーは薄くなっていた。: The ice had already melted, and the whiskey was watered down.
久遠がカウンタに入り、棚に並ぶ洋酒からスコッチの瓶を手に取った。: Qwon went behind the counter and reached for a bottle of Scotch from the shelf.
奏介「それじゃなくて白札を頼む」: Sousuke: “Not that one, give me a White Label.”
軽口を叩いたつもりだったが、久遠は普通に納得してしまったようだ。: I’d intended it as a bit of lighthearted banter, but Qwon seemed to take it at face value.
奏介「お前も何か飲むか？」: Sousuke: “You want something to drink too?”
久遠「お金がありません。一文無しです」: Kuon: “I have no money. I’m penniless.”
久遠はカウンタの中でしゃがみ込む。立ち上がった時には手にソーダ水の瓶が握られてい: Qwon crouched behind the counter. When she stood up, she was holding a bottle of club soda.
彼女はこの辺りにある遊郭で働く新造だろうか。それでも表を出歩くなら無一文というこ: Was she a newcomer at one of the brothels around here? Even so, she wouldn't be walking around with nothing if she were just out and about.
ならば遊郭を抜け出したのか。それなら金を持っていないのも納得がいくが、どうも彼女: Perhaps she’d escaped? That would explain the lack of money, but her atmosphere didn't suggest that at all.
どちらかと云えば上流階級の娘――それにしては服装も華美ではなく、むしろ質素なほう: If anything, she looked like the daughter of an upper-class family—though her clothes weren't flashy, rather on the plain side. Still, they suited her strangely well.
本当に何者だろうか。: Who the hell was she, really?
久遠を見ると、彼女はグラスを両手で持ち、表情を曇らせていた。: Looking at Qwon, she held the glass with both hands, her expression clouding over.
奏介「そりゃ、ただの炭酸水だからな。砂糖を溶かしてやるといい」: Sousuke: “Well, it’s just plain soda water. You should dissolve some sugar in it.”
苦笑を洩らしながら助言してやる。: I gave her the advice with a wry smile.
カウンタにある珈琲用の砂糖壺を渡してやる。: I handed her the sugar jar used for coffee from the counter.
ソーダ水の中で砂糖の粒が舞う。: Grains of sugar danced in the soda water.
久遠「――今年の雪は凄かったですね」: Kuon: “—The snow this year was truly something, wasn't it?”
奏介「ん……ああ、そうだな」: Sousuke: “Hm... oh, yeah, I guess so.”
二月の初めだったか、東京に五十年振りの大雪が降った。: It was early February, I think, when Tokyo had its heaviest snowfall in fifty years.
バーが面した小径も雪で埋まり、除雪に駆り出されたのを覚えている。: I remembered the narrow alley the bar faced being buried in snow and having to help clear it.
久遠はじっとグラスの中で砂糖が舞う様子を眺めている。: Qwon stared intently at the sugar dancing in her glass.
その問いに、彼女は僅かに視線を上げる。: At my question, she raised her gaze slightly.
久遠「御巫久遠です。それ以外の名は――ありません」: Kuon: “I am Mikanagi Qwon. I have no other name.”
よく通る声。: Her voice was clear and carried well.
久遠「――ええ。貴方と同じ程度には変でしょうね」: Kuon: “—Yes. About as strange as you, I imagine.”
くすり、と久遠が笑う。言葉とは違い、邪気のない微笑み。: Qwon gave a little chuckle. Unlike her words, her smile was innocent.
それでも彼女にはないだろう。: Still, she likely didn't have the same problems I did.
俺のように官憲に追われ、身を隠さねばならないような問題は。: Being hunted by the law and forced into hiding.
久遠がカウンタから出てくる。: Qwon stepped out from behind the counter.
久遠「――それでは、私はこれで。ごちそうさまでした」: Kuon: “—Well then, I’ll take my leave. Thank you for the drink.”
どうせ俺もそろそろ帰ろうと思っていたのだ。多少の寄り道は問題ない。: I’d been thinking of heading home soon anyway. A little detour wouldn't be a problem.
少女にひとり夜道を往かせる事に抵抗があったのもある。まあ――指名手配犯が送るのも: Part of it was a reluctance to let a young girl walk the night streets alone. Though—there were likely issues with a wanted criminal being her escort.
久遠「いいえ。私は大丈夫です。それよりも――」: Kuon: “No. I’ll be fine. More importantly—”
久遠と視線を交わす。彼女の目は硝子玉のように俺を見返している。: I met Qwon’s gaze. Her eyes stared back at me like glass beads.
久遠「ラプラスの悪魔は近年、量子力学によって否定されていますから」: Kuon: “Laplace’s Demon has been disproven by quantum mechanics in recent years, after all.”
随分と前の事を蒸し返してきたようだ。: She was bringing up something from a while ago.
久遠「――それでも未来は不変かもしれませんけど」: Kuon: “—Though the future may still be unchangeable.”
久遠が意味ありげに俺の目を見る。: Qwon looked into my eyes meaningfully.
何か――全てを見透かされているような気がする。: I felt as if—as if she could see right through me.
なんとかそれだけを絞り出す。: It was all I could manage to squeeze out.
大層な名前だ。やはり良家の出身だろうか。だがそれよりも気になることは――: Quite a name. She must be from a good family after all. But what really concerned me—
奏介「……訳がわかんねえな……」: Sousuke: “...I don't understand her at all...”
あの何を考えているのか判らない瞳、だろうか。: It was those eyes, the ones that made it impossible to tell what she was thinking.
目が覚めると、すでに時刻は正午を回っていた。: By the time I woke up, it was already past noon.
上体を起こし、部屋の中を確認する。: I sat up and scanned the room.
今日は仕事がないと云う事だろうか。: Maybe there was no work today.
それならそうでゆっくり出来る。: If that was the case, I could actually relax.
俺は再びベッドの上に寝転んだ。: I sprawled back out on the bed.
昨日の仕事によって、官憲に追われる確率は低くなったのだから外へ出てもいいが、どう: Thanks to yesterday’s job, the risk of being hunted by the law had dropped, so I could go outside, but I felt strangely sluggish.
かと云って此処に居てもする事がない。: Then again, there was nothing to do if I stayed here.
どうせ外に出た処で何もしないのだろうから、このまま部屋でピアノを弾いていると云う: I wouldn't be doing anything even if I went out, so staying in and playing the piano didn't seem like a bad alternative.
立ち上がり、ピアノの側へ。: I stood up and walked over to the piano.
指を動かしていれば、ある程度は余計な事を忘れられる。: As long as I kept my fingers moving, I could forget the unnecessary thoughts—to an extent.
だが――ある程度、でしかなかった。: But it was only—to an extent.
ダメだ。気が乗らない。: It was no use. My heart wasn't in it.
もしかしたら、聴いている人がいないからだろうか。: Maybe it was because there was no one to listen.
バーで弾いている時でさえ、俺は全く客の事など意識してはいなかった。: Even when I played at the bar, I was never conscious of the customers.
いまさらひとりで弾くのがつまらないと云ったら――ただの寂しがりのようではないか。: If I were to say it was boring to play alone now—I’d sound like nothing more than a lonely child.
今更そう感じた所でどうする事も出来ない。: Even if I felt that way now, there was nothing I could do about it.
何処かで時間を潰し、夜になったらバーへ行こう。: I’d kill some time somewhere and head to the bar when night fell.
マスターに愚痴るつもりはないが、酒を呑めば少しは気が紛れるような気がした。: I didn't intend to whine to the Master, but I felt like a drink might help clear my head.
外へ出ると、自然に足がこの街へ向かっていた。: Once outside, my feet naturally led me toward that part of town.
この時間では、バーに行くにはまだ早い。もう少し時間を潰したいが、あまりふらつくの: It was still too early for the bar. I wanted to kill more time, but I felt like wandering aimlessly wasn't a good idea either.
とりあえずは、人通りの少ない所に居る方がよさそうだ。: For the time being, it seemed best to stay somewhere with fewer people.
此処ではあまりにも――人目に付きすぎる。: In a place like this—I’d draw far too much attention.
大通りから一本内側の路地に入ると、途端に静かになる。: The moment I stepped into an alley one street away from the main thoroughfare, everything grew quiet.
猫が一匹、目の前を横切っていった。: A single cat darted across the path in front of me.
落ち着いた、静寂の街――だと思っていたのだが。: A calm, silent town—or so I thought.
単なる痴情の縺れなら無視して通り過ぎるのだが――: If it were just a lovers' spat, I’d ignore it and keep walking, but—
面倒には関わりたくない。: I didn't want to get involved in any trouble.
先日の夜のような事はもうこりごりなのだが――: I’d had quite enough after the other night—
何処かで聴いた声だと思えば……。: Just as I thought the voice sounded familiar...
男「色街をひとりで歩いてるなんざ、男を誘ってるとしか思えねえだろ？」: Man: “A girl walking through the pleasure district alone? You’re practically begging for a man to notice you, aren't you?”
男「大人しく俺らと一緒によぉ……へへへ……」: Man: “Just come along with us, nice and quiet... heh-heh-heh...”
綾音「だから、そんなんじゃないですってばっ！！」: Ayane: “I told you, it’s not like that at all!!”
見かけてしまった以上、声をかけなければならないだろう。: Now that I’d seen it, I had to say something.
安っぽい正義感など持ち合わせては居ないが、見覚えのある女が絡まれているのを無視す: I didn't possess any cheap sense of justice, but it left a bad taste in my mouth to ignore a woman I recognized being harassed.
男「ああん？ 何だテメェ？」: Man: “Huh? Who the hell are you?”
男「やんのか、コラァ！？」: Man: “You want some of this, punk!?”
少しは恫喝の文句を工夫して貰いたいもんだ。: I wish they’d put a little more effort into their threats.
綾音が俺を見て息を呑む。: Ayane gasped when she saw me.
奏介「どうして、と言われてもな」: Sousuke: “Even if you ask me why...”
単なる成り行きと答えるべきだろうか。: I suppose I should just call it a twist of fate.
男「チッ……兄ちゃん、横から来てかっさらう気かよ」: Man: “Tch... brother, you think you can just swoop in and steal her away?”
男「女と知り合いだか何だか知らねぇが、邪魔すんじゃねぇよ」: Man: “I don't care if you know her or not, don't get in our way.”
一歩前へ踏み出す。男たちは綾音を手放す気などないのか、彼女から離れようとはしない: I took a step forward. The men showed no sign of letting go of Ayane; they wouldn't move away from her.
男「へへっ……色男ぶりやがっ――」: Man: “Heh... acting like a tough guy—!”
男が言い終わるよりも早く、俺はそいつの顎を蹴り上げていた。: Before the man could finish his sentence, I’d delivered a kick to his jaw.
奏介「――だから五月蠅ぇって」: Sousuke: “—I told you to shut up.”
倒れた男に向かって吐き捨てる。: I spat the words at the man on the ground.
残った男が綾音を突き飛ばし、懐からナイフを取り出した。: The remaining man shoved Ayane aside and pulled a knife from his coat.
片足を引き、腰を落として腕を前に出す。: I pulled one leg back, dropped my center of gravity, and held my arms out.
腰溜めにナイフを構えて突進してくる男をかわし、その首筋に向け手刀を叩き込む。: I dodged the man as he lunged with the knife held low, and delivered a knife-hand strike to his neck.
転げ落ちたナイフを遠くに蹴り飛ばし、蹲る男の腹に蹴りを入れる。: I kicked the dropped knife far away and delivered a kick to the stomach of the crouching man.
奏介「――弱すぎるクセに粋がるなよ」: Sousuke: “—Don't act so tough when you’re this weak.”
反論も出来ずにうめく男を尻目に、俺は綾音に向き直った。: Leaving the man to groan without a comeback, I turned back to Ayane.
奏介「――こんな処ひとりでふらついていたら、そりゃどんな女でも男に引っかけられる: Sousuke: “—If you wander around a place like this alone, any woman is bound to get picked up by some man.”
綾音「なっ……！？ そ、そんな言い方ってないと思いますけどっ！！」: Ayane: “Wha—!? T-that’s a horrible way to put it!!”
綾音「わたしはただ散歩してただけですからっ！！」: Ayane: “I was just taking a walk!!”
俺の言い種が癇に障ったのか、綾音は頬を膨らませて俺に詰め寄ってきた。: Perhaps my words had touched a nerve, as Ayane puffed out her cheeks and stomped over to me.
奏介「まあこれで、借りは返しただろ」: Sousuke: “Well, I suppose this settles my debt.”
綾音「貸しなんて作った覚えありませんからっ！」: Ayane: “I don't recall you having any debt to me!!”
奏介「この前助けられただろ。それで、今日助けてやってチャラだ」: Sousuke: “You helped me out the other day. So today, we’re even.”
綾音「助けて欲しいなんて……言ってませんから」: Ayane: “I never said... I wanted to be helped.”
奏介「じゃあ放っておいて構わなかったのか？」: Sousuke: “So I should have just left you alone?”
奏介「そこらにのびてる連中を叩き起こしてやろうか？」: Sousuke: “Shall I go wake those guys up for you?”
綾音「やめてくださいっ！！ ……もぉ、なんでそんな意地悪いこと言うんですかっ！？: Ayane: “Please don't!! ...Honestly, why do you have to be so mean!?”
奏介「お前が素直じゃないからだ」: Sousuke: “Because you aren't honest with yourself.”
綾音「どうしてわたしが悪いみたいな言い方されないといけないんですかっ！？」: Ayane: “Why do you have to talk as if I’m the one in the wrong!?”
綾音「あなただって警察に追われたりしてて悪者みたいじゃないですかっ！！」: Ayane: “You’re the one being chased by the police like a common criminal!!”
奏介「悪者には助けられたくない、か……」: Sousuke: “I suppose you don't want to be helped by a criminal, then...”
綾音「あ、いやっ、そんなんじゃなくてっ……」: Ayane: “Ah, no, it’s not like that...!”
奏介「やれやれ……踏んだり蹴ったりだ」: Sousuke: “Typical... it’s just one disaster after another.”
まあ、いきなり通報されるよりましだろう。: Well, I suppose it was better than her calling the police on the spot.
奏介「……じゃあな。あんまりひとりでふらついてるんじゃねえぞ」: Sousuke: “...See you. Don't go wandering around alone so much.”
奏介「俺みたいな――殺人鬼が彷徨いてるかもしれねえからな」: Sousuke: “A—murderer like me might be lurking around, after all.”
綾音「昨日……新聞で読みました」: Ayane: “Yesterday... I read about it in the newspaper.”
綾音がうつむき加減で口を開く。: Ayane spoke with her head down.
綾音「一昨々日、この近くであった遊女殺人事件……玖藤さんの名前がありました。《兇: Ayane: “The murder of the geisha that happened near here three days ago... your name was in there. They called you a ‘vicious murderer.’”
新聞に名前まで載るとは。昨日の仕事――恐らく官憲の買収工作は遅かったと云う事か。: So my name made it into the papers. It seemed yesterday’s job—likely the attempt to bribe the authorities—was too late.
綾音「あの時わたしは、玖藤さんが兇悪犯に見えないと言いましたよね？ それは今でも: Ayane: “I told you back then that you didn't look like a criminal, didn't I? That hasn't changed.”
綾音「玖藤さん……本当は殺してなんかいないんじゃないですか？」: Ayane: “Kudou-san... you didn't really kill her, did you?”
綾音「理由は今言いました。玖藤さんが人を殺すとは思えません」: Ayane: “I just told you the reason. I don't think you’re the kind of person who could kill someone.”
じっと俺の目を見てくる綾音。まっすぐな、澄んだ瞳。: Ayane stared intently into my eyes. Straight, clear eyes.
何も穢れを知らない純真無垢な――: Pure and innocent, knowing nothing of the world’s filth—
奏介「――俺が殺したと言ったら？」: Sousuke: “—And if I told you I did kill her?”
なぜ其処まで断言できるのだろうか。: How could she be so certain?
俺自身でさえ、自分が殺っていないと云う確たる根拠すらないと云うのに――: When even I didn't have any firm proof that I hadn't done it—
近くまで警察が迫っていた。放置されて死ぬような事はない筈だ。: The police were closing in. There was no way she could have been left to die.
殺しては――いない筈だ。: I shouldn't—I shouldn't have killed her.
綾音「……貴方が殺人を犯したとは、到底思えません」: Ayane: “...I simply cannot believe you committed murder.”
綾音「普通、殺人を犯した直後の人間は、異常な興奮状態にあります。それが玖藤さんに: Ayane: “Normally, someone who has just committed murder is in a state of abnormal excitement. I didn't see that in you at all.”
綾音「――まぁ、いきなり口説かれそうにはなりましたけど」: Ayane: “—Though, you did almost try to seduce me out of nowhere.”
平板な声で睨んでくる綾音。: Ayane glared at me, her voice flat.
奏介「……酔ってたんだ。でなきゃこんな変な女引っかけるか」: Sousuke: “...I was drunk. Why else would I pick up such a strange woman?”
綾音「変な女って言わないでくださいっ！」: Ayane: “Don't call me a strange woman!!”
奏介「……世の中には平然と人殺しをやってのける連中もいるだろうに」: Sousuke: “...There are people in this world who can kill without a second thought, you know.”
綾音「玖藤さんはそう云う人間なんですか？ 違うでしょう？」: Ayane: “Are you that kind of person? I don't think so.”
少なくとも、平然と人を殴れる人間であることは間違いない。: At the very least, I was definitely someone who could hit someone without a second thought.
つまり――いつでも平然と人を殺せる人間になれると云う事だ。: Which meant—I could become someone who could kill without a second thought at any time.
綾音「はっきりしてくださいっ！ なんで、そうやって曖昧にして自分を誤魔化そうとす: Ayane: “Be clear about it!! Why do you keep trying to deceive yourself with such ambiguity!?”
俺が何をしたかは俺がよく知っている訳で――: I knew what I’d done better than anyone—
だから誤魔化しているように見えるのか。: Is that why it looked like I was deceiving myself?
聞いているし、効いている。: Listening, and feeling the weight of it.
無関係な綾音の言葉が、当事者であるのに何も覚えていない俺の莫迦な脳髄に効いている: The words of the uninvolved Ayane were hitting home in my own foolish brain, which couldn't remember a thing despite being the one involved.
綾音「とにかくっ、自信を持ってくださいっ！ 玖藤さんはやってないんですからっ！！: Ayane: “Anyway, have some confidence!! You didn't do it!!”
奏介「どうしてそう言い切れる？」: Sousuke: “How can you be so sure?”
綾音「あの晩、玖藤さんは雨でぐしょ濡れでした。腕の怪我もあって血まみれでしたけど: Ayane: “That night, you were soaking wet from the rain. You were covered in blood because of the injury to your arm... but you weren't covered in anything like blood spatter.”
綾音「人を殺したのなら、必ず返り血を浴びてしまうはずです」: Ayane: “If you’d killed someone, you would have definitely been covered in blood spatter.”
奏介「……扼殺の場合は出血がないと思うがな」: Sousuke: “...I imagine there wouldn't be much blood in the case of strangulation.”
綾音「それはないと思います。新聞には《現場には夥しい鮮血が流れていた》と書かれて: Ayane: “I don't think that was it. The newspaper said ‘a large amount of fresh blood was spilled at the scene.’”
綾音「多少新聞記者が誇張して書いた部分もあるかと思いますが、基本的に嘘は書かれて: Ayane: “I’m sure the reporters exaggerated a bit, but I don't think they’d outright lie about the basics.”
奏介「やれやれ……まるで探偵だな」: Sousuke: “Good grief... you’re like a detective.”
思わず苦笑する。: I couldn't help but let out a wry smile.
綾音「そんな大した事じゃないですよ。わたしはただ目に付いた情報を集めただけですも: Ayane: “It’s not that big a deal. I just gathered the information that caught my eye.”
奏介「情報を集めるだけでも大したもんだ」: Sousuke: “Even just gathering information is quite a feat.”
俺には出来そうもない。: I didn't think I could do it.
まあ――情報を集めるという行為が面倒くさいと云うのが一番の理由だが。: Well—mostly because the act of gathering information was such a pain.
綾音「それと……勘かな。わたし、結構勘が鋭いって言われるんです」: Ayane: “And... maybe it’s intuition. People often tell me I have a sharp intuition.”
綾音「わたしは玖藤さんが無実だと信じてます。だから、絶対他に真犯人が居る筈なんで: Ayane: “I believe you’re innocent. So there must be a real culprit out there somewhere.”
ひとり盛り上がる綾音に、俺は苦笑混じりの溜息を吐いた。: Watching Ayane get all worked up on her own, I let out a sigh mixed with a wry smile.
奏介「どうしてそんなに首を突っ込みたがる？ 俺とアンタはそもそも無関係だし、決し: Sousuke: “Why are you so determined to stick your nose into this? You and I are strangers, and this is far from safe, you know?”
奏介「ハッ――現実と物語は全く別モンだぞ？」: Sousuke: “Hah—reality and stories are completely different things, you know?”
綾音「でも……少年探偵団って楽しそうだと思いませんか？」: Ayane: “But... don't you think a ‘Boy Detectives Club’ sounds like fun?”
綾音「知らないんですか？ 江戸川乱歩の小説ですよ。明智探偵を助ける小林少年たちの: Ayane: “You don't know? It’s from Edogawa Ranpo’s novels! The stories of young Kobayashi and the others helping the detective Akechi. You haven't heard of the Fiend with Twenty Faces?”
奏介「乱歩は名前だけなら知ってるが……小説なんざ読んだこともない」: Sousuke: “I know Ranpo by name... but I’ve never read any of his novels.”
奏介「――ま、協力してもらえるのはありがたいがな」: Sousuke: “—Well, I suppose I’m grateful for the help.”
頼れる者もいない現状では、味方となってくれる人がいるというだけで心強い。: With no one else to turn to, having someone on my side was a comforting thought.
綾音「それじゃ、もっといろいろと調べてみますね」: Ayane: “In that case, I’ll look into things some more!”
こんな俺に味方して、彼女になんの得があるのだろうか。: What did she have to gain by taking my side?
ただ単に、刺激を求めているお嬢様のようだが……。: She seemed like nothing more than a young lady looking for a thrill...
――ま、よほど変な事をしでかさない限り心配ないだろう。: —Well, as long as she didn't do anything too reckless, she should be fine.
綾音「よろしくお願いします、玖藤さんっ！」: Ayane: “I look forward to working with you, Kudou-san!”
差し出された手は、小さくて柔らかくて、忘れかけていた人の温もりを思い出させてくれ: The hand she held out was small and soft, and it seemed to remind me of a human warmth I’d almost forgotten.
綾音「ところで玖藤さんは何処に住んでいるんですか？」: Ayane: “By the way, where are you living, Kudou-san?”
奏介「ん……今は文化アパートだな。お茶の水の」: Sousuke: “Hm... I’m at the Culture Apartment right now. In Ochanomizu.”
住んでいるというよりは住まわされているといった方が正しいが。: Though it was more like I was being forced to live there than choosing to.
聞き慣れない言葉だ。そうも呼ばれているのだろうか。: An unfamiliar term. Was that what it was called too?
綾音「知らないんですか！？ 明智探偵が事務所を構えてるんですよ！ 二階のふた部屋: Ayane: “You don't know!? That’s where the detective Akechi has his office! He reportedly lived in two rooms on the second floor—”
俺の声がつれなかった所為か、途端に綾音は落ち着かない様子でせわしなく視線を右往左: Perhaps my tone was too cold, as Ayane suddenly looked unsettled, her gaze darting about nervously.
綾音「違いますっ！！ もおっ……デリカシーってものがないんですか！？」: Ayane: “No I don't!! Honestly... don't you have any delicacy!?”
頬を赤く染めて綾音が抗議してくる。: Ayane protested, her cheeks flushing red.
まあ――女性に対して今の言い方は我ながら迂闊だったとは思うが。: Well—I suppose my choice of words for a lady was a bit careless.
綾音「悪いと思うんなら態度で示してくださいっ」: Ayane: “If you’re really sorry, then show it with your actions!”
綾音「デート一回、で赦してあげます」: Ayane: “I’ll forgive you if you take me on one date.”
綾音は人差し指を立てて俺の目の前に突きつけた。: Ayane held up her index finger and thrust it in front of my face.
あはは、と綾音は快活に笑みをこぼした。: Ayane let out a bright, cheerful laugh.
綾音と別れたあと、俺はその足でバーへ向かった。: After parting with Ayane, I headed straight for the bar.
この時間だとまだバーは営業しているかもしれない。: At this hour, the bar might still be open.
だが人通りの多い道を歩くよりは、バーの片隅で身を隠している方がいいだろう。: But rather than walking the crowded streets, I felt it was better to hide away in a corner of the bar.
マスター「まだ営業中だぞ、奏介」: Master: “We’re still open for business, Sousuke.”
奏介「……見たところ客も居ないじゃねえか」: Sousuke: “...Doesn't look like you have any customers to me.”
マスターはカウンタから出ると、フロアを横切り、入口の扉に鍵をかけた。: The Master stepped away from the counter, walked across the floor, and locked the entrance door.
マスター「――厄介者が来たお陰で今日は店仕舞だ」: Master: “—Thanks to this nuisance, we’re closing early for the day.”
奏介「悪いな、厄介者で」: Sousuke: “Sorry for being such a nuisance.”
マスターは俺の返事を待たずに、グラスにウィスキーを注ぐ。: The Master poured whiskey into a glass without waiting for my reply.
マスター「先日憲兵が店を訪れて以来、閑古鳥が鳴いていたからな」: Master: “Ever since the military police visited the shop the other day, it’s been as quiet as a grave.”
マスター「元々この辺りは文人が多く住んでいるからか、反戦思想の取り締まりと称して: Master: “Perhaps it’s because many literary types live around here, but they’ve always been wandering about under the pretext of cracking down on anti-war sentiment, so it’s not necessarily your fault.”
奏介「まだ憲兵は此処に来るのか？」: Sousuke: “Are the MPs still coming here?”
マスター「いや、最近は全く姿を見ないな。他の所を見回っているんじゃないか？」: Master: “No, I haven't seen hide nor hair of them lately. Maybe they’re patrolling somewhere else?”
葵の言った事は本当だったと云う事か。: So what Aoi said was true, then.
マスター「暫くは此処に来ても問題ないだろう」: Master: “It should be fine for you to come here for a while.”
グラスをあおる。喉をアルコールが通り過ぎ、身体の芯が暖まるような感覚がする。: I drained the glass. The alcohol burned down my throat, and I felt a warmth spread through my core.
奏介「しかし……いいのか？ 俺の事を警察に突き出さなくても」: Sousuke: “But... is it okay? You aren't going to turn me over to the police?”
マスター「一応お前を信じているからな。本当にやったのなら突き出すさ」: Master: “I believe in you, for now. If you’d really done it, I’d hand you over.”
マスター「――お前は人を殺すような人間ではない」: Master: “—You aren't the kind of person who kills people.”
思わず酒を吹き出しそうになってしまった。: I almost spit out my drink.
奏介「そこまでマスターに買って貰ってるとは思わなかったな。……信じて貰えるのは有: Sousuke: “I didn't realize you held me in such high regard, Master. ...But I’m grateful for the trust.”
マスター「……人を殺した奴はな、臭いで判るんだよ」: Master: “...You can tell someone who’s killed by their scent.”
マスター「殺した相手の死臭がな、何時までも漂っている。どれだけ洗い流そうとしたと: Master: “The scent of their victim’s death... it lingers forever. No matter how much you try to wash it away, it never disappears.”
奏介「マスター……人を殺した事があるのか？」: Sousuke: “Master... have you ever killed anyone?”
その一言で判ってしまった。: With that one sentence, I understood.
詳しくは知らないが、マスターは元陸軍兵士だ。: I didn't know the details, but the Master was a former soldier.
日露戦争の際は激戦が繰り広げられたと聞く。: I’d heard that fierce battles had been fought during the Russo-Japanese War.
果たしてマスターがどれだけの戦果を挙げたのか――それを聞くのは躊躇われた。: I hesitated to ask just how much he’d seen—how many lives he’d taken.
マスター「……苦いモンだ。他人の人生を終わらせる行為ってのは」: Master: “...It’s a bitter thing. Ending another person’s life.”
マスターは自らグラスに口を付けた。バーテンダーが味見以外で酒を飲むというのは滅多: The Master took a sip from his own glass. It was rare for a bartender to drink anything other than a taste test.
それほど思い出したくない話題だったのだろう。: It must have been a topic he truly didn't want to remember.
煙草の箱を取り出しつつ、嫌煙家であるマスターの貌を窺う。: I pulled out my pack of cigarettes and looked at the Master, who was a known hater of smoke.
何も言わないが、明らかに不満げな表情をしている。: He didn't say anything, but his expression was clearly one of disapproval.
箱をしまい、代わりにグラスをあおった。: I put the pack away and took another drink instead.
マスター「何時かは報われる日も来るさ」: Master: “One day, your reward will come.”
数枚の硬貨をカウンタに置いて立ち上がる。: I placed a few coins on the counter and stood up.
軽く片手を挙げ、俺はバーを出た。: I raised a hand slightly and left the bar.
外へ出てすぐ煙草に火を付けた。: Once outside, I immediately lit a cigarette.
紫煙が闇の空へ吸い込まれるように消えていく。: The purple smoke vanished into the dark sky as if being swallowed up.
腐臭が自分の身体から漂ってくるような感覚。: The sensation of a rotting smell drifting from my own body.
あの夜死んだ女の死臭。: The scent of death from the woman who died that night.
殺していないはずなのに。: Even though I hadn't killed her.
――結局、アパートに戻ってベッドに倒れ込んで意識を失うまで、俺の鼻腔は臭わないは: —In the end, until I returned to my apartment and collapsed onto the bed, losing consciousness, my nostrils continued to catch that scent of death that shouldn't have been there.
目が醒めると、俺は真っ先に風呂場へ向かい、身体の汗を流し落とした。: The moment I woke up, I headed straight for the bath and washed the sweat from my body.
自分の身体から腐臭が漂っている気がして仕方がなかった。: I couldn't shake the feeling that the scent of death was drifting from my own skin.
いくらシャボンで身体をこすった所で臭いの元は消えない。: No matter how much I scrubbed with soap, the source of the smell wouldn't disappear.
手拭いを肩にかけたままベッドへ座り込む。擦りすぎた所為か、肌が赤く染まっていた。: I sat on the bed with a towel draped over my shoulder. My skin was flushed red from over-scrubbing.
俺は殺していないのに、臭いが消えないとは。: I didn't kill her, yet the scent wouldn't fade.
締め切った部屋に居ると、空気まで腐臭を放ちそうだ。: If I stayed in this closed room, even the air felt like it was starting to smell of rot.
幻の臭いから逃げるように、俺は部屋を出た。: As if escaping the phantom scent, I left the room.
外へ出るなり、聞き覚えのある女の声で呼び止められた。: As soon as I stepped outside, I was stopped by a familiar woman’s voice.
葵「――どこへ行くのかしら？」: Aoi: “—And where do you think you’re going?”
アパートの玄関口に葵が居た。: Aoi was at the entrance of the apartment.
葵「あたしが来たから逃げようとした……という訳ではなさそうね」: Aoi: “It doesn't look like you were trying to run just because I arrived... I suppose.”
逃げるのならもっと目立たないようにやる。: If I were going to run, I’d do it a lot more discreetly.
今は死臭を忘れていられるのなら何だっていい。: As long as it helped me forget the scent of death, anything was fine.
単純すぎて涙が出てくる。: It was so simple it was almost pathetic.
葵「本社からお金を借りてそのままのお客がいるのよ」: Aoi: “There are customers who borrowed money from the head office and haven't paid it back.”
俺の呟きに、葵は一瞬表情を強張らせた。: At my murmur, Aoi’s expression tightened for a moment.
葵「……とにかく行くわよ。今回は貴方だけじゃ頼りないから他にも連れて行くけど、仲: Aoi: “...Anyway, let’s go. I’m bringing others along since I can't trust you alone just yet, so try to get along with them.”
奏介「ハッ――餓鬼の遠足じゃあるまいし」: Sousuke: “Hah—it’s not like we’re on a school field trip.”
葵「……餓鬼みたいな連中だから言ってるのよ」: Aoi: “...I’m saying it because they act like children.”
そう言い捨てるなり歩き出す葵。: With those words, Aoi started walking.
三万円もの大金をぽんと出す処といい、何らかの背後組織が関わっているとは思っていた: Between the thirty thousand yen and everything else, I’d figured there was some kind of back-end organization involved.
葵に連れてこられた先は、浅草の北の外れだった。: Aoi led us to the northern edge of Asakusa.
後ろからは見慣れない男がふたり付いてきている。: Two unfamiliar men were following behind us.
？？？「新入りのお守りなんて、僕たちがやる仕事じゃねーよな」: ???: “Playing babysitter for a newcomer? This ain't our job.”
？？？「……男とは余計な口をきかないものだ」: ???: “...A man shouldn't speak more than necessary.”
葵「自己紹介がまだだったわね。ふたりともこちらにいらっしゃい」: Aoi: “I haven't introduced you yet, have I? Come here, both of you.”
奇妙な取り合わせの男が近寄ってくる。: A strange pair of men approached.
よく考えてみればどこかで見た覚えがある男だが――: Come to think of it, I felt like I’d seen them somewhere before—
？？？「……よぉ、こないだは世話になったな」: ???: “...Yo. Thanks for the help the other day.”
？？？「散々人を殴っといて忘れてんじゃねーよ！！」: ???: “Don't go forgetting after you beat the hell out of me!!”
俺がむしゃくしゃしていた時に絡んできた奴か。: The ones who’d picked a fight when I was in a foul mood.
まあ、そんな連中の事を俺が覚えている筈もない。: Well, it’s not like I’d remember people like that.
？？？「――まあいいさ。僕は各務。アンタの噂は聞いてるぜ、いろいろと……な」: ???: “—Whatever. I’m Kagami. I’ve heard rumors about you, all sorts of things...”
各務と名乗った男が手を差し伸べてきたが、馴れ合うつもりはない。: The man who introduced himself as Kagami held out his hand, but I had no intention of being friendly.
葵「街中で喧嘩はしないでちょうだい」: Aoi: “Don't go fighting in the middle of the street.”
各務「気に喰わねえ……そう思わねえか、熊坂？」: Kagami: “I don't like him... don't you agree, Kumasaka?”
？？？「――個人的嗜好は仕事に関係ない」: ???: “...Personal preferences have no place in our work.”
熊坂と呼ばれたもうひとりの男が答える。: The other man, called Kumasaka, replied.
各務「下の名前で呼ぶんじゃねえっ！！」: Kagami: “Don't call me by my first name!!”
熊坂「……どう呼べばいいんだ、ハチ」: Kumasaka: “...Then what should I call you, Hachi?”
呆れ果て、葵に向かって肩を竦めてみせる。: Exasperated, I shrugged my shoulders at Aoi.
葵「……貴方には関係ない事だから気にしないでいいわ」: Aoi: “...It doesn't concern you, so don't worry about it.”
葵が眉を寄せているのは、彼女もこいつらに手を焼いていると云う事だろうか。: Aoi’s brow furrowed; she seemed to be having a hard time with them too.
クマ「……姐御には文句を言わないんだな」: Kuma: “...You don't talk back to the sister, do you?”
ハチ「……たりめーだろ！？ テメーと姐さんじゃ格が違いすぎるってーのっ！！」: Hachi: “...Of course not!? You and the sister are in completely different leagues!!”
クマ「興奮するな、ハチ」: Kuma: “Don't get so worked up, Hachi.”
葵が低い声で呻った。: Aoi growled in a low voice.
なるほど――使えるかどうかはともかく、よく飼い慣らされてはいるようだ。: I see—whether they were useful or not, they were well-trained.
俺だけが葵の側にいて、ハチとクマは少し離れた後ろからついて来ている。: Only I walked by Aoi’s side, while Hachi and Kuma followed a short distance behind.
これじゃ俺が葵の金魚のフンのようで、あまり愉快な気分ではない。: It made me feel like Aoi’s shadow, which wasn't exactly a pleasant feeling.
こっちの気持ちを知ってか知らずか、葵は上機嫌で話しかけてくる。: Whether she knew my thoughts or not, Aoi spoke to me in high spirits.
奏介「……そういった事も調べがついてるんじゃないのか？」: Sousuke: “...Haven't you looked into that already?”
葵「あら、あたしが折角話題を提供してあげたのに、答えないつもり？」: Aoi: “Oh? I’m going out of my way to provide a topic of conversation, and you aren't going to answer?”
葵「必要のない情報ですもの。あたしが知ってるのは、妹さんがいる事くらいよ」: Aoi: “It’s not information I needed. All I know is that you have a little sister.”
その他には何もない。むしろ妹――柚芭がいることも俺とは関係のない事だから、結局の: There was nothing else. Or rather, the fact that I had a sister—Yuzuha—had nothing to do with me, so in the end—I had nothing.
葵「貴方に教える事は何もないわ」: Aoi: “I have nothing to tell you.”
何の気なしに訊ねた言葉だが、葵は固い声ですげなく返してきた。: I’d asked without thinking, but Aoi’s voice was cold as she snapped back.
まあいい。どうせ興味はない。: Fine by me. I wasn't interested anyway.
葵「くだらない事訊かないでちょうだい」: Aoi: “Don't go asking such pointless things.”
家族とうまくいってないのか、それとも――: Either she didn't get along with her family, or—
――俺と同じように両親を亡くしているか、どちらかだろう。: —Like me, she’d lost her parents. One or the other.
長屋が密集した細い路地、目の前には半ば傾きかけた家屋があった。: In a narrow alley crowded with row houses, a half-collapsed building stood before us.
本当に人が住んでいるのか不安になるほど荒んでいる。これでは冬場などとても暮らして: It was so dilapidated it made me wonder if anyone actually lived there. It looked like it would be impossible to survive the winter.
だが、扉だけはしっかりと閉ざされていた。: Yet, the door was firmly closed.
葵「――お前たちは裏へ行きなさい」: Aoi: “—You two, go around to the back.”
ハチとクマに指示を出す葵。: Aoi gave instructions to Hachi and Kuma.
男たちは俺に侮蔑めいた嘲りの視線を向けながら散っていく。: The men scattered, casting mocking, contemptuous looks at me as they went.
葵「愉しみなさい。こんな生き方――そう出来ないんだから」: Aoi: “Try to enjoy it. It’s not like everyone gets to live like this.”
こんな生活、こっちから願い下げだ。: I’d never ask for a life like this.
葵「ここに住んでいる男はね、半年前に五十円借りたのよ。……それを返さずにいたばか: Aoi: “The man living here borrowed fifty yen six months ago. ...Because he hasn't paid it back, he now owes two hundred.”
葵「あら、本来なら利子だけで二百円以上なのよ？ ちゃんと返すならって条件で安くし: Aoi: “Oh? Normally the interest alone would be over two hundred, you know? We’ve lowered it on the condition that he pays it back, so he should be grateful.”
奏介「……アンタのトコから金は借りないようにする」: Sousuke: “...I’ll make sure never to borrow money from your lot.”
葵「ええ、それが賢明ね。……もう手遅れとは思うけど」: Aoi: “Yes, that would be wise. ...Though I think it’s already too late for you.”
こいつは……ことあるごとに俺が逃げられないような事を言ってくる。: This woman... she never misses a chance to remind me that I can't escape.
まあ――柚芭のこともあるし、逃げられないのは事実なのだが。: Well—given Yuzuha, it was true enough.
葵「ほら、中に居るか確認してちょうだい」: Aoi: “Check and see if he’s inside.”
俺に指示を下し、葵が一歩下がる。: Aoi gave me the order and stepped back.
扉の前に立つ。崩壊しかけた建物には分不相応なほど重厚な扉。: I stood before the door. It was disproportionately heavy for such a collapsed building.
軽く扉を叩くと、鈍い音が響く。: I knocked lightly, and a dull sound echoed.
僅かに中で何かが動く気配がした。: I felt a faint movement inside.
住人は居るようだ。もしかしたら猫かもしれないが。: Someone was there. Maybe just a cat, though.
居留守を決め込むつもりだろうか、応対に出てくる様子はない。: It didn't seem like they were going to answer, likely pretending not to be home.
まあ――外に借金取りが来ていると解れば出てこないのも当然だろうが。: Well—it was only natural not to want to answer when a debt collector was outside.
此処に居る奴が金を返そうが返すまいが、俺には関係のない事だ。: Whether the person inside paid back their debt or not had nothing to do with me.
そう葵に告げ、彼女の横を通り過ぎる。: I told Aoi as much and tried to walk past her.
葵の膝が鳩尾に入り、俺は思わず蹲った。: Aoi’s knee connected with my solar plexus, and I doubled over involuntarily.
葵「何ふざけた事言ってるのかしら？ 中で誰かが動いたでしょ？ ちゃんと仕事なさい: Aoi: “Are you mocking me? Someone moved inside, didn't they? Do your job properly.”
呻きながら葵を睨み付けるが、彼女は意にも介さずに扉へ歩み寄る。: I glared at Aoi as I groaned, but she ignored me and walked toward the door.
葵「出てくるまで待つ。出てこない場合は――」: Aoi: “I’ll wait until he comes out. If he doesn't—”
葵が扉に向かって回し蹴りを放つ。: Aoi delivered a roundhouse kick to the door.
静かだった路地裏に、激しい音が響いた。: The crash echoed through the quiet alley.
厚い扉はまっぷたつに裂け、荒れ果てた屋内が丸見えになっていた。: The heavy door split in two, revealing the dilapidated interior.
葵「――無理やりにでも引きずり出すのよ。解ったかしら？」: Aoi: “—Drag him out by force if you have to. Understood?”
ここに居る奴が金を返そうが返すまいが俺には関係ない話なのだが、後ろで恐いお姉さん: I didn't care if the person inside paid or not, but I had a terrifying woman glaring at me from behind.
奏介「――判ってる。少し離れてろ」: Sousuke: “—I know. Stand back a bit.”
手を振り、葵を下がらせる。: I waved a hand, telling Aoi to step back.
奏介「重そうな扉だが――なんとかなるだろ」: Sousuke: “The door looks heavy—but I can manage.”
足元をならし、俺は脚を振り上げた。: I braced myself and swung my leg.
葵の方へ向き直り、俺は肩をすくめてみせた。: I turned back to Aoi and shrugged my shoulders.
扉の破壊音を聞き付けたのか、裏へ回っていたふたりが戻ってきて家の中へ入っていった: Perhaps hearing the sound of the door breaking, the two who had gone around to the back returned and entered the house.
申し訳程度に残っている家具を蹴倒しながらハチが奥へ入っていく。: Hachi headed further inside, kicking aside the meager furniture that remained.
奏介「やれやれ……乱暴なモンだな」: Sousuke: “Good grief... they’re a violent bunch.”
葵「まだまだよ。あれじゃ怖がるだけでお金は出さないもの」: Aoi: “Hardly. That’ll just scare him, it won't make him pay.”
葵「暴力じゃ何も解決しないわ」: Aoi: “Violence doesn't solve anything, you know.”
奏介「……よく言うぜ、全く……」: Sousuke: “...You’ve got a lot of nerve saying that, honestly...”
葵「あら、本当の事よ？ ……だから貴方にも暴力はふるわなかったでしょう？」: Aoi: “Oh? It’s the truth. ...After all, I didn't use any violence on you, did I?”
奏介「……散々嬲られた気はするけどな」: Sousuke: “...I feel like I’ve been tormented enough as it is.”
葵「――少なくとも、仲間に引き入れる時には紳士的だったわよ。仲間になってからのこ: Aoi: “—At the very least, I was a gentleman when I invited you to join us. What happened after you joined... well, that’s just education.”
根っからの加虐嗜好者のようだ。: She was a sadist to her very core.
家の中に入っていった連中が、貧相な男を引きずるように連れてきた。: The men who had gone inside dragged out a wretched-looking man.
すでに何度も殴られたのか、男の顔面は青黒く腫れ上がっていた。: His face was already swollen and bruised, likely from being hit several times.
まったくそうは思っていないような口ぶりで葵は言うと、男の眼前まで歩み寄った。: Aoi spoke with a tone that suggested she didn't care at all as she approached the man.
葵「借りたお金は返さないといけないわよね？ ……それなのに、家の中に閉じこもっち: Aoi: “You have to pay back the money you borrowed, don't you? ...And yet, you hide away inside... I wonder if you’re really alright?”
男「ひっ……！ か、返すつもりはあるんですっ！ だ、だから……っ、もう少し……っ: Man: “H-eek...! I-I intend to pay it back! S-so... please... just a little more time...!!”
葵「――つもりだけじゃ駄目なのよ。それくらい……ウチからお金を借りたんだから判る: Aoi: “—Intentions aren't enough. You should know that much... since you borrowed from us, right?”
葵「まさか、謝っていればなんとかなるとは思ってないわよね？」: Aoi: “Surely you didn't think an apology would be enough to settle things?”
笑顔を見せ、優しげな口調ながらも、その内容は厳しいものだ。: Even as she smiled and spoke in a gentle tone, her words were merciless.
ハチ「姐さん、ヤッちまいますか？」: Hachi: “Sister, should we finish him off?”
葵「そうね。……殺しちゃ駄目よ。お金が戻って来ないし、後片付けが面倒だから」: Aoi: “Let’s see. ...Don't kill him. We won't get our money back, and cleaning up is such a hassle.”
脅えきった男は逃げ出しもせず、蹲っているしかできないようだった。: The terrified man didn't even try to run; he could do nothing but crouch there.
ハチ「借りたモンはちゃんと返すのが世間の常識だろーが！！」: Hachi: “Paying back what you borrowed is just common sense in this world!!”
卑屈に蹲るだけの男も、弱い者だけには強いちんぴらも、どちらも同価値にくだらない。: The man crouching in servility and the thug who was only strong against those weaker than him were both equally pathetic.
頬を叩かれ、襟首を掴まれる。: She slapped my cheek and grabbed me by the collar.
葵「あたしは命令してるの。解る？」: Aoi: “I gave you an order. Do you understand?”
奏介「弱い奴をいたぶる趣味はない」: Sousuke: “I’m not in the habit of tormenting the weak.”
葵「自分はか弱い女を刺し殺したのに？」: Aoi: “Even though you stabbed and killed a helpless woman?”
まあ――俺のそれも似たような事になっているだろう。: Well—I suppose I was in a similar position myself.
ハチ「姐さん、そんな腰抜けに構うこたぁありませんよ」: Hachi: “Sister, don't bother with a coward like him.”
得意げなハチの声。: Hachi’s voice was full of triumph.
ゆっくりとハチの近くまで歩み寄る。: I slowly walked over to Hachi.
ハチ「お、腰抜けがやるのか？ へへっ、殴り方知ってるのかよ？ 下手に殴ると手首折: Hachi: “Oh? The coward’s gonna do it? Heh, you even know how to throw a punch? You’ll break your wrist if you do it wrong, you know.”
奏介「殴り方――知らねえな」: Sousuke: “A punch—no, I don't know how to do that.”
ハチの身体が宙を舞う。: Hachi was sent flying through the air.
奏介「――俺は蹴りしか使わない主義でね」: Sousuke: “—I’m the type who only uses my legs.”
倒れ込んだハチに背を向け、黙り込んでいたクマと向かい合う。: I turned my back to the fallen Hachi and faced the silent Kumasaka.
クマ「――男とは、無駄な争いを好まないものだ」: Kuma: “—A man shouldn't seek out unnecessary conflict.”
連中に背を向ける。: I turned my back on them.
もう此処に居る必要もない。: There was no need for me to be here any longer.
葵「何処へ行くつもりかしら？」: Aoi: “And where do you think you’re going?”
奏介「さあな。……どうせあんたらのことだ。俺が言わなくとも簡単に突き止めるんだろ: Sousuke: “Who knows. ...I’m sure you’ll find me soon enough even if I don't tell you, right?”
葵「――フフッ。地獄までは追わないから安心なさい」: Aoi: “—Heh. Don't worry, I won't chase you all the way to hell.”
ハチが何かわめいているような気がしたが、俺はその場を立ち去る事にした。: I felt like Hachi was shouting something behind me, but I decided to leave the scene.
今日もまた此処を訪れてしまった。: I found myself visiting this place again today.
隠れ家として用意されたアパートは退屈で、ピアノを弾く以外にする事がない。: The apartment provided as my hideout is stiflingly dull; there’s nothing to do but play the piano.
此処ならば酒は呑めるし、誰かが居る。: Here, I can drink, and there’s actually someone around.
案外自分は人恋しいのかもしれない。: Perhaps I’m more starved for human contact than I care to admit.
とてもじゃないが他人には話せない。: I could never say it out loud to anyone.
誰かと共に居たいからバーへ来ているなんて。: That I come to a bar just because I want to be near another living soul.
入口のドアは締まっていたので、合鍵を使って中へ入る。当然ながら店内には誰もいない: The front door was locked, so I used my spare key to let myself in. Naturally, the shop was empty.
マスターも後かたづけを終えて帰宅したのだろう。カウンタのスツールも綺麗に並んでい: The Master must have finished cleaning up and headed home. The stools were lined up neatly along the counter.
カウンタの中へ入り、棚に並べられた無数の酒からバーボンの壜を見繕う。: I stepped behind the bar and scouted out a bottle of bourbon from the countless labels on the shelves.
オールド・ファッションド・グラスに電気冷凍庫の氷を放り込んで酒を注ぐ。: I dropped some ice from the electric freezer into an Old Fashioned glass and poured the liquor.
パチパチと氷が爆ぜる音が静かな店内に響いた。: The sharp crackle of the ice popping echoed through the silent shop.
まったく――俺は何をしたいんだろうか。: Seriously—what am I even trying to do?
こそこそと逃げ隠れしながら、胡散臭い仕事を引き受けて報酬を得る。そうしながら酒を: Skulking around in hiding, taking on shady jobs for a paycheck. Drinking while living in a luxury apartment.
不自由ではあるが、不具合はない。むしろ普通に働いて稼ぐよりも良い生活を送っている: It’s restrictive, but I’m not exactly hurting. In fact, I’m living better than I ever did working a real job.
でも俺は――此処から逃げ出したい。: But I—I want to run away from all of this.
いや、逃げ出さないといけない。このままだといつか、取り返しのつかない事になりそう: No, I *have* to run. I have this sickening premonition that if I stay like this, something irreversible is going to happen.
そもそもあの――相馬葵が何故俺に近づいてきたのか。それも気懸かりだ。: And then there’s her—why the hell did Souma Aoi approach me in the first place? That’s been gnawing at me too.
絶対裏に何かがある――そう思っても、それを調べる手だてがない。: There’s definitely something moving behind the scenes—but even thinking that, I have no way to investigate it.
バーボンに口を付ける。溶けた氷でほどよく冷え、口当たりもなめらかになっている。: I took a sip of the bourbon. The melting ice had chilled it perfectly, making the mouthfeel smooth.
まずはあの日の詳細を調べる必要があるだろう。新聞も読んでいないので、どのようにし: First, I need to look into the details of that day. I haven't even read a newspaper, so I don't even know how I ended up being branded the killer.
あの時俺を助けた女――綾音と云ったか――は新聞を読んだと言っていた。: That woman who helped me back then—Ayane, was it?—said she’d read the papers.
彼女に訊けば何か解るだろうか。: Would I learn anything if I asked her?
いや、それよりも――美華夏に訊いた方が早いのか。あいつは出版社に勤めている。: No, wait—it might be faster to ask Mikage. She works for a publisher.
あの事件以来会っていないから、いつか会いに行く必要があるだろう。: I haven't seen her since the incident; I’ll have to go find her eventually.
まあ――あいつが俺を殺人犯だと思っているなら会うわけにもいかないだろうが。: Though—if she thinks I’m a murderer, I suppose I can't exactly go see her.
――思われているだけならまだマシなのか。: —Is it really better if it’s just a misunderstanding?
真実、俺が殺人犯なのかもしれないのだから。: For all I know, I really might be the killer.
――やれやれ、目撃者が居れば、俺の無実も証明できるのかもしれないが。: —Good grief, if only there were a witness, maybe I could prove my innocence.
いや――無理か。目撃者は俺を殺人犯だと思ったからこそ通報したのだろう。: No—that’s impossible. The witness likely reported me *because* they thought I was the killer.
まったく――頭が混乱している。酒の所為ではなく、俺自身が自分のしでかした事をはっ: Damn it—my head is a mess. Not from the booze, but because I can't even prove to myself what I did or didn't do.
バーの奥の方で物音がした。: A noise came from the back of the bar.
まさか泥棒という事はないだろうが、用心に越したことはない。: It’s probably not a thief, but it pays to be cautious.
武器になるような物は――ない。厨房に行けば包丁はあるかもしれないが、何処に置いて: As for a weapon—I’ve got nothing. There might be a knife in the kitchen, but I have no idea where it’s kept.
仕方なしに手ぶらで立ち上がる。拳は使いたくない。: I stood up empty-handed, having no choice. I didn't want to use my fists.
カウンタからは陰になって見えないところから、千花が転がるように出てきた。: Chika came tumbling out from a spot hidden by the counter’s shadow.
奏介「なんだ――千花か。なにしてるんだ？」: Sousuke: “Oh—it’s just you, Chika. What are you doing?”
千花「あ、あのっ……決して隠れていたという訳じゃなくてっ……そのっ……」: Chika: “A-ah, um... I certainly wasn't hiding on purpose... it’s just... um...”
奏介「……まあ、俺ひとりしか居なければ出て来辛いのは判るな」: Sousuke: “...Well, I suppose it’s hard to come out when I’m the only one here.”
千花「ああっ！？ そうじゃないんですっ！ 違うんですっ！！あの……えっと……」: Chika: “Ah!? That’s not it! You’ve got it wrong!! Um... well...”
徐々に声が沈んでいく。: Her voice gradually trailed off into a whisper.
自分で何を言いたいのか判らなくなったのだろう。: She’d clearly lost track of what she was even trying to say.
謝られても困るのだが、そうとしかいえない。: Being apologized to just makes things awkward, but there wasn't much else to say.
千花「玖藤さんが来ていたのは判っていたんです。でも……その、何か考え込んでいる様: Chika: “I knew you were here, Kudou-san. But... well, you looked like you were deep in thought, so...”
千花「そっ……そんなことは……ないんですけどっ……」: Chika: “I-it’s not... not that I was scared, exactly...”
そんな顰め面をしていた覚えはないのだが、恐らく険しい表情をしていたのだろう。: I didn't recall making a scary face, but I must have looked pretty grim.
そこまで卑屈にならなくともいいだろうとは思うが、それが彼女の性格なのだろう。: I thought she didn't need to be so servile, but I suppose that’s just her nature.
そういう生き方もあるのだろう――きっと。: Everyone has their own way of surviving—I’m sure.
煙草に火を付ける。マスターが見たら文句を言うかもしれないが、本来この店は禁煙とい: I lit a cigarette. The Master might complain if he saw me, but it’s not like this place has some stuffy no-smoking rule.
ふと千花が何かに気付いたようにカウンタの中へ入った。: Chika suddenly seemed to realize something and went behind the counter.
スチール製の灰皿を受け取る。: I took the steel ashtray from her.
煙が緩やかな渦を巻き、天井近くで拡散する。: The smoke swirled in gentle eddies, diffusing near the ceiling.
俺と千花の間に会話がない所為かもしれないが。: Maybe because there was no conversation between Chika and me.
グラスの氷は半ば溶け、バーボンの色も少々薄くなっている。: The ice in my glass was half-melted, the bourbon turning a paler shade.
手に取ると、カウンタに輪になった水たまりが出来ていた。: When I picked it up, a ring of condensation had formed on the counter.
沈黙に耐えられなくなったのは千花の方だった。: It was Chika who finally couldn't stand the silence.
千花「あの……ラジオでもつけましょうか……？」: Chika: “Um... should I turn on the radio...?”
千花がカウンタの奥にあるラジオの電源を入れる。: Chika switched on the radio located at the back of the counter.
普段は俺がピアノを弾いているのでラジオが鳴る事は滅多にない。: Usually, since I’m playing the piano, the radio is rarely ever on.
これはこれで、なかなか新鮮な夜だった。: This was a rather fresh way to spend the night.
千花はおとなしくカウンタの中で突っ立っている。: Chika stood quietly behind the counter.
時折俺の様子を窺うように見ているのが視線の端に映るが、俺が僅かでも動くとすぐに貌: Out of the corner of my eye, I could see her stealing glances at me, but the moment I moved even a fraction, she’d avert her gaze.
ラジオ『――それでは、市民を恐怖に陥れている連続殺人事件についての続報です』: Radio: “—And now, a follow-up on the serial murder cases that have plunged the citizenry into terror.”
ラジオからの声にぴくり、と背筋が震えた。: The voice from the radio sent a chill down my spine.
ラジオ『――本日市内日本橋区において、まことに奇妙なる殺人が起き、警視庁ではこれ: Radio: “—Today, in Nihonbashi District, a truly bizarre murder has occurred. The Metropolitan Police Department is investigating potential links to previous cases—”
プツッ、とラジオの音が途切れる。: Click—the sound from the radio cut off.
千花がスイッチを切ったようだ。: It seemed Chika had turned the switch off.
下手に気を遣われても、こちらが困る。: It only makes things harder for me when people try to be overly considerate.
ますます縮こまる千花。: Chika shrank even further into herself.
グラスに残ったバーボンを一気に喉へ通す。: I drained the remaining bourbon in my glass in one go.
グラスを受け取り、ぱたぱたとカウンタ内を行き来する千花。: Chika took the glass and scurried back and forth behind the counter.
黙ってじっとしているよりは動いていた方が気は紛れるだろう。: Moving around was probably better for her than standing still and overthinking.
沈黙したままこちらの様子を窺われているよりはずっといい。: It was certainly better than having her watch me in total silence.
角がまだ残っている氷がグラスの縁に当たり、澄んだ音をたてた。: The ice, its edges still sharp, clinked against the rim with a clear ring.
ほんの僅かだが、千花が微笑んだような気がした。: For just a fleeting second, I thought I saw Chika smile.
最近は物騒な事件が多すぎる。: There are too many violent incidents lately.
まあ――俺もその一端を担っているのかもしれないが。: Well—I suppose I might be part of the problem.
確かに奇妙きわまりない。俺自身が――殺したかどうか判らないなんて。: It certainly is bizarre beyond belief. That I—I don't even know if I killed someone or not.
奇妙なのはそれだけではない。: But that’s not the only strange thing.
あの時俺を襲った女の常軌を逸した行動のほうが、世間から見たら何倍も奇妙に映るだろ: The insane behavior of that woman who attacked me that night would seem far more bizarre to the world.
現に、突如狂った人間による殺人事件の方が遙かに多く、人々の関心を集めている。: In fact, murder cases by people who have suddenly gone mad are far more common and draw more public attention.
先程のラジオ放送で流れていた奇妙な殺人という報道も、おそらくはそちらの方だろう。: The "bizarre murder" report on the radio just now was likely one of those.
じゃあ何故、俺の時は立場が逆転した――？: Then why, in my case, were the roles reversed—?
千花の声で俺は我に返った。: Chika’s voice snapped me back to reality.
千花は何も言わずにうつむいている。: Chika looked down without a word.
どうも居心地が悪い。: I felt incredibly out of place.
時間も遅い。もう帰った方がいいだろう。: It was late. I should probably head back.
深夜遅くに出歩くと、かえって目立ってしまうかもしれない。: Wandering around late at night would only make me stand out more.
奏介「――酒代は一円でいいか？」: Sousuke: “—Is one yen enough for the drink?”
一円紙幣をカウンタに置き、立ち上がった。: I placed a one-yen bill on the counter and stood up.
奏介「それじゃ、邪魔したな」: Sousuke: “Well then, sorry for bothering you.”
出て行くときも、千花の表情は曇ったままだった。: Even as I left, Chika’s expression remained clouded.
目を醒ますと、俺は手早く外出着に着替えた。: The moment I woke up, I quickly changed into my street clothes.
今日は昼から出かけるつもりだった。: I intended to go out starting from noon today.
ここ最近、葵からの仕事がない時にはぶらぶらと出歩いていたのだが、目的があって外出: Lately, I’d been wandering aimlessly when I didn't have work from Aoi, but this might be the first time I was going out with a specific purpose.
奏介「美華夏の仕事場は神保町だったよな……」: Sousuke: “Mikage’s office was in Jimbocho, wasn't it...?”
あの事件の直前にバーで言葉を交わして以来、美華夏には会っていない。: I hadn't seen Mikage since we’d spoken at the bar right before the incident.
まあ、会っていないのはさして気にする事でもない。出版社に勤めている美華夏は重要な: Well, not seeing her wasn't something that particularly bothered me. I’d simply realized that Mikage, working at a publishing house, was an important source of information.
仕事の内容までは知らなかった。興味もなかったし、何をしていようと彼女の勝手だ。: I didn't know the specifics of her job. I wasn't interested, and whatever she did was her business.
ただ美華夏の就職が決まったとき、これでモガの仲間入りだと自慢げに話していたのを覚: I just remembered her bragging about finally joining the ranks of the "Moga" when she landed the job.
確か神田の夢譚堂といったはずだ。: Mutando in Kanda, I believe it was called.
神保町ならお茶の水から充分歩いて行ける距離だ。: Jimbocho was well within walking distance from Ochanomizu.
服に袖を通し、帽子を被り、色眼鏡をかける。: I pulled on my sleeves, put on my hat, and donned my tinted glasses.
安全とはいえないので、可能な限り変装をしてみたのだが、姿見に映してみると……なん: Since it wasn't exactly safe, I tried to disguise myself as much as possible, but looking in the full-length mirror... I looked suspicious as hell.
これでは変装と云うよりも仮装だ。: This was less of a disguise and more of a costume.
とはいえ、他に化けられるような服もない。コートなど着れば、余計に胡散臭いだろう。: Then again, I didn't have any other clothes to change into. If I wore a coat, I’d look even more shady. It was the middle of summer, after all.
奏介「仕方ねえ――これでいくか」: Sousuke: “Can't be helped—this’ll have to do.”
まずは神保町まで出向き、どこかのカフェに美華夏を呼び出すとするか――: First, I’ll head to Jimbocho and call Mikage out to a cafe somewhere—
アパートから南へ歩くと九段下に出る。: Walking south from the apartment brought me to Kudanshita.
交差点の脇にあった公衆電話へ近寄り、電話番号簿をめくる。: I approached a public telephone by the intersection and flipped through the directory.
神田区の夢譚堂の番号を確認し、五銭硬貨を投じてダイアルを回して暫く待つ。: I confirmed the number for Mutando in Kanda District, tossed in a five-sen coin, and dialed, waiting a while.
女『――こちら夢譚堂編集部、桂と申します――』: Woman: “—Mutando Editorial Department, this is Katsura speaking—”
女『え……？ 失礼ですが、どちら様でしょうか？』: Woman: “Eh...? Excuse me, but who is calling?”
咄嗟に琢磨の名前を出してしまった。: I’d blurted out Takuma’s name on impulse.
あいつなら刑事だし、警察に追われている俺の名をだすよりもずっといい。: Since he’s a detective, it was a hell of a lot better than using the name of a man being hunted by the police.
美華夏が直接電話に出てくれれば問題なかったのだが――: It wouldn't have been a problem if Mikage had answered directly—
美華夏『――もしもし？ わざわざ電話なんて何の用事なの？』: Mikana: “—Hello? What on earth do you want, calling me here?”
不機嫌そうな声だったが、明らかに美華夏だ。: Her voice sounded irritated, but it was unmistakably Mikage.
素っ頓狂な大声が飛び出し、思わず受話器を耳から離す。: She let out such a startlingly loud yelp that I instinctively pulled the receiver away from my ear.
美華夏『ちょっとぉ……驚かせないでよぉ……だいたい連絡もなしで家にも帰らないで…: Mikana: “Honestly... don't scare me like that... and you haven't been in touch, you haven't come home... I was so worried...”
このままだと延々と恨み言を訊かされかねない。電話は音声が聞き取りにくいし、第一金: If I let her keep going, I’d be listening to her grievances forever. The audio on the phone was hard to hear, and besides, it was costing me money.
奏介「訊きたい事があるんだが、今から出てこられるか？ ――そうだな、俎橋のそばに: Sousuke: “There’s something I want to ask you. Can you come out now? —Let’s see, I’ll be waiting at the cafe near Manaitabashi.”
美華夏『――えぇ、わかったわ。……私も訊きたい事たくさんあるし』: Mikana: “—Fine, I understand. ...I have plenty I want to ask you, too.”
こちらも覚悟が必要か。: I’d need to be prepared for that.
美華夏『……それじゃ、すぐに行くわね』: Mikana: “...Well then, I’ll be there soon.”
出来るだけ目立たない、奥の席を取った方がいいだろう。: I’d better take a seat in the back where I won't stand out.
思惑通り店内の奥まった箇所のテーブルに腰を落ち着け、入口の方を窺いながら美華夏を: Exactly as planned, I settled into a table in a secluded corner of the shop, waiting for Mikage while keeping a watchful eye on the entrance.
暫くすると、入口のドアを開け、美華夏が店内に入ってきた。: After a while, the front door opened, and Mikage stepped inside.
軽く片手を挙げ、美華夏に合図を送る。: I raised a hand slightly, signaling to her.
気付いた美華夏がつかつかと早足でこちらに向かってきた。: Noticing me, Mikage walked toward me with a brisk, hurried pace.
走って来たのだろう、頬が上気し、息が上がっている。: She must have run; her cheeks were flushed and she was out of breath.
美華夏「このあたりに何件カフェがあると思ってるのよ！？ かなり探しちゃったじゃな: Mikana: “How many cafes do you think there are around here!? I had to search all over!! You could have at least told me the name of the place, couldn't you!?”
奏介「……まあ、座れよ。あまり目立ちたくない」: Sousuke: “...Just sit down. I don't want to draw attention.”
早口でまくし立てる美華夏を落ち着かせるつもりだったのだが、逆効果だったようだ。: I’d intended to calm her down, but it seemed to have the opposite effect.
美華夏「目立ちたくないって……凄く目立ってるわよ！ なによその格好！？ 余計に怪: Mikana: “Don't want to draw attention? You stand out like a sore thumb! What is with that outfit!? It only makes you look more suspicious!”
頬を膨らませつつも美華夏は席に着いた。: Mikage took her seat, still puffing out her cheeks in annoyance.
美華夏「本当に……奏介がやったの？」: Mikana: “Did you... did you really do it, Sousuke?”
美華夏「だって、本当に奏介が犯人なら、私の所に相談なんて来られないでしょ？」: Mikana: “After all, if you were really the culprit, you wouldn't be coming to me for advice, would you?”
美華夏「わざわざ危険を冒してまで来るって事は、何らかの厄介事に巻き込まれた……違: Mikana: “The fact that you’d go to such lengths and take such a risk means you’ve been caught up in some kind of mess... am I wrong?”
やれやれ――洞察力が鋭いというか、俺の行動が読まれているというか……まあ、信用し: Good grief—either her insight is incredibly sharp, or she’s just got my number... well, I was grateful for her trust.
俺自身が自分を信用しているとは言い難いのに。: Even if I found it hard to trust myself.
そこで俺は美華夏に事のあらましを話して聞かせることにした。: I decided to tell Mikage the whole story.
女に突然襲われた事や、怪しげな組織の保護を受けている事、お茶の水の文化アパートに: The sudden attack by the woman, being taken under the wing of a shady organization, and even the fact that I was hiding out in the Culture Apartment in Ochanomizu.
終始美華夏は神妙に頷いて聞いていた。: Mikage listened intently, nodding solemnly throughout.
だいたい話し終え一息ついたとき、美華夏がぽつりと呟いた。: When I’d finished and took a breath, Mikage murmured softly.
あの日以来、柚芭とは直接連絡を取っていない。バーで千花に伝言を頼んではいたが、心: I hadn't been in direct contact with Yuzuha since that day. I’d asked Chika to pass on a message at the bar, but telling her not to worry was an impossible request.
奏介「なんとかしてやらないとな……」: Sousuke: “I have to do something for her...”
女給が注文を取りに来たので、ふたりとも冷やし珈琲を注文する。: A waitress came to take our order, and we both asked for iced coffee.
俺は努めて平静を装っていたが、内心気が気ではなかった。: I tried my best to appear calm, but internally I was on pins and needles.
冷やし珈琲が運ばれ、女給が去ったあとに美華夏が囁きかけてきた。: After the iced coffee was delivered and the waitress left, Mikage whispered to me.
美華夏「別に奏介が有名人って訳じゃないんだし、ね？」: Mikana: “It’s not like you’re some kind of celebrity, right?”
解っては居るが、やはり落ち着かない。: I knew it, yet I still couldn't relax.
落ち着きのなさを美華夏に見破られるとは思わなかったが。: I hadn't expected Mikage to see right through my restlessness, though.
美華夏「ところで相談ってなに？ 私に出来る事ならしてあげられるけど……」: Mikana: “By the way, what did you want to consult me about? I’ll help if I can, but...”
奏介「事件についての情報を集めて欲しい」: Sousuke: “I want you to gather information about the incident.”
美華夏「ええ、わかったわ。でも……どこまで調べられるか判らないわよ？ 出版社と云: Mikana: “Fine, I understand. But... I don't know how much I’ll be able to find, okay? Even though it’s a publisher, we mostly handle literary magazines...”
奏介「出来る範囲で頼む。俺は新聞すらまだ読んでないんだ」: Sousuke: “Just do what you can. I haven't even read a newspaper yet.”
美華夏「……わかったわ。でも……それなら琢磨に訊いた方がいいんじゃない？ 警官だ: Mikana: “...Understood. But... wouldn't it be better to ask Takuma? He’s a policeman; he must know a lot more than I do.”
奏介「――あいつに頼めると思うか？」: Sousuke: “—Do you really think I can ask him?”
美華夏は盛大に嘆息した。: Mikage let out a heavy sigh.
美華夏「いいかげん仲直りしたら？」: Mikana: “Why can't you two just make up already?”
奏介「この状況でか？ それに、別に喧嘩している訳じゃない」: Sousuke: “In this situation? Besides, it’s not like we’re actually fighting.”
美華夏「ある程度調べたら連絡するわ。文化アパートでいいのよね」: Mikana: “I’ll get in touch once I’ve looked into it a bit. At the Culture Apartment, right?”
奏介「それかバーで伝言を頼んでくれてもいい」: Sousuke: “Either that, or you can leave a message at the bar.”
美華夏「ええ。――それじゃ、また今度」: Mikana: “Alright. —Well then, I’ll see you later.”
美華夏が席を立つ。つられて俺も立ち上がる。: Mikage stood up. I followed suit.
これ以上此処にいる理由もない。: There was no reason to stay here any longer.
奏介「支払いは俺が持つ」: Sousuke: “I’ll take care of the bill.”
美華夏「報酬にしては安いと思うけど」: Mikana: “It’s a bit cheap for a down payment, don't you think?”
くすり、と小さく美華夏が笑った。: Mikage gave a small, quiet chuckle.
日が暮れてしばらく経った後、神楽坂に足を踏み入れた。: Some time after the sun went down, I stepped foot into Kagurazaka.
相変わらずの混雑振りで、酔客やそれを誘う客引きが入り交じっている。: It was as crowded as ever, a jumble of drunkards and the touts trying to entice them.
その合間を縫うようにして坂を登り、バーへ繋がる横道へ入った。: Weaving through the throng, I headed up the slope and turned into the side street that led to the bar.
バーの扉には“ＯＰＥＮ”という札がかけられていた。: The sign on the door said “OPEN.”
来るにはまだ早かったようだ。: It seemed I was still too early.
どこかで時間を潰すか、諦めてアパートへ帰ってしまうか。: Should I kill some time somewhere, or just give up and head back to the apartment?
店内に客が居なければ、マスターは文句を言うかもしれないが入っても構わないだろう。: If there were no customers inside, the Master might complain, but he’d likely let me in.
窓から薄暗い店内の様子を窺う。: I peered through the window into the dimly lit interior.
誰かが端のカウンタ席にいるようだ。: Someone was sitting at the far end of the counter.
誰であるかを確認したとき、ひとりでに舌打ちが洩れた。: The moment I confirmed who it was, a click of the tongue escaped me involuntarily.
いや――自分自身に対する不快感か。: No—maybe it was disgust toward myself.
俺は身を翻すと、急いでバーの扉をくぐった。: I spun around and hurried through the bar door.
店内に入った俺を見て、テーブルを拭いていた千花が小さく声を上げた。: Seeing me enter, Chika, who was wiping a table, let out a small cry.
その脇を通り過ぎ、さらに奥へ。: I brushed past her and headed further back.
スツールから立ち上がり、こちらを呆然と見上げている柚芭。: Yuzuha stood up from her stool, staring at me in a daze.
その頬に、すっ――と涙が一筋伝い落ちた。: A single tear traced a path down her cheek.
駆け寄ってきた柚芭が俺の胸に飛び込んでくる。: Yuzuha ran to me and threw herself into my chest.
柚芭「お兄ちゃん……お兄ちゃん、お兄ちゃんっ！！ ……うっ……ぐっ、ひぐっ……ふ: Yuzuha: “Big brother... big brother, big brother!! ...*sob*... *hic*... waaaaah...!”
本格的に泣き出してしまった柚芭の頭を撫でてやる。: I stroked the head of the now-sobbing Yuzuha.
柚芭「バカぁ……お兄ちゃんなんて嫌いだぁ……」: Yuzuha: “You idiot... I hate you, big brother...”
拳で弱々しく胸を叩いてくる。: She feebly pounded on my chest with her fists.
柚芭「……あのね、お兄ちゃんが家を出たあとでね、たくさん人が来たの……」: Yuzuha: “...You know, after you left home, so many people came by...”
しゃくり上げながらも柚芭は話し始めた。: Still sobbing, Yuzuha began to speak.
柚芭「訊いたらね、憲兵だって……。ねぇ、おかしいよね？ どうして憲兵の人たちがお: Yuzuha: “When I asked, they said they were military police... Hey, that’s strange, isn't it? Why are the MPs looking for you, big brother?”
柚芭「だって憲兵隊って、軍隊の中の警察でしょ？ お兄ちゃんはただのピアノ弾きだよ: Yuzuha: “The military police are the police for the army, right? You’re just a piano player, big brother. ...And they said you’d killed someone...”
柚芭「ねぇ……お兄ちゃん、お兄ちゃんがそんなことするはずないよね……？」: Yuzuha: “Hey... big brother, there’s no way you’d do something like that, right...?”
奏介「……当たり前だろ、そんなの」: Sousuke: “...Of course not, don't be ridiculous.”
柚芭の頭に手を置き、くしゃくしゃと髪を撫でる。: I placed a hand on Yuzuha’s head and rumpled her hair.
泣き笑いのように柚芭が貌を歪める。: Yuzuha’s face twisted in a mix of tears and a smile.
奏介「俺がそんな事すると思うか？」: Sousuke: “You really think I’d do something like that?”
柚芭「……思わないよ。思わないけど……でもっ……！」: Yuzuha: “...I don't. I don't, but... still...!”
奏介「……あまり心配するな。俺は大丈夫だから」: Sousuke: “...Don't worry so much. I’ll be fine.”
一瞬、答える事が出来なかった。: For a second, I couldn't find the words.
俺は殺していないと断言することができなかった。: I couldn't bring myself to declare with absolute certainty that I hadn't killed anyone.
適当に言葉を濁し、柚芭から視線を逸らす。: I remained vague and averted my gaze from Yuzuha.
不安がる柚芭を慰める事すら出来ないとは――俺も余裕がないようだ。: I can't even comfort my own terrified sister—I’ve really lost my edge.
いや、余裕がないのではなく――自信がないのか。: No, it’s not that I’ve lost my edge—it’s that I’ve lost my confidence.
自分でしでかしたことであるはずなのに――: Even though I’m the one who supposedly did it—
身体を揺さぶられて我に返る。: I was snapped back to reality by her shaking my body.
柚芭「お兄ちゃん……本当に大丈夫なの……？」: Yuzuha: “Big brother... are you really alright...?”
まったく……どうかしている。: Seriously... what is wrong with me?
さらに心配させてどうするというんだ、俺は。: Making her even more worried—what the hell am I doing?
少々落ち着いてきた柚芭が口を開いた。: Once she’d calmed down a bit, Yuzuha spoke again.
