美華夏は引くつもりもないらしい。なんとかやり過ごしたい所だが――: It seemed Mikage had no intention of backing down. I wanted to brush it off somehow, but—
綾音「玖藤さん、お皿はどこにあるんですか？」: Ayane: “Kudou-san, where are the plates kept?”
美華夏が綾音の元へ歩み寄る。綾音が一歩後退った。: Mikage walked up to Ayane. Ayane took a step back.
美華夏「どういうことかしら？」: Mikana: “I wonder what this is all about?”
かつかつと靴を鳴らす美華夏。かなり怒っている。: Mikage’s heels clicked sharply on the floor. She was clearly incensed.
まったく落ち着いているとは見えない美華夏が睨んでくる。: Mikage, looking anything but calm, glared at me.
不意に綾音がぽん、と手を打ち鳴らした。: Suddenly, Ayane clapped her hands together.
綾音「……あ、そーゆーことだったんですか」: Ayane: “...Ah, so that’s how it is.”
美華夏が不振げに眉をひそめた。: Mikage furrowed her brows in suspicion.
美華夏「当たり前でしょ！？ どうしてこんなのと……ただ昔なじみなだけよっ！」: Mikana: “Of course not! Why would I be with... we’re just childhood friends!”
美華夏「決まってるじゃないのっ！ なんでこんな奴と……っ！」: Mikana: “It’s obvious, isn't it! Why would I ever... with a guy like this...!”
奏介「……よりによってこんな奴呼ばわりかよ……」: Sousuke: “...You had to go and call me 'a guy like this'?”
美華夏「貴女こそなんなのよっ！？」: Mikana: “Who do you think you are, anyway!?”
綾音「白河綾音です。玖藤さんとはつい最近知り合いました」: Ayane: “I’m Shirakawa Ayane. I only met Kudou-san very recently.”
綾音「あ、でも、わたしは全然そーゆーのと違いますから安心してくださいね」: Ayane: “Ah, but don't worry, I’m not that kind of thing at all, so please rest easy.”
奏介「ところで美華夏、何しに来たんだ……？」: Sousuke: “By the way, Mikage, what did you come here for...?”
美華夏「優雅に逃亡生活を送っている碌でもない男の取材よ」: Mikana: “To interview a worthless man living an elegant life on the run.”
美華夏「まあそれは置いといて……これ、見てないんでしょ？」: Mikana: “Well, putting that aside... you haven’t seen this, have you?”
美華夏が手提げ鞄から新聞を取り出した。: Mikage pulled a newspaper out of her handbag.
美華夏「事件後の朝刊よ。第一報だから内容は薄いけど、どうせこれ以上の情報は世間に: Mikana: “It’s the morning edition from after the incident. It’s just the initial report, so the content is thin, but no more information than this has been released to the public anyway.”
一週間ほど前の紙面を眺める。確かに目新しいものではなかった。俺の名前が被疑者とし: I looked over the pages from about a week ago. It certainly wasn't anything new. I felt a sense of wrongness, almost like a headache, seeing my name listed as a suspect.
美華夏「例えば――目撃者については報道されていないわ。通行人の女性だったらしいけ: Mikana: “For example—they haven’t reported on the witness. Apparently, it was a female passerby...”
思わず綾音を見る。: I couldn't help but look at Ayane.
彼女は確かにあの時近くにいた。: She was certainly nearby at that time.
だが関係はないだろう。綾音は俺を助けて庇ってくれたのだ。通報する道理はない。: But that shouldn't matter. Ayane helped me and shielded me. There’s no reason for her to report me.
だが、あの路地で通行人がいれば俺も気付くはずだ。: But if there was a passerby in that alleyway, I should have noticed.
美華夏「それと……凶器についてね。新聞ではただ『凄惨たる遺体』としか書かれていな: Mikana: “And... about the murder weapon. The newspaper only wrote 'a gruesome corpse,' but apparently, it was actually a stabbing.”
反射的にズボンのポケットに手を入れる。硬い手触り。いつも通り、そこには折りたたみ: Reflexively, I put my hand into my trouser pocket. A hard sensation. As usual, my folding knife was resting there.
美華夏の求めに応じてナイフを取り出し、テーブルの上に置く。: In response to Mikage’s request, I pulled out the knife and placed it on the table.
横から綾音が覗き込んできた。: Ayane peeked at it from the side.
奏介「どこにでもある安物だぞ」: Sousuke: “It’s just a cheap thing you can find anywhere.”
手にとって留め金を外し、刃先を露出させる。あまり手入れもしていないが、錆もなく切: I picked it up, released the catch, and exposed the blade. I hadn't maintained it much, but there was no rust, and the sharpness seemed fine.
無論――血の曇りもない。: Needless to say—there was no clouding from blood, either.
美華夏「襲われたとき……これを使ったの？」: Mikana: “When you were attacked... did you use this?”
殺していないと信じつつも、ナイフを使っていないとは言い切れなかった。: While I believed I hadn't killed anyone, I couldn't say for sure that I hadn't used the knife.
あの時――俺は確かに何かを握りしめていた。それでなんども相手を打ったのだが、血は: At that time—I was certainly clutching something. I had hit the opponent with it many times, but there shouldn't have been any blood.
そもそも意識が朦朧としていた中での出来事だ。自分自身の記憶すらあやふやのため、何: In the first place, it happened while my consciousness was hazy. Since even my own memories were fuzzy, I couldn't be certain about anything.
俺の手からナイフをもぎ取り、綾音は刃先を仕舞った。: Ayane snatched the knife from my hand and tucked the blade away.
綾音「少なくとも、あの夜にわたしが見た玖藤さんは、人を殺した様には思えませんでし: Ayane: “At the very least, the Kudou-san I saw that night didn’t seem like the type of person who had killed someone.”
美華夏「それは貴女の主観でしょう？」: Mikana: “That’s just your subjective opinion, isn't it?”
綾音「ええ、そうですよ。何か問題がありますか？」: Ayane: “Yes, it is. Is there a problem?”
美華夏「大ありよ。そんな見た目の印象で決めつけても証拠にはならないわよ」: Mikana: “A big one. Deciding based on an impression like that doesn't count as evidence.”
美華夏「なっ……！？ し、信じてるに決まってるじゃない！！」: Mikana: “Wh...!? O-of course I trust him!!”
綾音「でしたら――それでいいじゃないですか。他人がどう思おうと、玖藤さんは無実だ: Ayane: “Then—isn't that enough? Regardless of what others think, Kudou-san is innocent.”
美華夏「それじゃ意味がないでしょう！？ この先ずっと隠遁生活を送れとでも言うの！: Mikana: “That wouldn't mean anything, would it!? Are you saying he should just live as a hermit for the rest of his life!?”
言い争っていたふたりが一斉に俺の方へ振り向いた。: The two of them, who had been arguing, suddenly turned toward me at the same time.
綾音「玖藤さんはどうして事件当時の事を覚えていないんですか？」: Ayane: “Kudou-san, why is it that you don't remember the time of the incident?”
美華夏「そんなに飲んでたかしら？」: Mikana: “Were you really drinking that much?”
奏介「二、三杯しか飲んでなかったが……あの時は帰り道で急に回ってきたな」: Sousuke: “I only had two or three glasses... but at that time, it hit me suddenly on the way home.”
綾音「お酒ってそんな酔い方するんですか？」: Ayane: “Can alcohol make you get drunk like that?”
美華夏「人それぞれじゃないかしら？」: Mikana: “It varies from person to person, I suppose.”
奏介「後にも先にも、あんな酔い方をしたのはあの時だけだな」: Sousuke: “That was the only time, before or since, that I’ve ever gotten drunk like that.”
綾音「普段はそんな酔い方しないんですよね？」: Ayane: “You don't usually get drunk like that, do you?”
奏介「そうだな……格別強い酒を飲まない限りは」: Sousuke: “Yeah... unless I drink something exceptionally strong.”
あの日はウイスキーだったから、それほど強いというわけでもない。それでも酔ってしま: That day it was whiskey, so it wasn't particularly strong. Even so, the fact that I got drunk must have been because I was tired, after all.
綾音「被害者についての心当たりはありますか？」: Ayane: “Do you have any leads regarding the victim?”
俺が答えようとしたとき、美華夏が大きな声で遮ってきた。: Just as I was about to answer, Mikage interrupted with a loud voice.
美華夏「素人探偵じゃあるまいし、どうしてそう暴き立てようとするの！？ 奏介の気持: Mikana: “You’re not some amateur detective, so why are you trying to dig everything up like this!? Have some consideration for Sousuke’s feelings!”
綾音「そんなつもりじゃありませんっ！ 訊かないと判らないじゃないですかっ！！」: Ayane: “I didn't mean it like that! I won't know unless I ask!!”
奏介「綾音の言うとおりだ。俺が出来るだけ思い出さないとな」: Sousuke: “Ayane is right. I have to remember as much as I can.”
先ほどまでとは打って変わり、冷静な口調で綾音は頷いた。: Ayane nodded with a calm tone, quite a change from a moment ago.
奏介「まだ俺自身、少し混乱してるんだ。思い出すようにはするが……」: Sousuke: “I’m still a bit confused myself. I’ll try to remember, but...”
綾音「……わかりました。ですけど、これだけは訊かせてください」: Ayane: “...I understand. However, please let me ask just this one thing.”
諦めるつもりはないのか、綾音はなおも尋ねてきた。: It seemed she had no intention of giving up, as Ayane pressed further.
綾音「わたしが疑問に思うのは、どうして玖藤さんが巻き込まれたのかということです」: Ayane: “What I wonder is why Kudou-san got caught up in this.”
綾音「被害者は女性です。……玖藤さんは自ら進んで女性を殴りに行くような人じゃない: Ayane: “The victim was a woman. ...Kudou-san isn't the kind of person who would go out of his way to hit a woman, right?”
綾音「あの日玖藤さんは創傷を負っていました。だったらその女性が凶器を持って襲いか: Ayane: “That day, Kudou-san had injuries. In that case, it’s only natural to think that the woman attacked him with a weapon, and he had no choice but to fight back.”
綾音「ここで疑問がひとつ浮かびます。どうして玖藤さんが襲われる羽目に陥ったのか、: Ayane: “This brings one question to mind. I feel it’s important to think about why Kudou-san ended up being attacked in the first place.”
奏介「俺が襲われた理由……ね」: Sousuke: “The reason I was attacked... huh.”
さあ――何故だろう。いきなり芸妓に襲われるような理由はない――はずだ。: Well—I wonder why. There’s no reason—shouldn't be—for a geigi to suddenly attack me.
芸者遊びをするほど裕福でもないのは当然だが、あの女とは逢ったこともない。: It goes without saying that I’m not wealthy enough to indulge in geisha parties, but I hadn't even met that woman before.
本当に唐突だった。: It was truly out of the blue.
美華夏「知らずに恨みを買っていた――という事はないわよね？」: Mikana: “You didn’t unwittingly buy someone’s resentment—did you?”
大人しく聞いていた美華夏が口を挟む。: Mikage, who had been listening quietly, interjected.
奏介「――さあな。人なんざ何時何処で恨みを買うかわからねえよ」: Sousuke: “—Who knows. You never know when or where you might buy someone's resentment.”

美華夏「――待って。そうすると最近の《連続狂人殺傷事件》と同じ事に……」: Mikana: “—Wait. If that’s the case, then it’s the same as the recent 'Serial Madman Stabbing Incidents'...”

綾音「その被害者が玖藤さんだったという事になりませんか？」: Ayane: “Could it be that Kudou-san was the victim of that?”

綾音「ただ……あの時はすぐに憲兵がやってきました。玖藤さんが殺していないとすれば: Ayane: “However... the military police showed up immediately at that time. If Kudou-san didn't kill her, she must have been killed and witnessed right after he left.”
綾音「そうすると、目撃者と真犯人が同じである可能性もあり得るんです」: Ayane: “In that case, it’s possible that the witness and the true culprit are the same person.”
綾音「つまり――玖藤さんに罪を被せるのを目的としていたとしか思えないんです」: Ayane: “In other words—I can only think that the objective was to pin the crime on Kudou-san.”
美華夏「――それに何の意味があるのかしら？」: Mikana: “—And what would be the point of that?”
綾音「解りません。殺された芸子さんに恨みがあったのか、単純に人を殺してみたかった: Ayane: “I don't know. Whether they had a grudge against the geisha who was killed, or if they simply wanted to try killing someone...”
綾音「……そうですね。起こった事象を全てバラバラに考えないとダメなのかもしれませ: Ayane: “...You’re right. Maybe we have to think about every event that happened separately.”
奏介「女が俺に襲いかかってきたこと、何者かによって殺されたこと、憲兵がすぐに追い: Sousuke: “The woman attacking me, her being killed by someone, and the military police chasing me immediately... huh.”
綾音「それと、玖藤さんの意識がはっきりしていないこともです」: Ayane: “And also the fact that Kudou-san’s consciousness wasn't clear.”
奏介「あの夜――ほろ酔いだったのは確かだが、記憶を失うほど飲んじゃいない。だが―: Sousuke: “That night—I was certainly tipsy, but I hadn't drunk enough to lose my memory. Yet—only when I was attacked by that woman, my consciousness became vague.”
奏介「もしかしたら……目撃者がいたとしても気付かなかったかもしれない」: Sousuke: “Maybe... even if there was a witness, I might not have noticed.”
美華夏「――私、一度出版社の方へ戻るわ」: Mikana: “—I’m going back to the publishing office for a bit.”
美華夏「それもあるけど……《連続狂人殺傷事件》についても調べてみないと。関係があ: Mikana: “There’s that, but... I also have to look into the 'Serial Madman Stabbing Incidents.' There’s a connection, right?”
美華夏「貴女にいわれなくても調べるわよ」: Mikana: “I’ll look into it even if you don't tell me to.”
綾音に対する美華夏の態度はずっと素っ気ない。: Mikage’s attitude toward Ayane had been curt the entire time.
美華夏「それと、私の名前は橘美華夏。覚えておきなさい」: Mikana: “And also, my name is Tachibana Mikage. Remember it.”
美華夏「……貴女も暗くなる前にお帰りなさい。最近は物騒なんだから」: Mikana: “...You should also head home before it gets dark. It’s been dangerous lately.”
美華夏「――それじゃ奏介、また今度」: Mikana: “—Well then, Sousuke, see you later.”
そう言い残し、美華夏は部屋を出て行った。: Leaving those words, Mikage left the room.
綾音「……凄い人ですね」: Ayane: “...She’s an impressive person, isn't she?”
おっかなびっくりといった様子で綾音が話しかけてきた。: Ayane spoke to me, looking a bit intimidated.
奏介「……ま、目の敵にされていたようだな。理由はよく解らんが」: Sousuke: “...Well, she seemed to have it out for you. I don't really know the reason.”
奏介「自意識過剰よりは少しぐらい鈍感の方が良いって――な」: Sousuke: “They say it’s better to be a bit thick-skinned than self-conscious—right?”
嘯いて窓辺に寄りかかり、バットの箱を手に取る。: I boasted as I leaned against the window and picked up the pack of Bats.
煙草を買えなかったのを思いだし、誤魔化すように俺は空き箱を握りつぶした。: Remembering that I couldn't buy cigarettes, I crushed the empty box as if to hide it.
夜半過ぎ、ふらりとバーに立ち寄った。: Past midnight, I drifted into the bar.
既に営業は終えているのか、店内から光は漏れてこない。: The business hours seemed to have already ended, as no light leaked from inside.
預かっている鍵を取り出し、扉を開ける。: I pulled out the key I was holding and opened the door.
明かりをつける。当然のことながら誰もいない。: I turned on the light. Naturally, there was no one there.
千花も既に眠ってしまっているのだろうか。: I wondered if Chika had already fallen asleep.
騒ぐつもりもないが、今晩は早めに退散するとしよう。: I didn't intend to make a scene, so I decided to make a quick exit tonight.
カウンタに並んだ酒瓶の中から、ドライ・ジンを抜き取る。: I pulled out a bottle of dry gin from the line of liquor bottles on the counter.
グラスに少量注ぎ、一息にあおる。辛みが喉を刺激する。: I poured a small amount into a glass and drained it in one gulp. The sharpness stung my throat.
不意にかけられた少女の声に振り向く。: I spun around at the girl's voice that was suddenly called out.
以前ここで出会った少女――御巫久遠。: The girl I had met here before—Mikanagi Qwon.
薄暗い店内で気配も何もなく佇んでいたのだろうか。: I wondered if she had been standing there without a trace of a presence in the dim shop.
久遠が微笑んだような気がしたが、目の錯覚か。: I felt like Qwon smiled, but was it just an optical illusion?
彼女は無表情に近い眼差しで俺を見上げている。: She was looking up at me with a gaze that was close to expressionless.
久遠「ですが、死者は何も語りません」: Kuon: “However, the dead do not speak.”
死人が喋りだしたら、墓場は五月蠅くて仕様がないだろう。: If the dead started talking, the graveyard would be too noisy for words.
もし喋る死者があれば――それは既に怪談の部類だ。現実的ではない。: If there were a dead person who talked—that would already belong in the realm of ghost stories. It wasn't realistic.
あの路地で死んだ芸妓が喋れば――俺の立場も変わるだろうか。俺が殺していないと証明: If the geigi who died in that alley talked—would my situation change? Would it be proven that I hadn't killed her?
さすがにそんな現実離れした事はあり得ないのだが――頼りたくもなる。: As expected, such a far-fetched thing couldn't happen, but I found myself wanting to rely on it.
久遠「死者は語りませんが、死体は無言で語ります。殺された死体は特に」: Kuon: “The dead do not speak, but corpses speak silently. Especially murdered ones.”
久遠「私はあいにく人体の解剖をしたことはありません」: Kuon: “Unfortunately, I have never performed an autopsy on a human body.”
久遠は曖昧に頷くと、ピアノの側に歩いていく。: Qwon nodded vaguely and walked over to the piano.
久遠「何か弾いてください。……聴いたことのない曲を」: Kuon: “Play something for me. ...A song I’ve never heard.”
奏介「聴いたことのない曲、か……まあ、いつも適当に弾いているから、ほとんどが聴い: Sousuke: “A song you’ve never heard, huh... well, I usually play on a whim, so most of them will be songs you’ve never heard.”
久遠は鍵盤の蓋を持ち上げ、赤いフェルトの覆いを外す。: Qwon lifted the lid of the keyboard and removed the red felt cover.
奏介「俺は音大でも出来の悪い方だったからな。音楽理論もなにもなく、勝手気儘に弾い: Sousuke: “I was on the less successful side even at the music college, after all. I’m a simple man who’s satisfied as long as I can play however I want, without any music theory or anything.”
奏介「――お陰で途中で飛び出しちまったけどな」: Sousuke: “—Thanks to that, I ended up dropping out halfway through, though.”
空になったグラスに、さらにジンを注ぐ。: I poured more gin into the empty glass.
久遠「――自由を満喫しているつもりですか」: Kuon: “—Do you intend to fully enjoy your freedom?”
久遠「どんなに自由に旋律を奏でたところで、音階や音調は既存のもの――基本に縛られ: Kuon: “No matter how freely you play a melody, the scales and keys remain established—bound by the basics.”
久遠が鍵盤を叩く。ド、ミ、ソの和音だ。: Qwon struck the keys. A C major chord: do, mi, so.
久遠「この音だって決められたもの。いや――ピアノの音階そのものが十二に割られた枠: Kuon: “Even this sound is predetermined. No—the very scales of the piano are contained within a framework divided into twelve.”
奏介「基本を外せば、それは不協和音になる」: Sousuke: “If you step outside the basics, it becomes dissonance.”
久遠「不協和音でも構わないのではないでしょうか」: Kuon: “Is there anything wrong with dissonance?”
奏介「――綺麗な音にはならない」: Sousuke: “—It won't make a beautiful sound.”
久遠「綺麗な音で奏でる音楽が優れているとは思いません。私は――不協和音にこそ興味: Kuon: “I do not believe that music played with beautiful sounds is superior. I—find myself drawn to dissonance.”
久遠「互いにぶつかり合い、譲らずに主張しています。調和のとれた世界よりも楽しげで: Kuon: “They clash with each other, asserting themselves without giving way. It seems more enjoyable than a world in harmony, doesn't it?”
玩具を扱うように鍵盤を叩き続ける久遠。: Qwon continued to strike the keys as if handling a toy.
奏介「……どうだろうな。今の世界が楽しいとは思えない」: Sousuke: “...I don't know about that. I can't say the current world is enjoyable.”
久遠「奏介は今の世の中が不協和音だらけだと感じているのですね」: Kuon: “You feel that today’s world is full of dissonance, don't you, Sousuke.”
奏介「調和がとれているとは思えない」: Sousuke: “I can't say it’s in harmony.”
久遠はピアノから離れ、俺の側に来た。: Qwon moved away from the piano and came to my side.
久遠「調和――統制はとれています。昨今の事件――今年起こった二・二六事件に代表さ: Kuon: “Harmony—control is maintained. Through the recent incidents—the attempted coups involving the military, represented by the February 26 Incident that happened this year.”
久遠「方法が間違っていようと、調和は調和です。特高、憲兵が幅を効かせ、共産主義、: Kuon: “Even if the methods are wrong, harmony is harmony. The special police and military police exert their influence, and communism and liberalism are eliminated—”
久遠「政党政治は終焉を迎え、軍閥主導で統制を謀っています。これこそがつまらない―: Kuon: “Party politics has come to an end, and they are seeking control under the leadership of military factions. This is exactly what’s boring—harmony.”
久遠はじっと俺の目を見つめている。不穏分子のような事を言いながらも、その瞳は澄ん: Qwon was staring straight into my eyes. While saying things that sounded like a subversive element, her eyes were clear and transparent.
いや――済んだ瞳をしているからこそ、今の世の中に対してつまらないと言っているのだ: No—was it because she had clear eyes that she was saying the current world was boring?
奏介「――俺には難しい事はよく解らねえよ」: Sousuke: “—I don't really understand difficult things.”
久遠「奏介が世界を不協和音だらけだと感じるのは、貴方自身が世界から見て不協和音だ: Kuon: “The reason you feel the world is full of dissonance is likely because you yourself are dissonance in the eyes of the world.”
難しい事を言っているようだが、結局彼女は俺が枠からはみ出したあぶれ者だと言いたい: She seemed to be saying difficult things, but in the end, she likely wanted to say that I was an outcast who had stepped outside the framework.
にこりともせずに久遠は頷く。: Qwon nodded without so much as a smile.
久遠「この世はくだらなく、つまらない事の積み重ねで構成されていますから」: Kuon: “Because this world is composed of an accumulation of worthless and boring things.”
前に久遠が言った言葉を真似てみる。: I tried mimicking the words Qwon had said before.
久遠「ですがその単純な世界の中で皆生きています。単純なのが嫌ならば――死ぬしかな: Kuon: “However, everyone lives within that simple world. If you dislike its simplicity—you have no choice but to die.”
久遠がカウンタに並ぶ瓶からブランデーの小壜を手に取った。: Qwon picked up a small bottle of brandy from the line on the counter.
栓を抜き、ジンを注いだグラスに近づける。: She pulled the cork and brought it near the glass filled with gin.
中で揺れる琥珀の液体が、彼女の手にあるだけで猛毒のように見えてくる。: The amber liquid swaying inside looked like a deadly poison just by being in her hand.
久遠「そのグラスの中にこれを入れれば――どうなるでしょうね」: Kuon: “If I were to put this into that glass—I wonder what would happen.”
久遠が壜の口をグラスに当てた。: Qwon pressed the mouth of the bottle against the glass.
奏介「――そいつがチェリー・ブランデーで、他にソーダ水とレモンジュースがあればシ: Sousuke: “—If that’s cherry brandy, and you have soda water and lemon juice, it’ll become a Singapore Sling.”
久遠「――そうなんですか。それでは不協和音にはなりませんね」: Kuon: “—Is that so. In that case, it won't be dissonance, will it?”
久遠はブランデーの壜を仕舞う。: Qwon put the brandy bottle away.
奏介「混ぜ過ぎたカクテルの味は不協和音だろうがな」: Sousuke: “The taste of a cocktail that’s been mixed too much would be dissonance, though.”
久遠が並べられた多くの酒瓶に目を向ける。その瞳が好奇心で輝いているように思えた。: Qwon looked at the many liquor bottles lined up. It seemed her eyes were shining with curiosity.
奏介「……勘弁してくれ。酒ぐらいは調和のとれたものを飲ませろ」: Sousuke: “...Give me a break. At least let me drink something in harmony when it comes to alcohol.”
グラスに残ったジンを一気に飲み干した。: I drained the remaining gin in the glass in one gulp.
今更ながら、目の前の奇妙な少女について考えを巡らす。: Belatedly, I pondered about the strange girl before me.
少なくとも、俺がこのバーでピアノを弾いている時には見たことがない。こんな少女がい: At the very least, I had never seen her when I was playing the piano at this bar. If such a girl were here, she would surely catch my eye whether I wanted her to or not.
鍵のかかっていた店内にいた事といい――本当に幽霊のようだ。: Between being inside the locked shop and everything else—she really was like a ghost.
久遠「奏介、ピアノ……弾いてください」: Kuon: “Sousuke, the piano... please play it.”
思い出したかのように久遠が再び呟いた。: As if remembering, Qwon murmured again.
ピアノの椅子に腰を下ろす。久遠がそばに来て手元を覗き込んできた。: I sat down on the piano stool. Qwon came close and peeked at my hands.
奏介「指を見ていたってつまらないだろう」: Sousuke: “It’ll be boring just watching my fingers.”
久遠「いいえ。音楽とは聴くものではなく、感じるものです。五感を駆使し、音を聞き、: Kuon: “No. Music isn't something to listen to; it’s something to feel. Exercising all five senses: hearing the sound, seeing the movement, touching—smelling—tasting.”
久遠の指が俺の手に触れる。同時に、背筋の神経を舌が這いずるような感触が駆け上った: Qwon’s fingers touched my hand. At the same time, I felt a sensation as if a tongue were crawling up the nerves of my spine.
久遠「――芸術とは本来そういったものです」: Kuon: “—Art is originally that sort of thing.”
奏介「……俺のピアノが芸術とは思えないがな」: Sousuke: “...I can't say my piano playing is art, though.”
そうだ。芸術とはほど遠い――世俗に満ちた生活の手段だ。: That’s right. It’s far from art—it’s a means of living filled with secularity.
もっとも、その機会を奪われた今では単なる趣味――手慰みでしかないのだが。: Though, now that I’ve been robbed of that opportunity, it’s nothing more than a simple hobby—a pastime.
久遠「自らの作品を芸術だと評する者は、余程の自信家か身の程知らずです」: Kuon: “A person who evaluates their own work as art is either extremely confident or doesn't know their place.”
鍵盤の上で指を滑らせる。頭の中で旋律を考えるよりも先に手が動き、音を奏でる。: I let my fingers slide over the keys. My hands moved and played sounds even before I thought of a melody in my head.
騒々しくないようにだけ気を付けて、静かに曲を紡いでいく。もう既に深夜といってもよ: Careful only not to be noisy, I quietly wove a song. It was already a time that could be called late at night.
そう考えると、俺の手の動きを興味深げに見つめている少女はいつまでここにいるつもり: Thinking about it, I wondered how long the girl who was watching my hand movements with such interest intended to stay here.
家があるのなら帰らないといけないだろうに、そんな様子もおくびに出さない。: If she had a home, she’d have to go back, but she didn't show even a hint of that.
まあ――この辺りの遊郭ならば明け方近くまで営業しているから、そういった所で生活し: Well—since the pleasure quarters around here are open until near dawn, there might not be a problem if she’s living in such a place.
だが――そうは見えないのもまた確かだ。: But—it was also true that she didn't look like that.
振り返り、久遠に訪ねる。: I turned around and asked Qwon.
久遠「――まさか。演奏者の気分が乗らない時に音楽を奏でられても、気の滅入る曲を聴: Kuon: “—Not at all. Even if music is played when the performer isn't in the mood, one is only forced to listen to a depressing song.”
今の俺では、久遠の言うとおり、陰鬱な曲を弾いてしまうだろう。: The current me would surely play a gloomy song, just as Qwon said.
久遠「代わりに少し話しましょう」: Kuon: “Let's talk for a bit instead.”
奏介「話……ね。いつもここにいるのか？」: Sousuke: “Talk... huh. Are you always here?”
久遠「私は此処だけにいる訳ではありません。奏介と私が此処で逢っているというだけで: Kuon: “I am not only in this place. It is just that you and I meet here.”
――なるほど。少なくとも自縛霊や座敷童の類ではないということか。: —I see. At least she isn't the type to be a tethered ghost or a house spirit.
久遠「日常的に滞在している場所もあります。ただ――帰る場所はありませんけれど」: Kuon: “There are also places where I stay on a daily basis. Though—I have no place to return to.”
奏介「帰れる場所がない、か……俺と同じだな」: Sousuke: “No place to return to, huh... the same as me.”
柚芭と住み暮らした江戸川橋のアパートには戻れない。文化アパートはそれこそ一時凌ぎ: I can't go back to the apartment in Edogawabashi where I lived with Yuzuha. I’m only staying at the Bunka Apartments as a temporary measure.
久遠「奏介とは境遇が全く異なると思いますけれど」: Kuon: “I think my circumstances are completely different from yours, though.”
まさか、久遠が指名手配犯という事はないだろう。: There’s no way Qwon is a wanted criminal.
そんな馬鹿げた話、俺ひとりで充分だ。: One person caught in such a ridiculous story is more than enough.
奏介「やっぱり……花街住まいか？」: Sousuke: “Are you... living in the pleasure quarters after all?”
かねてより気になっていた事を訊ねてみる。さすがにこの手の質問では、返答など期待し: I asked the question that had been on my mind for some time. As expected, I wasn't even hoping for an answer to this kind of question.
久遠「――答える必要はないと思います」: Kuon: “—I do not believe there is a need to answer.”
予想通りと言うべきだろうか。だがその黙秘が何よりも雄弁に物語っているような気がし: Should I say it was as expected? But that silence seemed to speak more eloquently than anything.
久遠「――誤解されないように言っておきますと、私は別に舞妓ではありません。住処を: Kuon: “—To make sure I’m not misunderstood, I’m not exactly a maiko. I just... do not want to be prying into my residence—into myself.”
その気持ちはわかる。個人的領域は他人に踏み荒らされたくない。: I understood that feeling. I don't want my personal territory to be trampled over by others.
ましてや官憲から身を隠している今の立場ではなおさらだ。: Especially in my current position of hiding from the authorities.
奏介「そう言ってくれると助かる」: Sousuke: “It helps when you say that.”
久遠「私は単なる――どこにでもいる、矮小で脆弱な人間です」: Kuon: “I am just—a small, fragile human being who can be found anywhere.”
奏介「そいつは――卑下しすぎだろ」: Sousuke: “That’s—belittling yourself too much, isn't it?”
この聡明で博識な少女が矮小で脆弱なら、俺はどうなる。: If this intelligent and knowledgeable girl is small and fragile, then what does that make me?
いや――俺ばかりではなく、大多数の人間が存在価値のないものに成り果ててしまうだろ: No—not just me, but the vast majority of people would end up being something with no value of existence.
奏介「少なくとも俺から見たら、充分に天才の領域に入っていると思うぞ」: Sousuke: “At least from my perspective, I think you’ve sufficiently entered the realm of a genius.”
意外なほど力強く久遠は俺の言葉を否定した。: Qwon denied my words with surprising strength.
久遠「奏介、天才の定義を言ってみてください」: Kuon: “Sousuke, please state the definition of a genius.”
奏介「天才の定義……か」: Sousuke: “The definition of a genius... huh.”
そう言われると、果たして天才とはどういった人間の事を指すのだろうか。: When put like that, just what kind of person does 'genius' refer to?
様々な知識を備える人: A person equipped with various knowledge
奏介「頭脳だけに限らず、何らかの秀でた能力を持つ人間のことじゃないか？」: Sousuke: “Isn't it a person with some kind of superior ability, not just limited to intellect?”
天才と呼ばれるのは、何も学問に限られるものではないはずだ。: Those called geniuses shouldn't be limited only to academia.
久遠「……それもありますね」: Kuon: “...There is that too, I suppose.”
そう言いつつも、久遠の表情は晴れない。: While saying so, Qwon’s expression didn't brighten.
奏介「様々な知識を備える人間を天才と呼ぶんじゃないか？」: Sousuke: “Wouldn't you call a person equipped with various knowledge a genius?”
久遠「――いいえ、それは単なる知識人です。興味と記憶力があれば誰にでもなれるでし: Kuon: “—No, that is simply an intellectual. Anyone can become one if they have interest and a good memory.”
奏介「記憶力が凄いのも充分天才的だと思うけどな」: Sousuke: “I think having an incredible memory is sufficiently genius-like, though.”
久遠「ですが、それだけでは天才とは呼べません」: Kuon: “However, that alone cannot be called a genius.”
そう言いながら、久遠は首を振った。: Saying so, Qwon shook her head.
久遠「独逸の精神医学者は、天才を『積極的な価値感情を広い範囲の人々に、永続的かつ: Kuon: “A German psychiatrist defined a genius as 'a personality who can evoke positive value-feelings in a wide range of people, permanently and with rare intensity.'”
久遠「ですが――『広い範囲の人々に』と云うくだりが理解できないのです。天才とは、: Kuon: “However—I cannot understand the part about 'a wide range of people.' Must a genius be widely recognized by others?”
奏介「他人が呼ばないと、天才とはいわれないだろ」: Sousuke: “If others don't call you that, you won't be called a genius, will you?”
自称天才などあてにならない。: A self-proclaimed genius can't be relied upon.
久遠「……シュレーディンガーの猫のようですね。観測されない限り、その人は天才であ: Kuon: “...It’s like Schrodinger's cat, isn't it? As long as they are not observed, that person is both a genius and an ordinary person.”
久遠「――無論、天才という曖昧な記号が量子化されているものなのかは判りませんが」: Kuon: “—Of course, I do not know if the ambiguous sign of 'genius' is quantized.”
奏介「……難しい話はさっぱりだ」: Sousuke: “...I don't understand difficult talk at all.”
少なくとも、俺はどんな定義に当てはめても天才ではないようだ。: At the very least, it seemed I wasn't a genius no matter which definition was applied.
それはそれで――気楽ではある。: That was—relaxing in its own way.
久遠「だから私は、天才とは思考の形態が常人と異なっている人を指すと思います」: Kuon: “That is why I think 'genius' refers to a person whose form of thought is different from ordinary people.”
何となく解る。古来より天才と呼ばれた人間は、その才知よりも常軌を逸した発言や行い: I kind of understood. From ancient times, people called geniuses have been made into laughingstocks by society for their unconventional words and deeds, rather than their talent and wisdom.
久遠「だから――他人からは理解されない。観測されない以上、天才と愚才は同価値でそ: Kuon: “That is why—they are not understood by others. As long as they are not observed, the genius and the fool exist there with equal value.”
奏介「……もう少し俺にも解るように説明してくれないか？ 眩暈がしてきた」: Sousuke: “...Could you explain it in a way that I can understand a bit more? I’m starting to get dizzy.”
苦笑混じりにこめかみを押さえてみせる。: I pressed my temples with a bitter smile.
久遠「つまり、私は天才と呼ばれるような存在ではないと云うことです」: Kuon: “In short, I am not the kind of existence that should be called a genius.”
奏介「そりゃ、随分と端折った言い方だな」: Sousuke: “That was quite a simplified way of putting it.”
久遠「天才と呼ばれるべき人は――」: Kuon: “The person who should be called a genius is—”
不意に久遠が言葉を途切れさせた。: Suddenly, Qwon cut off her words.
久遠「――さあ、だれでしょうね」: Kuon: “—Who knows who it might be.”
何事もなかったかのように、久遠は言葉を濁した。: As if nothing had happened, Qwon spoke evasively.
余計な詮索をせず、時計を見て久遠に訊ねる。: Without prying further, I looked at the clock and asked Qwon.
久遠「時間の経過など無意味なのですが……そうですね、そろそろ夜が明けてしまいます: Kuon: “The passage of time is meaningless, but... you’re right, it’s almost dawn.”
奏介「夏の夜明けは早いからな。……送って行くか？」: Sousuke: “Summer dawns are early, after all. ...Shall I walk you back?”
ぴしゃりと冷たい返事。当然だろう、先程住処の詮索はされたくないと言ったばかりだ。: A flat, cold reply. It was only natural, as she had just said she didn't want any prying into her residence.
久遠「――ええ、奏介。また近いうちにお逢いしましょう」: Kuon: “—Yes, Sousuke. Let's meet again soon.”
ぺこり、と頭を下げ、久遠はバーの扉へ歩いていく。: With a small bow, Qwon walked toward the door of the bar.
聞き慣れない単語を呟いた久遠は、俺の反応を待たずにバーを出て行ってしまった。: Qwon, who murmured an unfamiliar word, left the bar without waiting for my reaction.
前回此処で逢った時よりも、さらに彼女に対する疑問が増えてしまったような気がした。: I felt like the questions I had about her had increased even more than when we met here last time.
今日の目覚めは決して良いものではなかった。: Today’s awakening was far from pleasant.
いや――むしろ、最悪に近かった。: No—in fact, it was closer to the absolute worst.
奏介「……なんで此処にいるんだよ……」: Sousuke: “...Why the hell are you in here?”
身体をベッドの上で起こしたまま、俺は頭を抱え込んだ。: Sitting up in bed, I clutched my head in my hands.
まったく――非常識にも程がある。: Good grief—this is beyond a lack of common sense.
葵「仕事よ。早く支度してちょうだい」: Aoi: “Work. Hurry up and get ready.”
奏介「朝飯も喰わせてもらえないのか？」: Sousuke: “Don’t I even get to eat breakfast?”
葵「終わってから食べればいいでしょう？ 時間がないのよ」: Aoi: “You can eat when we’re finished, can’t you? We don’t have time.”
時計を見るとまだ朝の五時を過ぎたばかりだった。: I checked the clock; it was barely past five in the morning.
こんな朝早くから何をしようというのだろうか。: What exactly are we doing this early?
ベッドから出て衣服を着替えることにした。寝ている時は下着すら身につけていなかった: I got out of bed and started changing. I wasn't even wearing underwear while I slept, but I grabbed my clothes stark naked, indifferent to Aoi’s gaze.
下穿きに脚を通しながら文句をたれる。: I grumbled as I stepped into my trousers.
葵「いつから男娼になったのかしら？」: Aoi: “Since when did you become a male prostitute?”
奏介「変な組織に軟禁されるようになってからじゃねえのか？」: Sousuke: “Probably since I started being kept under house arrest by a freaky organization.”
シャツに腕を通す。: I slid my arms into my shirt sleeves.
口元に妖艶な笑みを浮かべ、葵が近づいてくる。: Aoi approached me, a bewitching smile playing on her lips.
彼女の指が釦をかけていないシャツの間から侵入してくる。: Her fingers invaded the opening of my unbuttoned shirt.
奏介「……時間がないんじゃなかったのか？」: Sousuke: “...I thought you said we didn't have time?”
胸元に鋭い痛み。: A sharp pain bloomed in my chest.
見ると葵が爪先で胸元の肉を抉るようにつねっていた。: Looking down, I saw Aoi pinching the flesh of my chest as if to gouge it with her nails.
葵「――せっかく目を掛けてあげてるんだから増長しないでちょうだい」: Aoi: “—Don't let it go to your head just because I’ve taken a liking to you.”
滲み出た俺の血を舐め取り、葵はぴしゃりと釘を刺してきた。: Licking away the blood that seeped out, Aoi drove the point home with chilling finality.
外に出ると、電車通りのガードレールに寄りかかるようにして待っているハチとクマの姿: When we stepped outside, Hachi and Kuma were waiting, leaning against a guardrail along the tram lines.
ハチ「――姐さんの手を煩わせてんじゃねーよ」: Hachi: “—Stop making trouble for the Sister.”
奏介「……あいつが勝手にしたことだ」: Sousuke: “...She’s the one who did whatever she wanted.”
クマ「――止めろ、八郎。姉御にどやされたいのか？」: Kuma: “—Drop it, Hachirou. Do you want the Boss Lady to snap at you?”
忌々しげに睨み付けてくるハチの視線を軽く受け流す。: I casually shrugged off the glare Hachi shot my way.
アパートを出てきたときから葵の姿が見あたらない。: I hadn't seen a sign of her since we left the apartment.
ハチ「先に行ったぞ。姐さんは僕らだけじゃなく、《清鳳会》全体を面倒見ねえとならね: Hachi: “Went on ahead. The Sister doesn't just watch over us; she has to take care of the entire Seihoukai.”
ハチ「借金取り。――ま、アンタにゃお似合いの仕事だろ」: Hachi: “Debt collection. —Well, it’s a perfect job for a guy like you, ain't it?”
専業の借金取りにだけはなりたくない。: The last thing I wanted to become was a professional debt collector.
湯島天神の裏手、路地と住居が複雑に密集した区画で、俺は葵の姿を見つけた。: Behind the Yushima Tenjin shrine, in a district where alleys and residences were intricately packed together, I found Aoi.
奏介「――悪ぃな。こいつらが迷ってよ」: Sousuke: “—My bad. These guys got lost.”
後ろのハチを指さす。: I pointed to Hachi behind me.
ハチ「テメェがちんたらしてるからだろーがっ！！」: Hachi: “That’s 'cause you were taking your sweet-ass time!!”
静かながらも鋭い声で葵が制す。: Aoi restrained him with a quiet but razor-sharp voice.
葵「吠えすぎる犬は番犬にも使えないのよ」: Aoi: “A dog that barks too much isn't even fit to be a watchdog.”
ハチ「は……はいっ！ おいクマ、行くぞっ！！」: Hachi: “Y-yes, ma'am! Hey Kuma, let’s go!!”
慌てたようにハチはクマを引き連れて路地の奥へ消えた。: Flustered, Hachi dragged Kuma along and disappeared into the depths of the alley.
ハチ「オラァ菊池ィ！！ 出てこいやコラァ！！」: Hachi: “Hey, Kikuchi!! Get your ass out here!!”
ハチの罵声が遠くから聞こえてくる。: Hachi’s verbal abuse could be heard from a distance.
葵「簡単に仕事の内容を説明するわ。今回はそこの家に住んでいる男の借金を回収するこ: Aoi: “I’ll briefly explain the job. This time, we’re collecting the debt from the man living in that house. Including interest, down to the last cent.”
葵「金貸しは儲かるのよ。利子は勿論のこと、借金のカタに、って云う物でもね」: Aoi: “Money lending is profitable. Not just the interest, but even things taken as collateral.”
葵「無論、穏便に済ませるのが一番よ。解ったらさっさと仕事を開始してちょうだい」: Aoi: “Naturally, it’s best to settle things peacefully. If you understand, get to work.”
弱い連中をいたぶるなんざ――退屈しのぎにすらならない。: Tormenting the weak—it isn't even enough to kill time.
とはいえ、やらないわけにもいかない。: Even so, I have no choice but to do it.
葵に指定された家の前へ。: I walked to the front of the house Aoi had specified.
すでにハチとクマが突入したのか、扉は開け放たれていた。: The door was already flung wide open, likely from Hachi and Kuma’s intrusion.
中からは言い争うような声と、物が壊れる音が響いてくる。: The sounds of an argument and things breaking echoed from inside.
いくら暴力を振るったところで、払えぬものは払えないだろうに――: No matter how much violence they use, a man who can’t pay, won’t pay—
家の中からクマが出てきた。: Kuma stepped out of the house.
クマが背後を親指で示すとほぼ同時、玄関から男が飛び出してきた。: Almost the instant Kuma jerked his thumb behind him, a man came bolting out of the entrance.
ハチ「おいおい――そっちにゃ僕なんかよりもも～っとおっかねえ兄ちゃんがいるんだぜ: Hachi: “Hey, hey—there’s an even scarier guy over there than me, you know?”
男の尻を追い立てるようにハチも出てきた。: Hachi followed him out, as if nipping at the man’s heels.
ハチ「へっ……先生よぉ、やっちまってくださいや」: Hachi: “Heh... go ahead, Teacher, show him what for.”
俺に向かってハチがそんな軽口を叩いてきた。: Hachi directed that bit of light banter toward me.
そう俺が鼻を鳴らして応えた時だった。: It was just as I let out a snort in response.
前とは違う、よく晴れた夏の朝。: Different from before, a clear summer morning.
前と同じ、眩暈と頭痛――: Same as before, the dizziness and the headache—
前とは違い、酒も入っていないのに――何故だ？: Unlike before, I haven't had a drop to drink—so why?
地面が近づく。いや――俺が地面に近づく。: The ground is coming closer. No—I’m getting closer to the ground.
とすれば、この後には――: If so, what comes next is—
貌を上げる。霞む視界の中――俺の様子を見て戸惑っているらしいハチとクマが見える。: I lifted my head. Through my blurring vision—I saw Hachi and Kuma, looking bewildered by my state.
そしてその後ろ、ゆっくりと立ち上がる男の手に光る物が――: And behind them, in the hand of the man slowly getting to his feet, was something glinting—
振り向いたハチの脇腹を、男が刃物で薙ぎ払った。: As Hachi turned around, the man slashed across his flank with a blade.
やはり、あの時の女と同じだ。: Just as I thought, he's the same as that woman back then.
目に尋常ではない光を宿し、犬歯をむき出しにして嗤っている。: A bizarre light dwelt in his eyes, and he bared his canines in a sneer.
ハチ「な……なんなんだよ、このっ……！」: Hachi: “W-what the hell is up with this guy...!”
脅えた声をハチが漏らす。: Hachi let out a terrified whimper.
叫ぶと同時、俺は男に飛びかかっていった。: Simultaneously with my shout, I lunged at the man.
刃物が地面に落ち、ハチが呻きながら倒れ込む。: The blade hit the ground, and Hachi collapsed, groaning.
俺はそのまま男に組み付き、暴れられないように押し倒して腕を極めた。: I grappled the man, pinning him down and locking his arm so he couldn't struggle.
俺が不自由なく動けていることに気付いた。: I realized I was moving without any difficulty.
気付けば男も大人しく――ぐったりとしている。: Before I knew it, the man had gone quiet—limp.
気絶するほど強く押し倒した覚えはない。: I didn't remember pinning him hard enough to knock him out.
当然の事ながら――死んでなどいないはずだ。: Needless to say—he shouldn't be dead.
刺された箇所を手で押さえながら、ハチは情けない声で呻いた。: Hachi groaned pitifully, clutching the spot where he’d been stabbed.
クマ「……痛みに耐えてこそ男というものだ」: Kuma: “...A man is one who endures pain.”
奏介「やれやれ――クマ、ちょっとこいつを押さえてろ」: Sousuke: “Good grief—Kuma, hold this guy down for a second.”
クマに男を押さえつけさせ、ハチの元へ歩み寄る。: I had Kuma pin the man and walked over to Hachi.
ハチ「さ……触んなっ！ 痛ぇんだからよぉ……」: Hachi: “D-don’t touch it! It hurts, dammit...!”
半ば無理やりハチの手をどかせ、脇腹の傷を診る。: I half-forced Hachi’s hand away and examined the wound on his side.
傷は深くない。深くないというよりも――: It wasn't deep. In fact, calling it a wound was—
ほんの少し血が滲んでいる程度だ。: There was barely any blood seeping out.
ハチ「どっ、毒でも塗られてたらどうすんだよぉ！？」: Hachi: “W-what if he put poison on it or something!?”
ハチの悲鳴を聞き付けたのか、周囲の家屋から野次馬も出てきたようだ。: Likely hearing Hachi’s scream, a crowd of onlookers started emerging from the surrounding houses.
人混みをかき分けて葵が姿を現した。: Aoi appeared, pushing through the crowd.
手には拳銃を提げていたが、すぐに仕舞い込んだ。警戒しての事だろうが、さすがに衆目: She was clutching a handgun, but she tucked it away immediately. Likely a precaution, but she clearly had no intention of exposing such a dangerous object in public.
と、クマが押さえつけている男を指す。: I pointed to the man Kuma was pinning.
感慨なさげに呟き、葵は男へ近づく。: Aoi murmured without emotion and approached the man.
男の髪を掴み、貌を上げさせる。男は既に意識を回復していたらしく、脅えた目を葵に向: She grabbed the man’s hair and forced his head up. He seemed to have already regained consciousness, as he turned terrified eyes toward Aoi.
男「ひっ――ち、違うんです……俺じゃ……」: Man: “Hie—no, you’re wrong... it wasn't me...”
男「俺じゃないっ！！ 俺は何もやっちゃいない！！」: Man: “It wasn't me!! I didn't do anything!!”
男は葵の言葉を無視して叫んだ。: The man ignored Aoi’s words and screamed.
男「知らねえよぉ！ 勝手に身体が……うっ！！」: Man: “I don't know anything! My body just moved on its own... ugh!!”
葵「――黙れと言ったのよ」: Aoi: “—I told you to be quiet.”
男の鳩尾に葵の膝がめり込んでいた。: Aoi’s knee buried itself in the man’s solar plexus.
葵の乱暴さよりも先に、男の不可解な言動――行動が気にかかった。: Before I could process Aoi’s violence, I was concerned by the man’s inexplicable words—and actions.
葵「どうもこうも――借金返済を迫られたこの男が、逆上して落ちていた刃物でハチを刺: Aoi: “Nothing special—this man, pressed for debt repayment, flew into a rage and stabbed Hachi with a blade he found on the ground—that’s all.”
くだらなさそうに葵は男から手を離した。: Aoi let go of the man with a look of utter boredom.
葵「――誰かが警察を呼んだようね。まったく――余計な事を……」: Aoi: “—It seems someone called the police. Honestly—how unnecessary...”
面倒くさそうに葵は呟く。: Aoi murmured with an air of annoyance.
葵「万一のこともあるわ。貴方は一旦此処を離れて、警官から目撃されないようにしなさ: Aoi: “There’s a slight chance of trouble. You leave this place at once so the officers don't spot you.”
奏介「――俺の事は話がついてるんじゃないのか？」: Sousuke: “—I thought my situation had been settled?”
葵「――だから万一よ。上層部は買収できていても、現場の警官がもし貴方を見知ってい: Aoi: “—That’s why I said 'a slight chance.' Even if the higher-ups have been bought, if an officer on the scene recognizes you—it will cause chaos.”
組織的な捜索は打ち切らせたが、一度現場に回覧された手配書の記憶までは消せないとい: The organized search was called off, but I suppose the memory of the wanted posters circulated to the officers on the beat can’t be erased.
確かにこんな現場で職務質問でもされた日には――混乱は必至だ。: Certainly, if I were questioned in a place like this—total chaos would be inevitable.
葵「ハチ、クマ。貴方たちは残って警官に証言なさい。被害者なのだから、その点を忘れ: Aoi: “Hachi, Kuma. You stay behind and give your statements to the police. You’re the victims, so don't forget that.”
追い立てるように、葵は俺に向かって手を振った。: Aoi waved her hand at me, ushering me away.
誰からも見咎められなかったのを幸い、大きく区画を迂回して野次馬の後ろまで戻ってき: Fortunate that no one challenged me, I took a wide detour around the block and circled back behind the crowd.
どうしてもここから離れられなかった。: I simply couldn't leave this place.
あの男――そして俺自身、あの雨の夜と同じ状況だった。: That man—and I, myself—were in the same situation as that rainy night.
何とかしてそれを突き止めなくてはならなかった。: I had to find a way to figure it out.
今――俺が巻き込まれている全ての事項の元凶。: The source of all the matters I’m currently embroiled in.
その解法が見つかるかもしれない。: I might find the solution right here.
現場となった家の前を窺うと、既に警察が到着しているようで、制帽を被った警官が何人: Spying on the house from the front, it seemed the police had already arrived; several officers in peaked caps were milling about.
男は気を失ったまま拘束されたらしく、警官に挟まれたまま横たわっている。ハチとクマ: The man seemed to have been restrained while unconscious, lying between two officers. Hachi and Kuma were also surrounded by police, likely being questioned.
葵は背広姿の男と話しているようだ。: Aoi appeared to be speaking with a man in a business suit.
刑事だろうか。だが初動の段階で刑事部の者がいるのは妙だ。かといって葵と話している: A detective, perhaps? But it’s strange for someone from the detective division to be there at the initial stage. Then again, considering he’s speaking with Aoi, he doesn't seem like an ordinary citizen.
微かに『またか』『これで五回目だぞ』などと警官が喋っているのが聞こえてくる。: Faintly, I could hear an officer saying things like "Again?" and "This is the fifth time."
何が『また』で、何が『五回目』なのか。: What is "again," and what is "the fifth time"?
こんな事件が『また』『五回目』なのか。: Has an incident like this happened "again," for a "fifth time"?
《連続狂人殺傷事件》――その言葉が脳裏をよぎった。: The "Serial Madman Stabbing Incidents"—those words flashed through my mind.
善良な市民が突如狂ったように暴れ出し、他者を傷つける。: Honest citizens suddenly going on a rampage and hurting others.
借金を抱えていた男が善良であったかどうかはともかく、あの――狂気に歪んだ表情は、: Whether the man in debt was honest or not, that—expression distorted by madness didn't seem like something born of being cornered.
その貌が雨の夜に逢った芸妓と重なる。: That face overlapped with the geigi I met on the rainy night.
そしてあの夜の俺自身の貌とも――: And with my own face from that night—
あの時、俺はあんな貌をしていたのだろうか。: Did I have a face like that back then?
あの夜、俺もあのように狂っていたのだろうか。: Was I mad like that that night?
あの夜、俺はあの女を殺したのだろうか――: Did I kill that woman that night—?
葵と話していた背広姿の男――琢磨と。: The man in the suit who was talking to Aoi—Takuma.
野次馬の喧噪の中、奴の舌打ちがやけにはっきりと聞こえた。: Amidst the clamor of the crowd, his clicking tongue sounded incredibly clear.
舌打ちしたいのは俺の方だ。: I’m the one who wants to click my tongue.
周りの野次馬に不審がられないよう、ゆっくりと後退る。: I slowly backed away so as not to arouse the suspicion of the onlookers.
奴のことだ。上層部から捜査中止を言い渡されようが関係なく俺を追ってくるだろう。: He’s the type who would pursue me regardless of the order to stop the investigation from the higher-ups.
その予想を裏切らず、奴がこちらに脚を踏み出す。: Living up to that expectation, he took a step toward me.
奏介「――厄介な奴だな、ったく……」: Sousuke: “—A real pain in the ass, honestly...”
これ以上此処に止まるのは危険だ。: It was dangerous to stay here any longer.
俺は踵を返し、周囲に不審に思われない程度の早足でその場を後にした。: I turned on my heel and left the area at a pace fast enough to escape but not so fast as to seem suspicious.
いくつかの角を折れ、現場からは随分と離れたが、まだ琢磨はついてきているようだった: I turned several corners and was quite a distance from the scene, but Takuma still seemed to be following me.
不意に幼い頃を思い出す。近所の連中と警泥をやったときも、警察組のあいつは執拗に泥: Suddenly, I remembered when we were kids. When we played Cops and Robbers with the neighborhood kids, he, on the police side, would relentlessly hunt down the thieves.
無論俺は最後までしっかりと逃げていたのだが。: Naturally, I was always the one running right until the end.
そんな過去の通り、奴は刑事になり、俺は犯罪者になって――とんだお笑い種だ。: True to that past, he became a detective, and I became a criminal—what a joke.
奴はまだ追いかけてきている。過去の思い出に浸っている場合ではなかった。: He’s still chasing me. This was no time to drown in nostalgia.
足を速め、路地を駆け抜ける。: I sped up, sprinting through the alleys.
適当に走っていれば撒けるだろうと考えていたのだが――: I thought I could lose him if I just ran at random, but—
無闇やたらに走り回った所為か、此処がどの辺りかも判らなくなっている。: Perhaps because I’d been running around recklessly, I didn't even know where I was anymore.
このまま隠れてやり過ごせるだろうか。: Could I hide and wait it out like this?
だが近くまで琢磨は迫ってきていて――: But Takuma was already closing in—
袋小路の入口に奴が現れた。: He appeared at the entrance of the cul-de-sac.
琢磨「随分と逃げたモンだな、玖藤」: Takuma: “You ran quite a way, Kudou.”
いや――日頃の運動不足が祟ったのか、俺の方が若干苦しいか。: No—perhaps because of my daily lack of exercise, I was the one struggling a bit more.
奏介「手前ェもよく追いかけてきたな」: Sousuke: “You sure did a good job of chasing me.”
琢磨「……世間を賑わす兇悪犯だからな」: Takuma: “...Well, you’re a vicious criminal who’s making quite a stir, after all.”
仕事熱心なことで――と続けようとした矢先、琢磨が動いた。: I was about to say "Dedicated to your job, as always," but Takuma moved.
逃れようと反射的に動いたが、逃げ切れない。: I moved reflexively to escape, but I couldn't get away.
奴の手に襟首を掴まれ、体勢を崩して倒れ込む。: He grabbed my collar, throwing me off balance, and I tumbled to the ground.
のし掛かられ、腕を極められる。首根っこを抑え付けられたままで、頬に尖った砂利が食: He loomed over me, locking my arm. With my neck pinned down, sharp gravel bit into my cheek.
俺は身体の力を抜いた。ここまで完膚無きまでに押さえ込まれてしまえば暴れるだけ無意: I let my body go limp. Once I was pinned this thoroughly, struggling was pointless.
奏介「――逮捕したければ逮捕しろ」: Sousuke: “—If you want to arrest me, go ahead.”
半ば自棄になり、背中の琢磨に向けて言い放つ。こいつに手柄を立てさせるのは癪だが、: In half-despair, I threw the words back at Takuma. It rankled to let him get the credit, but it was better than being caught by those other guys—the military police.
琢磨「……逮捕してやりたい所だけどな」: Takuma: “...I’d love to arrest you, but...”
そう琢磨は呟くと戒めの力を抜いた。: Takuma murmured and loosened his grip.
楽にはなったが、まだ琢磨は俺を放すつもりはないらしい。しっかりと腕は掴まれている: I was relieved of the pressure, but Takuma didn't seem to have any intention of letting me go yet. He still had a firm hold on my arm.
琢磨「何処をほっつき歩いてやがったんだ、この野郎」: Takuma: “Where the hell have you been wandering, you idiot?”
俺が動けないのをいいことに頭を小突いてくる。: Taking advantage of the fact that I couldn't move, he poked me in the head.
琢磨「関係あるんだよ。俺は刑事だぞ？」: Takuma: “It is my business. I’m a detective, remember?”
奏介「――ハッ、そんなに手柄が欲しいのか？」: Sousuke: “—Hah, do you want the credit that badly?”
頬に奴の拳が降ってきた。: His fist came down on my cheek.
琢磨「お前に構ってる暇はねーんだよ」: Takuma: “I don’t have time to mess around with you.”
奏介「だったらとっとと退けやがれ」: Sousuke: “Then get the hell off me.”
琢磨は退ける気配を見せない。: Takuma showed no sign of moving.
琢磨「お前に訊きたい事がある」: Takuma: “There’s something I want to ask you.”
ここ最近、いろいろありすぎた。公僕のこいつなら山程訊きたい事があるだろう。: Lately, too much has happened. For a servant of the public like him, there must be a mountain of things he wants to ask.
――求めに応じて答えてやるほどお人好しではないが。: —Not that I’m nice enough to answer just because he asks.
その問いに答えてやる程――お人好しじゃない。: I’m not nice enough to answer that question.
その問いに答えられる程――何も覚えちゃいない。: And I don't remember enough to be able to answer it.
呆れたように琢磨。奴は諦めたのか心底嫌になったのか、俺の上から身体を退け、戒めを: Takuma sounded exasperated. Whether he’d given up or was just sick of it all, he got off me and released his hold.
奏介「……逃げるぞ？」: Sousuke: “...I’m going to run, you know?”
――やれやれ。そう云われてしまっては動けない。――裏がないとも限らない。: —Good grief. When put like that, I can’t move. —There might be a hidden catch.
奏介「……俺が殺ったと言ったらどうするつもりだ？」: Sousuke: “...What would you do if I said I did kill her?”
琢磨「逮捕した上で――取り調べだな。この事件、あまりにも不可解な点が多すぎる」: Takuma: “I’d arrest you and then—interrogate you. There are too many inexplicable points in this case.”
それは一介の刑事如きが調べられる事ではないだろうに。: As if a mere detective could investigate something like this.
琢磨「お前、何に巻き込まれた？」: Takuma: “What exactly did you get involved in?”
奏介「俺を追って来たって事は解ってんだろ？」: Sousuke: “You followed me, so you should know, right?”
琢磨「解ってたら訊かねーよ。不可解すぎるって言ってんだろ？ 殺しがあり、すぐさま: Takuma: “If I knew, I wouldn't be asking. I told you it’s too inexplicable, didn't I? A murder happens, and immediately you’re wanted as the culprit.”
琢磨「そしたらどうだ。次の日には捜査打ち切り、余所が後を引き継ぐときた。俺みたい: Takuma: “And then what? The next day, the investigation is shut down, and another department takes over. You have to assume some kind of power that guys on the scene like me don't understand is at work.”
苛立たしげに足を踏み鳴らす琢磨。: Takuma stamped his foot in frustration.
なるほど――《清鳳会》の裏工作はしっかりと力を及ぼしていると云うことか。: I see—the Seihoukai’s behind-the-scenes maneuvering is exerting its power correctly.
琢磨「ただでさえ最近の一般警察官の力は弱えんだ。余所と違ってな」: Takuma: “The power of the regular police has been weak lately, anyway. Unlike some other places.”
琢磨「少なくともお前は、殺人の重要被疑者ではあるが、思想犯や共産主義者ではない。: Takuma: “At the very least, you’re an important suspect in a murder, but you aren't a political criminal or a communist. I know that much. That’s why—it’s inexplicable.”

マスター「……すまない。少々はしゃぎすぎたようだ」: Master: “...Sorry. We got a little carried away.”

千花「……わかればいいんです。わかれば」: Chika: “...As long as you understand. As long as you understand.”
千花は溜息を吐き、階段を上っていった。: Chika let out a sigh and headed back up the stairs.
マスター「寝入っていた所を起こされれば機嫌も損ねるだろうさ」: Master: “Anyone would be in a bad mood if they were woken up from a deep sleep.”
マスター「お前が煽ったからだ」: Master: “You’re the one who egged me on.”

マスター「そうだな。珈琲を淹れて貰えなくなれば大問題だ」: Master: “Yeah. It would be a huge problem if she stopped brewing coffee for us.”

マスターが肩をすくめ、少しふて腐れたように呟いた。: The master shrugged, mumbling with a hint of sulkiness.
扉を叩いている音で目が醒めた。: The sound of someone knocking on the door woke me up.
だが、葵なら勝手に室内へ入ってくるはずだ。: But if it were Aoi, she’d just barge in on her own.
ベッドから出て扉に近づく。銃を持っていないのが悔やまれる。外に誰がいるかわからな: I get out of bed and approach the door. I regret not having my gun. I have no idea who’s outside.
ナイフを折りたたみ、鍵を開けて綾音を部屋に招き入れる。: I fold the knife, unlock the door, and invite Ayane inside.
綾音「ちょっと話したいことが……」: Ayane: “There’s something I wanted to talk to you about...”
視線を逸らし、何か言い難そうに口を小刻みに動かしている。: She looks away, her lips moving slightly as if she’s struggling to say something.
そういや、起きてすぐだった所為で下着以外何も身につけていなかった。: Right, I’d just woken up, so I wasn't wearing anything but my underwear.
綾音は耳たぶまで真っ赤に染めてそっぽを向いている。: Ayane’s ears were turning bright red as she looked elsewhere.
ズボンを穿きながら呟く。: I mumble while putting on my pants.
奏介「男の裸なんて見たことねえだろ」: Sousuke: “I take it you’ve never seen a naked man before.”
奏介「よかったな、フリチンじゃなくて」: Sousuke: “Lucky for you I wasn't buck naked.”
シャツを羽織ると、ようやく落ち着いたのか綾音はこちらを向いた。: Once I threw on a shirt, Ayane finally looked back, seemingly composed.

さすがに冬だと、下着一枚は辛い。: Even if it’s winter, being in just underwear is a bit much.
まだぶつくさと綾音は文句を垂れている。: Ayane is still grumbling her complaints.
奏介「……で？ 何しに来たんだ、お前は」: Sousuke: “...So? What are you here for?”

綾音「そんなわけないじゃないですかっ！！」: Ayane: “As if that would ever happen!!”
ばんっ、とテーブルを叩く。: She slams her hand down on the table.
綾音「あのですねっ、一度現場を見に行きませんかって誘いに――」: Ayane: “Listen, I came to invite you to go see the scene of the incident with me—”
綾音に叩かれたはずみでテーブルが数十センチ移動した。: The table shifted several inches from the force of Ayane’s strike.
綾音「ちゃんと事件現場を見て、証拠を捜すんですよ」: Ayane: “We’re going to properly look at the crime scene and search for evidence.”
奏介「……もう遅い。あれからどれだけ時間が経ったと思ってるんだ？」: Sousuke: “...It’s too late. Do you have any idea how much time has passed since then?”
もう十日は過ぎている。いくら圧力を受けたとはいえ、殺人事件の現場検証は警察が行っ: It’s already been over ten days. No matter how much pressure they were under, the police should have conducted a forensic investigation of the scene.
憲兵隊が捜査したとしても、余計な詮索をされないために徹底して証拠となりそうな物は: Even if the Kempeitai were the ones investigating, they would have thoroughly removed anything that could serve as evidence to avoid any unwanted scrutiny.

警官や憲兵が張っているかもしれない。警察は捜査をしていないとはいえ、憲兵はとにか: The police or the Kempeitai might be staking the place out. Even if the police aren't investigating, the Kempeitai are—well, they’re suspicious.
本当に俺は捕まらないという保証があるのだろうか。憲兵隊の詰所で名乗ってみるという: Is there really any guarantee I won't be caught? I’m not stupid enough to test a foolish plan like walking into a Kempeitai station and giving them my name.
なにせ連中は、いつでも白を黒に変えられる程の権力と暴力を兼ね備えた集団だからだ。: After all, those guys are a group that possesses both the power and the violence to turn white into black whenever they want.
綾音「犯人は現場に戻る、なんてよくいいますよね。確かに火付け犯は現場に戻って燃え: Ayane: “They often say the criminal returns to the scene, right? There are stories of arsonists returning to watch the fire burn, but—”

綾音「――そういう安心感を得るために、犯人は現場に戻るんですよ」: Ayane: “—The criminal returns to the scene to gain that kind of sense of security.”

綾音「それに……玖藤さんだって何か思い出すかもしれないじゃないですか」: Ayane: “Besides... you might remember something, Kudou-san.”
思い出したとしても、それは最悪の記憶なのではないだろうか。: Even if I did remember, wouldn't it be the worst possible memory?
人を殺したという記憶がないのはある意味幸せなのかもしれない。: In a way, maybe it’s a blessing that I don't have any memory of killing someone.

神楽坂から少し北に行った小径で俺は足を止めた。: I stopped at an alley a little bit north of Kagurazaka.

全く――殺しには適した場所だ。: Truly—a perfect place for a killing.

水を吸って茶色に変色した煙草の吸殻が転がっているくらいだ。: There were just some cigarette butts that had turned brown from soaking up water.
しばらくの間路地を眺めていた綾音が呟く。: Ayane, who had been staring at the alley for a while, murmured.
