ただそれだけだったのだが、妙に気にかかった。: It was just that, but it weighed on my mind for some reason.
奏介「……ただ馴れ馴れしくなっただけか」: Sousuke: “...I guess we’re just getting familiar.”
バーの中にマスターの姿は見えなかった。: The master was nowhere to be seen inside the bar.
奏介「なんでお前がいるんだよ……ひとりで来るなと言わなかったか？」: Sousuke: “What are you doing here...? Didn't I tell you not to come alone?”
頬を膨らませ柚芭は口を尖らせる。: Yuzuha puffs out her cheeks and pouts.
笑いながら柚芭が否定する。: Yuzuha denies it with a giggle.
柚芭「友達と一緒だもん。ねっ、千花ちゃん」: Yuzuha: “I'm with a friend. Right, Chika-chan?”
奏介「……友達となら来てもいいとは言ってないだろ。そもそも、千花は此処の店員だろ: Sousuke: “...I didn't say it was okay to come just because you’re with a friend. Besides, Chika, aren't you staff here?”
柚芭「少しくらいいいじゃない……。最近お兄ちゃんが帰ってこないから寂しかったんだ: Yuzuha: “What’s the harm in a little visit...? I was lonely because Big Brother hasn't been coming home lately...”
奏介「……それを言われると辛いな」: Sousuke: “...When you put it like that, it stings.”
頬を掻く。結局は俺の所為か。: I scratch my cheek. So in the end, it’s my fault.
柚芭「……お兄ちゃんが悪い訳じゃないから謝らないでいいよ」: Yuzuha: “...It’s not like it’s your fault, Big Brother, so you don't have to apologize.”
無理して作った笑顔が痛々しい。: Her forced smile is painful to look at.
まったく――俺は柚芭にこんな表情をさせてしまう程どうしようもないのか。: Damn it—am I really so pathetic that I make Yuzuha wear an expression like that?
千花「あっ……柚芭ちゃん、この前言ってた事……今日やってみない？」: Chika: “Ah... Yuzuha-chan, that thing you mentioned before... do you want to try it today?”
千花「うん。替えもあるし、ちょうどいいでしょ？」: Chika: “Yeah. I have a spare, so it’s perfect, right?”
奏介「……何の話だ？」: Sousuke: “...What are you two talking about?”
柚芭「内緒のお話なんだから、お兄ちゃんは聞いちゃダメなの」: Yuzuha: “It’s a secret, so Big Brother isn't allowed to hear.”
苦笑混じりに肩をすくめ、カウンタに並ぶ酒からモルト・ウィスキーの瓶を取る。グラス: With a wry smile, I shrug and pull a bottle of malt whiskey from the shelf behind the counter. I pour a glass and savor it in small, slow sips, taking it straight.
千花「裏に置いてあるから、そっちで……ね」: Chika: “It’s in the back, so let’s go over there...”
柚芭を伴い、千花が裏へ続く通路へ入っていく。: Accompanied by Yuzuha, Chika heads into the passage leading to the back.
まあ友達同士の他愛ない遊びだろう。: Well, it’s probably just some harmless game between friends.
に、しても――千花が普通に喋っているのを見るのは初めてかもしれない。: Even so—it might be the first time I’ve seen Chika speak normally.
俺が怖がらせているから、俺の前では硬い口調になってしまっているのだろうが。: Maybe it’s because I intimidate her that she uses such a stiff tone around me.
それも俺が悪いのだろうな。: I suppose that’s my fault too.
やれやれ――俺はどこへ行っても周囲に迷惑ばかり振りまいている――: Good grief—no matter where I go, I’m nothing but a nuisance to everyone around me—
柚芭に呼ばれて振り返った。: I turn around at Yuzuha’s call.
奏介「お前なあ……」: Sousuke: “You’ve got to be kidding me...”
さっきまで泣きそうだったのが嘘のようだ。: It’s like her being on the verge of tears earlier was all a lie.
千花の替えの服だろう、ご丁寧にエプロンまで身につけている。: It must be Chika’s spare clothes; she’s even gone so far as to put on an apron.
柚芭「かわいいでしょ、これ？ 一回着てみたかったんだ」: Yuzuha: “Isn't this cute? I’ve wanted to try wearing one once.”
千花「ふふっ……汚しても大丈夫ですよ。元々汚れても構わない素材で出来ていますから: Chika: “Fufu... it’s okay if it gets dirty. It’s made of a material meant for that sort of thing anyway.”
千花「え……違いますよ、わたしの趣味です」: Chika: “Eh... no, it’s just a personal hobby of mine.”
マスターの趣味じゃないだけマシかもしれない。: At least it’s better than it being the master’s hobby.
柚芭「ねぇねぇお兄ちゃん、どっちが可愛い？」: Yuzuha: “Hey, hey, Big Brother, who’s cuter?”
奏介「――なかなか似合ってるぞ、柚芭」: Sousuke: “—It actually suits you quite well, Yuzuha.”
柚芭「ホントっ！？ ……えへへっ、改まって言われると照れちゃうなぁ……」: Yuzuha: “Really!? ...Ehehe, it’s kind of embarrassing when you say it so formally...”
奏介「馬子にも衣装ってやつだな」: Sousuke: “Fine feathers make fine birds, I suppose.”
柚芭「ひっどーい！！ 可愛い妹に対していう言葉かなぁ！？」: Yuzuha: “That’s mean!! Is that any way to talk to your cute little sister!?”
そう千花が俺に同意を求めてくる。: Chika looks to me for confirmation.
ったく――身内を褒めるほど居心地の悪いことはない。: Good grief—nothing makes me more uncomfortable than praising my own family.
それをまあ――こいつはまともに受けやがって……。: And of course—she takes it completely at face value...
柚芭が抱きついてきた。: Yuzuha lunges at me and hugs me.
千花「ふふっ……仲がいいんですね」: Chika: “Fufu... you two really are close.”
柚芭の頭を撫でてやりながら答える。: I answer while patting Yuzuha on the head.
――俺は迷惑かけてばかりだけどな。: —Even though I do nothing but cause trouble for her.
ふたり見比べると、どうしても千花の方が違和感なくまとまっているような印象を受ける: Comparing the two, I can't help but feel that Chika looks more natural in it.
普段から見慣れているからだろうか。: Maybe it’s just because I’m used to seeing her that way.
柚芭「そりゃ千花ちゃんは着慣れてるだろうけどさぁ……ちょっとくらい褒めてくれたっ: Yuzuha: “Well, Chika-chan is used to wearing it, but... you could at least praise me a little...”
千花「あっ、でも、柚芭ちゃんだって可愛いですよ」: Chika: “Ah, but Yuzuha-chan is cute too.”
千花がフォローをしてくれたおかげで、柚芭は少々機嫌を直したのか居住まいを正した。: Thanks to Chika following up, Yuzuha seems to have regained some of her mood as she straightens her posture.
柚芭「……お兄ちゃんもそう思う？」: Yuzuha: “...Do you think so too, Big Brother?”
柚芭「むー……なんかずるい言い方……」: Yuzuha: “Muu... that’s such a cowardly way to put it...”
身内を褒めるほど恥ずかしい事はない。: Nothing is more embarrassing than praising my own kin.
奏介「……やけに上機嫌だな」: Sousuke: “...You’re in an awfully good mood.”
口では否定しつつも、千花の表情は明るい。: Despite her verbal denial, Chika’s expression is bright.
同年代の柚芭と一緒にいるからだろうか。: Maybe it’s because she’s with Yuzuha, who’s around her age.
奏介「――これからも、柚芭の事頼むな」: Sousuke: “—I’m counting on you to look after Yuzuha from now on.”
俺よりもずっと柚芭の側に居られるだろうと期待しての言葉だったが――: I meant it as a way of expressing my hope that she could be by Yuzuha’s side much more than I could, but—
千花は、小さく頭を振った。: Chika gave a small shake of her head.
千花「……ダメです。柚芭ちゃんを守れるのは家族である玖藤さんだけですから。それに: Chika: “...I can’t. The only one who can protect Yuzuha-chan is you, her family, Kudou-san. Besides—”
千花は一旦言葉を止め、俺の貌を見つめた。: Chika paused for a moment, staring into my face.
千花「――今の言葉、もう二度と逢えない様な感じがして好きじゃありません」: Chika: “—I don't like the way you said that. It sounds like we’re never going to see each other again.”
奏介「そういうつもりじゃ――ねえんだけどな」: Sousuke: “I didn't mean it like—well, never mind.”
頬を掻く。どうも重く受け取られてしまったらしい。: I scratch my cheek. It seems she took it much more heavily than I intended.
千花「あっ、その……別に玖藤さんを責めているわけじゃなくて……」: Chika: “Ah, um... I’m not exactly trying to blame you, Kudou-san...”
そのためには、出来る限りそばにいてやらねばならないのだが――: To do that, I need to stay by her side as much as possible, and yet—
柚芭に見つめられ、決まり悪く俺は視線を逸らせた。: Under Yuzuha’s gaze, I awkwardly averted my eyes.
千花に別れを告げ、バーから出る。: After saying goodbye to Chika, I leave the bar.
あとは文化アパートに帰るだけなのだが――: All that’s left is to go back to the Bunka Apartments, but—
柚芭は俺の手をしっかりと握りしめている。: Yuzuha is gripping my hand tightly.
小さく首を振る。: She gives a small shake of her head.
そう云われても、ひとりで帰す訳にもいかない。: Even if she says that, I can't just let her go back alone.
柚芭「お兄ちゃんの部屋……行ってもいい？」: Yuzuha: “Can I... go to your room?”
握られたままの手に力がこもった。: The grip on my hand tightened.
柚芭「どうして？ いいでしょ、お兄ちゃんがどんな生活してるのか気になるし……」: Yuzuha: “Why? It’s fine, isn't it? I’m curious about the kind of life you’re living, Big Brother...”
奏介「道楽であんな処に居る訳じゃない」: Sousuke: “I’m not staying there for fun.”
柚芭が手を放した。: Yuzuha let go of my hand.
俺のいざこざに柚芭を巻き込みたくない。あのアパートに近づけば、《清鳳会》の連中に: I don't want to drag Yuzuha into my mess. If she gets close to that apartment, she might be spotted by the Seifoukai’s men.
俺が逆らえないよう柚芭を拐かす事だってありえる――奴らなら。: They might even kidnap her to make sure I don't step out of line—those bastards are capable of it.
危険な事には可能な限り近づけたくない。: I want to keep her as far away from danger as possible.
柚芭「判ってるんだよ……判ってるんだけど……お兄ちゃんのこと心配だから……」: Yuzuha: “I know... I know, but... I’m just worried about you, Big Brother...”
そっと柚芭の頭に手を載せる。: I gently place my hand on Yuzuha’s head.
柚芭「……ううん、お兄ちゃんは悪くないよ」: Yuzuha: “...No, you haven't done anything wrong, Big Brother.”
――その柚芭の言葉が、逆に俺を責めているように感じる。: —Hearing her say that actually makes me feel like she’s blaming me even more.
少なくとも俺自身が、俺を悪いと思っている。: At the very least, I blame myself.
俺がしっかりしていれば――俺が前後不覚になるまで酔ったりしなければ――あの夜の惨: If I’d been more solid—if I hadn't gotten so drunk that I lost all sense of myself—maybe that nightmare that night could have been avoided.
柚芭「ううん、いいよ。お兄ちゃんだってあまり出歩いちゃいけないでしょ？ あたしな: Yuzuha: “No, it’s fine. You shouldn't be wandering around too much either, right, Big Brother? I’ll be okay.”
柚芭は笑顔を見せる。ほんの少し目元が潤んでいるのが痛々しかった。: Yuzuha shows me a smile. The way her eyes were just slightly watery was painful to see.
柚芭は振り返ることなく走っていってしまった。: Yuzuha ran off without looking back.
飲み直す気にもなれず、まっすぐ部屋に戻ってきた。: I didn't feel like drinking anymore, so I went straight back to my room.
灯りを消し、ベッドに倒れ込む。: I turn off the light and collapse onto the bed.
別に構わないだろう。もう時間も遅いし、今から江戸川橋のアパートへ送るよりは連れて: I suppose it’s fine. It’s late anyway, so it’s better to take her back with me than to try and send her to the Edogawabashi apartment now.
柚芭「ホントっ！？ えへへっ、お兄ちゃんと一緒っ」: Yuzuha: “Really!? Ehehe, I get to be with Big Brother.”
奏介「あんまりくっつくなって」: Sousuke: “Don't cling to me so much.”
さすがに柚芭相手では邪険に扱うわけにもいかず、彼女は俺の手を掴んだままぶんぶんと: I couldn't exactly be harsh with Yuzuha, so she kept swinging my hand around while holding it.
柚芭「こうしてたら、あたしたち恋人に間違えられちゃったりして」: Yuzuha: “If we do this, maybe people will mistake us for lovers.”
しっかり《お兄ちゃん》と呼ばれているし。: She’s clearly calling me “Big Brother,” after all.
奏介「……何が不満だよ」: Sousuke: “...What are you so upset about?”
今度は眉を吊り上げ、俺の腕にしがみついてきた。: This time, she furrowed her brows and clung to my arm.
奏介「お前なあ……ガキじゃねーんだから」: Sousuke: “Hey... you’re not a kid anymore.”
それほど我が侭を言わない柚芭だが、やはりアパートにひとり残されていたのが寂しかっ: Yuzuha doesn't usually act this selfish, but maybe being left alone in that apartment was lonelier than I thought.
――好きにさせてやるか。: —I’ll just let her have her way.
そのまま柚芭を引きずるように、俺はアパートの方角に向かって歩き出した。: Dragging Yuzuha along like that, I started walking toward the apartment.
部屋に通すと、柚芭は目を大きく見開いたまま固まってしまった。: Once I showed her into the room, Yuzuha froze with her eyes wide open.
望んでいた生活ではないのだが、柚芭の様子を見ていたら苦笑するしかない。: It wasn't exactly the kind of life I wanted, but seeing Yuzuha’s reaction, I couldn't help but let out a wry chuckle.
江戸川橋のアパートでは狭い和室に兄妹で暮らしていたのだから、広々とした洋間を見れ: Since we’d been living together in a cramped Japanese-style room in the Edogawabashi apartment, it was only natural for her to be envious of a spacious Western-style room.
また柚芭が声をあげた。今度は感嘆というよりも驚嘆に近かったが。: Yuzuha cried out again. This time it was closer to shock than admiration.
柚芭「……お兄ちゃん、ちゃんとお洗濯してる？」: Yuzuha: “...Big Brother, are you actually doing your laundry?”
部屋のそこかしこに脱いだままの衣類が散乱している。: My discarded clothes were scattered all over the room.
柚芭「はあっ……当然お掃除もしてないよね」: Yuzuha: “Haah... and I assume you haven't been cleaning either.”
柚芭は部屋の隅に溜まった埃を見て嘆息した。: Yuzuha let out a sigh as she looked at the dust gathered in the corners of the room.
柚芭「やっぱりお兄ちゃんは、あたしがいないとダメなんだからっ」: Yuzuha: “I knew it, Big Brother, you really can't handle things without me!”
何が嬉しいのか、声を弾ませる柚芭。: Yuzuha’s voice was bouncy with some kind of joy.
世話を焼くのが好きなんだろう。: I guess she just likes taking care of people.
少し前、独り暮らしをしていた俺の元に転がり込んできた時もそうだった。: It was the same when she moved in with me while I was living alone a while back.
それまで俺の生活は頽廃しきっていたのだが、柚芭のお陰でなんとか人並みの暮らしがで: My life had been completely decadent until then, but thanks to Yuzuha, I was finally able to live like a normal human being.
柚芭「文化アパートって、確かお掃除やお洗濯も頼めばやってくれるんじゃなかった？」: Yuzuha: “The Bunka Apartments... can't you ask them to do the cleaning and laundry for you?”
散らばった衣類をまとめながら柚芭は言う。: Yuzuha spoke as she started gathering up the scattered clothes.
奏介「……ここは一応隠れ家だからな、あまり他人と接したくない。下にあるラウンジや: Sousuke: “...This place is supposed to be a hideout, so I don't really want to interact with others. I haven't even stepped into the lounge or cafe downstairs.”
柚芭「……なんかもったいないね」: Yuzuha: “...That seems like such a waste.”
《清鳳会》の裏工作が成功しているのだから、もっと堂々としていても構わないのかもし: Since the Seifoukai’s cover-up was successful, maybe I could afford to be more open.
だが俺は、連中の言葉を鵜呑みにすることは出来なかった。: But I couldn't bring myself to take their words at face value.
とてもじゃないが街角で大手を振って歩く気分にはなれない。: I certainly wasn't in the mood to walk around the streets with my head held high.
柚芭「――へぇ、お風呂までついてるんだね」: Yuzuha: “—Heh, it even has its own bath.”
浴室の方を覗き込んでいた柚芭は戻ってくるなり、襟元をつまんで広げると鼻を鳴らしは: Yuzuha, who had been peeking into the bathroom, came back and started sniffing the air after pulling at her collar.
柚芭「ねぇねぇお兄ちゃん、あたし汗くさくない……？」: Yuzuha: “Hey, hey, Big Brother, do I smell like sweat...?”
いくら汗ばむ陽気とはいえ、そうすぐに汗臭くなるものでもない。: Even if it’s a warm day, you don't start smelling like sweat that quickly.
別段止める理由もない。: I had no reason to stop her.
柚芭「それじゃ、浴びてくるね。……あ、お兄ちゃん？」: Yuzuha: “Then, I’ll go take one. ...Ah, Big Brother?”
洋風の部屋に舞い上がっているのだろうか。: Maybe she’s just excited about the Western-style room.
奏介「……美華夏の家も洋館だったな」: Sousuke: “...Mikage’s house was a Western-style mansion too.”
震災で家を失って以来、つい最近まで美華夏の家で暮らしていた柚芭にとって、洋風の部: Since Yuzuha had been living at Mikage’s house until recently after losing her home in the earthquake, she should be used to Western-style rooms.
まあそれでも、このアパートのように生活様式までが洋風であった訳ではないだろうが。: Though, the lifestyle here at this apartment probably wasn't quite as Western as this.
……正直、靴を履いたまま部屋に入るというのは未だに抵抗がある。: ...To be honest, I still have some resistance to entering a room with my shoes on.
突然、浴室から柚芭の悲鳴が聞こえてきた。: Suddenly, I heard Yuzuha scream from the bathroom.
柚芭「おっ、お兄ちゃんっ！！ ちょっと来てっ！！」: Yuzuha: “B-Big Brother!! Come here for a second!!”
柚芭のせっぱ詰まった声に、どうもただならぬ雰囲気を感じ、俺は浴室へ続く戸を開けた: Sensing a desperate tone in Yuzuha’s voice, I felt that something unusual was happening and opened the door to the bathroom.
浴室は一面水浸しだった。: The bathroom floor was completely flooded.
柚芭「止まらないよぉ……お兄ちゃん助けてっ！！」: Yuzuha: “It won't stop... Big Brother, help me!!”
泣きそうになりながら――既に泣いているのかもしれないが――柚芭が助けを求めてくる: On the verge of tears—though she might have been crying already—Yuzuha begged for help.
柚芭「何もしてないよっ……蛇口ひねったら戻らなくなっちゃって……」: Yuzuha: “I didn't do anything!! ...I turned the faucet and it just won't go back...”
すでに柚芭は濡れ鼠だ。しっかりと水を吸ってしまった服がぴったりと身体に張り付いて: Yuzuha was already soaked like a drowned rat. Her clothes, which had absorbed a lot of water, were clinging tightly to her body.
壁の上方に固定されている吹き出し口から、水が止めどなく降り注いでくる。: Water was pouring down relentlessly from the showerhead fixed high on the wall.
――まずシャワーの水を止めるべきだろう。: —I should stop the shower water first.
蛇口の栓を握りしめていた柚芭の手をどかし、栓を締めようとする。: I moved Yuzuha’s hands, which were gripping the faucet handle, and tried to close it.
柚芭がひねった拍子に何かに引っかかってしまったのかびくともしない。: It wouldn't budge; something must have gotten caught when Yuzuha turned it.
その間もずっと真上から水が降り注いでいる。: Meanwhile, water kept pouring down from directly above.
俺もすっかり頭から水浸しだ。: I was also completely soaked from head to toe.
奏介「お前は離れてろ。いくら夏でも風邪ひくぞ」: Sousuke: “Just stay away. Even if it’s summer, you’ll catch a cold.”
頷きながらも柚芭はその場を動こうとはしない。: Despite nodding, Yuzuha showed no sign of moving from the spot.
栓を両手で握り力をこめると、突然握り手が回り出した。同時に水流も弱まる。: When I gripped the handle with both hands and put some muscle into it, the handle suddenly turned. At the same time, the flow of water weakened.
柚芭「さすがお兄ちゃん、力持ちだねっ」: Yuzuha: “As expected of Big Brother, you’re so strong!”
奏介「……いいからお前は、さっさと服を着替えろ」: Sousuke: “...Just go and change your clothes already.”
ずぶ濡れのまま背後から抱きついてくる柚芭に、俺はそれしか言えなかった。: Yuzuha hugged me from behind while soaking wet, and that was all I could say to her.
交代でシャワーを浴び、ようやく落ち着く。: After taking turns showering, things finally settled down.
やはり浴びるなら、熱い湯の方がいい。: If I’m going to take one, I definitely prefer a hot bath.
くしゃみのあと、柚芭は袖から半分も出ていない手で貌をおさえた。: After the sneeze, Yuzuha covered her face with her hands, which were barely even sticking out of her sleeves.
さすがに俺のシャツでは大きすぎたか。: I guess my shirt was too big for her after all.
奏介「寝た方がいいんじゃないか？」: Sousuke: “Maybe you should go to sleep?”
風邪をひかれたら困る。: It would be bad if she caught a cold.
そのまま柚芭はぱたっ、とベッドの上に倒れ込んでしまった。: With that, Yuzuha collapsed onto the bed.
つい最近までは、この生活が普通だったのに。: Even though this kind of life was normal for me until just recently.
まあ、今日は柚芭が少々はしゃぎすぎていたこともあるだろうが。: Well, maybe it was partly because Yuzuha was being a little too high-spirited today.
時計を見ると、既に深夜零時を過ぎている。: Looking at the clock, it was already past midnight.
はた、とベッドを見て気付く。: Then it hit me as I looked at the bed.
この部屋にベッドはひとつ。幅広とはいえ、さすがに妹と同じ布団で眠るというのは抵抗: There was only one bed in this room. Even if it was wide, I still felt some resistance to sleeping in the same bed as my sister.
まあ床で寝てしまっても構わないのだが――: I didn't mind sleeping on the floor, but—
まだ本格的な眠りに就く前だったのか、柚芭が薄目を開けた。: Maybe she wasn't quite fully asleep yet, as Yuzuha opened her eyes slightly.
奏介「――柚芭、悪いがもう少し端に寄ってくれ」: Sousuke: “—Yuzuha, sorry, but could you move over a bit more?”
半分夢の中に足を踏み入れているのか、柚芭は寝ぼけたように不満の声を洩らす。: Half-submerged in a dream, Yuzuha let out a sleepy groan of dissatisfaction.
奏介「……まあ、そのままでもいいけどな」: Sousuke: “...Well, I guess you can stay where you are.”
仕方ない、床かソファで――: I’ll just have to use the floor or the sofa—
踵を返した俺のズボンの裾を柚芭が掴んできた。: As I turned to leave, Yuzuha grabbed the hem of my pants.
柚芭「お兄ちゃんなら……いいよ」: Yuzuha: “If it’s you, Big Brother... it’s okay.”
奏介「だったらもう少し向こうに――」: Sousuke: “Then move over a little bit—”
動こうとしない柚芭の身体を脇に押しやると、部屋の明かりを消し、柚芭の隣に寝転ぶ。: I pushed Yuzuha’s unmoving body to the side, turned off the light, and lay down next to her.
身体の力を抜く。: I let the tension out of my body.
――まあ、こんな日もあるだろう。: —Well, I suppose there are days like this.
俺は片腕に柚芭の温もりを感じながら目を閉じた。: I closed my eyes, feeling Yuzuha’s warmth against my arm.
バーへ足を踏み入れると、店仕舞いをしたばかりなのか、千花がホウキで床を掃いていた: When I stepped into the bar, Chika was sweeping the floor with a broom, as if she had just finished closing up.
掃除の邪魔にならぬよう、入ってすぐにある椅子に腰掛ける。客の残したものだろうか、: Not wanting to get in the way of her cleaning, I sat down on a chair near the entrance. A basket of nuts was sitting on the table, likely left by a customer.
砕けた落花生の殻に混じってアーモンドが転がっていた。それをつまみ上げ口にくわえる: Among the broken peanut shells, I found an almond rolling around. I picked it up and popped it into my mouth.
璃宝「――みみっちいことしてないで、こっちいらっしゃいよ」: Riho: “—Stop acting so pathetic and come over here.”
奥から出てきた璃宝に目を向ける。: I looked over at Riho as she came out from the back.
璃宝「アカペラで歌うのも慣れてきたわよ」: Riho: “I’m starting to get used to singing a cappella.”
立ち上がり、カウンタの席へ。すぐ隣に璃宝が腰掛ける。: I stood up and took a seat at the counter. Riho sat down right next to me.
千花「何か飲まれますか？」: Chika: “Would you like something to drink?”
璃宝「水割りをお願い」: Riho: “A whiskey and water for me, please.”
千花が背を向ける。: Chika turned her back to us.
奏介「俺は結構この店に来てるんだけどな」: Sousuke: “I’ve been coming to this shop quite often, though.”
璃宝「いいのかしら？ 出歩くのは危険じゃない？」: Riho: “Is that okay? Isn't it dangerous for you to be out and about?”
呆れたように璃宝は溜息を吐いた。: Riho let out an exasperated sigh.
ラジオから音楽が流れている。: Music was playing from the radio.
俺と璃宝の前にグラスが置かれる。: Glasses were placed in front of Riho and me.
璃宝「そうね……奏介の無事を祈って、なんてどうかしら？」: Riho: “Let’s see... how about a toast to your safety, Sousuke?”
祈るくらいで安全が確保できるのなら、いくらでも祈ってやりたい気分だ。: If praying could ensure my safety, I’d be in the mood to pray as much as it took.
璃宝「千花も何か飲みなさいな」: Riho: “Chika, you should have something to drink too.”
璃宝「気にしないの。ホラ、好きなの飲んじゃいなさいな」: Riho: “Don't worry about it. Go ahead, drink whatever you like.”
遠慮がちに、千花はグラスにオレンジジュースを注ぐ。: Hesitantly, Chika poured herself some orange juice.
三人で軽くグラスを合わせる。: The three of us lightly clinked our glasses.
気怠げな璃宝に声をかける。: I spoke to Riho, who seemed languid.
璃宝「……前はこんな風に飲むのが当たり前だったんだけどねぇ……」: Riho: “...It used to be so normal for us to drink like this...”
バーで演奏したあと、ささやかに杯を交わすのが日課だった。: After playing at the bar, having a modest drink together was part of our daily routine.
適当に理由を作って乾杯をしていたのも、今は昔の話だ。: Coming up with whatever reason to have a toast was all a story from the past now.
やれやれ――水割りが苦く感じる。: Good grief—the whiskey and water tastes bitter.
璃宝「――ま、アンタが無事ならそれでいいわ」: Riho: “—Well, as long as you’re safe, that’s all that matters.”
そう言うと璃宝はグラスをあおった。: With that, Riho finished her drink.
いつの間にかラジオは報道番組に切り替わっていた。: Before I knew it, the radio had switched to a news program.
千花が俺の方を見て息を呑む。: Chika gasped as she looked at me.
奏介「――止めなくていいぞ」: Sousuke: “—You don't have to stop it.”
前にも同じような事があったと思いだし、俺は先に千花を制した。: Remembering that something similar had happened before, I cut Chika off.
どうせ俺の関わった事件は報道されない。――おそらく。: They probably won't report on the incident I was involved in anyway. —Most likely.
ラジオ『先日麻布区六本木で起こった殺人事件について、当局は《連続狂人殺傷事件》と: Radio: “Regarding the murder that occurred in Roppongi, Azabu-ku recently, the authorities are proceeding with the investigation under the belief that it is closely related to the ‘Serial Lunatic Stabbings’—”
俺とは直接関わりのない事件だが、最近この手の話題が巷を賑わしている。: It’s an incident that has nothing to do with me directly, but this kind of topic has been the talk of the town lately.
璃宝「――アンタもこのクチかい？」: Riho: “—Are you one of those too?”
璃宝が水割りのグラスを持ったまま流し目で問いかけてきた。: Riho asked with a side-glance, still holding her glass of whiskey and water.
横目で璃宝を睨み付ける。: I glared at Riho out of the corner of my eye.
璃宝「ふふ――そうするわ。おっかないもの」: Riho: “Fufu—I’ll do just that. You’re scary.”
おどけるように璃宝は言うと、またグラスに口を付けた。: Riho spoke jokingly and then took another sip from her glass.
そんなこと――言われなくても判っている。: Something like that—I already know it without being told.
先日ハチが男に刺されたとき、狂気に彩られた男の表情が、最初に俺に襲いかかってきた: When Hachi was stabbed by that man the other day, his expression, colored with madness, overlapped with the expression of the woman who first attacked me.
そして――俺もあんな狂った貌をしていたのだろう。: And—I must have had a crazed face like that too.
酒を一気にあおる。喉が灼けるように痛んだ。: I downed my drink in one go. My throat burned as if it were being scorched.
あの夜の再現だけは絶対にしたくない。: The one thing I absolutely never want to do is repeat that night.
璃宝「千花、アタシにはおかわり頂戴ね？」: Riho: “Chika, give me a refill, okay?”
千花「璃宝さんもあまり飲まない方が……」: Chika: “Riho-san, maybe you shouldn't drink so much either...”
璃宝「アタシはいいのよ。酔っても奏介のトコ行けばいいし」: Riho: “I’m fine. Even if I get drunk, I can just go to Sousuke’s place.”
璃宝の言葉に、グラスに酒を注いでいた千花の頬が一瞬にして赤く染まる。: At Riho’s words, Chika’s cheeks instantly turned red as she was pouring the drink.
奏介「ウチには泊めねーぞ」: Sousuke: “I’m not letting you stay at my place.”
璃宝「いけずだねぇ、アンタも。ま、後腐れがなくていいんだけどね」: Riho: “How cold of you. Well, I suppose it’s better not to have any lingering attachments.”
お互い、ずるずると関係を引き摺るようなことはしない。: Neither of us was the type to let a relationship drag on.
いくら肌を重ねようが、所詮は他人。お互いを縛り付けるようなことはない。: No matter how much we shared our skin, we were ultimately strangers. There was nothing to bind us to each other.
そういう璃宝との関係が一番楽だ。: That’s why my relationship with Riho was the easiest.
最初の事件――女を殴った時の俺は明らかに狂っていた。: During the first incident—when I hit that woman—I was clearly insane.
酔っていたというだけでは説明が出来ない。: It couldn't be explained just by being drunk.
自分の与り知らぬ処で、自分が殺人を犯していたとすれば深刻にもなる。: If I had committed a murder without my own knowledge, it was something to be serious about.
奏介「俺も――狂っていたんだろうな」: Sousuke: “I guess—I must have been crazy too.”
俺の言葉を否定したのは千花だった。: It was Chika who denied my words.
例え気休めでも、他人から言われると気分が楽になる。: Even if it’s just for peace of mind, hearing it from someone else makes me feel better.
璃宝「フフッ……やっぱり千花は奏介の肩を持つのね」: Riho: “Fufu... as expected, Chika takes Sousuke’s side.”
驚いた千花の手からグラスが滑り落ちた。: In her surprise, the glass slipped from Chika’s hand.
千花「あ、あのっ、わたしそんなつもりじゃなくて……」: Chika: “A-ah, um, I didn't mean it like that...”
あわてふためく千花。オレンジジュースがカウンタの上に広がっていく。: Chika panicked. Orange juice began to spread across the counter.
素早く璃宝が雑巾を拾い上げ、ジュースが流れるのを止めた。: Riho quickly picked up a rag and stopped the juice from flowing.
千花もカウンタを拭き始める。: Chika began to wipe the counter as well.
まあ――こんなのも、日常だった。: Well—even this was part of our daily routine.
璃宝「ほら、アンタも手伝いなさいな」: Riho: “Come on, you help out too.”
渡された雑巾で床にまでこぼれたジュースを拭き取る。: I used the rag I was handed to wipe up the juice that had spilled all the way to the floor.
奏介「服は汚れてないよな？」: Sousuke: “Your clothes didn't get dirty, right?”
自らの身体を眺め回し、千花が答える。: Chika answered after looking over her own body.
本当に――こんな日常は久しぶりだ。: Really—it’s been a while since I’ve had a daily life like this.
璃宝「――ま、今日はこのくらいにしときましょうか」: Riho: “—Well, I guess that’s enough for today.”
グラスに半分以上酒を残し、璃宝は立ち上がった。: Leaving more than half of her drink in the glass, Riho stood up.
璃宝「――あ、そうそう。言い忘れてたけどさ、奏介」: Riho: “—Ah, that’s right. I forgot to mention, Sousuke.”
扉の所で璃宝が振り返る。: Riho looked back at the door.
璃宝「アタシは千花みたいに無条件でアンタを信じるわけじゃないけど……例えアンタが: Riho: “I’m not like Chika; I don't believe in you unconditionally... but even if you really were the culprit, I’d still be on your side.”
千花は俺の無実を信じてくれている。璃宝は事実がどうあろうと、俺の味方で居てくれて: Chika believes in my innocence. Riho stays on my side regardless of the facts.
こんな俺でも味方で居てくれる人がいる。: There are people who will stay on my side, even for someone like me.
璃宝が去った店内、おずおずと千花が声を掛けてきた。: In the shop after Riho had left, Chika spoke up hesitantly.
去り際に軽く千花の肩を叩き、俺は店を出た。: I gave Chika a light pat on the shoulder as I left the shop.
昼前に目覚め、ふらりと神楽坂にやってきた。: Waking up before noon, I wandered over to Kagurazaka.
相変わらずの人出ではあるが、やはり銀座や浅草と比べれば見劣りする。: The crowds were the same as ever, but compared to Ginza or Asakusa, it still looked inferior.
華やかさに欠けている――中途半端に時代に取り残された街。: It lacked glamour—a town that was half-left behind by the times.
そんな中、俺は道ばたに異様なものを見つけてしまった。: In the middle of all that, I found something bizarre on the side of the road.
見なかったことにする: I noticed him, but I decided to pretend I didn't.
タコ焼きを買う: I could just buy some takoyaki.
あんな親父と目が合うことなんざない。: There’s no way I’d lock eyes with an old man like that.
親父の屋台に客はいない。: There were no customers at the old man’s stall.
それどころか、通行人すら避けて歩いているように見える。: If anything, it looked like the passersby were even avoiding walking past it.
ぽっかりと通りの一部に空間が出来ていた。: A gap had opened up in part of the street.
そんな異空間に足を踏み入れることなどできやしない。: I couldn't bring myself to step into such a strange space.
親父の視線を感じながら足早に通り過ぎる。: Feeling the old man’s gaze on me, I hurried past.
まあ――ここいらには食い物屋が多いから、別に屋台で食うこともない。: Well—there are plenty of places to eat around here, so I don't have to eat at a stall.
もう少しぶらついて、アパートに戻ろう。: I’ll wander around a bit more and then head back to the apartment.
ここで遭遇してしまったのも何かの縁だろう。: Encountering him here must be some kind of fate.
奏介「――親父、タコ焼きを売ってるのか？」: Sousuke: “—Old man, are you selling takoyaki?”
親父は無言でタコ焼きをひっくり返している。: The old man flipped the takoyaki in silence.
生地が飛び散るのを見て指摘する。: I pointed it out as I watched the batter splatter.
ぎろり、と睨まれた。: He gave me a sharp glare.
この親父――人を何人も殺してきたような目をしやがる。: This old man—he has eyes like he’s killed a lot of people.
なるほど――これでは人も寄りつくまい。: I see—it’s no wonder people won't come near him.
奏介「タコ焼きを……ひと包みくれ」: Sousuke: “Give me... one order of takoyaki.”
親父「それは……同情か？」: Old Man: “Is that... out of pity?”
親父「たしかにタコ焼きなんざ、東京の奴は見たこともねえだろうよ。寄りつかねえのも: Old Man: “It’s true that people in Tokyo have probably never seen takoyaki before. I understand why they won't come near.”
屋台に寄りつかないのはタコ焼きの所為ではないだろう。: The reason people won't come near your stall isn't the takoyaki.
親父「だがな、莫迦な連中に踊らされて商品に群がってくるような奴は、こっちから願い: Old Man: “But I have no use for people who just flock to a product because they’re being led by idiots.”
親父「俺は、こいつの本質が理解できる奴にしか売らねえのさ」: Old Man: “I only sell to those who understand the true nature of this stuff.”
口端を歪め、嗤う親父。: The old man smirked, his lip curling.
奏介「親父は……タコ焼きを東京に広めようとしてるのか？」: Sousuke: “Are you... trying to spread takoyaki to Tokyo, old man?”
親父「……そんな大それた事考えちゃいねえよ。俺はただ、職人としてこいつを作るだけ: Old Man: “...I’m not thinking of anything so grandiose. I’m just making this as a craftsman.”
奏介「親父……生地がちゃんと混ざってねえんじゃないか？」: Sousuke: “Old man... the batter isn't mixed properly, is it?”
所々に小麦粉が固まりになって浮いている。: Lumps of flour were floating on the surface here and there.
くわっ、と親父の目に殺気が宿る。: A sudden flash of bloodlust appeared in the old man’s eyes.
奏介「……いや、俺は門外漢だから詳しくは知らねえよ」: Sousuke: “...No, I’m just an outsider, so I don't know much about it.”
親父が作業に戻る。: The old man went back to work.
――手に持った千枚通しが飛んでくるかと思った。: —For a moment, I thought the pick in his hand was going to come flying at me.
奏介「……親父、俺はひと包みだけ頼んだんだが――」: Sousuke: “...Old man, I only asked for one order—”
にやり、と口の端だけで嗤う親父。: The old man smirked, only the corner of his mouth curling.
親父「代金はひとつ分でいいさ……毎度」: Old Man: “One order’s worth is fine for the price... thanks for your business.”
まあ――言われずとも十銭しか払うつもりはなかったのだが。: Well—I wasn't planning on paying more than ten sen anyway.
奏介「――さて、どうするか……」: Sousuke: “—Now then, what should I do...”
親父の屋台から離れ、ひとりごちる。: I murmured to myself as I moved away from the old man’s stall.
いや――量はさほどの問題ではない。: No—the amount isn't really the problem.
奏介「――ま、歩いてりゃそのウチ冷めるだろ」: Sousuke: “—Well, if I walk, it’ll probably cool down soon enough.”
もう口の中を火傷するのはご免だ。: I’m done with burning the inside of my mouth.
アパートの玄関口に見覚えのある後ろ姿を見つけ、俺は足を止めた。: I froze at the entrance of the apartment when I spotted a familiar back.
俺を訪ねてきたのだろうか。だがそうなら中に入ればいいものを、落ち着かない様子で中: Was she here to see me? If so, she could have just gone inside, but instead, she was peeking through the doorway with restless, fidgety energy.
……玄関から覗いたところで何も見えないだろうに。: ...Peeking through the entrance wasn't going to show her a damn thing.
奏介「……なにしてんだ、美華夏」: Sousuke: “...What the hell are you doing, Mikage?”
背後から声をかけ、肩を叩く。: I called out from behind her, giving her shoulder a light tap.
こちらが驚くほどの悲鳴をあげ、美華夏が振り返る。: Mikage let out a shriek so loud it actually startled me, then spun around.
美華夏「ちょ、ちょっとぉ！ いきなり声かけないでよぉ……」: Mikana: “H-Hey! Don’t just sneak up on me like that...!”
美華夏「だ、だってっ……部屋にいると思ってたから……」: Mikana: “B-Because... I thought you were in your room...”
そう言うと、美華夏は急に声を顰めて続ける。: Having said that, Mikage suddenly lowered her voice and continued.
美華夏「出歩いても大丈夫なの……？」: Mikana: “Is it... is it really okay for you to be walking around outside...?”
美華夏「自分の事なんだから、そう他人事みたいに考えるのやめなさいよ」: Mikana: “This is your life we’re talking about, so quit acting like it's someone else's problem.”
そして美華夏は俺の腕を掴み、アパートの中に引っ張って行った。: Mikage then grabbed my arm and dragged me into the apartment.
部屋に入るなり、美華夏は室内を怪訝そうに見回す。: The moment we stepped into the room, she began scanning the interior with a suspicious, searching gaze.
幾分か声の調子をやわらげ、美華夏は呟いた。: Mikage muttered, her voice softening just a fraction.
先程路上で買った物があった。: I had something I’d bought on the street earlier.
テーブルの上に置かれた包みを見て、美華夏が怪訝そうに首を傾げる。: Mikage tilted her head curiously, eyeing the package I’d set on the table.
美華夏「タコ……には見えないけど」: Mikana: “They don't... exactly look like octopus.”
奏介「中に入ってるらしい」: Sousuke: “The octopus is supposed to be inside.”
美華夏は箸でひとつ摘むと口に放り込んだ。: Mikage picked one up with her chopsticks and popped it into her mouth.
感想を求めると、美華夏は貌を顰めた。: When I asked for her thoughts, Mikage’s face contorted in a grimace.
生地が混ざっていないのだろう。: The batter probably hadn't been mixed right.
美華夏「やっぱり、って……知ってて食べさせたの？」: Mikana: “Wait, ‘figures’...? You knew and still made me eat it?”
奏介「作ってるのを見て、何となくそんな気はしてた」: Sousuke: “I watched him make them. I had a feeling they’d be like that.”
美華夏「そんな物食べさせないでよ……」: Mikana: “Then don't feed me stuff like this...!”
美華夏「――ん、調べてきた事を報告にね」: Mikana: “—Right. I came to report on what I’ve looked into.”
手提げ鞄から何枚かの紙を取り出す。: She pulled several sheets of paper from her handbag.
美華夏「《連続狂人殺傷事件》、調べてくれって言ってたでしょう？」: Mikana: “You asked me to investigate the ‘Serial Madman Stabbings,’ didn't you?”
美華夏「一週間じゃあまり調べられなかったわ。――時間も少なかったし、なにより情報: Mikana: “I couldn't find much in just a week. —There wasn't much time, and besides, almost no information has been made public.”
憲兵隊が関わっている時点で察してはいたが、やはり情報は少ないか。: I’d suspected as much since the Kempeitai were involved, but so the information really was scarce.
美華夏「今のところ、関係していると思われる事件は四件。この中に奏介が関わったもの: Mikana: “So far, there are four cases believed to be related. The ones Sousuke was involved in aren't included in this list.”
美華夏「二件……？ ちょっと、それ聞いてないんだけど」: Mikana: “Both...? Wait, I haven't heard about a second one.”
手短に、俺は先日借金取り中に遭遇した事件のことを話した。もちろん仕事の内容は伏せ: Briefly, I explained the incident I’d stumbled upon while out collecting debts. I kept the specifics of my work hidden, of course.

久遠「締め付けすぎた箍を外す事は案外簡単なのですよ」: Kuon: “It is unexpectedly easy to remove hoops that have been tightened too far, you see.”
綾音「ちょっと切れ目を入れるだけで、勝手にはじけ飛んでしまう……」: Ayane: “With just a small cut, they’ll fly off on their own...”
それこそ、木樽の箍が外れてバラバラになるように。: Just as the hoops of a wooden barrel would break and let it fall apart.
綾音「……箍が張った状態にある人を、何らかの方法で見つけ出して、ちょっと切れ込み: Ayane: “...If you were to find someone whose hoops were taut by some method, and were to give them just a small cut...”
精神的に張りつめている人はこの東京市内にも多くいるだろう。↓そういう人に何らか: There must be many people in this city of Tokyo who are mentally stretched to their limit. If you gave such a person some kind of trigger—
綾音「《連続》して《狂人》が現れ、《殺傷事件》を起こす――」: Ayane: “A ‘Serial’ of ‘Madmen’ would appear and cause ‘Stabbings’—”
久遠は綾音の目を見てそう言った。: Qwon said that as she looked into Ayane’s eyes.
時が止まっているかのように澄んだ、揺らめきのない綺麗な瞳だと綾音は感じた。: Ayane felt her eyes were clear and beautiful, without a single flicker, as if time had stopped.
方々を捜し回り、俺は神楽坂に戻ってきた。: After scouring the area, I finally made my way back to Kagurazaka.
まさか交番に駆け込んで綾音の行方を訊ねるわけにも行かず、闇雲に走り回り、疲れ果て: It wasn't like I could just waltz into a koban and ask about Ayane's whereabouts; I’d been running around blindly until I was utterly exhausted.
綾音が行きそうな所――大塚や四谷の方まで脚を伸ばしてみたものの、手がかりは全くと: I’d pushed as far as Otsuka and Yotsuya—places I thought Ayane might go—but I hadn't found so much as a single lead.
単に嫌な予感がしただけなのだから、こうもむきにならなくてもいいはずなのだが、どう: It was just a bad feeling, so I shouldn't have been this worked up, but I couldn't get her out of my head.
誰にも見つからないように《清鳳会》の事務所の近くまでも行ったが、人がいる様子はな: I even lurked near the Seifoukai office, staying out of sight, but there was no sign of anyone there.
もしかしたら文化アパートに戻っているかもしれない。: Maybe she’d already gone back to the Bunka Apartments.
だが帰る前に、念のためバーに寄ることにした。: But before heading home, I decided to swing by the bar, just in case.
近くまで来ると、バーから誰かが出てきた。一瞬身を隠そうかとも思ったが、出てきた人: As I approached, someone stepped out of the bar. For a split second, I thought about diving into the shadows, but I stopped once I saw who it was.
俺が声を掛ける前に、久遠は俺を見つけて呼びかけてきた。: Before I could even open my mouth, Qwon spotted me and called out.
久遠に訊ねても綾音の行き先はわからないだろうが、念のため訊ねてみることにした。: Asking Qwon probably wouldn't get me anywhere regarding Ayane’s location, but I figured it was worth a shot.
久遠「奏介が捜している人は、ここにいます」: Kuon: “The person Sousuke is looking for is in there.”
訊ねる前に久遠はバーの扉を指し示した。: Before I could even ask, Qwon pointed toward the bar’s door.
久遠「――そう言えば、お名前を伺っていませんでした」: Kuon: “—Come to think of it, I didn't ask for her name.”
奏介「どうして俺の尋ね人だと判った？」: Sousuke: “How did you know she was the one I was looking for?”
久遠「彼女が奏介の事を話しているように思えたからです」: Kuon: “Because she seemed to be talking about Sousuke.”
余計な事を話していなければいいが。: I hoped to hell she hadn't said anything she shouldn't have.
そもそも全て、俺が招いた事だ。: In the end, I was the one who’d invited all of this.
ほんの少しだけ久遠が微笑んだような気がした。: I thought I saw the faintest trace of a smile on Qwon’s face.
久遠「女同士の会話に耳を挟むのは野暮ですよ」: Kuon: “It’s uncouth to pry into a conversation between women.”
これだから女は困る。: This is why women are such a pain.
久遠「――早く行った方がいいですよ」: Kuon: “—You should probably go in soon.”
久遠に促され、俺は中に入ろうと扉に近づいた。: Prodded by Qwon, I stepped toward the door to head inside.
久遠「――大切な人ならしっかりと守ってあげなさい」: Kuon: “—If they are precious to you, make sure you protect them well.”
振り向いたが、久遠は既に背を向けて立ち去ったあとだった。: I turned back, but Qwon had already turned her back and walked away.
久遠を追うことも考えたが、今はこの中にいるのが綾音であることを確かめるのが先だ。: I considered going after her, but confirming that Ayane was inside came first.
入るなり、カウンタ席にいた綾音と目が合った。: The moment I entered, my eyes met Ayane’s, who was sitting at the counter.
綾音がそっぽを向く。よっぽど探し回ったことを言ってやろうかと思ったが、止めておく: Ayane looked away. I thought about telling her just how much I’d looked for her, but I decided against it. It wouldn't do any good to let her stay in a huff.
綾音の近くに座る。わざとらしく綾音は反対側を向いた。: I sat down near her. Ayane pointedly turned her head the other way.
そっぽを向いたままだったので綾音には良く聞こえなかったようだ。: Since she was still looking away, it seemed she hadn't heard me clearly.
奏介「俺が謝っちゃいけないのか？」: Sousuke: “Is it a crime for me to apologize?”
綾音「……奏介に謝られる理由はないもの」: Ayane: “...There’s no reason for you to apologize to me.”
奏介「……言い争いを止めるのにきつく言い過ぎた」: Sousuke: “...I was too harsh when I told you to stop arguing.”
綾音「……わたしが悪いんだもの、当たり前じゃない」: Ayane: “...It was my fault anyway, so it’s only natural.”
このままでは売り言葉に買い言葉で余計に綾音の機嫌を損ねてしまいそうだ。: At this rate, we were just going to keep trading barbs until her mood got even worse.
どうするべきかとしばし考える。: I thought for a moment about how to handle this.
綾音「……わたしが悪いのは解ってるよ」: Ayane: “...I know it was my fault.”
ぽつりと綾音が洩らした。そこに強がっている雰囲気は感じられない。: Ayane murmured softly. I couldn't hear any of her usual bravado in her voice.
綾音はなにやら悩んでいるようだ。: Ayane seemed to be grappling with something.
ひとり頷き、俺の方に向き直る。: She nodded to herself and turned toward me.
急に頭を下げられ、俺は面食らって思わず仰け反ってしまった。: She suddenly bowed her head, catching me so off guard that I nearly recoiled.
綾音「わっ、わたしもつい頭に血が上っちゃって……むきになっちゃってたから……その: Ayane: “I-I just got a little heated... and I was being so stubborn... so, um...”
俺は呆れ、下げられたままの綾音の頭を軽く指で小突く。: I sighed and gave her bowed head a light flick with my finger.
奏介「……お前はいちいち気合いを入れないと謝れないのか？」: Sousuke: “...Can you not apologize without psyching yourself up every single time?”
綾音「……どーして人の好意を素直に受け取れないかなぁ……」: Ayane: “...Why can't you just accept someone’s kindness gracefully...?”
奏介「……お前が素直になれよ」: Sousuke: “...Why can't you just be honest?”
拗ねた女の扱いは面倒くさい。: Dealing with a sulky woman is exhausting.
しばらく放っておけば少しは機嫌も直るだろうか。: Maybe if I just left her alone for a bit, she’d eventually cheer up.
綾音「――言いたいことがあるならちゃんと言ってよ」: Ayane: “—If you’ve got something to say, just say it.”
唐突に綾音が口を開いた。: Ayane spoke up out of the blue.
奏介「……もう一度お前の説を聞かせて欲しい」: Sousuke: “...I want to hear your theory again.”
綾音「……わたしの意見なんていらないんじゃないの？」: Ayane: “...I thought you didn't need my opinion?”
綾音「黙ってろって言った」: Ayane: “You told me to keep my mouth shut.”
それを言ったのは美華夏だが、今美華夏の名前を出すとさらに話がこじれるような気がし: It was Mikage who’d said that, but I figured bringing her name up now would only make things messier, so I kept it to myself.
じっと綾音に見つめられ、俺は居心地悪くそっぽを向いた。: Ayane stared at me intently, and I looked away, feeling self-conscious.
綾音「……本当に聞きたいの？」: Ayane: “...Do you really want to hear it?”
本当の所を言うと、俺には全く理解が出来ない話なのだが。: To be honest, it was a conversation I barely understood.
綾音「仕方ないなぁ、だったら話してあげるかぁ」: Ayane: “I guess I have no choice. I suppose I'll tell you, then.”
奏介「お前が偉そうだから」: Sousuke: “Because you’re acting so high and mighty.”
綾音「いいじゃない、説明してあげるんだから」: Ayane: “What’s wrong with that? I’m the one explaining it to you.”
カウンタに並んだ壜からウォッカとオレンジジュースを抜き取り、グラスに注いで適当に: I grabbed vodka and orange juice from the bottles lined up on the counter, poured them into a glass, and gave them a quick mix.
氷はないが構いやしない。: There was no ice, but I didn't give a damn.
綾音「……勝手に飲んでいいの？ そもそももうお店しまっちゃってるよね？」: Ayane: “...Is it okay to just drink like that? I mean, the shop’s already closed, isn't it?”
奏介「ツケで呑ませて貰ってる。だいたい俺が開店時にここで呑んでる訳にもいかねえだ: Sousuke: “I drink here on credit. Besides, I can't exactly be drinking here during business hours, can I?”
綾音「ああ……そっか。誰に見られてるかわからないもんね」: Ayane: “Ah... right. You never know who might be watching.”
呟き、グラスに口を付ける。少々ウォッカが多かったようだ。アルコールが鼻に抜ける。: I muttered as I took a sip. Seemed I’d gone a bit heavy on the vodka. The alcohol stung my nose.
綾音「……わたしを酔わせてどうするつもり？」: Ayane: “...What are you planning to do, getting me drunk?”
奏介「どうもしねえよ。むしろ酔えば少しは大人しくなるだろ」: Sousuke: “Nothing. If anything, maybe you’d finally pipe down if you were tipsy.”
俺の手からグラスが消えた。: The glass vanished from my hand.
まだほとんど飲んでいないグラスを奪われ、俺は横目で綾音を睨み付けた。: I glared at Ayane out of the corner of my eye as she snatched the glass before I’d even had more than a sip.
綾音「味見くらいいいでしょ？」: Ayane: “It’s fine to have a taste, right?”
俺の返事を待たずに綾音はグラスを傾けた。: Without waiting for an answer, Ayane tilted the glass.
綾音「……あ、おいしい。これなら結構飲めそう」: Ayane: “...Oh, it’s good. I could probably drink quite a bit of this.”
さらに飲もうとする綾音からグラスを取り上げる。: I took the glass back as she tried to take another sip.
奏介「オレンジジュースで呑みやすくなってるが、結構アルコール度数は高いんだぞ」: Sousuke: “The orange juice makes it easy to drink, but the alcohol content is pretty high.”
綾音「ふーん……ねぇ、なんて名前のカクテルなの？」: Ayane: “Hmm... hey, what’s the name of this cocktail?”
綾音「ねじ回し？ 全然見た目と違うんだね」: Ayane: “A screwdriver? It looks nothing like its name.”
もうひとつのぴったりな別名――《女殺し》を言う気にもなれず、俺はグラスに残った酒: I didn't feel like telling her its other, more fitting name—the ‘Lady Killer’—as I drained the rest of the drink in one go.
綾音「……ねえ奏介。話聞いて貰ってもいいかな？」: Ayane: “...Hey, Sousuke. Do you mind listening to me for a bit?”
ジュースをグラスに注ぎ入れていた手を止め、貌を上げる。: I stopped pouring juice into the glass and looked up.
綾音「さっき話した仮説だけどね、補足というか……少しだけ可能性が見えてきたの」: Ayane: “About that hypothesis I mentioned earlier... I have a bit of a supplement... I think I can see a bit of possibility.”
綾音「うん。……あっ、聞きたくないなら別にいいよ。わたしもまだうまく飲み込めてな: Ayane: “Yeah. ...Ah, if you don't want to hear it, it’s fine. I might not have fully wrapped my head around it yet either.”
奏介「なんだ……？ やけに殊勝だな」: Sousuke: “What’s this...? You’re being unusually humble.”
綾音「……聞くつもりないなら話さないよ」: Ayane: “...I won't talk if you’re not going to listen.”
奏介「だから拗ねるなって」: Sousuke: “I told you, stop being so sulky.”
奏介「どこからどう見ても拗ねてるだろ。……いいから話せよ」: Sousuke: “No matter how you look at it, you’re sulking. ...Just talk.”
綾音を促すと、彼女はしばらく逡巡していたがやがてぽつぽつと話し出した。: When I prodded her, Ayane hesitated for a moment before finally starting to speak.
綾音「ねえ奏介、襲ってきた人が暗示にかかりやすそうだとか感じなかった？」: Ayane: “Hey, Sousuke, didn't you feel like the people who attacked you were easily susceptible to suggestions?”
奏介「はぁ？ ……そんなの知るかよ。具体的にどんな状態を指してるんだ？」: Sousuke: “Huh? ...How the hell should I know? What exactly does that look like?”
綾音「うーん……精神的に張りつめていたとか、いっぱいいっぱいというか……悩みを抱: Ayane: “Hmm... like being mentally high-strung, or being at their limit... or maybe carrying some kind of deep trouble.”
奏介「見てただけではよくわからんがな……」: Sousuke: “I couldn't tell just by looking at them...”
あの時を思い起こす。俺に襲いかかってきた女は――腹痛で蹲っていたくらいか。ほんの: I thought back to that time. The woman who’d attacked me—all I knew was that she’d been crouching in pain. We’d barely spoken, so there was no way I could know what was going on inside her.
強いて言えば、あの雨の中裏路地でひとり佇んでいたことか。何か切羽詰まった理由がな: If I had to say something, it was the fact that she’d been standing alone in that back alley in the rain. No one would be in a place like that unless they had a desperate reason.
ハチを襲った、借金を抱えていた男はわかりやすい。借金返済を迫られ、かなり追い込ま: The man who’d attacked Hachi and was saddled with debt was easier to understand. He’d been pushed to the brink by the pressure to pay it back.
奏介「……そういうところはあったかもしれないな」: Sousuke: “...Maybe there was something to that.”
綾音「そっか……。そういう人が暗示にかかりやすいんだって」: Ayane: “I see... They say people like that are susceptible to suggestions.”
奏介「どうやって暗示をかけるつもりだ？」: Sousuke: “How do you plan on planting a suggestion?”
綾音「うーん……それはまだわかんないけど……催眠術じゃ時間がかかりすぎるらしいし: Ayane: “Hmm... I don't know that yet... but apparently hypnotism takes too long...”
奏介「……俺が遭遇した時は、ほとんど一瞬で豹変したぞ」: Sousuke: “...When I encountered them, they changed in an instant.”
奏介「ああ。最初は酔っていたからだと思っていたが、二回目――俺が襲われた訳じゃな: Sousuke: “Yeah. At first I thought it was just because I was drunk, but the second time—though I wasn't the one being attacked—I felt that same dizziness.”
綾音「偶然……じゃないよね。他には？」: Ayane: “That can't be... a coincidence. Anything else?”
奏介「眩暈がしたのとほぼ同時だ。周りの一切の音が消えた。耳がおかしくなったという: Sousuke: “It was almost at the same time as the dizziness. Every sound around me vanished. It wasn't like my hearing had gone bad... it really felt as if the world itself had lost all sound.”
綾音は確認するように呟いた。: Ayane murmured, as if confirming it.
奏介「……いや、わからねえ。二度目はともかく、最初のは他に誰もいなかったからな」: Sousuke: “...No, I don't. Maybe not the second time, but there was no one else around the first time.”
綾音「あの路地……確かに狭かったけど……」: Ayane: “That alley... it was narrow, but...”
おとがいに指を当てて考え込む綾音。: Ayane rested her finger on her chin, lost in thought.
綾音「一瞬で暗示をかける方法……何かを使った？ ……薬とか」: Ayane: “A method to plant a suggestion in an instant... was something used? ...Like a drug?”
綾音「ううん、阿片よりも、ホームズも使っていたコカインみたいな覚醒作用のあるもの: Ayane: “No, not opium, something with a stimulant effect, like the cocaine Holmes used...”
奏介「まず、連中にそういう麻薬を使う暇はなかった。俺の元に現れる前に使っていたの: Sousuke: “First off, they didn't have time to use any kind of drug. Maybe they’d used it before appearing before me... but there was absolutely no sign of that.”
奏介「あとひとつ。少なくとも俺は――そんな類の薬をやっていない」: Sousuke: “One more thing. At the very least, I—I’m not on any kind of drug like that.”
薬なり他の手段なりで犯人の精神の箍を外したのかもしれないが、その中には俺も含まれ: They might have used drugs or some other means to break the hoops of the culprit's spirit, but I was included in that too—almost certainly.
二回目はともかく、最初の芸妓と遭った時は、俺もその精神の箍が外れた状態だったはず: Leaving aside the second time, when I met that first geigi, the hoops of my spirit must have been broken as well.
だから記憶も曖昧になっている。: That's why my memory was so hazy.
あの件について何かが使われた――第三者の関わりがあったとすれば、俺の罪も軽くはな: If something had been used in that incident—if there was third-party involvement—it might lighten my crime, but—
――少なくとも、麻薬のようなものではない。: —At the very least, it wasn't something like narcotics.
もっと他の、使用者にも気付かれないような何か。: It was something else, something that even the user wouldn't notice.
奏介「……精神の箍を外す何かが――俺にも使われたはずだ」: Sousuke: “...Something that breaks the hoops of the spirit—it must have been used on me too.”
綾音「眩暈と音の消失……それが鍵になりそうね」: Ayane: “Dizziness and the loss of sound... that might be the key.”
奏介「ただ……何かが使われたとしたところで、それが俺の無実を証明することにはなら: Sousuke: “Still... even if something was used, it doesn't prove my innocence.”
奏介「最初に襲われたとき、俺は相手を殺したのかもしれない……精神の箍が外れてな」: Sousuke: “When I was first attacked, I might have killed her... because the hoops of my spirit were broken.”
綾音「でもそれは、何かが使われたって事が証明できれば無罪になる可能性だって……」: Ayane: “But if you can prove that something was used, there’s a possibility you could be found not guilty...”
奏介「……無罪になろうが、無実にはならない」: Sousuke: “...Being found not guilty isn't the same as being innocent.”
罪に問われなくても、俺が殺したという事にかわりはない。: Even if I wasn't charged, the fact that I killed her wouldn't change.
俺がいくらやっていないと言い張ったところで、周りは意に介さないだろう。客観的な証: No matter how much I insisted I didn't do it, the people around me wouldn't care. Unless there was objective evidence—something that even I could accept.
どうせ根拠はないのだろうが、綾音は何度も俺にそう言ってきた。: She probably didn't have any proof, but Ayane had told me that time and again.
綾音「たとえ暗示を掛けられていようと、奏介が人を殺すようには見えないから……わた: Ayane: “Even if you were under a suggestion, you just don't seem like someone who would kill... my eyes don't lie...”
綾音はじっと俺の目を見つめてくる。: Ayane stared straight into my eyes.
俺を信頼しきっているような澄んだ瞳に見つめられ、決まり悪く視線を逸らした。: I looked away, feeling awkward as those clear eyes, filled with absolute trust, gazed at me.
俺はそんなに信じられるような人間じゃない。: I wasn't the kind of person someone should believe in that much.
綾音「……茶化さないでよ。わたしは本気なんだからね」: Ayane: “...Don't tease me. I’m being serious.”
綾音がそう言えば言うほど、妙に笑いがこみ上げてくる。: The more Ayane said those things, the more a strange urge to laugh welled up inside me.
頬を膨らませる綾音に構わず、俺はしばらく押し殺した笑い声をあげていた。: Ignoring Ayane as she puffed out her cheeks, I let out a low, suppressed chuckle for a while.
奏介「……一緒に綾音を探しに出て……そのままだ」: Sousuke: “...She went out to look for you with me... and that was the last of it.”
綾音「ちょっとぉ！ どうしてそのままにしちゃってるのよ！？このご時世に女の子ひと: Ayane: “Wait! Why did you just leave her like that?! Leaving a girl all alone in this day and age!”
奏介「……手分けして捜した方が手っ取り早いだろ」: Sousuke: “...I thought it’d be faster if we split up to search.”
綾音「……奏介、いい？ 今あちこちで殺人事件が起こってるんだよ？ それも手当たり: Ayane: “...Sousuke, listen. There are murders happening all over the place right now, okay? And at random—it's not just the victims, even the perpetrators are chosen haphazardly.”
綾音「もし橘さんが選ばれちゃったら――どうするつもり？」: Ayane: “What are you going to do if Tachibana-san is the one chosen?!”
それはまだ仮説の域を出ておらず、綾音の取り越し苦労のようにも思えるのだが――妙な: That was still just a hypothesis, and it felt like Ayane was worrying over nothing, but it had a strange sense of credibility to it.
本当に、被害者と加害者が無作為に選択されて殺し合いをしているような――: It really felt as if victims and perpetrators were being chosen at random to kill each other—

扉が叩かれている。: Just as the thought crossed my mind, a knock sounded at the door.
考えた側から来客か。: A visitor the second I think about it.
面倒くさい。もう一度ベッドに寝転がる。: What a pain. I slumped back onto the bed.
暫く放っておけばいなくなるだろう。: If I leave them alone long enough, they’ll probably clear off.

奏介「――ま、適当にぶらつくか」: Sousuke: “—Well, let's just wander around aimlessly.”
