綾音は汗を流したいと言って浴室へ行ってしまった。: Ayane said she wanted to rinse off and went to the bathroom.
俺はひとり部屋に残り、窓際の壁に寄りかかる。: I remained alone in the room, leaning against the wall by the window.
耳を澄ませば、隣室のシャワーの音が微かに聞こえてくる。: If I listened closely, I could faintly hear the sound of the shower in the next room.
脱ぎ捨てた上着から煙草を取り出し、口にくわえる。: I pulled a cigarette from the jacket I’d cast aside and put it between my lips.
火も付けることを忘れ、しばし快楽の余韻に浸っていた。: I forgot to even light it, and for a while, I soaked in the afterglow of pleasure.
心地よい気怠さ。どんな状況に置かれていようと、今はそれだけでいい。: A pleasant lethargy. No matter what situation I was in, for now, this was enough.
こんな感覚、久しぶりだ。: It had been a long time since I’d felt like this.
愛――などといった陳腐な言葉にはしたくない。: Love—I didn't want to use such a clichéd word.
そんなもの――とうに失ってしまった。: Something like that—I’d lost it long ago.
忘れていた物を取り戻させてくれる存在、だろうか。: A presence that allowed me to reclaim the things I’d forgotten, perhaps.
中は空で、火を付ける事もできない。: It was empty, and I couldn't light it.
部屋の中を眺め回すが、他に火を付ける道具は持っていなかったはずだ。: I looked around the room, but I didn't think I had any other way to light it.
銜えた煙草をテーブルに置き、棚に置かれていたウォッカの壜とグラスをふたつ持ってく: I set the cigarette on the table and brought over the bottle of vodka and two glasses from the shelf.
氷がないのは仕方あるまい。ひとつのグラスにウォッカを注ぐと軽く口を付ける。: It couldn't be helped that there was no ice. I poured vodka into one glass and took a light sip.
癖のない甘みが舌先に広がる。: A clean sweetness spread across the tip of my tongue.
彼女にはこのままだと強すぎるだろう。何か割る物があればいいのだが、あいにく何もな: It would be too strong for her as it was. It’d be nice if there were something to mix it with, but unfortunately, there was nothing.
奏介「――ま、酔わせてどうにかする、って事もないけどな」: Sousuke: “—Well, it’s not as if I’m trying to get her drunk to do anything, though.”
振り返ると、浴室から出てきた綾音と目が合った。: When I turned around, my eyes met Ayane’s as she emerged from the bathroom.
綾音「あ……あのさ、服これしかなかったんだけど……」: Ayane: “Ah... um, this was the only clothing I could find...”
俺のシャツを羽織り、所在なくこちらを窺う。: She was wearing my shirt, and she eyed me restlessly.
奏介「そりゃ下に何も付けてないからだろ」: Sousuke: “That’s because you’re not wearing anything underneath.”
そういいつつも、綾音はシャツの裾を下に抑え付ける。その所為で胸元が見えそうになる: While saying so, Ayane pressed the hem of the shirt down. In doing so, it looked like her chest was about to be revealed, but—
何も見えないので酒を勧める。: Since I couldn't see anything, I offered her a drink.
勢いよく頷き、空のグラスを手に取る。: She nodded vigorously and picked up the empty glass.
綾音の言葉を無視し、グラスの下三分の一程度だけ注いでやる。: Ignoring Ayane’s words, I poured her about a third of a glass.
綾音「むーっ、莫迦にされてる気分……」: Ayane: “Hmph, I feel like I’m being underestimated...”
ひとしきりぶつぶつと呟いたあと、グラスに口を付け一気にあおった。: After muttering to herself for a while, she put the glass to her lips and drained it in one go.
しゃっくりにも似た声を発する。: She let out a sound like a hiccup.
ふた口目に行こうとする綾音を制する。: I restrained Ayane as she tried to go for a second sip.
奏介「そういう奴が一番危険なんだよ」: Sousuke: “People like you are the most dangerous.”
ベッドの上で綾音と並んで壁に背中を預ける。: On the bed, I leaned my back against the wall beside Ayane.
暫くそっとしておいて風呂へ行こうとしたら、俺の腕をしっかりと綾音が掴んでいて離そ: When I tried to leave her alone for a bit to go to the bath, Ayane grabbed my arm firmly and wouldn't let go.
奏介「……お前な、少しだけだって言っただろ」: Sousuke: “...Listen, I told you to only have a little.”
綾音「……あんな強いお酒出す奏介がいけないんだ」: Ayane: “...It’s Sousuke’s fault for giving me such a strong drink.”
綾音「いーや、奏介が悪いのっ」: Ayane: “Yes it is, it’s Sousuke’s fault.”
まあ――今は従っておくのが得策だろう。: Well—for now, it was probably best to just play along.
綾音「……まだ何か挟まってる感じがする……」: Ayane: “...It still feels like something’s wedged in there...”
奏介「そのうち慣れるだろ」: Sousuke: “You’ll get used to it eventually.”
綾音「慣れるまで、こういう事してくれるの？」: Ayane: “Until I get used to it, will you keep doing this for me?”
綾音「うー……、わたしが淫乱みたいな言い方しないでよぉ……絶対奏介が助平だよ。わ: Ayane: “Mmm... don't make it sound like I’m some kind of nymphomaniac... Sousuke, you’re definitely the lewd one. Even though it was my first time, you went and finished inside me...”
綾音「だったらどうするの？ 責任取ってくれる？」: Ayane: “What would you do if it was? Would you take responsibility?”
言葉の途中で思い切り頬をつねられた。: In the middle of my sentence, she gave my cheek a powerful pinch.
綾音「こんな可愛い娘を誑かしといて、逃げちゃうような甲斐性なしなんだ、奏介ってば: Ayane: “Sousuke, you’re such a man without any sense of duty, seducing such a cute girl and then running away.”
綾音「――冗談だよ。ちゃんと今日は安全な日だからっ」: Ayane: “—I’m joking. Today is definitely a safe day.”
綾音「――でもいつか、奏介との子供欲しいなぁ……」: Ayane: “—But someday, I’d like to have Sousuke’s child...”
綾音は天井を見つめ、呟いた。: Ayane stared at the ceiling and muttered.
そんな未来が来るのだろうか。: I wondered if such a future would ever come.
俺は犯罪者として追われている。: I was being hunted as a criminal.
安息の日が果たして――来るのだろうか。: I wondered if a day of peace would ever—actually come.
奏介「なあ、綾音。もし俺が殺人犯だったとしたら――」: Sousuke: “Hey, Ayane. If I really were a murderer—”
俺の言葉は再び綾音によって封じられた。: My words were once again silenced by Ayane.
今度は、細い人差し指を口元にあてられて。: This time, she pressed a slender index finger to my lips.
綾音「――ダメ。そんな事言っちゃ。奏介は絶対にそんな人じゃない。自分でそう思いこ: Ayane: “—No. You mustn't say that. Sousuke, you’re definitely not that kind of person. If you convince yourself of that, someday it’ll really become the truth.”
綾音「だから、絶対に信じて。わたしの事と――何より、奏介自身を。ねっ？」: Ayane: “So, you have to believe. In me—and more than anything, in yourself. Okay?”
他に信じる物もない俺だ。: I had nothing else to believe in.
そのふたりくらいは信じたい。: I wanted to believe in those two things, at least.
か細い声に様子を窺うと、綾音は瞼を閉じ、うつらうつらと船を漕いでいた。: At the sound of her thin voice, I looked at her and found Ayane’s eyes closed, her head nodding as she drifted off.
やはり先程のウォッカが回ってしまったのだろう。: It seemed that earlier vodka had finally hit her.
そっと綾音の髪を直してやると、軽くその頬に口づける。: I softly fixed Ayane’s hair and gave her cheek a light kiss.
そう囁き、綾音の身体を横たえると、俺も息を吐いて瞼を閉じた。: Whispering that, I laid Ayane’s body down, let out a breath, and closed my eyes.
――微睡みの中、俺は夢を視る。: —Amidst a light slumber, I have a dream.
ぶくぶくと沈んでいく。: Sinking down with a series of bubbles.
ふたつに割れ、四つに割れ、八つに割れ――: Splitting in two, splitting in four, splitting in eight—
爪の先よりも小さくなり、水に溶けていく。: Becoming smaller than a fingernail and dissolving into the water.
いつの間にか浮かんでいた。: Before I knew it, I was floating.
頭から浴びる。: It showers down over my head.
水面を転がる。: Rolling along the surface of the water.
くるくる、くるくる。: Round and round, round and round.
手には巻き貝。: A spiral shell in my hand.
くるくると回り、砂に埋まる。: It spins round and round and is buried in the sand.
巻き貝の中から水が溢れる。: Water overflows from inside the spiral shell.
呼吸を落ち着けようとする。: I tried to settle my breathing.
酷い汗だ。: I was drenched in a cold sweat.
心臓が早鐘のように激しく動悸を繰り返している。: My heart was racing violently, like a sounding bell.
――吐きそうだ。: —I felt like I was going to throw up.
立ち上がろうと被っていた布団をはね除け、ベッドに手を付ける。: I threw off the covers I’d been under and reached out to the bed to stand up.
まさか寝小便という歳でもない。: It wasn't as if I were at an age where I’d wet the bed.
まさか綾音がという訳でもないだろう。隣で眠って――: It didn't seem likely that Ayane would either. She was sleeping beside me—
一瞬、自分の目を疑った。: For a moment, I doubted my own eyes.
べっとりと手のひらを染めたのは、紛うことなく――血。: The thing that had thickly stained my palm was, without a doubt—blood.
突き立つのは、左胸――心臓の真上。: It was plunged into her left breast—right over her heart.
すべてが夢の中のような、現実離れした光景。: Everything felt like it was out of a dream, a surreal scene.
生気のない瞳が俺を捉えていた。: Lifeless eyes were staring at me.
ぞわり、と何かが背筋を上ってくる。: Something crawled up my spine, a cold shiver.
震える指で彼女の肢体に触れる。: With trembling fingers, I touched her body.
昨夜の熱を帯びた身体とはまったく違う。: It was entirely different from her heat-filled body last night.
指先に血が付いていた。: There was blood on my fingertips.
そこで、俺はようやく、自分が何をしてしまったのかをはっきりと認識してしまった。: There, I finally, clearly recognized what I’d gone and done.
嗚咽はすれども、涙が出ない。: Though I sobbed, no tears would come.
――声にならない絶叫を、喉の奥から迸らせていた。: —A voiceless scream erupted from the back of my throat.
俺は、極めて普通に返事をした。: I answered with total, mundane normalcy.
照れているのか、綾音ははにかんだ笑みを浮かべる。: Ayane gave a shy, bashful smile, perhaps feeling a bit embarrassed.
綾音の格好を見て首を傾げる。: I tilted my head, looking at what Ayane was wearing.
綾音「朝ご飯つくってたの。材料がほとんどなかったから簡単なのしか作れなかったけど: Ayane: “I was making breakfast. There weren't many ingredients, so it’s pretty simple, but you’ll eat, won't you?”
綾音「じゃ、用意してるから、奏介はちゃんと服着てよね」: Ayane: “Okay, I’ll go get it ready. Make sure you put some clothes on, Sousuke.”
言われて、俺が下着姿のままだった事に気付く。: I realized then that I was still in nothing but my underwear.
奏介「……俺は別にこのままでも構わねえんだけどな」: Sousuke: “...I don't really mind staying like this.”
屈託なく、綾音は笑いながら台所へと戻っていった。: Without a care in the world, Ayane laughed and headed back to the kitchen.
何か――厭な夢を見たような気がするが、そんな事をすっかり忘れてしまえるような目覚: I felt like I’d had a terrible dream, but the way I’d woken up made me forget it entirely.
――どれだけの時間、ここでこうしていただろうか。: —How much time had I spent here like this?
目の前に横たわる綾音はぴくりとも動かず、目覚めた時のままだ。: Ayane lay before me, not moving an inch, exactly as she had when I first woke up.
すぐに起きあがって、おはようの挨拶をしてきてくれるのではないか――そんな淡い期待: I had this faint, pathetic hope that she’d suddenly sit up and say good morning—but the sight of the knife plunged into her chest crushed it.
綾音を血に染め、亡き者にしたナイフ――間違いなく俺が持っていたものだ。: The knife that had stained Ayane in red and snuffed her out—it was, without a doubt, the one I’d been holding.
恐る恐る手を延ばし――ナイフの柄に触れる。ごぽっ、と疵口が唸り、濁った血液が僅か: I reached out with trembling fingers—and touched the handle of the knife. The wound gave a wet gurgle, and a bit of dark, sluggish blood overflowed.
綾音のことを考えたら引き抜くべきだった。: If I’d really been thinking of Ayane, I should have pulled it out.
一瞬触れたときの肉の動き――筋肉の緊張がない、生肉に包丁を入れたような感覚。: The way the flesh moved when I touched it—the lack of muscular tension, a sensation like a blade slicing into raw meat.
ナイフから手を離す。: I let go of the knife.
何故、こんな終わり方をする？: Why does it have to end like this?
殺人鬼である俺と共にいたから――殺して――: Because she was with a killer like me—I killed her—
口を抑えて便所へ駆け込むと、便器に向けて胃の中身を全部ぶちまける。: I covered my mouth and scrambled to the toilet, emptying the entire contents of my stomach into the bowl.
酸っぱい胃液と血の味とがない交ぜになって脳を侵す。: The sour taste of gastric juices mixed with the flavor of blood, invading my brain.
鏡を見ると血まみれだ。: I looked in the mirror; I was covered in blood.
鏡の中の俺の眼は血走り、一目見て尋常ではない――殺人者の目をしていた。: My eyes in the mirror were bloodshot and, at a glance, clearly not normal—they were the eyes of a killer.
そのまま浴室へ向かい、頭から熱湯を浴びる。: I headed straight for the bath and showered in scalding water.
排水溝に赤く染まった水が吸い込まれていった。: The water, stained red, was sucked down the drain.
もう吐き出す物など残っていないはずなのに。: Even though there shouldn't be anything left to throw up.
喉のどこかが切れたのか、唾液に赤いものが混じっていた。: Maybe I’d torn something in my throat; my saliva was mixed with red.
俺の身体から流れ落ちた綾音の血と混じり合い――: It mingled with the blood of Ayane that had washed off my body—
何も変わっていない部屋に戻る。: I returned to the room where nothing had changed.
綾音の遺体を目の当たりにしても、涙すら流れない。: Even faced with Ayane’s corpse, I couldn't shed a single tear.
血に染まっていないシャツに腕を通し、色眼鏡を掛ける。: I slipped my arms into a shirt that wasn't stained with blood and put on my tinted glasses.
この歪んだ犯罪者の眼を誰にも見られたくなかった。: I didn't want anyone to see these twisted, criminal eyes.
入口の扉の鍵を開け、外に出た。: I unlocked the front door and stepped outside.
振り返ることは――出来なかった。: I couldn't—bring myself to look back.
何も代わり映えのない――いつもの朝。: A morning like any other—completely unchanged.
ただ呆然と、路を歩く。: I just walked the path, dazed and hollow.
何処へ向かうかなんて考えていない。: I wasn't thinking about where I was going.
此処から離れられるのならば何処でもよかった。: Anywhere was fine as long as I could get away from here.
ただただ逃げたかった。: I just wanted to run and run.
電車通りをふらりと歩く。: I drifted along the streetcar tracks.
向こうからトラックが走ってくる。: A truck came driving from the other direction.
貌を伏せ、色眼鏡の位置を直してやり過ごそうとする。: I lowered my head and adjusted my tinted glasses, trying to let them pass.
トラックがすぐ横を通り過ぎ、砂煙が舞い上がる。: The truck drove right past me, kicking up a cloud of dust.
気付かれなかった事に安堵する。: I felt a sense of relief that I hadn't been noticed.
向こうだってまさか道ですれ違った男が兇悪な殺人犯だとは気付かないと思うが。: I didn't think they’d realize a man they just passed on the street was a vicious murderer, anyway.
それでも俺は貌を伏せ、他人の目から逃れるように歩く。: Even so, I kept my head down, walking as if to escape the eyes of others.
官憲に影響力を持つ《清鳳会》の連中に保護され、気持ちが緩んでいたのだが、本来俺は: I’d been under the protection of the Seifoukai, who had influence over the authorities, and my guard had slipped—but in truth, I was already a murderer.
何処をどう歩いたのかすら解らない。: I didn't even know where or how I’d walked.
気付けばいつもの店の前だった。: Before I knew it, I was in front of the usual shop.
まだ営業時間には早い。: It was still too early for business hours.
マスター「すまないがまだ開店前――」: Master: “Sorry, but we’re not open yet—”
貌を上げたマスターに片手をあげて合図を送る。: I raised a hand in a signal to the Master as he looked up.
マスター「……奏介か。こんな朝早くにどうした？」: Master: “...Sousuke. What are you doing here so early in the morning?”
喉が灼けるように痛かった。: My throat burned with a searing pain.
酒を飲む気分にはなれなかった。: I wasn't in the mood for a drink.
いっそ酔いつぶれてしまえば楽なのかもしれないが。: Though it might be easier if I just drank myself into a stupor.
目の前のカウンタに氷入りのグラスが置かれる。: A glass with ice was placed on the counter before me.
俺の尋常ではない様子を察したのだろう、マスターが静かに問いかけてきた。: Sensing my unusual state, the Master asked quietly.
それには答えずにグラスを持つ。冷えた水が通り過ぎ、渇いた喉を僅かに潤す。: I didn't answer and picked up the glass. The cold water slid down, providing a bit of moisture to my parched throat.
奏介「少し前に知り合った……女だ。今朝……俺が殺した」: Sousuke: “A woman... I met a little while ago. This morning... I killed her.”
マスターは驚いた様子で聞き返してきた。: The Master asked back, looking startled.
脳裏に綾音の遺体が浮かび上がる。: Ayane’s corpse rose to the surface of my mind.
ああ……綾音をそのままにして出てきてしまった。: Ah... I’d left Ayane just like that and walked out.
せめて上からなにか羽織らせておけばよかった。: I should have at least put something over her.
もしかしたら、来るときにすれ違った憲兵隊のトラック――あれは俺の部屋に向かう途中: Maybe that Kempeitai truck I’d passed on the way here—maybe they were headed to my room.
マスター「……落ち着け奏介。焦るとろくな事はないぞ」: Master: “...Calm down, Sousuke. Nothing good comes from panic.”
むしろ落ち着いている方だ。: If anything, I was on the calmer side.
人を殺しておいて落ち着いているのは、やはり俺が殺人鬼だからだろうか。: Was the reason I was so calm after killing someone because I really was a murderer?
綾音はよく探偵小説の話をしていた。小説に登場する犯人は大抵不貞不貞しく、大胆不敵: Ayane used to talk about detective novels a lot. The culprits in those novels were usually insolent and audacious.
俺は――とてもそうはなれそうもない。: I—didn't feel like I could ever be like that.
奏介「――マスター。俺を……警察に連れて行ってくれ」: Sousuke: “—Master. Take me... take me to the police.”
奏介「俺は……綾音を殺してしまった。罪は償うべきだ」: Sousuke: “I... I killed Ayane. I should pay for my crime.”
マスター「お前がそう言うのなら止めはしないが、俺は連れて行かないぞ」: Master: “If that’s what you want, I won't stop you, but I’m not the one who’s going to take you there.”
マスター「本当に罪を償うつもりなら、最初の時――芸者を殺したと言ってきたときに警: Master: “If you really intended to pay for your crime, you should have gone to the police the first time—when you told me you’d killed that geisha.”
それが出来なかった俺はどうすればいいのだろう。: What should I do, when I couldn't even do that?
力なく頷いて立ち上がる。: I nodded weakly and stood up.
何処に隠れろと言うのだろう。: Where was I supposed to hide?
もう逃げ隠れするのは疲れた。: I was tired of running and hiding.
いっそのこと、このまま交番にでも駆け込んでやろうか。: I felt like just running into a koban right now.
マスター「――奏介よ、自棄になるなよ。お前のことは皆心配しているのだからな」: Master: “—Sousuke, don't do anything desperate. Everyone is worried about you, after all.”
鼻で笑い飛ばす。心配されたところで――俺の立場は変わらない。: I scoffed. Even if people were worried—it wouldn't change my situation.
むしろ、迷惑が掛けられない分さらに厄介だ。: In fact, it was more of a problem since I couldn't afford to get them involved.
《清鳳会》の手は借りられない。: I couldn't take any help from the Seifoukai.
これ以上連中に借りを作りたくない。: I didn't want to owe those bastards anything more.
奏介「自分で――何とかするしかねえか」: Sousuke: “I’ve got to—do something about it myself, huh.”
スツールから腰を上げる。: I lifted myself from the stool.
またどこかに隠れ潜む事にしよう。: I’d just have to find somewhere else to hide.
マスター「――奏介よ、困ったときに力を貸してくれる奴こそ本当の友人だぞ」: Master: “—Sousuke, the ones who lend a hand when you’re in trouble are the real friends, you know.”
奏介「――アンタが助けてくれるのかよ」: Sousuke: “—And are you going to help me?”
マスター「俺がお前を庇えば足が着きやすい。もっと関わりの薄い奴を頼った方がいいだ: Master: “It’d be too easy to track you if I sheltered you. You’d be better off relying on someone with fewer ties to you.
交流があるのは身近な人間ばかりだ。: The only people I had any connection with were those close to me.
唯一身近じゃなかった人間は――俺が殺してしまった。: The only person who wasn't close like that—I’d killed her.
マスター「お前――まさか死ぬつもりじゃないだろうな」: Master: “Sousuke—you aren't planning on dying, are you?”
奏介「……まさか。そんなの――綾音に申し訳がたたねえよ」: Sousuke: “...As if. If I did that—I’d have no way to apologize to Ayane.”
俺が死んで、彼女の元に往く事を望んでやしないだろうか。: Would she want me to die and come to her side?
口ごもりながらも、俺はマスターにそう答えていた。: Though I spoke hesitantly, that was how I answered the Master.
バーを出て神楽坂を歩く。: I left the bar and walked along Kagurazaka.
さて……これからどうするべきだろうか。: Now... what the hell should I do?
お茶の水のアパートには戻れない。: I couldn't go back to the apartment in Ochanomizu.
以前から俺が居た街だし、ここには《清鳳会》の事務所もある。: It was a neighborhood I’d been in for a long time, and the Seifoukai office was here.
連中と関わり合いになるのはもうご免だ。: I was done with getting involved with those guys.
奏介「……頼るしかねえか」: Sousuke: “...I guess I have to rely on her.”
力なく呟き、足を南の方へ向けた。: I muttered weakly and turned my steps toward the south.
三、四十分ほど歩いただろうか。: I must have walked for thirty or forty minutes.
赤坂の住宅街、閑静な一角にある洋館にやってきた。: I arrived at a Western-style house in a quiet corner of the Akasaka residential area.
以前は良く訪れていた処ではあったが、ここ最近全く近づくことはなかった。: I used to visit here often, but I hadn't come near the place at all lately.
まだ居るだろうか。仕事に行かれていては無駄足だ。: I wondered if she was still here. If she’d gone to work, this would be a wasted trip.
時計がないため正確な時間も判らない。: I didn't have a watch, so I couldn't tell the exact time.
とはいえここで突っ立っている訳にもいかない。何せ俺は犯罪者だ。迷惑を振りまくよう: But I couldn't just stand here. I was a criminal, after all. I didn't want to go around making a nuisance of myself.
何処か隠れられる場所――と辺りを見回していると。: I looked around for a place to hide.
建物から出てきた美華夏と目が合った。: My eyes met Mikage’s as she stepped out of the building.
美華夏「どうしたの？ こんな朝早くにうちに来るなんて珍しいじゃな……い」: Mikana: “What’s wrong? It’s rare for you to come to my place so early in the mor... ning.”
最初は弾んでいた声が、俺の様子を見るにつれだんだんとしぼんでいく。: Her voice, which had been bright at first, gradually withered as she took in my state.
当然だろう。それこそ俺は、死人のような表情をしていたのだろうから。: It was only natural. I must have looked like a dead man walking.
恐る恐る美華夏が問いかけてくる。: Mikage asked with a hint of fear.
美華夏が鞄を地面に落とした。: Mikage dropped her bag on the ground.
呆然と、理解が出来ないといった様子で訊ねてくる。: She asked blankly, looking like she couldn't comprehend it.
美華夏が目を見開いて息を呑む。: Mikage’s eyes widened and she caught her breath.
美華夏「何で？ どうして？ 奏介が綾音さんを殺す理由なんて、何もないじゃない！」: Mikana: “Why? How? There’s no reason for you to kill Ayane-san, none at all!”
美華夏は泣き声に近い感じて絶叫する。: Mikage shrieked, her voice close to a sob.
急に美華夏は声の調子を落としたかと思うと、俺の手を引いて家の方へ戻っていく。: Mikage’s tone suddenly dropped, and she took my hand, leading me back toward the house.
美華夏「あまり――外で話す内容じゃないわ」: Mikana: “This isn't something... to be discussed outside.”
美華夏の部屋を訪れるのは何年ぶりだろうか。: I wondered how many years it had been since I’d visited Mikage’s room.
部屋の所々の記憶は残っているから初めてということはないはずなのだが、今はそれどこ: I remembered bits and pieces of the room, so it shouldn't have been the first time, but I had more important things on my mind right now.
部屋の扉を閉じた途端、美華夏が俺に飛びついてきた。: The moment the door to the room closed, Mikage lunged at me.
美華夏「何でっ！ どうして綾音さんが死ぬのよっ！！」: Mikana: “Why?! How could Ayane-san be dead?!”
美華夏「せっかく……友達になれると思ってたのにっ……」: Mikana: “And I... I finally thought we could be friends...!”
貌を伏せ嗚咽する美華夏。肩が小刻みに震え、床にぽたぽたと涙の雫が落ちる。: Mikage bowed her head and sobbed. Her shoulders quivered, and tears dripped onto the floor.
美華夏を慰めようと、腰に手を回す。: I wrapped my arms around her waist, trying to comfort her.
泣き続ける美華夏をどうすることも出来ず、俺は暫く立ちつくしていた。: I could do nothing as Mikage continued to cry, so I just stood there for a while.
どんっ、と拳で胸を叩かれる。: *Thump*, her fist struck my chest again.
美華夏「あんたがっ……しっかりしてないからっ……！」: Mikana: “Because you... because you didn't have your shit together...!”
俺がしっかりしていないのは事実だ。: The fact that I didn't have my shit together was the truth.
繰り返し叩かれるも、俺は暫くされるがままで突っ立っていた。: She struck me again and again, but I just stood there and let her.
目尻に涙が溜まっている。: Tears were pooled in her eyes.
美華夏「どうして……守ってあげられなかったのよ……」: Mikana: “Why... why couldn't you protect her...?”
美華夏「謝るべきなのは綾音さんでしょう？」: Mikana: “The one you should be apologizing to is Ayane-san, isn't it?”
ひとしきり泣いた後、美華夏が俺から離れた。: After she’d finished crying, Mikage moved away from me.
美華夏「……電話してくる」: Mikana: “...I’m going to make a phone call.”
俺の言葉に、美華夏の背中が震えた。: Mikage’s back tensed at my words.
奏介「……通報してくれるならそれでいい。俺も――楽になれる」: Sousuke: “...If you want to report me, go ahead. It’ll be—a relief for me, too.”
美華夏「莫迦言ってるんじゃないわよっ！！」: Mikana: “Don't talk like such an idiot!!”
――今のは効いた。頬がじんじんと痛む。: —That one stung. my cheek throbbed with a dull pain.
美華夏「奏介が殺すはずないじゃない！」: Mikana: “There’s no way Sousuke would kill her!”
美華夏「私はっ……認めないからっ……誰がなんと言おうと、奏介が綾音さんを殺すはず: Mikana: “I... I won't accept it... no matter what anyone says, there's no way Sousuke would kill Ayane-san...”
俺を信じてくれるのは嬉しい。: I was happy she believed in me.
だが俺は、俺自身が信じられなくなっている。: But I couldn't even believe in myself anymore.
美華夏「……琢磨に電話して、何らかの情報がないか探ってみる。……絶対に奏介の事は: Mikana: “...I’ll call Takuma and see if I can find any information. ...I won't say a word about you, Sousuke.”
そう呟き、美華夏は部屋を出て行った。: Muttering that, Mikage left the room.
ひとり取り残され、俺は床に座り込んだ。: Left alone, I slumped onto the floor.
俺は何をしてるんだろう。: What the hell am I doing?
昨夜の夢――水の中で溺れる夢。: That dream last night—the dream of drowning in water.
苦しくて苦しくて――、暴れたかもしれない。: It was so painful, so agonizing—I might have lashed out.
綾音を刺したという感覚は……ない。: I had no... sensation of stabbing Ayane.
だがあの状況では俺以外殺人者がいない訳で――: But in that situation, there were no other killers besides me—
もし――俺以外の誰かが彼女を殺したとしたら。: If—if someone else besides me had killed her.
いや――駄目だ。部屋には鍵が掛かっていた。: No—that was impossible. The room was locked.
誰かが室内に潜んでいたということもない。: It wasn't as if anyone had been hiding in the room.
唯一部屋の中で隠れられそうな浴室にも俺は行っている。: The only place someone could hide was the bathroom, and I’d gone there myself.
抜け道なんてあるはずもない。: There was no way there was a secret passage.
探偵小説でよくある密室殺人だ。: It was a locked-room murder straight out of a detective novel.
ああ――俺は昨晩、何をした？: Ah—what did I do last night?
美華夏「奏介が……殺人事件の被疑者として指名手配されたって……」: Mikana: “Sousuke... you’ve been put on the wanted list as a suspect in the murder...”
ぼそぼそと美華夏が告げる。: Mikage spoke in a low, hushed voice.
奏介「さすがは警察……行動が迅速だな」: Sousuke: “The police are certainly... acting quickly.”
美華夏「そんなこと言ってる場合じゃないでしょう！？」: Mikana: “This is no time for comments like that?!”
美華夏が俺の腕を掴んで立ち上がらせる。: Mikage grabbed my arm and pulled me up.
俺の頬を張った体勢のまま、美華夏は低い声で唸る。: With her hand still in a position as if she’d slapped me, Mikage growled in a low voice.
美華夏「千駄木にうちが管理している空き家があるの。そこならたぶん見つからないと思: Mikana: “We have a vacant house in Sendagi that we manage. I don't think they’ll find you there.”
美華夏「……絶対に奏介は人なんて殺しちゃいない。綾音さんだってそう。何か……厄介: Mikana: “...Sousuke definitely didn't kill anyone. Not even Ayane-san. You’ve been... caught up in something troublesome.”
美華夏「だってそうでしょう？ 前の時だって妙な物を使われたとかなんとか……綾音さ: Mikana: “Isn't that right? Even before, you said something about some strange thing being used... Ayane-san said it too, didn't she?”
妙な物――第三者が関わっている可能性か――: A strange thing—the possibility of third-party involvement—
だがあの時の議論では――: But in that argument we’d had—
奏介「……何かが使われたにしろ、俺が綾音を殺したという事実は変わらない」: Sousuke: “...Even if something was used, the fact remains that I killed Ayane.”
朧気だった綾音殺人の想像が、第三者の介入によって明確に形を成してしまう。: The hazy image of Ayane's murder took a clear shape through the idea of a third party’s intervention.
俺が綾音を殺してしまった理由が――出来てしまう。: The reason why I’d killed Ayane—had been created.
美華夏「……そうとは限らないでしょう？」: Mikana: “...That’s not necessarily the case, is it?”
奏介「わからねえよ……俺には」: Sousuke: “I don't know... I just don't know.”
溜息を吐き、美華夏が俺の手を引く。: Mikage let out a sigh and took my hand.
抵抗することもないまま、俺は夢遊病者のように虚ろな眼差しで美華夏について部屋を出: Without offering any resistance, I followed Mikage out of the room like a sleepwalker with a hollow gaze.
何処をどう歩いたか覚えちゃいない。: I don't remember where or how I walked.
仕事を休んだ美華夏と共に、貌を隠し、市電を乗り継いでの移動。: With Mikage, who’d taken the day off work, I hid my face and moved by transferring between streetcars.
美華夏が足を止めたのは、古ぼけた一軒家の前だった。: Mikage stopped in front of an old, weathered house.
震災前から建っているのだろう、正面の硝子扉は白く濁り、中の様子は窺えない。: It must have been built before the earthquake; the glass door at the front was clouded white, making it impossible to see inside.
美華夏「元々古書店だったんだけど、数年前に店仕舞いしたらしいわ」: Mikana: “It used to be a secondhand bookstore, but it seems they closed down a few years ago.”
鞄から鍵を取り出し、入口の戸を開ける。: She pulled a key from her bag and opened the entrance door.
がらがらと開かれた扉の隙間から、すえた黴の臭いが漂ってきた。: As the door rattled open, the smell of stale mold wafted out from the gap.
辺りの様子を窺いながら、美華夏は低い声で俺を促した。: While checking the surroundings, Mikage prompted me in a low voice.
中に入るとさらに黴の臭いが鼻についた。: Once inside, the smell of mold was even more pungent.
背の高い書架には本が並び、床にも机の上にも本が積み重なっている。: Books were lined up on the tall shelves, and more books were piled on the floor and tables.
一歩進む度に埃が舞い、目が痛くなる。: Dust swirled with every step I took, making my eyes sting.
美華夏「さすがに使っていないと汚れてるわね……」: Mikana: “It’s certainly gotten dirty since it hasn't been used...”
厚く積もった埃に貌を顰めながら美華夏が奥へ進む。: Mikage walked further in, furrowing her brow at the thick layer of dust.
美華夏「……奥には厨房とお風呂場があるわ。水は裏の井戸から、瓦斯は来ていないから: Mikana: “...There’s a kitchen and a bathroom in the back. You’ll have to get water from the well in the back, and the gas isn't connected, so you’ll have to start a fire with wood.”
美華夏に促されるまま、俺は無言で階段を上った。: As prompted by Mikage, I silently climbed the stairs.
美華夏「電気は……よかった、来てるみたい」: Mikana: “The electricity... good, it seems to be working.”
数度電球のスイッチを引いて美華夏は安堵の溜息を洩らす。: Mikage let out a sigh of relief after pulling the lightbulb switch a few times.
ここもひどい埃だ。: The dust here was terrible, too.
元は売り物だったのか、ここにも壁際に本が積まれている。: Maybe they used to be for sale; books were piled up against the wall here as well.
美華夏「ここも掃除しないと駄目ね」: Mikana: “I’ll have to clean this place up, too.”
美華夏が窓を開けると、床に積もっていた埃が舞い上がり、思わず貌を顰めた。: When Mikage opened the window, the dust piled on the floor swirled up, and I instinctively furrowed my brow.
美華夏「奏介、井戸から水を汲んできてちょうだい」: Mikana: “Sousuke, go fetch some water from the well.”
小さく頷き、部屋を出ようとする。: I gave a small nod and tried to leave the room.
本当に無茶な話だ。: It really was an impossible request.
原因はどうあれ、人を殺してしまって憔悴しきっている俺がしっかり出来るはずもない。: Whatever the cause, there was no way I could have my shit together when I was utterly exhausted from killing someone.
美華夏「こんなときだからこそしっかりしなきゃ駄目じゃない！少し忘れるくらいじゃな: Mikana: “It’s exactly at a time like this that you have to! If you don't forget about it a little, you’re going to go crazy?!”
奏介「おかしくなる、か……。それならもう手遅れだな」: Sousuke: “Go crazy, huh... It’s already too late for that.”
ずっと以前から、俺はおかしくなっている。: I’d been going crazy for a long time now.
美華夏「――奏介っ！！ 貴方ひとりが苦しんでるわけじゃないんだからねっ！？ 私だ: Mikana: “—Sousuke!! You’re not the only one suffering, you know?! I’m... I’m hurting too...! But just being sad won't do any good, will it?!”
弱り果てている俺とは大違いだ。: You’re nothing like the broken man I am.
美華夏「何かしてないと押しつぶされそうだから。出来るだけ考えないように……でも、: Mikana: “I just feel like I’ll be crushed if I’m not doing something. I’m trying not to think... but I’ll never forget about Ayane-san.”
俺には到底真似できない。: I could never hope to imitate her.
奏介「バケツとか……そのへんにあるやつ使っていいんだな？」: Sousuke: “I can use... the bucket and stuff around here, right?”
美華夏「……あ、う、うん。たぶんあると思うわ」: Mikana: “...Ah, y-yeah. I think they’re there.”
意表を突かれたように美華夏は頷く。: Mikage nodded, looking caught off guard.
奏介「――それじゃ、掃除するか」: Sousuke: “—In that case, let's start cleaning.”
美華夏「――これくらいでいいかしらね」: Mikana: “—I think this should be enough.”
部屋の掃除が終わる頃には、既に太陽が西の空に沈みかけていた。: By the time the cleaning was finished, the sun was already starting to set in the western sky.
綺麗に掃き清められた畳に寝転がり息を吐く。: I lay down on the freshly swept tatami mats and let out a breath.
美華夏「これでなんとか暮らせるとは思うけど……」: Mikana: “I think you can manage to live here for now, but...”
美華夏が額に浮いた汗を拭う。: Mikage wiped the sweat from her forehead.
美華夏「調べ物は私がするから、しばらくの間奏介は此処でじっとしていて。此処なら警: Mikana: “I’ll do the investigating, so stay put here for a while, Sousuke. I don't think the police will find you here... probably.”
だが警察の捜査の手が美華夏に伸びた場合、此処も危険になるだろう。: But if the police investigation reached Mikage, this place would be dangerous too.
そのときはそのときだ――俺は諦観に近い感覚で頷いた。: Well, what happens happens—I nodded with a sense bordering on resignation.
美華夏「食べ物も持ってくるし……布団もそうね。ここにあったのは黴びていて駄目にな: Mikana: “I’ll bring food... and bedding, too. The stuff that was here was all moldy and ruined. I don't have anything for you to cover yourself with today, but—it’ll be fine, right? It’s summer, after all.”
階段の手前で美華夏が振り向く。: Mikage turned back before the stairs.
美華夏「……約束して。絶対……諦めたりしないって」: Mikana: “...Promise me. Promise me you won't... ever give up.”
一瞬哀しげな表情を浮かべ、美華夏は階段を下りていった。: With a momentary look of sadness, Mikage went down the stairs.
今は何も考えたくない。: Right now, I didn't want to think about anything.
綾音のことは考えたくない。: I didn't want to think about Ayane.
考えたく、ないのに――: I didn't want to, and yet—
目を閉じるのが怖かった。: I was afraid to close my eyes.
闇の中に綾音の姿が浮かび上がりそうな気がした。: I felt like Ayane’s form would appear in the darkness.
俺に対して恨み言を吐きにやってくるような気がした。: I felt like she’d come to curse me.
今夜は――眠れそうになかった。: It didn't seem like I’d be getting any sleep tonight.
美華夏は仕事が終わると神楽坂へ立ち寄った。: After finishing work, Mikage stopped by Kagurazaka.
既に辺りは暗く、酒場やバーの酔客が道をふらついていた。: It was already dark, and drunken patrons from the pubs and bars were stumbling along the path.
絡まれたりしないよう、遠回りで避けて歩く。: She walked a detour to avoid getting caught up with them.
今は少しでも面倒事に関わり合いになりたくはなかった。: Right now, she didn't want to get involved in any trouble, no matter how small.
いつもとは反対の方向からバーの前に出た。: She approached the bar from the opposite direction than usual.
営業はしているようだ。: It seemed they were open for business.
カウンタでボトルを磨いていたマスターに挨拶をする。: She greeted the Master, who was polishing a bottle at the counter.
美華夏が奏介を古書店に匿ったのはもう三日前のことだ。: It had been three days since Mikage had hidden Sousuke in the bookstore.
マスター「……さすがは雑誌記者、か」: Master: “...As expected of a magazine reporter, I suppose.”
美華夏「新聞を読んでいれば、誰だって知ることが出来るわよ」: Mikana: “Anyone who reads the newspaper would know.”
お茶の水の文化アパートでの殺人は、事件の翌日の朝刊にはすでに紙面を賑わせていた: The murder at the Bunka Apartments in Ochanomizu had already been featured in the morning edition the day after the incident.
当然そこには、白河綾音殺害の被疑者として現場に住んでいた玖藤奏介の名がしっかり: Naturally, it featured the name of Kudou Sousuke, who had lived at the scene, as the suspect in the murder of Shirakawa Ayane.
先日の芸妓殺害事件の被疑者であったことも併せて表記され、当人を発見せし場合は速: It also noted that he was the suspect in the previous geisha murder case and stated that anyone who found him should immediately report to the nearest koban or Kempeitai station.
一時捜査が中止になっていた事は一切書かれていなかった。: There was no mention at all of the fact that the investigation had been temporarily halted.
美華夏「――綾音さんのご両親に面会してきたの」: Mikana: “—I went to meet Ayane-san’s parents.”
綾音が遺体で発見され――彼女の故郷に連絡が行き、両親が東京にやってきた。: Ayane’s body was discovered—her hometown was contacted, and her parents had come to Tokyo.
美華夏はそれを同僚の新聞記者から聞き、友人として宿泊しているホテルまで面会に行: Mikage had heard this from a fellow newspaper reporter and had gone to the hotel where they were staying to meet them as a friend.
憔悴しきっていたふたりを前に、美華夏は言葉を継げなかった。: Faced with the two of them, who were utterly devastated, Mikage couldn't find the words.
当然奏介のことなど言えるはずもなく、美華夏は長い間立ち尽くしていた。: Naturally, she couldn't say anything about Sousuke, so Mikage had just stood there for a long time.
美華夏「私は話せなかったけれど……話をした人がいて、その人が言うには、犯人が憎い: Mikana: “I couldn't speak to them... but someone who did told me they were crying and saying they hated the murderer...”
胸が締め付けられる。↓綾音を殺した犯人が憎いのは美華夏も一緒だ。: Her chest tightened. Mikage also hated the person who’d killed Ayane.
だが――綾音の両親が言う犯人とは、奏介のことだ。その相違を埋めることはできない: But—the murderer Ayane’s parents were talking about was Sousuke. She couldn't bridge that gap.
悔しいけど、世間一般の人々は玖藤奏介が犯人であると認識してしまっているだろう。: It was frustrating, but the general public would recognize Kudou Sousuke as the culprit.
美華夏だって――盲目的に信じているだけだ。: Even Mikage—she was only blindly believing in him.
真実は、果たしてどちらなのだろうか。: I wonder which one the truth really is.
マスターが美華夏の前に細長いグラスに入った酒を出した。: The Master set a drink in a tall, slender glass before Mikage.
礼を言い、美華夏はグラスに口を付ける。↓レモンの酸味が残っていながらも、仄かに: She thanked him and took a sip from the glass. It had a lingering lemon acidity, yet was faintly sweet.
美華夏「……ええ。今は団子坂の古書店だった建物に隠れてます」: Mikana: “...Yes. Right now he’s hiding in a building that used to be a bookstore in Dangozaka.”
マスター「警察や憲兵に見つからないのか？」: Master: “Won't he be found by the police or the Kempeitai?”
美華夏「大丈夫……だと思います。出歩かないよう言ってあるので」: Mikana: “I think... he’ll be fine. I told him not to wander around.”
マスターはボトル磨きに戻った。: The Master went back to polishing his bottles.
美華夏はトム・コリンズを少しずつ飲みながら、奏介の事を考える。: Mikage thought about Sousuke while sipping her Tom Collins little by little.
なんとかして奏介の無罪を証明しなくてはならない。: She had to find a way to prove Sousuke’s innocence.
ただ、奏介が断片的に語った現場の状況では、他に犯人が居る可能性は極めて低いと美: However, given the situation at the scene that Sousuke had partially described, Mikage couldn't help but think the possibility of another culprit was extremely low.
もしかしたら――本当に奏介が殺してしまったのかもしれない。: Maybe—maybe Sousuke really had killed her.
無論本人の意志ではないだろうと美華夏は思っている。↓生前綾音が言っていた、何ら: Of course, Mikage believed it hadn't been his own will. The existence of some kind of machine—the one Ayane had mentioned before she died—that used suggestions to commit murder.
ただ、問題がひとつある。↓例えそんな機械が存在し、それが使用されたとしても、結: But there was one problem. Even if such a machine existed and was used, the fact remained that Sousuke was the one who killed Ayane.
それを知ったら――奏介はどうなってしまうだろうか。: If he found that out—what would happen to Sousuke?
古書店の二階に奏介を匿って三日、美華夏は毎日仕事の合間を縫ってそこを訪れていた: It had been three days since she’d hidden Sousuke on the second floor of the bookstore, and Mikage had visited every day in between her work.
布団や食料品、そして幾ばくかのお金。↓渡しはしたけれど奏介はほとんど反応らしい: Bedding, food, and a bit of money. She’d given them to him, but Sousuke had shown almost no reaction.
まるで廃人のように、寝そべったまま虚空を見つめていた。: Like a shell of a person, he just lay there, staring into the void.
美華夏は何度もしっかりしろと奏介を叱咤した。↓だが無反応。↓生きる気力を失って: Mikage had scolded Sousuke many times to get a grip. But there was no response. To him, who seemed to have lost the will to live, she could only sigh and leave the room.
私はどうすればいいのだろう――: What am I supposed to do—?
美華夏「……正直、ね……。あんな……魂の抜けたような奏介は見たくないわ」: Mikana: “...Honestly, yes. I don't want to see Sousuke like that... as if his soul has been sucked out.”
マスター「今の奴には支えてくれる人が必要だ。できることなら側に居てやってほしい」: Master: “He needs someone to support him right now. If you can, I want you to stay by his side.”
今の奏介は――綾音に囚われている。: Right now, Sousuke—is haunted by Ayane.
美華夏が入り込める余地はない。: There was no room for Mikage to enter.
内にいる綾音の方ばかり向いていて、こちらを振り向いてくれないのが辛い。: It was painful that he was only looking at the Ayane within him and wouldn't look her way.
＄ 死んでしまった相手に嫉妬するなんて――最低だ。: Jealous of someone who’s dead—I’m the worst.
＄ 美華夏は自責の念に駆られ、目の前のグラスを一気にあけた。: Mikage was overcome with self-reproach and drained her glass in one go.
マスター「薄めには作ったが……飲み過ぎるなよ」: Master: “I made it a bit weak... but don't drink too much.”
グラスに入っていたレモンの薄切りを囓る。↓少し苦めですっぱい味がした。: She nibbled on the lemon slice that had been in the glass. It tasted a bit bitter and sour.
どれだけの時間、ここでこうしているだろう。: I wonder how much time I’ve spent here like this.
布団の上に寝そべり、虚空を見上げている。: I lay on the bedding, staring up into the void.
天井は見ない。: I don't look at the ceiling.
天井の板に出来た染みが、綾音の貌に見えてきてしまう。: The stain on the ceiling boards starts to look like Ayane’s face.
目を閉じたところで、脳裏では鮮明に綾音の姿が浮かんできてしまう。: Even when I close my eyes, Ayane’s form appears vividly in my mind.
結局まんじりとも出来ないまま、寝ているのか起きているのか解らない状態で寝そべって: In the end, unable to sleep a wink, I just lie there in a state where I can't tell if I’m awake or asleep.
何度か美華夏が来たようだ。この布団も持ってきた。: It seems Mikage has come several times. She brought this bedding, too.
食い物も持ってきたようだが手を付けていない。: She brought food, too, but I haven't touched it.
怒った美華夏に無理やり喰わせられたが、吐き出してしまった。身体が何も求めていない: Mikage got angry and forced me to eat, but I just threw it back up. It felt as if my body didn't want anything.
美華夏は調べた事をいろいろと話して聞かせてくるが、ほとんど覚えちゃいない。: Mikage tells me all sorts of things she’s looked into, but I barely remember any of it.
俺には関係のない事だ。: It has nothing to do with me.
もう――放っておいて欲しい。: I just—want to be left alone.
俺はこのまま此処で朽ち果てていくべきだ。: I should just rot away right here.
そのほうが、向こうで待つ綾音も寂しがらないだろう。: That way, the Ayane waiting on the other side won't be lonely either.
――とはいえ、自ら命を絶つことも出来ない。: —And yet, I can't bring myself to take my own life.
自殺出来るほど、俺に気力が残っていない。: I don't have enough willpower left to commit suicide.
自殺出来るほど、俺は――強くない。: I’m not—strong enough to commit suicide.
がたん、と背中越しに震動を感じた。: *Thump*, I felt a vibration through my back.
空き巣でも侵入ってきたのだろうか。: Maybe a burglar had broken in.
どうせ俺の物で盗まれて困るようなのは何もない。: There’s nothing of mine that I’d be troubled by if it were stolen.
居直り強盗にでもなってくれれば、そちらの方がありがたい。: If it were a burglar who’d kill me, I’d actually be grateful.
願わくば、苦しまずに殺してくれ――: I hope they kill me without making me suffer—
階下の物音は微かにだが聞こえてくる。: The sounds from downstairs were faint, but audible.
何かを物色している様子は窺えない。: I couldn't hear any sign of someone ransacking the place.
まあそれでも構いやしない。: Well, I wouldn't mind that either.
逮捕したいのならすればいい。: If they want to arrest me, they can.
抵抗を見せれば、もしかしたら射殺してくれるかもしれない。: If I show some resistance, maybe they’ll shoot me.
指の先すら動かすのも億劫だが、射殺されれば後々の面倒がなくなる。: It’s a chore to even move a fingertip, but being shot would save me a lot of trouble later.
――面倒だがそうするか。: —It’s a pain, but I’ll do it.
階段を誰かがのぼってくる。: Someone is coming up the stairs.
だとしたら警官ではなさそうだ――やはりこそ泥か。: If so, it’s probably not a policeman—so a petty thief after all.
襖にもたれかかるようにして、久遠が立っていた。息が上がっているのか、肩を上下させ: Qwon stood there, leaning against the sliding door. Her shoulders were heaving as if she were out of breath.
久遠「……暫くの間……此処に居させてください」: Kuon: “...Please... let me stay here for a while.”
呼吸を落ち着けながら久遠は言う。: Qwon said as she settled her breathing.
俺を殺しに来た訳ではないことに少々失望しつつ、適当に答える。: I answered carelessly, feeling a bit disappointed that she hadn't come to kill me.
奏介「風呂や何やらは下だ――好きに使え」: Sousuke: “The bath and everything are downstairs—use them as you like.”
久遠「……奏介は食べていないのですね」: Kuon: “...You haven't eaten, have you, Sousuke?”
奏介「……面倒臭え。腹減ってるなら自分で作ってくれ」: Sousuke: “...What a pain. If you're hungry, make something yourself.”
久遠「それは困ります。私は料理を作ったことがありませんから」: Kuon: “That would be a problem. I’ve never cooked before.”
溜息を吐いて布団から起きあがる。: I let out a sigh and sat up from the bedding.
俺だって料理などろくにしたことはないのだが。: It wasn't as if I’d done much cooking myself, though.
立ち上がりかけ、久遠の膝の汚れに気付く。: As I started to stand up, I noticed the dirt on Qwon’s knees.
久遠「ここへ来る途中、転んだだけです」: Kuon: “I just fell on the way here.”
膝頭が砂にまみれている。血が滲んでいるのか、黒く湿っているようだ。: Her kneecaps were covered in dirt. They were dark and damp, as if blood were seeping through.
血は見たくない。: I don't want to see blood.
血にまみれた身体など見たくはない。: I don't want to see a body covered in blood.
奏介「――下に、井戸があるからそこで……」: Sousuke: “—There’s a well downstairs, so there...”
餓鬼の頃から血なんて見慣れていたはずなのに。: I should have been used to seeing blood since I was a brat.
あの視界一面の血を見てしまったあとでは――: But after seeing that sight of blood everywhere—
もう血は付いていないのに。: Even though there was no blood on it anymore.
俺が久遠を傷つけた訳ではないのに。: Even though I wasn't the one who’d hurt Qwon.
俺が綾音を殺したのは――: The reason I’d killed Ayane was—
奏介「動かない？ ……ああ、力が足りねえのか」: Sousuke: “It’s not working? ...Ah, you don't have enough strength.”
手押し式のポンプは久遠の細い腕では動かせなかったのか。: I guess Qwon’s slender arms couldn't move the hand pump.
奏介「――仕方ねえな」: Sousuke: “—I guess I have no choice.”
立ち上がり、井戸に向かうことにする。: I stood up and decided to head to the well.
まあ――今は何かをしている方が気は紛れるだろう。: Well—I suppose doing something would take my mind off things for now.
少しぐらいなら久遠に付き合ってやってもいい。: I could keep Qwon company for a bit.
そうした方が、綾音のことを忘れていられるだろうから。: Maybe that way, I could stay lost in forgetting Ayane.
台所から皿を持った綾音が出てきた。: Ayane emerged from the kitchen, carrying a plate.
ベッドから立ち上がり、テーブルへ移動する。: I push myself up from the bed and move toward the table.
綾音「ここの部屋って、電気トースターもついてるんだね」: Ayane: “I didn't realize this room came with an electric toaster too.”
ここで料理などする事もないから全く気付かなかった。: I never use this place for actual cooking, so it hadn't even crossed my mind.
綾音「だからトーストと目玉焼き作っちゃった。西洋風だよ」: Ayane: “So I made some toast and fried eggs. Western style!”
奏介「……まあ、俺は喰えればなんでもいい」: Sousuke: “...Well, as long as it's edible, I don't care.”
綾音「もおっ！ そんなんだったら食べさせてあげないよ？」: Ayane: “Geez! If you're going to be like that, maybe I won't let you eat at all!”
奏介「いや――喰わせて貰えるのなら和洋問わないと云う意味だぞ」: Sousuke: “No—I mean I'm fine with Western or Japanese, as long as you're the one feeding me.”
怒りかけた綾音を慌てて制す。: I quickly try to soothe Ayane before she actually gets angry.
綾音「ホントかなぁ……」: Ayane: “I wonder if you really mean that...”
訝しみながらも綾音は俺の前にも朝食を並べてくれる。: Despite her skepticism, Ayane sets the breakfast down in front of me.
テーブルの向かい側に腰を下ろし、綾音は挨拶をする。: Ayane takes a seat across from me and offers her thanks for the meal.
奏介「……その前に着替えろよ」: Sousuke: “...Get dressed before we do that.”
綾音は漸く自分の姿に気付いたのか貌を赤らめる。: Ayane finally seems to notice her state of undress, her face flushing a deep crimson.
そう言い残し、綾音は風呂場の方へ向かった。: Leaving me with that, Ayane heads toward the bathroom.
ワイシャツ一枚で部屋の中をふらついていた奴の言う事ではない。: She has no right to say that after wandering around the room in nothing but a single dress shirt.
――今更覗く事もしようとは思わないが。: —Not that I have any intention of peeking at this point, anyway.
すぐに着替えた綾音が戻ってきた。: Ayane returns, having quickly changed her clothes.
綾音「お洗濯物溜まってるみたいだったよ」: Ayane: “It looked like the laundry was starting to pile up.”
奏介「そのうち纏めて洗濯するさ」: Sousuke: “I’ll get around to doing it all at once eventually.”
綾音「わたしがやってあげよっか？」: Ayane: “Want me to do it for you?”
綾音「うんっ。――それじゃ改めていただきまーす」: Ayane: “Yay! —Okay, let’s try this again. Let’s eat!”
綾音「元気なのはいい事ですっ」: Ayane: “Being energetic is a good thing!”
奏介「……まあ、朝から気が沈んでいるよりは遥かにましだろうがな」: Sousuke: “...Well, it’s certainly better than moping around since sunrise, I suppose.”
目玉焼きを食べようと、テーブルの上に並べられた調味料に手を伸ばす。: I reach for the seasonings on the table to start on my fried egg.
奏介「綾音、醤油は出してないのか？」: Sousuke: “Ayane, didn’t you bring out the soy sauce?”
綾音「え？ お醤油って何にかけるの？」: Ayane: “Eh? What would you even use soy sauce for?”
トーストに醤油をかける奴はいまい。: Nobody puts soy sauce on toast.
綾音「ええーっ！？ 目玉焼きにはマヨネーズとソースじゃない？」: Ayane: “Whaaaat?! Don't you use mayo and sauce for fried eggs?”
奏介「ンな訳あるか。普通は醤油だろ」: Sousuke: “No way. Soy sauce is the standard.”
綾音「嘘でしょ？ 洋食なんだからソースじゃないと」: Ayane: “You’re lying! It’s a Western dish, so it has to be sauce.”
奏介「――まあ百歩譲ってソースは赦すとは云え、マヨネーズはねえだろ」: Sousuke: “—Look, I can compromise on the sauce, but mayonnaise? That’s crossing a line.”
綾音「どうしてよ？ 混ぜて食べればおいしいでしょ？」: Ayane: “Why?! It tastes great if you mix them together!”
奏介「目玉焼きとマヨネーズなんざ、どっちも卵だろ？」: Sousuke: “Fried eggs and mayo? They're both basically just eggs!”
頬を膨らませ、口を尖らせる綾音。: Ayane pouts, puffing out her cheeks.
綾音「わたしはこっちの方がおいしいんだし、きっと奏介もおいしいと思うよ？」: Ayane: “I think it tastes better this way, and I bet Sousuke-san would like it too!”
拗ねたように呻くと、綾音は目玉焼きの上にソースとマヨネーズを本当にかける。: Letting out a huff of annoyance, Ayane proceeds to drizzle both sauce and mayo onto the egg.
ふたつの調味料が混じり合い、何とも云えない色になる。: The two condiments swirl together into a color I can’t quite describe.
箸で摘んだ目玉焼きを俺の口許へ寄せてくる。: She lifts a piece of the egg with her chopsticks and brings it to my lips.
めげる事なく綾音は俺の口へ目玉焼きを押しつけてくる。: Undeterred, Ayane tries to force the egg into my mouth.
途端に口の中へ目玉焼きが押し込まれた。: Immediately, the egg is shoved inside.
味としては悪くないのだが、何か悔しいのでそう言葉を濁す。: It actually tastes decent, but I’m too stubborn to admit it, so I keep my answer vague.
奏介「……他に何を言えと？」: Sousuke: “...What else do you want me to say?”
綾音「感動したっ！ とか、うまいぞー！ とかない？」: Ayane: “Like, ‘I’m so moved!’ or ‘This is amazing!’? Anything?”
そこまで悩む事ではないと思う。: I don't think it's something worth agonizing over.
奏介「……まあ俺は醤油の方が好みだな」: Sousuke: “...Well, I still prefer soy sauce.”
綾音が動こうとしないので、仕方なしに立ち上がって自分で台所まで醤油を取りに行った: Since Ayane shows no sign of moving, I have no choice but to get up and fetch the soy sauce from the kitchen myself.
――何か負けたような気がして少々悔しい。: —I feel a twinge of frustration, like I’ve somehow lost this argument.
食後の珈琲を啜りながら、綾音が話を切り出す。: Ayane speaks up while sipping her post-meal coffee.
綾音「奏介、さっきうなされていたみたいだけど――厭な夢でも見ていたの？」: Ayane: “Sousuke-san, you seemed like you were having a nightmare earlier—did you see something unpleasant?”
何か苦しかったような気もするが、綾音の貌を見ただけですっかりと胸のつかえが取れた: I remember feeling some kind of suffocating pain, but just looking at Ayane's face felt like the weight on my chest had completely vanished.
俺の答えに納得したのかしていないのか、綾音は曖昧に頷く。: Ayane nods noncommittally, as if she can’t quite tell if I’m telling the truth.
綾音「――奏介に殺される夢」: Ayane: “—A dream where Sousuke-san killed me.”
一瞬、呼吸が止まった。: For a split second, my breath hitches.
綾音「奏介が悪いんだからね？ 散々わたしを殺しちゃうかもしれないって言ってたから: Ayane: “It’s your fault, you know? You kept saying you might end up killing me, so that’s why I had a dream like that!”
反論する事も出来ない。: I have no words to argue back.
綾音「……あんまり深刻な貌しないでよ。わたしが虐めてるみたいじゃない。何もなかっ: Ayane: “...Don't look so serious. You’re making it look like I’m bullying you. Nothing actually happened, so it’s fine, right?”
口許をほころばせ、綾音は言う。その笑顔に俺は救われたような気分になる。: Ayane speaks with a soft smile. That smile makes me feel as if I've been granted some kind of salvation.
綾音「奏介は人を殺すような人じゃないって言ったでしょ？ だから大丈夫」: Ayane: “I told you, Sousuke-san isn't the kind of person who could kill someone. So it's okay.”
奏介「まあ――お前が勝手に見た夢なんだから、俺が責任をとれる筈もないしな」: Sousuke: “Well—it was just some dream you had on your own. It’s not like I can take responsibility for that.”
綾音「どうしてそう云う事言うかなぁ……」: Ayane: “Why do you always have to say things like that...?”
お互い、冗談交じりに言葉を交わし合う。: We trade jokes back and forth.
こんな日常が続けば――平和に暮らせる日々が戻ればいいと、心の隅で俺は願い続けてい: If only this everyday life could continue—if only those peaceful days could return. Somewhere deep in my heart, I kept on praying for it.
奏介「……本当に大丈夫なのか？」: Sousuke: “...Are you really sure about this?”
声を顰めて隣の綾音に確認する。: I lower my voice, checking in with Ayane beside me.
綾音「奏介は気にし過ぎだって。案外人って他人のことは見ていないものだよ？」: Ayane: “Sousuke-san, you worry too much. People don't really pay that much attention to others, you know?”
辺りを見回す。人通りの多い神楽坂へ出てきた事で、厭でも他人の目が気になってしまう: I glance around. Now that we’ve come out to Kagurazaka with its heavy foot traffic, I can’t help but feel the weight of everyone’s gaze.
綾音「色眼鏡してるんだし、堂々としていれば大丈夫だよ」: Ayane: “You’re wearing your tinted glasses, so as long as you act confident, you’ll be fine.”
ばしっ、と綾音が背中を叩いてくる。: Ayane gives me a firm smack on the back.
背筋が伸ばされ、俺は周囲をもう一度だけ見回した。: My spine straightens, and I take one more look around.
まあ当然と云うか――誰も俺に気を留めている様子はなかった。: Well, as expected—no one seems to be paying the slightest bit of attention to me.
綾音「少しくらい格好いいからって、自惚れちゃダメなんだからね？」: Ayane: “Just because you’re a little handsome, don't go getting full of yourself, okay?”
何事もなくバーまでやってきた。: We manage to make it to the bar without incident.
綾音「……そういえば、ここにふたりで来るのって初めてだね」: Ayane: “...Come to think of it, this is the first time the two of us have come here together.”
奏介「……何度か来てるだろ」: Sousuke: “...We’ve been here several times before.”
綾音「来てないよ？ ……誰と間違えてるのよ」: Ayane: “No, we haven't! ...Who are you confusing me with?”
奏介「いや――店で何度か顔合わせてるだろ？ この前だって――」: Sousuke: “No—I mean we’ve seen each other in the shop plenty of times, right? Just the other day—”
綾音「……一緒にドアをくぐるのが初めてだって言いたいの、わたしはっ」: Ayane: “...I’m saying this is the first time we’ve walked through the door *together*!”
前に此処で綾音と逢ったのは、彼女が美華夏と言い争いをして部屋を飛び出した時だ。: The last time I met Ayane here was when she had that argument with Mikage-san and stormed out of the room.
あの時は探すのに疲れ果て、このバーへやってきたのだ。そこに綾音が居たのは全くの偶: I had been exhausted from searching for her and ended up at this bar. Finding her here was nothing but a complete coincidence.

綾音「どうして……わたしを殺したの？」: Ayane: “Why... why did you kill me?”
不意に目の前にあらわれた綾音に。: Faced with Ayane, who suddenly appeared before my eyes.
俺は、声にならない絶叫を発していた。: I let out a wordless scream.
今度こそ俺は目が醒めた。: This time, I truly wake up.
脳裏に甦ってくる綾音の声。: Ayane’s voice echoing in the back of my mind.
だから眠りたくなかった。: This is why I didn't want to sleep.
だからこうして――夜な夜な夢に現れる。: That’s why—night after night, she appears in my dreams.
厨房の隅に置かれた甕に汲んであった水を浴びるように飲む。: I drink the water from the jar in the corner of the kitchen as if I were drowning in it.
水が気管に入り、盛大にむせる。: The water goes down the wrong pipe, and I cough violently.
水に貌をつけて溺れ死ぬことができれば、どんなに楽だろう。: If I could just drown by sticking my face in the water, how much easier would that be?
貌も洗い、ある程度落ち着いたところで二階に戻る。: After washing my face and calming down to some extent, I return to the second floor.
久遠が布団から起きあがり、こちらを見ていた。: Qwon has sat up from her futon and is watching me.
久遠「いいえ、ずっと起きていました」: Kuon: “No, I have been awake this entire time.”
久遠「ええ。奏介がずっとうなされていたようでしたから」: Kuon: “Yes. It seemed as if you were suffering from a nightmare, Sousuke-san.”
久遠「構いません。それほど眠くもありませんから」: Kuon: “It is no matter. I am not particularly sleepy, anyway.”
久遠「それは貴方にも言える事です」: Kuon: “The same could be said for you.”
久遠は俺の目を見つめ、静かに告げてきた。: Qwon stares into my eyes, her voice quiet.
久遠「殺人を犯してしまえば、精神的に参ってしまうのが普通です」: Kuon: “It is only natural for someone’s mental state to suffer after they have committed murder.”
久遠「新聞で読みました。彼女――被害者には以前酒場で逢いました」: Kuon: “I read it in the newspaper. I met her—the victim—once before at the tavern.”
綾音が俺の部屋を飛び出したあの日、彼女はバーで久遠と話していた。: On the day Ayane stormed out of my room, she had been talking to Qwon at the bar.
久遠「良い話し相手になると思っていたのですが――惜しい人を亡くしました」: Kuon: “I thought we could have become good conversation partners—it is a shame to have lost such a person.”
知らぬところで久遠にまで影響を及ぼしていたとは、なんとも――やりきれない。: To think I had affected even Qwon in a place I didn't know—it’s just—unbearable.
久遠「奏介は悪くありません。これは――彼女の運命だったのでしょう」: Kuon: “You are not to blame, Sousuke-san. This was—likely her fate.”
奏介「……そんな運命なんてあるかよ」: Sousuke: “...How can there be a fate like that?”
怒ることすら出来ず、俺は力なく呟く。: I can't even find the strength to get angry; I only mutter the words weakly.
何の慰めにもなりはしない。: It offers me no comfort whatsoever.
久遠「運命論はお嫌いのようですね」: Kuon: “It seems you are not a fan of fatalism.”
何もかもがずっと前から決められているなんて――我慢できない。: The idea that everything was decided from the very beginning—I can't stand it.
綾音が俺に殺される運命だっただなんて――赦せない。: The idea that Ayane was fated to be killed by me—I can't forgive it.
久遠「――それでは、彼女は何故死んでしまったのでしょうね」: Kuon: “—In that case, I wonder why she had to die.”
あの日、俺が綾音を抱かなければ。: If I hadn't held Ayane that day.
夜遅いからと言って帰していれば。: If I had sent her home because it was late.
久遠「――でしたら、私も殺されてしまいますね」: Kuon: “—If that is the case, then I will be killed as well.”
彼女は、薄く微笑んでいた。: She was wearing a faint smile.
奏介「……俺が、殺してしまう前に――ここから、出て行ってくれ」: Sousuke: “...Before I... end up killing you—please, get out of here.”
久遠「――どこにも行く宛はありません」: Kuon: “—I have nowhere else to go.”
久遠「今はひとりぼっちです。いえ――奏介が居るからふたりきり、でしょうか」: Kuon: “I am all alone now. No—since you are here, Sousuke-san, perhaps we are all alone together.”
奏介「俺と一緒にいたら……殺してしまうかもしれない」: Sousuke: “If you stay with me... I might end up killing you.”
久遠「……奏介が一番に心配しているのは私の事ではありませんね」: Kuon: “...It is not me that you are most worried about, is it?”
俺はただ、久遠が俺に殺されてしまうことを心配して――: I’m just worried that Qwon will be killed by me—
久遠「――奏介は、自分がまた殺人を犯してしまうという事を心配しているに過ぎません: Kuon: “—You are merely worried about the fact that you will commit murder once again.”
確かに――そのとおりかもしれない。: She’s right—that might be exactly it.
俺が連続殺人鬼であることを――何よりも恐れている。: More than anything, I’m terrified of being a serial killer.
奏介「だからといって……お前の事を心配していない訳じゃない」: Sousuke: “But that doesn't mean... I’m not worried about you.”
久遠「そうでしょうね。心配していてくれなかったとしたら――さすがに傷つきますから: Kuon: “I suppose so. If you weren't worried about me—I would be quite hurt, after all.”
奏介「だから――俺の近くにはいないほうがいい。いつか必ず――殺してしまう」: Sousuke: “That’s why—it’s better if you aren't near me. One day, for sure—I’ll kill you.”
俺自身が与り知らぬところで――また殺人を犯してしまう。: In a place where I myself have no knowledge of it—I’ll commit murder again.
もう誰も巻き込みたくない。: I don't want to involve anyone else anymore.
久遠は口の端を笑みの形に作りながら言葉を続ける。: Qwon continues, her lips curling into the shape of a smile.
久遠「――私は、貴方程度には殺されませんから」: Kuon: “—I will not be killed by someone of your caliber.”
ぞくっ、と背筋に猛烈な悪寒が奔った。: A terrifying chill ran down my spine.
暑すぎるという事もなく、のどかすぎる夏の午後だ。: It is a tranquil summer afternoon, neither too hot nor too still.
俺には息苦しい時間。: For me, however, time itself feels suffocating.
開けた空間に居ることが苦しい。: Being in an open space is agonizing.
もっと地下室のようなじめじめとした暗い空間で、膝を抱えて蹲っている方が安心できる: I feel as though I would be more at ease hunkered down with my knees pulled to my chest in some damp, dark space—like a cellar.
誰も、何もない空間――そのほうが、人を傷つける事もなく、誰にも見つからず――朽ち: An empty space with no one and nothing... I think I could simply rot away there, without hurting anyone, without being found by anyone.
久遠「――本が揃っているのは良い環境といえますね」: Kuon: “—I must say, having such a collection of books makes for an excellent environment.”
壁に背中を預けて本を読んでいた久遠が口を開いた。: Qwon, who had been reading with her back against the wall, spoke up.
久遠「円本の全集から、国内未翻訳の洋書まで揃っています。涙香の巌窟王や泉鏡花、乱: Kuon: “From complete sets of yen-books to Western literature yet to be translated into Japanese. There are copies of Ruiko’s 'The Count of Monte Cristo,' works by Izumi Kyoka, and several volumes by Ranpo as well.”
本を読まない俺にとって、書名なのか著者名なのかすら判らない名前が並ぶ。: Names that I can’t even identify as titles or authors—since I don't read—are rattled off.
乱歩は知っている。……綾音がよく話していた。: I know Ranpo, at least. ...Ayane used to talk about him often.
久遠が本を置き、寝そべったままの俺に近づいてくる。: Qwon sets her book aside and approaches me as I lie there.
久遠「――貴方がどれだけ苦悩しようと、死者は還っては来ませんよ」: Kuon: “—No matter how much you agonize, the dead will not return.”
……気付けば、久遠を押し倒してのし掛かっていた。: ...Before I knew it, I had pinned Qwon down, looming over her.
奏介「何が……わかるってんだ……」: Sousuke: “What the hell... do you know...?”
動じることなく久遠は答える。: Qwon answers without a hint of agitation.
久遠「苦悩するよりも、しなくてはならないことがあるはずです。今のままでは何も変わ: Kuon: “There must be things you have to do, rather than simply agonizing. Nothing will change if you stay like this.”
久遠「でしたら、解決のための努力をするべきです。まずは――自らの置かれた立場を認: Kuon: “Then you should make an effort toward a solution. First—recognize the position you have been placed in.”
久遠は俺の下から這い出ると、壁際にあった本を手に取り、頁をめくる。: Qwon crawls out from under me, picks up a book by the wall, and flips through the pages.
久遠「ここにディクスン・カーの新作があります。英文ですけれど興味深い内容です」: Kuon: “There is a new work by Dickson Carr here. It is in English, but the content is intriguing.”
久遠「――雪の夜、とある教授の元に謎の人物が訪れ、部屋の中から銃声が聞こえました: Kuon: “—On a snowy night, a mysterious figure visits a certain professor, and a gunshot is heard from within the room. The professor died from a bullet through the chest, but the guest had vanished—”
俺は無意識のうちに立ち上がっていた。: I found myself standing up unconsciously.
奏介「……俺の時も《密室》だった」: Sousuke: “...It was a 'locked room' in my case, too.”
扉の鍵はかかっていた。窓も――閉まっていたはずだ。: The door was locked. The windows—they should have been closed, too.
久遠「――《密室》の意味がありません。なぜなら――犯人とされる奏介は、その内に居: Kuon: “—The term 'locked room' holds no meaning. Because—Sousuke-san, the supposed culprit, was inside it.”
――だから、俺が犯人なのだ。: —That is why I am the culprit.
久遠が本を片手に語り出した。: With book in hand, Qwon began to lecture.
久遠「――この本には、《密室講義》と題された章があります。密室殺人のトリックを分: Kuon: “—In this book, there is a chapter titled 'The Locked-Room Lecture.' It classifies and explains the tricks of locked-room murders, but it divides them into two major categories.”
久遠「殺害時、室内に犯人が居なかった場合と、扉の鍵が掛かっているかのように見せか: Kuon: “Cases where the culprit was not in the room at the time of the murder, and methods to make it appear as though the door is locked; however, in this instance—”
久遠「――奏介が犯人ではない場合、後者の鍵が掛かっているように見せるトリックは使: Kuon: “—If Sousuke-san is not the culprit, the latter trick of making the door seem locked becomes unusable.”
俺は自分で鍵が掛かっていた事を確認している。: I confirmed with my own eyes that the door was locked.
久遠「ならば犯人が部屋の中にいなかったという事になります。その場合は七種類の状況: Kuon: “Then it follows that the culprit was not in the room. In that case, the situation is classified into seven varieties, but I believe none of them apply here.”

久遠「どうです、どれも当てはまらないと思いますが」: Kuon: “What do you think? I believe none of these fit the circumstances.”
久遠があげた事柄全てについて、綾音の側に俺が居た状況では考えられない。: Regarding all the points Qwon raised, none were conceivable given the situation where I was right by Ayane's side.
久遠「――もうひとつ除外された方法として、秘密の通路というものがありますが、それ: Kuon: “—As for one more excluded method, there is the secret passage, but we likely need not consider that.”
久遠「ただ――奏介が犯人だと考えた場合、この事件は密室殺人ではなくなります。……: Kuon: “However—if we assume Sousuke-san is the culprit, this incident ceases to be a locked-room murder. ...After all, the culprit himself unlocked the door and left the room.”

久遠「私が警官でしたらそう考えます」: Kuon: “If I were a police officer, that is how I would think.”
俺の瞳を見据えて、久遠はきっぱりと言った。: Gazing straight into my eyes, Qwon spoke decisively.

そんなこと――改めて言われなくともはっきりしている。: Something like that—it’s obvious even without being told again.
久遠「――ですが、生憎私は警官でも何でもありません。犯人が誰であるのか突き止める: Kuon: “—But, unfortunately, I am not a police officer or anything of the sort. I have no reason to track down who the culprit is.”
久遠「無論――奏介を告発するつもりもありません」: Kuon: “Naturally—I have no intention of reporting Sousuke-san, either.”
奏介「……通報しても構わないぞ」: Sousuke: “...You can go ahead and call it in.”
久遠「奏介が捕まってしまえば、私も困ります。此処以外行くあてもありませんから」: Kuon: “It would trouble me if Sousuke-san were arrested. I have nowhere else to go but here.”
久遠「そう思わせているだけです」: Kuon: “I am only making you think so.”
微かに久遠が微笑んだような気がした。: I felt as though Qwon smiled faintly.
久遠「さて――ここで奏介の立場を確認しましょう。貴方が殺人を犯したか、犯していな: Kuon: “Now—let us confirm Sousuke-san’s position. Whether you committed murder, or you did not.”

奏介「そんなの――慰めにもなりゃしない」: Sousuke: “Something like that—it's no comfort at all.”
久遠「周囲が敵だらけという状況よりも遥かにましなはずです」: Kuon: “It should be much better than a situation where you are surrounded by enemies.”
逃げ続ける生活よりは、安心できる場所がある方がずっといい。: A place where I can feel at ease is much better than a life of constant flight.
ここは――安心できる場所だろうか？: Is this—a place where I can be at ease?
要塞でも監獄でもないただの家だ。: It’s just a house, neither a fortress nor a prison.
壁なんて簡単に破れてしまう木と紙の家だ。: A house of wood and paper whose walls could be breached easily.
大勢に押しかけられればひとたまりもない狭い家だ。: A cramped house that wouldn't last a moment if a crowd rushed in.
久遠「――少なくとも、私はここに居ると安心できます」: Kuon: “—At the very least, I can feel at ease being here.”
俺のすぐ隣に腰を下ろし、久遠がもたれかかってくる。: Qwon sits down right next to me and leans against me.
久遠「だから奏介も――安心できるはずです」: Kuon: “Therefore, Sousuke-san—should be able to feel at ease, too.”
理由も何もないはずなのに、俺は心なしか安堵していた。: Though there shouldn't be any reason for it, I felt somewhat relieved.
奏介「そうか――お前がそう言うのなら大丈夫なんだろうな」: Sousuke: “I see—if you say so, I suppose it'll be okay.”
久遠「ええ――心配には及びません」: Kuon: “Yes—there is no need for concern.”
ほんの少しだけ、俺は休息できるような気がした。: I felt as though I could rest, if only for a little while.
日が西の空に沈みかけている。: The sun is beginning to sink into the western sky.
何かをするわけでもなく、俺たちは部屋で呆としていた。: Without doing anything in particular, we were just spacing out in the room.
いや――久遠は相変わらず壁に寄り掛かり本を読んでいる。: Or rather—Qwon is leaning against the wall reading a book as usual.
久遠に呼ばれ貌を向ける。: Called by Qwon, I turn my face toward her.
頷きはしたものの、その場を動かない。: I nod, but I don't move from the spot.
奏介「……何が食いたい？」: Sousuke: “...What do you want to eat?”
久遠「特に希望はありません。胃が満たされれば何であれ構いません」: Kuon: “I have no particular preference. Anything will do, so long as it fills my stomach.”
確か美華夏が持ち込んでいたはずだ。: I’m pretty sure Mikage-san brought some in.
久遠「炊いた事がありません」: Kuon: “I have never cooked it before.”
奏介「そう難しいことじゃない。水で研いで釜で蒸らせばいい」: Sousuke: “It’s not that difficult. You just wash it with water and let it steam in the pot.”
少なくとも、彼女が何時も考えているような事柄よりはずっと単純で簡単なはずだ。: At the very least, it should be much simpler and easier than the things she is always thinking about.
久遠「理論と実践では天と地ほどの差があります」: Kuon: “There is a world of difference between theory and practice.”
奏介「実験だと思えばいい」: Sousuke: “Just think of it as an experiment.”
久遠「――なるほど、それもそうですね」: Kuon: “—I see, you have a point.”
奏介「……できれば俺の分も頼む」: Sousuke: “...If you can, make some for me too.”
久遠「わかりました。ただ――奏介、なにぶん実験ですので出来上がりは保証しかねます: Kuon: “Understood. However—Sousuke-san, as this is an experiment, I cannot guarantee the results.”
口の端にほんの少しだけ笑みを浮かべ、久遠は階段を下りていった。: With the faintest of smiles touching the corner of her mouth, Qwon headed down the stairs.
少しだけ――久遠に料理を任せたことを後悔しつつあった。: I was starting to regret leaving the cooking to Qwon, if only a little.
日は完全に沈み、窓の外から流れてくる風もほんの少し涼しくなってきた。: The sun has completely set, and the breeze flowing through the window has grown slightly cooler.
彼女が炊いた飯は、まずまずの出来映えだった。研ぎ方も蒸らす時間も何も問題がない。: The rice she cooked turned out passably well. There were no issues with how it was washed or the steaming time.
やはりこれも彼女の才能だろうか。何をやらせてもそつなくこなしそうだ。: Is this another of her talents? She seems like she could handle anything flawlessly.
まあ――米飯しかないのだが。: Well—there was nothing but white rice, though.
奏介「今のお前に出来ない事って、何かあるのか？」: Sousuke: “Is there anything you can't do right now?”
久遠「――多々あります。《現在》の私が出来る事の方が少ないでしょう」: Kuon: “—There are many things. There are likely more things I cannot do in my 'current' state.”
彼女は数瞬考え込んだ後、言葉を続ける。: After pondering for a few moments, she continues.
久遠「ただそのほとんどの事柄は、《現在》と云う制約を外せば不可能ではなくなります: Kuon: “However, most of those matters would not be impossible if the constraint of 'the present' were removed.”

少々意地の悪い事を言ってみる。いくら久遠がこれから身体を鍛えたところで、自動車を: I try saying something a bit mean-spirited. No matter how much Qwon trains her body from now on, lifting a car would be impossible.
久遠「持ち上げるだけなら可能ですよ。自動車の下に油圧式のジャッキを挟めば簡単です: Kuon: “If it is just lifting it, it is possible. It is simple if you slide a hydraulic jack under the car.”
こともなげに久遠は答える。: Qwon answers as if it were nothing.
奏介「そいつは反則だ。道具を使ってるだろ？」: Sousuke: “That’s cheating. You’re using a tool, aren't you?”

むきになって反論してくる事もなく、久遠は蕩々と理由を述べていく。: Without becoming heated in her rebuttal, Qwon calmly laid out her reasoning.
奏介「なるほど――それで《現在》は出来ないということか」: Sousuke: “I see—so that’s why you can't do it 'right now'?”

まあつまり、彼女に出来ないことは何もないということだろう――環境が整ってさえいれ: Well, in short, it probably means there’s nothing she can't do—provided the environment is right.
俺にも解った。一階の硝子戸を何者かが開け、屋内に侵入してきている。: I realized it too. Someone had opened the glass door on the first floor and was intruding into the house.
話し声は聞こえてこない。ひとりだろうか。: I can't hear any voices. Is it just one person?
だが、こちらの話し声を聞かれた可能性がある。: But there’s a chance they heard our voices.
久遠「……大丈夫だと思います」: Kuon: “...I believe it will be alright.”
緊張した様子も見せず、久遠は落ち着いて答える。: Without showing any sign of tension, Qwon answers calmly.
久遠「本当の侵入者なら、私たちに気付かれないよう慎重を期するはずです。戸を開けた: Kuon: “If it were a true intruder, they would exercise caution so as not to be noticed by us. To be noticed just by opening the door means they either have no ill intent—or they are a mere amateur.”
そう久遠が言っている間に、足音は階段を上ってくる。: While Qwon was saying that, the footsteps began to climb the stairs.
久遠の言は納得できるが、油断はできない。: I can accept Qwon's reasoning, but I can't let my guard down.
身を起こして片膝立ちになったと同時、襖の向こうから声がした。: Just as I rose onto one knee, a voice came from beyond the sliding door.
言いながら美華夏が襖を開け部屋に入ってきた。: While speaking, Mikage-san opened the door and entered the room.
名前程度なら知っているが、その他の境遇などについては全く知らない。: I know her name, at least, but I know nothing of her circumstances.
さも当然とばかりに久遠は答えた。: Qwon answers as if it were perfectly natural.
虚を突かれたのか、美華夏は考え込む体勢のまま固まってしまった。: Caught off guard, Mikage-san froze in a thinking posture.
久遠「故あってこちらで暮らしている御巫久遠です。奏介がいつもお世話になっているよ: Kuon: “I am Mikanagi Qwon, living here for certain reasons. It seems Sousuke-san is always in your debt.”
美華夏の視線がこちらを向く。……頬が引きつっている。: Mikage-san’s gaze turns toward me. ...Her cheek is twitching.
美華夏「まさかとは思うけど……拐かしてきたんじゃないでしょうね……？」: Mikana: “I hope this isn't the case... but you didn't kidnap her, did you...?”
奏介「当たり前だろうが。突然此奴が転がり込んで来たんだよ」: Sousuke: “Of course not. She just rolled in here out of the blue.”
俺の言葉を信用していない美華夏は、直接久遠に訊ねた。: Not trusting my words, Mikage-san asked Qwon directly.
久遠「私と奏介の深い関係が説明されていません」: Kuon: “The deep relationship between myself and Sousuke-san has not been explained.”
はっきり言ってかなり怖い。: To be blunt, it’s quite terrifying.
奏介「ちょ……ちょっと待て久遠。深いも何も――」: Sousuke: “W-Wait a minute, Qwon. What do you mean, deep—”
久遠「――そうですか。奏介はあの夜の事をもう忘れてしまったのですね」: Kuon: “—I see. Sousuke-san has already forgotten about that night.”
奏介「お……俺は何も……っ、おい久遠っ、お前もちゃんと――」: Sousuke: “I... I didn't do anything... Hey, Qwon, you need to tell her properly—”
久遠「――あの夜、貴方はいろいろと私にしてくれました。一生大切にしたい思い出です: Kuon: “—That night, you did many things for me. It is a memory I wish to cherish for a lifetime.”
何かを吹っ切ったかのように晴れやかな声で美華夏は問うてきた。: Mikage-san asked in a voice so bright it felt like she had broken through something.
美華夏「奏介のことは信じたかったけど……残念だわ」: Mikana: “I wanted to believe in you, Sousuke... but it is a shame.”
奏介「久遠とは何もねえって！ 信じろよ、おいっ！！」: Sousuke: “I told you there’s nothing between me and Qwon! Believe me, hey!!”
と、美華夏は俺にではなく久遠に訊ねる。: And with that, Mikage-san asks Qwon, not me.
久遠「――あの夜、奏介はピアノを弾いてくれました。それは私にとってかけがえのない: Kuon: “—That night, Sousuke-san played the piano for me. It is an irreplaceable memory for me.”
いや、むしろ俺もからかわれたのか。: No, rather, was I the one being teased?
久遠「私は真実を述べただけですけど」: Kuon: “I was only stating the truth, though.”
奏介「……頼むからお前は少し黙っていてくれ」: Sousuke: “...I’m asking you to please just be quiet for a moment.”
美華夏に久遠と知り合った経緯を説明する。: I explain to Mikage-san the circumstances under which I met Qwon.
――とはいえ、バーで遭遇しただけの関係でしかないのだが。: —Though, it’s really just a relationship where we happened to run into each other at the bar.
美華夏「――お嬢ちゃんはどうしてここに？ ご両親は？」: Mikana: “—Young lady, why are you here? What about your parents?”
久遠「他に行くあてがなかったからです。帰る処をなくしましたから」: Kuon: “Because I had nowhere else to go. I lost the place I could return to.”
久遠「謝られる必要はありません。両親を殺したのはもう随分と昔の話ですから」: Kuon: “There is no need to apologize. It was a long time ago that my parents were killed.”
ここに転がり込んできた時点で両親は亡くなっていると感じてはいたものの、まさか――: I felt that her parents were deceased at the point she rolled in here, but I didn't think—
久遠「――その昔、私達が住んでいた家に兇賊が押し入りました。私は無事でしたが、両: Kuon: “—A long time ago, bandits broke into the house where we lived. I was safe, but my parents were killed by them.”
久遠「いいえ。紛うことなく――私の責任です」: Kuon: “No. It was, without a doubt—my responsibility.”
久遠「……そのようなものでしょうね」: Kuon: “...It was something like that, I suppose.”
あくまで普段通り、淡々と久遠は喋る。どこか客観的な、醒めた視点だ。: Qwon speaks as calmly as ever. It is an objective, detached perspective.
両親が死んだというのに――まるで他人事だ。: Even though her parents died—she sounds as if it's someone else's business.
久遠「――以来、私はひとりきりです。だから奏介がどのような人間であろうと、私には: Kuon: “—Since then, I have been alone. Therefore, no matter what kind of person Sousuke-san may be, it does not concern me.”
久遠「殺人犯として手配されているのは承知しています。それが真実であろうと冤罪であ: Kuon: “I am aware that he is wanted as a murderer. Whether that is the truth or a false accusation, it does not matter to me.”
美華夏「冤罪だと――思うの？」: Mikana: “A false accusation—do you think so?”
美華夏はその単語にだけ食いついてきた。: Mikage-san bit only on that word.
久遠「さあ――どうでしょう。ただ、私はここで数日間奏介と寝食を共にしてきましたが: Kuon: “Well—I wonder. However, I have shared meals and sleep with Sousuke-san here for several days, and fortunately, I am still alive.”
久遠「奏介が真性の殺人鬼ならば、隣にこんな美少女が眠っていたら、犯し尽くし、陵辱: Kuon: “If Sousuke-san were a genuine killer, with such a beautiful girl sleeping beside him, he would likely have violated her completely, Ravished her completely, and then slaughtered her completely.”
美華夏「――ところで奏介、《ＣＡＤＥＮＺＡ》のマスターが何処に行ったか知らないか: Mikana: “—By the way, Sousuke, do you know where the Master of 'CADENZA' went?”
奏介「マスター？ ……いや、俺は――ッ！？ 何かあったのか！？」: Sousuke: “The Master? ...No, I—! Did something happen?!”
俺が知るはずもない。: There was no way I could know.
だが美華夏が改めて訊いてくるということは、何かあったに違いなかった。: But the fact that Mikage-san was asking me again meant something must have happened.
美華夏「それが判らないのよ。数日前から店に来ていないって、給仕の高梨さんが……」: Mikana: “We don't know. Takanashi-san, the waitress, said he hasn't been to the shop for several days...”
――嫌な予感が脳裏をよぎる。: —A bad premonition flickers through my mind.
奏介「……まさか、俺を庇ったのがばれて――」: Sousuke: “...Could it be, it was found out that he was sheltering me—”
美華夏「……判らないわ。ただ――近所の噂では憲兵に連れて行かれたのかもしれない、: Mikana: “...I don't know. But—rumors in the neighborhood say he might have been taken away by the military police...”
やはり――俺に関われば、こうなるのか。: As I thought—this is what happens if anyone gets involved with me.
美華夏「ただの噂よ。連れて行かれたのを見た人もいないし、そんな――一般市民が拘束: Mikana: “It’s just a rumor. No one saw him being taken away, and there’s no—report that a private citizen was detained like that.”
猛禽類を思わせる鋭い目つきの女の事を思い出した。: I remembered the woman with the sharp eyes of a bird of prey.
奴なら、アパートから俺が居なくなった事を察知して、関係の深いマスターを拉致するこ: If it's her, she might have sensed that I disappeared from the apartment and kidnapped the Master, who is closely related to me.
いや――マスターよりも関係の深いのが――: No—someone even more closely related than the Master—
美華夏「え……ええ。今のところは。ただ……やっぱり警察が話を聞きに来たみたい」: Mikana: “Eh... yeah. For now. But... it seems the police came to talk to her, after all.”
奏介「警察が……？ 憲兵じゃなくてか？」: Sousuke: “The police...? Not the military police?”
美華夏「本来殺人事件の捜査に憲兵隊が出てくる事はないわ。……っ、ごめん、私……」: Mikana: “Normally, the military police do not come out for a murder investigation. ...Ah, sorry, I...”
奏介「……謝られることじゃねえよ」: Sousuke: “...It’s not something you need to apologize for.”
殺人事件と言った事を美華夏自身が咎めたのだろう。: Mikage-san likely blamed herself for using the term 'murder case.'
奏介「……犯人が俺だろうが、他の誰かであろうが、綾音が殺された事には変わりない」: Sousuke: “...Whether the culprit is me or someone else, it doesn't change the fact that Ayane was killed.”
奏介「だから――お前が気に病む事じゃない」: Sousuke: “So—it’s not something you should worry about.”
奏介「……今は、マスターが居なくなった事の方が重要だ。もし俺の巻き沿いを食ったの: Sousuke: “...Right now, the fact that the Master is gone is more important. If he was caught up in my mess—”
美華夏「判らないけど……たぶん奏介絡みの事で連れて行かれたとするなら――私だって: Mikana: “I don't know, but... if he was taken away because of something involving Sousuke—then I should have been arrested, too. I’ve been asking around quite a bit.”
やっと、少しだけ美華夏が笑顔を見せた。: Finally, Mikage-san showed a bit of a smile.
奏介「少しでも危険だと感じたら――俺から離れていろ。関わらない方が安全だ」: Sousuke: “If you feel even a little bit of danger—stay away from me. It’s safer not to be involved.”
美華夏「……あのね奏介。危険だと感じたときにはもう遅いのよ？乗りかかった船だし、: Mikana: “...You know, Sousuke. By the time you feel danger, it’s already too late. I’m already in this boat, and besides—I don't want to be the only one in a safe place.”
久遠「――大丈夫ですよ。奏介が捕まらなければ――問題ありません」: Kuon: “—It will be alright. So long as Sousuke-san is not caught—there will be no problem.”
部屋の片隅に積まれた本が捲り上がり、埃を飛ばす。: Books piled in a corner of the room are flipped open, scattering dust.
どこからか、スルメを焼く臭いがしてきた。: From somewhere comes the smell of grilled squid.
俺の気分は相変わらず、暗く沈んでいた。: My mood remained, as ever, sunk in darkness.
それどころかマスターまでいなくなったらしい。単にどこかへ出かけているのならいいの: On the contrary, it seems even the Master is gone. It would be fine if he had simply gone out somewhere, but—
その間、バーは休業中だろうか。残ったのが千花と璃宝では、カクテルのひとつも出てき: Is the bar closed in the meantime? With only Chika-san and Riho-san left, it doesn't seem like even a single cocktail would be served.
千花の珈琲を淹れる腕は逸品なのだから、純喫茶でもやっていけそうではあるが――とん: Chika-san’s skill at brewing coffee is top-notch, so she could probably make it as a coffee shop owner—but she’d likely make some catastrophic blunder.
まあ何をするにせよ――璃宝は何もしないだろう。: Well, no matter what is done—Riho-san probably won't do anything.
そういや――と、俺は部屋の隅で本を読む久遠を盗み見る。: Come to think of it—I steal a glance at Qwon, who is reading a book in the corner of the room.
彼女と出会ったのはバーの中だ。マスターと何らかの関係があるのだろうか。: I met her inside the bar. Does she have some kind of relationship with the Master?
親子――いや、それはない。久遠の両親は兇賊に殺されたと言っていた。: Parent and child—no, that’s not it. Qwon said her parents were killed by bandits.
その後はどうだろうか。マスターが引き取ったりしていれば違和感はないものの、釈然と: What about after that? It wouldn't be strange if the Master had taken her in, but it doesn't feel quite right.
もしそうならば、俺の前にもっと先に現れているだろう。そもそもマスターと久遠の間に: If that were the case, she would have appeared before me much sooner. In the first place, I can't feel any connection between the Master and Qwon.
久遠「――人の貌を窺いながら考え事をするのは止めた方がいいですよ」: Kuon: “—You should stop pondering while staring at someone’s face.”
俺の方に向くこともなく久遠は小さく呟いた。: Without even turning toward me, Qwon murmured softly.
久遠「世の中には異性と目があっただけで恋に落ちてしまう人もいるのですから」: Kuon: “There are people in this world who fall in love just by meeting the eyes of the opposite sex, after all.”
久遠は頁をめくる手を止め、窓の外に視線を移した。: Qwon stops the hand flipping the page and shifts her gaze out the window.
久遠「いいえ、追っ手なら私でも判ります」: Kuon: “No, I can tell even if it's a pursuer.”
そっと窓から外の道路を覗き見る。: I peek out at the road from the window.
久遠「お知り合いのようですね」: Kuon: “It seems it is an acquaintance.”
奏介「――ああ、……知りすぎるほど知ってるさ」: Sousuke: “—Yeah, ...I know her only too well.”
そうとだけ答え、俺は部屋を出た。: I answer only that and leave the room.
外の様子なんて窺っている余裕はない。: I don't have the luxury of checking the situation outside.
俺は表に飛び出すなりその――彼女に声を掛けた。: As soon as I burst out the front door, I call out to her.
一瞬柚芭が驚きの表情を見せる。: For a moment, Yuzuha shows a look of surprise.
柚芭「お兄ちゃん！ お兄ちゃんお兄ちゃんお兄ちゃんっ！ うっ……ぐすっ、ひっく…: Yuzuha: “Big Brother! Big Brother, Big Brother, Big Brother! Ugh... sob, hic...”
俺のことを呼びながら、何度も拳で俺の胸を叩いてくる。: While calling my name, she beats her fists against my chest again and again.
柚芭「ばかばかばかっ！！ ……おっ、お兄ちゃんの……バカぁ……っ！！」: Yuzuha: “Idiot, idiot, idiot!! ...Big Brother is... an idiot...!!”
泣き出してしまった柚芭に手を貸し、建物の中に入る。: Helping Yuzuha, who had burst into tears, we enter the building.
畳の上にへたり込んでからも、柚芭は暫く泣き続けていた。: Even after collapsing onto the tatami mats, Yuzuha continued to cry for a while.
俺はただ、ずっと側にいて柚芭の頭を撫でていた。: I simply stayed by her side, stroking her head.
久遠は我関せずといった様子で未だ本を読み続けている。: Qwon is still reading her book as if it's none of her business.
奏介「……お前が謝る事じゃねえよ」: Sousuke: “...It’s not something you should apologize for.”
柚芭「お兄ちゃん……お兄ちゃんが人を殺すなんてありえない……よね」: Yuzuha: “Big Brother... there’s no way Big Brother would kill someone... right?”
その通りだと頷くのは簡単だ。不安がっている柚芭のためにもそうした方がいいのは明ら: It would be easy to nod and say that’s true. It was obvious that I should do so for the sake of the anxious Yuzuha.
奏介「――お前が心配することはなにもねえよ」: Sousuke: “—You don't have to worry about a thing.”
奏介「心配する必要がないだけだ。俺は……大丈夫だからな」: Sousuke: “There’s just no need to worry. I... will be fine.”
柚芭を巻き込みたくはない。: I don't want to involve Yuzuha.
久遠「――貴女のお兄さんは問題ありませんよ」: Kuon: “—Your brother will be no problem.”
そこで初めて柚芭は久遠の存在に気付いたようだった。: It was only then that Yuzuha seemed to notice Qwon’s presence.
この場合、なんと説明するべきなのだろうか。: In this case, how should I explain it?
さらりと久遠は答え、頭を下げる。: Qwon answers smoothly and bows her head.
奏介「どういう事も何も……そのままの意味だろ。まあ――俺もここの家主って訳じゃね: Sousuke: “It means exactly what it sounds like. Well—I’m not the owner of this house either, so we’re both basically lodgers.”
柚芭「ふーん……ねえ、あなたいくつ？」: Yuzuha: “Hmm... hey, how old are you?”
思わず吹き出し、まじまじと久遠を見やる。: I instinctively burst out laughing and stare at Qwon.
柚芭がぽかんと口を開けているのは呆れているのか信じているのか。: Is Yuzuha’s open mouth a sign of amazement or belief?
久遠「以前、精神年齢は二十八歳程度だと言われました」: Kuon: “I was previously told that my mental age is about twenty-eight.”
驚かせる。: Don't give me a heart attack.
久遠「――私にとって、肉体の年齢に意味はありません」: Kuon: “—To me, physical age holds no meaning.”
久遠「肉体が思考に追いつけなくなった時、年齢を感じる程度でしょうか」: Kuon: “Perhaps I will feel age only when my body can no longer keep up with my thoughts.”
柚芭「うー、よく解らないよぉ……」: Yuzuha: “Uuu, I don't really get it...”
久遠「……理解する必要はありません」: Kuon: “...There is no need to understand.”
柚芭「あ、そうそう、あなたのお名前は？」: Yuzuha: “Ah, right, what is your name?”
柚芭「うん……久遠ちゃん、どうしてお兄ちゃんと一緒にいるの？」: Yuzuha: “Okay... Qwon-chan, why are you with Big Brother?”
久遠「成り行きです。奏介に手籠めにされたあげく、脅されて身の回りの世話をさせられ: Kuon: “It is a matter of circumstance. Please be assured that it is not a case where I have been ravished by Sousuke-san and then threatened into taking care of his needs.”
久遠「ところで――貴女は何か用事があって此処に来たようですが」: Kuon: “By the way—it seems you came here for some purpose.”
柚芭「あ……うん。お兄ちゃんが此処に居るって美華夏ちゃんから聞いたから」: Yuzuha: “Ah... yeah. I heard from Mikage-chan that Big Brother was here.”
奏介「お前を巻き込みたくない」: Sousuke: “I don't want to involve you.”
柚芭のところに警官がやってきたとすれば、今も尾行がついている可能性はある。: If the police had gone to Yuzuha, there’s a possibility that she is still being shadowed.
下手すると今日だって――: If things go badly, even today—
窓から外の道路を窺う。怪しげな人影はない。: I peek out at the road from the window. There are no suspicious figures.
柚芭「あたしを巻き込みたくないって――だったら、どうして久遠ちゃんはいいの？」: Yuzuha: “You don't want to involve me—then why is Qwon-chan okay?”
久遠「――私は無関係だからです。無関係だからこそ、危険の度合いも低い」: Kuon: “—Because I am unrelated. Precisely because I am unrelated, the degree of danger is low.”
奏介「なあ……久遠。お前、柚芭と一緒に居てくれないか？」: Sousuke: “Hey... Qwon. Can you—stay with Yuzuha?”
そうすれば――俺が急に誰かを殺してしまうような事はなくなる。: If I do that—I won't suddenly end up killing someone.
綾音のような被害者を出さなくて済む。: I can avoid making another victim like Ayane.
ひとりなら――自分以外殺せない。: If I’m alone—I can't kill anyone but myself.
久遠が非難めいた視線でこちらを向く。: Qwon turns toward me with a reproachful look.
柚芭「無関係なら……いてもしょうがないじゃない」: Yuzuha: “If you’re unrelated... there’s no point in being here.”
柚芭が小声で愚痴る。: Yuzuha grumbles in a small voice.
久遠「貴女が奏介に襲われたら――奏介の負担が大きすぎます」: Kuon: “If you were attacked by Sousuke-san—the burden on Sousuke-san would be too great.”
確かに、俺が柚芭を殺してしまう可能性だってある。そのときは――俺も生きてはいられ: Indeed, there’s a possibility that I might kill Yuzuha. In that case—I wouldn't be able to go on living, either.
大切な女性に続いて、大切な家族まで失ったとしたら――: If I were to lose a precious family member following a precious woman—
奏介「……だったら久遠、お前も居ない方がいい」: Sousuke: “...In that case, Qwon, it’s better if you aren't here either.”
久遠「私は奏介の監視も兼ねています。私が居なくなれば――貴方は、死を選ぶ」: Kuon: “I am also acting as Sousuke-san’s monitor. If I am gone—you will choose death.”
俺ひとり、此処でじっと息を潜めていたら。: If I were to hide here all alone, holding my breath.
今の俺なら――死にかねない。: In my current state—I might just die.
柚芭「あたしのお兄ちゃんは強い人だから、死なない。……絶対に」: Yuzuha: “My Big Brother is a strong person, so he won't die. ...Absolutely.”
柚芭「……ねえお兄ちゃん、菱谷さんに相談してみたらどうかな？ お巡りさんだし、い: Yuzuha: “...Hey, Big Brother, why don't you try talking to Hishiya-san? He’s a policeman, so he probably knows a lot of things...”
奏介「そうだな――最悪の時は考えておいた方がいいだろうな」: Sousuke: “Yeah—I suppose I should consider it for the worst-case scenario.”
出来るだけあいつに借りは作りたくない。: I want to avoid being in that guy’s debt as much as possible.
柚芭「……最悪だなんて言わないで頼ればいいのに」: Yuzuha: “...You should just rely on him instead of talking about worst-case scenarios.”
