一階に下り、そびえる本棚を見上げる。: We went down to the first floor, and I looked up at the towering bookshelves.
奏介「こん中から探せってのかよ……」: Sousuke: “You want me to find it in all this...?”
所々隙間はあるものの、結構な量だ。しかも一部の本を除いては置き方もばらばらで、背: There were gaps here and there, but the volume was significant. Moreover, except for a few books, they were placed haphazardly, and some weren't even spine-out.
久遠「こちらの方は探しました」: Kuon: “I have already searched this area.”
久遠が書架の一角を示す。それもごく一部でしかない。: Qwon pointed to a corner of the shelves. It was only a fraction of the total.
埃の積もる本棚に貌を顰め、ざっと本の背表紙を確認していく。: Frowning at the dust-covered shelves, I quickly checked the spines.
漱石や太宰、芥川龍之介の全集に混じって、ドイルやポォの翻訳小説なども置いてあった: Mixed in with the complete works of Soseki, Dazai, and Ryunosuke Akutagawa were translated novels by Doyle and Poe.
だが《新青年》という雑誌は見つからない。: But I couldn't find the magazine called "Shin Seinen."
雑誌らしき束を開いてみても、《苦楽》や《新世界》などといったよく似た物しか見つか: Even when I opened bundles that looked like magazines, I only found similar titles like "Kuraku" or "Shin Sekai."
本を動かす度に黴臭い埃が舞い上がり、しばしば貌を背けて呼吸をしなくてはならなかっ: Every time I moved a book, moldy dust swirled up, and I frequently had to turn my face away just to breathe.
久遠「――アクロイド殺人事件の原書がありました」: Kuon: “—I found the original manuscript of 'The Murder of Roger Ackroyd'.”
奏介「……ンな事云われても解らねえよ」: Sousuke: “...That doesn't mean anything to me.”
久遠「クリスティのポワロと云えばこの国でも有名ですよ」: Kuon: “Christie’s Poirot is famous even in this country.”
奏介「本を読む人間にとっては、だろ」: Sousuke: “To people who read books, maybe.”
俺には人の名前なのか本の名前なのかもよく解らない。: I wasn't even sure if she was talking about a person or a book.
久遠「クイーンの《国名シリーズ》はさすがに全部揃っていないようです」: Kuon: “It seems the 'Country Series' by Queen is not complete, unfortunately.”
奏介「……お前も少しは探す努力をしてくれ」: Sousuke: “...You could put a little effort into searching too.”
古事記の写本を脇にどかしながらぼやく。: I complained as I shoved a manuscript of the Kojiki aside.
久遠「――私は既に探しましたから。後は奏介の活躍にかかっています」: Kuon: “—I have already searched. The rest depends on your performance, Sousuke-san.”
がんばってくださいと云わんばかりに両手を握り締める久遠。: Qwon clenched both fists as if to say, "Do your best."
本棚の高いところにまた雑誌の束を見つけ、手に取ってみた。: I found another bundle of magazines high on the shelf and took it down.
久遠の目の前に雑誌をおろす。: I lowered the magazine in front of Qwon.
原色で塗られた表紙に《新青年》と書かれている。: "Shin Seinen" was written on the cover in primary colors.
久遠が一冊取り、表紙を眺めている。: Qwon took one and looked at the cover.
久遠「これは――古いですね。昭和四年の六月号です。乱歩の《押絵と旅する男》が載っ: Kuon: “This is—quite old. It is the June issue from the fourth year of Showa. It contains Ranpo’s 'The Man Traveled with the Brocade Picture'.”
奏介「……そうか。他のは？」: Sousuke: “...I see. What about the others?”
久遠「昭和九年のは……ありました。四月号も」: Kuon: “The ones from the ninth year of Showa... here they are. The April issue as well.”
やれやれとそばの机の上に腰を下ろす。: With a sigh of relief, I sat down on a nearby desk.
奏介「それで全部読めるのか？」: Sousuke: “So, can you read the whole thing now?”
久遠「連載の最終回です」: Kuon: “The final chapter of the serialization.”
それは確かに気になりそうだ。: That would certainly be a nuisance.
久遠「読み終わるまでに見つかればいいのですけど」: Kuon: “I hope it can be found by the time I finish reading.”
二階に戻ろうと踵を返しかけ――外を見た。: I was about to turn back to go upstairs—when I saw outside.
曇り硝子の向こうに誰かが居る。: There was someone beyond the frosted glass.
背丈は俺と同じくらいの男だ。: A man about the same height as me.
俺の視線の先に気付いたのか、久遠が無言で一歩下がる。: Noticing where my gaze had landed, Qwon silently took a step back.
硝子戸が音を立てて開いた。: The glass door opened with a rattle.
琢磨「……よぉ。こんなトコにいやがったか」: Takuma: “...Yo. So this is where you were hiding.”
どこから嗅ぎつけてきたのか。: Where the hell did he sniff me out from?
――やはり犬だからか。: —I guess he really is a dog.
琢磨「焦んじゃねーよ。……上がらせてもらうぜ」: Takuma: “Don't get ahead of yourself. ...I’m coming in.”
と、琢磨は入口の戸を閉めた。: And with that, Takuma closed the entrance door.
琢磨「今のところ逮捕するつもりはねーよ」: Takuma: “I don’t have any intention of arresting you for now.”
琢磨の返事を聞かず、俺は踵を返して階段を上った。: Without waiting for Takuma’s reply, I turned and headed up the stairs.
世間話をするつもりはない。: I had no intention of making small talk.
その空気は琢磨も察したようで、すぐに話を切り出してきた。: Takuma seemed to sense the atmosphere and got straight to the point.
琢磨「以前、《連続狂人殺傷事件》について調べてくれと言ったのを覚えてるか？」: Takuma: “Do you remember me telling you to look into the 'Serial Madness Slaying Case' before?”
前に捕まったときか。: Was that when I was caught before?
俺の返事で成果があったとは思えないのだろう、琢磨はあまり期待しているとは言い難い: He probably didn't think my answer would yield results; Takuma’s tone made it clear he didn't expect much.
奏介「何かあったとして――お前に話すと思うか？」: Sousuke: “Even if I had—do you think I’d tell you?”

奏介「ハッ――結局捕まえに来たんじゃねえか」: Sousuke: “Hah—so you did come to catch me after all.”

奏介「俺を逃がして大目玉でも食らったか？」: Sousuke: “Did you get in trouble for letting me escape?”

琢磨「お前が、な。連中――そこいらのちんぴらまで引き連れてお前を捜してるぞ」: Takuma: “For you, yeah. Those guys—they’re searching for you, and they’ve even brought in local thugs to help.”
ごろつきを使ったところでここまでは見つけられないだろう。: Even if they used thugs, they shouldn't be able to find this place.

久遠「単なる居候です。お気遣いなく」: Kuon: “I am a mere live-in guest. Do not mind me.”
久遠「警察関係の方に名乗る名前は持ち合わせていません」: Kuon: “I do not happen to have a name to give to someone associated with the police.”
琢磨が苦笑を洩らす。: Takuma let out a dry laugh.
琢磨「――何が危険だと思うんだ？」: Takuma: “—What do you think is dangerous?”
口調こそ穏やかだが、琢磨の様子は真剣だった。: Though his tone was calm, Takuma’s expression was serious.
久遠「――手段を選んでいないからです」: Kuon: “—Because they are not choosing their means.”

琢磨「警察は捜査資料まで憲兵に持っていかれたからな。受け渡す時、柄の悪い連中が憲: Takuma: “The police had even their investigation materials taken by the Military Police. When they were handing them over, some nasty-looking types were hanging around the MP station.”

舌打ちをする。柚芭の身に危険が迫るかもしれない。やはり美華夏に頼んで引き取って貰: I clicked my tongue. Yuzuha might be in danger. I really should ask Mikage to take her in—
琢磨「……柚芭のことなら心配いらねーよ。俺が出来る限り様子は見るようにしてる」: Takuma: “...You don’t have to worry about Yuzuha. I’m doing my best to keep an eye on her.”
奏介「……なんで手前ェが」: Sousuke: “...Why the hell are you doing that?”
琢磨「……仕方ねーだろ。お前がだらしないからだ」: Takuma: “...It can’t be helped. It’s because you’re so unreliable.”
次に美華夏が来たら、柚芭を預かってくれと頼もう。まさか厭とは云うまい。: The next time Mikage comes, I’ll ask her to look after Yuzuha. Surely she won't say no.
取りに行かせようとして、久遠が俺を少しきつめに睨んでいるのに気付いた。: I was about to send her to get it when I noticed Qwon was glaring at me quite sharply.

久遠「言葉通りの意味です。以前自宅に兇賊が入り込んだのはお話したはずです。その際: Kuon: “Exactly what the words imply. I believe I told you that robbers once broke into my home. At that time—my parents died, and I was taken by them.”

久遠はまるで他人事のように呟く。: Qwon muttered as if it were someone else's story.

久遠の謙遜。前も同じような事は云っていたが――: Qwon’s humility. She’d said the same thing before, but—

同じような事を考えているとしたら――充分に有り得る。: If she was thinking the same thing—it was entirely possible.

此奴は俺が関わっている件の捜査から外されている。何らかの内部情報を得ようとしても: This guy had been pulled off the investigation into the case I was involved in. Even if he tried to get internal information, it would be difficult.
琢磨「――ま、役立たずは帰るか」: Takuma: “—Well, a useless man should head home.”
琢磨はふらりと立ち上がり、部屋を出て行った。: Takuma stood up slowly and left the room.
その背中に向かって呼びかける。: I called out to his back.
奏介「本当に――逮捕しないのか？」: Sousuke: “Are you really—not going to arrest me?”
琢磨「今のところはな。まだ暫くは泳がせて様子を見るさ」: Takuma: “For now, yeah. I’ll let you run a little longer and see how things go.”
奏介「――ハッ、よく言うぜ、ったく……」: Sousuke: “—Hah, you talk a big game, seriously...”
つまりはいつでも逮捕出来るということか。: In other words, he could arrest me whenever he wanted.
奏介「今すぐ逮捕しない理由は何だ？」: Sousuke: “What’s the reason for not arresting me right now?”
琢磨「……お前が捕まると困る人がいるんだよ」: Takuma: “...There’s someone who’d be in trouble if you were caught.”
誰のことだろうか。: Who could he be talking about?
まあ――俺が捕まって困る人間などそうはいまい。: Well—there weren't many people who’d be troubled if I were caught.
奏介「……いっそ捕まっちまった方が困らせないような気はするがな」: Sousuke: “...I feel like it’d be less of a bother if I just got caught, though.”
少なくとも憲兵隊が俺の居場所を探るために誰かのところへ踏み込むという事はなくなる: At the very least, the Military Police would stop raiding people’s houses trying to find out where I am.
奏介「……さっきから柚芭の事ばかりだな」: Sousuke: “...You’ve been talking about nothing but Yuzuha for a while now.”
琢磨「……俺が心配しちゃ悪いのか？」: Takuma: “...Is it wrong for me to be worried?”
奏介「お前……まさか柚芭に変な事してんじゃねえだろうな？」: Sousuke: “You... you aren’t doing anything weird to Yuzuha, are you?”
琢磨「――ざけんな。俺は真面目に――」: Takuma: “—Don't screw with me. I’m being serious—”
貌を逸らした琢磨の襟元を掴む。: I grabbed the collar of Takuma, who had turned his face away.
琢磨「……俺と柚芭が付き合って何が悪い」: Takuma: “...What’s wrong with me and Yuzuha dating?”
手加減なしで殴りつける。: I punched him with everything I had.
琢磨「……お前の赦しなんざいらねーだろ」: Takuma: “...I don't need your damn permission.”
もう一度殴ろうとして、右腕を掴まれた。: I was about to punch him again, but he caught my right arm.
琢磨「……お前が柚芭を守ってやれてたか？ ふらふらと厄介事ばかり起こしやがって、: Takuma: “...Could you have protected Yuzuha? You’ve done nothing but wander around causing trouble, leaving her behind, haven't you?”
荒々しく俺の身体を押しのけ、琢磨は部屋を出て行く。: Aggressively shoving me away, Takuma headed out of the room.
琢磨「――柚芭は俺が守る」: Takuma: “—I’m the one who’ll protect Yuzuha.”
そう言い捨て、奴は階段を降りていった。: Throwing those words behind him, he went down the stairs.
握り拳を壁に叩きつける。震動と共にぱらぱらと埃が舞い散り、久遠が貌を顰めた。: I slammed my fist against the wall. Dust swirled down with the vibration, and Qwon frowned.

ったく――柚芭の奴、なんであんな野郎と――: Seriously—Yuzuha, why with a guy like that—
久遠「――兄の心妹知らず、ですね」: Kuon: “—The brother’s heart is unknown to the sister, it seems.”
下らない事を抜かす久遠を睨み付ける。: I glared at Qwon for her nonsense.
いくら悩んだところで意味は全くないのだが――: No matter how much I worried, it was completely meaningless—
奏介「……拳で殴るなんざ、ピアノ弾きとして失格だな」: Sousuke: “...Punching someone with my fist... I’m a failure as a pianist.”
まあ――もう弾くことも無いのかもしれないが。: Well—maybe I’d never play again anyway.
外からは気の早い秋の虫の音が聞こえてくる。: Outside, I can hear the sounds of impatient autumn insects.
それ以外は至って静かな夜。: Beyond that, it is a remarkably quiet night.
久遠も今は居ない――下で風呂に入っている。: Qwon isn't here right now—she’s downstairs taking a bath.
久遠「……やはり橘さんのような体型の女性の方が好みなんですね」: Kuon: “...I suppose a woman with a figure like Tachibana-san’s is more to your liking.”
奏介「……なんでそうなる」: Sousuke: “...How did you even reach that conclusion?”
……まあ、そんないつもの話だ。: ...Well, it was just the usual banter.
俺は壁際に積み重ねられた本を手に取る気にもなれず、ごろごろと畳の上に寝そべってい: I didn't even feel like picking up one of the books piled against the wall; I just lay there, sprawling across the tatami.
――このまま寝てしまおうか。: —Maybe I should just sleep like this.
身体を起こし、明かりを消す。: I pulled myself up and switched off the light.
ただでさえ静かだったのが、暗くなるとさらに静寂を感じるようになる。: The silence was heavy enough already, but the darkness made the stillness feel even more profound.
風鈴でも吊してあればまた違うだろうか。: Perhaps it would feel different if there were a wind chime hanging somewhere.
やけに足音が響いて聞こえる。: The sound of footsteps echoed with strange clarity.
襖を開け、久遠が戻ってきた。: The sliding door opened, and Qwon returned.
湯上がりの身体からは仄かに湯気が上がり、石鹸の香りも漂ってきそうだ。: A faint mist of steam rose from her freshly bathed body, carrying the scent of soap with it.
呼びかけられ、身体を起こす。: I sat up as she called my name.
久遠の長い髪を見てぼやく。: I grumbled, looking at her long, cascading hair.
久遠「結構大変なんです」: Kuon: “It is quite a difficult task.”
久遠が櫛を俺に押しつけてくる。: Qwon pressed the comb into my hand.
奏介「引っかけるかもしれねえぞ？」: Sousuke: “I might snag it, you know?”
久遠「痛かったら怒ります」: Kuon: “If it hurts, I shall be cross.”
俺のぼやきを無視して、久遠は俺の脚の間に身体を割り込ませてきた。: Ignoring my complaints, Qwon wedged herself into the space between my legs.
有無を言わせぬその行動に苦笑しつつ、久遠の長い髪をひと房手に取る。: I gave a wry smile at her forceful behavior and took a lock of her long hair in my hand.
湿り気のある髪が光沢を放っている。: The damp strands shimmered with a soft luster.
頭頂部に近い方から櫛の先をいれた。: I touched the teeth of the comb to the hair near the crown of her head.
後ろから久遠の頭を小突く。: I gave her a playful poke on the back of her head.
振り返った久遠はほんの少しだけ頬を膨らませていた。: Qwon looked back at me, her cheeks puffing out just a tiny bit.
久遠に構わず髪を梳かし始める。白銀の絹のような細い髪はさしたる抵抗もなくまっすぐ: I started combing, ignoring her pout. The fine, silver-silk strands offered no resistance as they were straightened into place.
何らかの香料を使ったのか、髪を梳くたびに芳香が漂ってくる。: Perhaps she’d used some kind of fragrance, because a sweet scent wafted up every time the comb passed through.
深く息を吐く久遠に訊ねる。: I asked as she let out a deep breath.
久遠「――こうしてもらうのも久し振りです」: Kuon: “—It has been a long time since I have had this done for me.”
久遠「姉様が生きていた頃はふたりで梳かし合いなどをしていました。瓜ふたつでしたか: Kuon: “When my sister was still alive, we used to comb each other’s hair. We were identical, so we had no need for a mirror.”

久遠「姉様の髪はとても綺麗で、まるで絹糸のようでした。いつまでも潤いを保持してい: Kuon: “My sister’s hair was so beautiful, like threads of silk. It was soft, as if it held a permanent moisture.”

――何でもない事のように。: —As if it were nothing at all.

グラスを傾ける。ひやりとした口当たりが暑い夜には心地よい。: I tilted my glass. The cold bite was a relief on a hot night.
奏介「いや……酒には合わねえだろ」: Sousuke: “No... they don't go with alcohol.”
美華夏「……いつも通りだと思うけど」: Mikana: “...Seems the same as always to me.”
カウンタの隅に置かれていた籠を手繰り寄せる。: I pulled a basket from the corner of the counter toward me.
硬い殻に覆われた胡桃がいくつか入っている。: Inside were several walnuts in their hard shells.
相変わらずと云うか、胡桃割りの道具は入っていなかった。: As usual, there was no nutcracker in sight.
奏介「あっても良さそうだけどな……まあいい」: Sousuke: “You’d think there’d be one... well, whatever.”
ポケットからナイフを取り出して刃を出す。: I took my knife from my pocket and flicked open the blade.
突然椅子を倒して立ち上がった綾音に目を向ける。: I looked over at Ayane, who had suddenly stood up, knocking her chair over.
綾音「あ、わたし……どうしたんだろ」: Ayane: “Ah, I... what’s wrong with me.”
倒れた椅子を戻し、美華夏が綾音を座らせる。: Mikage set the chair back up and had Ayane sit down.
しきりに首を傾げながら綾音は俺の手元を覗き込んでくる。: Ayane looked at my hands, tilting her head in confusion.
綾音「そのナイフ……奏介の？」: Ayane: “That knife... is it yours, Sousuke?”
胡桃の殻を割りながら答える。: I answered as I cracked a walnut shell.
奏介「昔から使ってるからな、随分と痛んできちまった」: Sousuke: “I’ve had it for a long time, so it’s gotten pretty beat up.”
それでも胡桃の殻を割る程度には役に立つ。: Even so, it was still good enough for cracking walnuts.
綾音「怒らないでね。……そのナイフ、芸者さんに襲われた時にも持っていたやつ？」: Ayane: “Don’t be mad, okay? ...Is that the same knife you had when the geisha attacked you?”
綾音「……あのね、本当にそのナイフが犯罪に使われたかどうかを調べる方法があるらし: Ayane: “...You know, I heard there’s a way to tell if a knife was used in a crime. I think it’s the Renoir reaction—”
その声は、店の奥から聞こえてきた。: The voice came from the back of the shop.
ピアノの脇、店の厨房へ続く通路から久遠が出てきた。: Qwon appeared from the hallway next to the piano that led to the kitchen.
綾音「そう、それ。一度血が付いちゃえば、いくら洗ってもその薬を使えば解っちゃうん: Ayane: “Right, that’s it. They say if blood gets on it once, no matter how much you wash it, that medicine can tell.”
綾音にではなく、久遠に確認する。: I looked to Qwon for confirmation, not Ayane.
久遠「……ええ。英国などでは既に犯罪捜査で利用されていると聞きます」: Kuon: “...Yes. I hear it is already being used in criminal investigations in places like England.”
美華夏「刑事の琢磨に頼んだら調べて貰えないかしら？」: Mikana: “I wonder if we could ask Takuma the detective to check it for us?”
久遠「国内ではまだ使われていないと思います。それに警察へ依頼するのは面倒が多すぎ: Kuon: “I do not believe it is being used in this country yet. Besides, asking the police would be far too much trouble.”
久遠「――仕方ありませんね」: Kuon: “—I suppose it can’t be helped.”
久遠「奏介、そのナイフを貸してください。私が検査をします」: Kuon: “Sousuke-san, let me borrow that knife. I shall conduct the test.”
久遠「ルミノールは入手が難しいですが、同様の検査結果が得られる無色マラカイトやベ: Kuon: “Luminol is difficult to obtain, but I may be able to find colorless Malachite Green or Benzidine, which yield similar results.”
久遠「もし血液反応が出れば、本来ならば人血である証明や血液型判別もしなくてはなら: Kuon: “If there is a blood reaction, we would normally need to prove it is human blood and determine the blood type, but for now, we need not worry about that.”
そこまで言うと、久遠は無言で俺に手を差し出してくる。: Saying that, Qwon silently held out her hand.
その小さな手に折りたたんだナイフを握らせる。: I pressed the folded knife into her small palm.
久遠「――あとひとつ、訊いておかなくてはならない事があります」: Kuon: “—I have one more thing to ask.”
久遠はナイフの刃を出し、それを眺めながら口を開く。: Qwon opened the blade and spoke while looking at it.
久遠「奏介はこのナイフで人を刺したことは――」: Kuon: “Have you ever stabbed a person with this knife, Sousuke-san—?”
奏介「ねえよ。ついでに云うと肉も魚も捌いたことはねえ」: Sousuke: “No. And for the record, I’ve never even used it for meat or fish.”
ナイフを元に戻し、久遠が頷いた。: Qwon folded the knife and nodded.
久遠「試薬を手に入れるまでどのくらい掛かるか判らないので、すぐに結果は出ないと思: Kuon: “I do not know how long it will take to get the reagents, so I do not believe the results will be immediate.”
久遠「……別に貴女の為ではありませんから」: Kuon: “...It is not for your sake.”
ぷい、と久遠は頬を膨らませて貌を背けた。: With a huff, Qwon puffed out her cheeks and turned away.
ピアノの天板を閉じ、俺は大きく息を吐いた。: I closed the fallboard of the piano and let out a long breath.
澄んだ音が室内に響く。: The clear tone echoed in the room.
ピアノの調律を終え、椅子に腰を下ろす。: I sat on the stool after finishing the tuning.
少し音程がずれているように思えていたので直したのだが、天板を開けて中に埃が詰まっ: The pitch had felt a little off, so I fixed it, though I was disgusted by how much dust was inside when I opened the lid.
――久し振りに弾いてやるか。: —Might as well play for a bit.
気分とは関係なしに旋律を紡ぎ出す。: A melody began to spin out, independent of my mood.
何も考えず、何にも囚われる事なく。: Without thinking, without being bound by anything.
ただ指の動くがまま、無心で演奏する。: I simply played with a vacant mind, letting my fingers move as they pleased.
グランド・ピアノ程の音の拡がりはないが、それでも充分な音色だ。: It didn't have the resonance of a grand piano, but the tone was more than sufficient.
気の赴くままの旋律は自由だと自分でも思う。: I felt that a melody played on a whim was truly free.
くだらない出来事など忘れてくれそうな程の落ち着きが――: The calm was enough to make me forget all the bullshit—
手を休めずに視線を転じる。: I looked over without pausing my hands.
綾音「なんだ、はないでしょ？」: Ayane: “'Oh'? That’s all you have to say?”
腰に手を当て、綾音は頬を膨らませる。: Ayane put her hands on her hips and puffed out her cheeks.
奏介「怪しい連中じゃなくて綾音で良かったって事だ」: Sousuke: “I’m just glad it’s you and not some suspicious types.”
ぶつぶつと呟きながらも、綾音は俺の隣まで来る。: Ayane muttered under her breath as she came over to stand beside me.
綾音「留守かなと思ったんだけど、鍵が掛かっていなかったから。ピアノの音も微かにし: Ayane: “I thought you might be out, but the door wasn't locked. And I could hear the piano a little.”
綾音「ふーん――奏介はいきなりわたしが入ってきたら困るような事をしてるんだ？」: Ayane: “Hmm—are you doing something you wouldn't want me to see, Sousuke?”
奏介「いや、退屈だったからピアノを弾いていただけだ」: Sousuke: “No, I was just playing because I was bored.”
奏介「さっき調律したばかりだからな」: Sousuke: “I just finished tuning it, after all.”
綾音「そうじゃなくて。……何て言うのかな、奏介が弾く曲が綺麗だな、って」: Ayane: “That’s not what I mean. ...How do I say it? The songs you play are beautiful, Sousuke.”
綾音「違うよ、ホントの事。わたし奏介のピアノが好きだもん。前に言ったよね？」: Ayane: “It’s not flattery, it’s the truth. I love your piano, Sousuke. I told you that before, didn't I?”
綾音「言ったよぉ。奏介の自由な演奏がすごい自然で、わたしの悩みも解決できたって」: Ayane: “I did! I said your free way of playing was so natural, and it even helped me with my worries.”
奏介「……あん時は楚々としたお嬢様だと思ったんだがな。蓋を開ければとんだじゃじゃ: Sousuke: “...I thought you were a refined young lady back then. But it turns out you’re quite a handful.”
綾音「むーっ……奏介がいきなり口説こうとするからいけないんだよ。びっくりしたんだ: Ayane: “Hmph... that’s your fault for trying to seduce me out of nowhere. I was surprised, you know?”
拗ねているのか、綾音は口を尖らせる。: Ayane pouted, looking a bit sulky.
綾音「でも不思議だよね……奏介の演奏って。なんだかすごく心が安らぐの」: Ayane: “But it’s strange... your playing. It’s so... peaceful.”
――まあ、そういう奴もいるだろう。: —Well, I suppose some people feel that way.
俺だって著名なピアニストの演奏を聴けば感銘を受ける事もある。: Even I can be moved by the performance of a famous pianist.
俺の拙い演奏でも感動してくれるのなら、こんなありがたい事はない。: If my clumsy playing can touch someone, there’s no greater blessing.
暫くの間ピアノを弾き続ける。: I continued to play for a while.
――これはこれで、良い雰囲気の夕暮れだ。: —It was a nice atmosphere for a twilight evening.
ソファに身体を預け、綾音は軽い寝息を立てていた。: Ayane was leaning back against the sofa, breathing softly.
苦笑を洩らし、曲調を変える。: I gave a wry smile and changed the tune.
綾音の妨げにならぬよう、おとなしめの曲を弾く。: I played something quieter, so as not to disturb her.
暖かい風が流れている。: A warm breeze was blowing through.
――人の部屋まで来て居眠りするのもどうかと思うが。: —I have to wonder about someone who comes to a man’s room just to take a nap.
普通男の部屋に来た時は警戒するモンじゃないのだろうか。: Normally, wouldn't you be on your guard?
まあ――こういう処が綾音らしいと云えばそうなのだが。: Well—I guess that’s just like her.
綾音の目が醒めるまで、俺は静かに子守唄を弾き続けていた。: I continued to play a quiet lullaby until she woke up.
久し振りに外へ出た。: It’s been a long time since I set foot outside.
神楽坂の空気は何も変わっていない。: The air in Kagurazaka hasn't changed a bit.
どこか退廃的な、それでも静かな活気に溢れた空気。: It’s decadent, yet thick with a quiet, pulsing vitality.
警官や憲兵の姿も見えず、少々の安堵感を胸に道を急ぐ。: With no sign of the police or the Military Police, I hurried along, a small sense of relief blooming in my chest.
路地を入り、《ＣＡＤＥＮＺＡ》の前へ出る。: I ducked into an alley and came out in front of "CADENZA."
照明は消えている。やはり営業はしていないようだ。: The lights were off. Just as I thought, they’re still closed.
さすがにマスターが居なければ何もできないか。: I guess without the Master, there’s not much they can do.
軽く扉を押すと、鍵はかかっていないらしく簡単に開いてしまった。: I gave the door a light push; it wasn't locked and swung open easily.
ぼやきつつ、薄明かりの灯る店内に入った。: Grumbling to myself, I stepped into the dim interior.
静かすぎる店内。: The shop was far too quiet.
カウンタに寄り掛かるように、千花が呆けていた。: Chika was slumped against the counter, staring vacantly into space.
千花の身体が飛び上がった。: Chika practically jumped out of her skin.
奏介「……そこまで驚かなくてもいいだろ」: Sousuke: “...You don’t have to be that surprised.”
奏介「マスターは……戻ってないか」: Sousuke: “Master... hasn't come back, has he?”
無人のカウンタに目をやり、スツールに座る。: I glanced at the empty space behind the bar and took a seat on a stool.
千花の言葉に頷き、店内を軽く見回す。: I nodded at Chika’s words and scanned the room.
いつも通りの店だ。マスターが居なくなってからも掃除を欠かさないのだろう、カウンタ: The place looked exactly the same. Even with the Master gone, she must not have missed a day of cleaning; there wasn't a speck of dust on the counter.
千花「……はい、一応は」: Chika: “...Fine, I suppose. For the most part.”
気になっていた事を訊ねてみた。: I finally asked the question that had been weighing on me.
ここに居てもマスターは戻ってこないかもしれない。どういう状況にあるかはわからない: Even if she stays here, the Master might never return. I don't know what kind of situation he’s in, but since he’s cut off all contact, it’s a distinct possibility.
千花「他に行くあてもありません」: Chika: “I have nowhere else to go.”
頬を掻く。あまり他者の過去の話を聞いたことがなかった所為か、長い付き合いであるに: I scratched my cheek. Maybe it was because I never really listened to other people’s pasts, but despite knowing her for a long time, I realized I knew nothing about her.
千花「別に……昔のことですから。それに、マスターに助けて貰ってからの生活の方が充: Chika: “It’s fine... that’s all in the past. Besides, my life since the Master took me in has been so much fuller and happier.”
千花「はいっ。……あ、玖藤さん、珈琲飲まれますか？」: Chika: “Yes. ...Oh, Kudou-san, would you like some coffee?”
千花「わかりました。少々お待ちくださいね」: Chika: “Understood. Please wait just a moment.”
千花はいそいそと厨房へ向かった。: Chika bustled off toward the kitchen.
気の滅入る話はお互いのためにしない方が良いだろう。: It was probably better for both of us not to dwell on depressing topics.
立ち上がり、ピアノへ近づく。: I stood up and approached the piano.
黒く光る表面には手垢も付いていない。俺が長らく触れていないのもあるだろうが、やは: The black, polished surface was free of even a single fingerprint. Part of that was because I hadn't touched it in so long, but it was clear she’d been cleaning this, too.
調律の必要もない、綺麗な音。: It didn't even need tuning; the tone was perfect.
久し振りに鍵盤を弾く。: I touched the keys for the first time in ages.
感覚の狂いはさほどないようだ。: My sense of the instrument hadn't dulled much at all.
ひとりでに指が動く。: My fingers began to move of their own accord.
やはり慣れというものだろうか。: I guess old habits die hard.
千花「……玖藤さん、珈琲が入りましたけど……」: Chika: “...Kudou-san, the coffee is ready, but...”
カウンタ席に戻る。: I returned to my seat at the counter.
湯気を立てるカップと、角砂糖の入った壜。: A steaming cup sat next to a jar of sugar cubes.
千花「……あっ、玖藤さんは……何も入れないんでしたっけ……？」: Chika: “...Ah, wait... Kudou-san, you... didn't take anything in your coffee, did you...?”
千花が店の壁際に並ぶ洋酒の壜を捜しに行く。: Chika went to search through the bottles of Western liquor lined up against the wall.
奏介「……頼むから躓いて割ったりするなよ」: Sousuke: “...Please don't trip and break anything.”
とはいえ、酒瓶を千花が割ったという話は聞かない。: Though, come to think of it, I’d never heard of Chika breaking a single bottle.
千花が琥珀色に光る壜を持ってきた。: Chika brought over a bottle glowing with an amber light.
奏介「コニャックか……随分高そうなのを選んできたな」: Sousuke: “Cognac, huh... you picked a damn expensive one.”
ラベルには一世紀以上前の年が書かれている。: The label indicated it was over a century old.
奏介「……まあいいさ。栓もあいているし、使わなきゃ損だ」: Sousuke: “...It’s fine. It’s already open, it’d be a waste not to use it.”
壜から別のグラスに少し注ぐ。特有の芳香が鼻腔をくすぐる。: I poured a small amount into a separate glass. The unique aroma tickled my nose.
千花「珈琲淹れたのに……飲むんですか？」: Chika: “I just made coffee... and you’re going to drink that?”
隣で千花が不思議そうに――少し不満げに眺めている。: Standing beside me, Chika watched with a look of confusion—and a hint of dissatisfaction.
グラスを回しながら、スプーンで角砂糖をすくい、カップの上に載せる。: Swirling the glass, I scooped up a sugar cube with a spoon and balanced it over the cup.
その角砂糖にブランデーを数滴垂らす。白い砂糖がすぐに茶色く染まった。: I let a few drops of brandy drip onto the cube. The white sugar was instantly stained brown.
ポケットから燐寸を取り出し、スプーンに近づける。: I took a match from my pocket and brought it close to the spoon.
ポッ、と小さい音をたて、炎が砂糖に燃え移った。: With a soft *pop*, the sugar caught fire.
千花が驚きの声をあげる。: Chika let out a small cry of surprise.
炎は数秒間燃え続け、不意に消えてしまった。: The flame danced for a few seconds before suddenly winking out.
後には形の崩れた角砂糖が残される。: Behind it, it left a misshapen, melted lump of sugar.
奏介「そしてこいつを中に入れれば完成だ」: Sousuke: “And once I drop this in, it’s finished.”
溶けた角砂糖を珈琲の中に落とし、スプーンでかき混ぜる。: I dropped the melted sugar into the coffee and stirred it with the spoon.
充分混ざったところでスプーンをあげ、ひと口飲む。: Once it was thoroughly mixed, I lifted the spoon and took a sip.
仄かな香りが湯気に含まれている。: A faint, elegant fragrance rose with the steam.
千花「なんという飲み物ですか？」: Chika: “What do you call that drink?”
奏介「カフェ・ロワイヤルといって、かのナポレオンも好んだ飲み物らしい」: Sousuke: “It’s called Café Royal. Supposedly, Napoleon himself was fond of it.”
千花「ふぇっ！？ わ、わたしですかっ！？ でっ、でもっ、わたしお酒飲めませんしっ: Chika: “Wh-!? M-me!? B-but... I can't handle alcohol...!”
奏介「火付けてほとんどアルコールも飛んじまってるから大丈夫だろ」: Sousuke: “I set it on fire, so most of the alcohol burned off. You’ll be fine.”
千花の前にカップを移動させる。: I slid the cup over in front of Chika.
千花はおずおずとカップを手に取った。: Chika tentatively picked up the cup.
暫く匂いを嗅いでいた千花だったが、意を決してカップに口を付けた。: After sniffing it for a moment, she plucked up her courage and took a sip.
見れば千花の貌は真っ赤に染まっている。: I looked over to see Chika’s face flushed a deep red.
明るく答えると、千花は俺のすぐ隣のスツールに腰を下ろした。: Answering brightly, Chika sat down on the stool right next to mine.
千花「アルコールは飛んだって言ったじゃないですかぁ。だからわたしは酔ってないんで: Chika: “You said the alcohol burned off, didn't you? So I couldn't possibly be drunk!”
明らかに普段の千花とは違う。: She was clearly not her usual self.
千花が残した珈琲に口を付ける。香りは残っているが、アルコールは感じられない。: I took a sip of the coffee she’d left. The fragrance remained, but I couldn't taste any alcohol.
珈琲を吹き出す。: I nearly choked on the coffee.
どうも妙な方向に話が進みそうだ。: This conversation was heading in a very strange direction.
千花が俺にもたれかかり、手を掴んできた。: Chika leaned against me and grabbed my hand.
千花「ほらぁ……心臓だって、こんなにゆっくり……」: Chika: “Look... even my heart is beating so slowly...”
そう言いながら俺の手を自分の胸に押しつけようとしてくる。: As she spoke, she tried to press my hand against her chest.
一応形だけは拒否の姿勢を見せて手を引く。: I made a show of resisting and pulled my hand back.
前後不覚に陥っているところを手籠めにするような趣味はない。: I didn't have a habit of taking advantage of women who’d lost their senses.
千花「あぅ……やっぱり、璃宝さんみたいにならないと駄目なんですね……」: Chika: “Aww... I guess I really do have to be more like Riho-san...”
はぁ、と千花は溜息を吐いてカウンタに置かれたままのグラスを手に取った。: Chika let out a long sigh and reached for the glass that was still sitting on the counter.
一気にグラスのブランデーがなくなる。: The brandy in the glass vanished in a single go.
なんだか、千花の上体がゆっくりと揺れ動いている。: Somehow, Chika’s upper body began to sway slowly.
奏介「……酒飲めないって言ってただろ」: Sousuke: “...Didn't you say you couldn't drink?”
見開かれた千花の目から涙が溢れ出す。: Tears began to well up in Chika’s wide eyes.
千花「わ……わたしっ、そんな事……言いませんよぉ……」: Chika: “I... I would never... say such a thing...!”
だがこの状況でそれを指摘することは出来ない。: But there was no way I could point that out in this situation.
本格的に泣き出してしまった千花を前に、俺はどうすることも出来なかった。: Faced with Chika, who had started crying in earnest, I had no idea what to do.
千花「わたしだって頑張ってるんですよぉ……？ それなのにいつも失敗ばかりしちゃっ: Chika: “I’m trying so hard, you know...? And yet I’m always failing at everything...”
放っておく訳にもいくまい。: I couldn't just leave her like this.
まずは泣きやませなければ。: First, I had to get her to stop crying.
ぴくっ、と千花が身体を震わせた。: Her body gave a small twitch.
主のいなくなった店をひとりで守り抜いて来たのだ。: She’d been protecting the shop all by herself since the Master disappeared.
不安だらけだったろうに、泣き言ひとつ言わず。: She must have been terrified, yet she hadn't uttered a single word of complaint.
酔ったときくらいは――いたわってやった方がいい。: The least I could do—since she was drunk—was show her some appreciation.
頭の両側でふたつに纏められた長い髪はよく手入れされているようで、さらさらと指の間: Her long hair, tied up in two bundles on either side of her head, was well-maintained and slid smoothly through my fingers.
暫くそうしていると、千花はしゃくりあげながらもだいぶ落ち着いてきたようだ。: As I continued, Chika’s sobbing eventually began to subside.

素面に戻ったのならいいだろう。: As long as she’s back to normal, it’s fine.

何をするでもなく、床に寝そべっている。: I’m just lying on the floor, doing nothing.
なんとかしなくてはならないと判っているのに、何をすべきかが判らない。: I know I have to do something, but I don't know what.
いや――何をすべきかも判っているのに、どうすればいいのかが判っていなかった。: No—I know what I should do, but I don't know how to go about it.

久遠「――彼女が殺されたのは、所謂密室状態にあった部屋でした」: Kuon: “—She was killed in a room that was in a so-called locked-room state.”
確かにあの時、俺は部屋の鍵を開けて外に出た。: It was true that at the time, I unlocked the door and went outside.
久遠「この場合、奏介が犯人ではないという証拠の前提として、外部から侵入したという: Kuon: “In this case, as a premise for evidence that you are not the culprit, we must find evidence of an intrusion from the outside.”
奏介「だが……密室殺人と認識しているのは俺だけだ」: Sousuke: “But... I’m the only one who recognized it as a locked-room murder.”
部屋を出るとき、俺は鍵を開けたが――そのままにして出てきてしまった。: When I left the room, I unlocked the door—but I left it like that.
後から警察か憲兵が踏み込んだときには、ただの殺人事件と判断しただろう。: When the police or the Military Police raided the place later, they must have judged it to be just an ordinary murder.
そうなると――おかしな処が見つかる。: If that’s the case—I find something strange.
奏介「……どうして連中は、俺が犯人だと即断できたんだ？」: Sousuke: “...Why were they able to immediately conclude that I was the culprit?”
久遠「其所に奏介が潜んで居たと知っていたからでしょうね」: Kuon: “Because they knew you were hiding there, most likely.”
いくら隠れ家にして居たとはいえ、そこに住んでいた者の素性など、官憲が調べればすぐ: No matter how much of a hideout it was, the authorities could easily find out the identity of the person living there if they investigated.
久遠「――今は密室の謎を解く方が先決でしょう」: Kuon: “—Solving the mystery of the locked room should be the priority right now.”
客観的な証拠にはならないが、俺が認識している現場は紛うことなく密室だったのだ。: It wouldn't be objective evidence, but the scene as I recognized it was undeniably a locked room.
部屋の外から鍵を開ける方法――もしくは、鍵を掛ける方法。: A way to unlock the door from outside—or perhaps a way to lock it.
人差し指を口唇にあて、思わせぶりに久遠は呟いた。: Placing her index finger against her lips, Qwon muttered suggestively.
久遠「例えば――そうですね、可能性のひとつとして考えられる事をお話ししてみましょ: Kuon: “For example—well, let me tell you one of the possibilities I’ve considered.
久遠「奏介がその部屋にいたことを知っていて、なおかつ――被害者に殺意を抱いていた: Kuon: “A person who knew you were in that room, and furthermore—harbored murderous intent toward the victim—”
俺が嵌められたのかとばかり思っていたが、なるほど、そういう可能性もあるのか。: I’d thought all along that I was being framed, but I see, there’s that possibility too.
久遠「――橘さんはどうでしょう。奏介に懸想していて、被害者を疎ましく思っていれば: Kuon: “—What about Tachibana-san? If she harbors feelings for you and found the victim to be a nuisance—the possibility is sufficient.”
久遠「奏介となら何時でも逢えますし、鍵も何時だって戻せます」: Kuon: “She can meet you whenever she wants, and she can return the key whenever she wants.”
静かな怒りをこめて俺は声を発した。: I spoke with quiet anger in my voice.
奏介「あいつは綾音が死んだと聞いて、本気で悲しんでいた。それが……芝居だったとは: Sousuke: “She was truly grieving when she heard Ayane was dead. I can't believe... that was an act.”
泣きながら俺の胸を叩いていた美華夏。: Mikage, crying and pounding on my chest.
奏介「それに……あいつが人を殺せるはずはない」: Sousuke: “Besides... there’s no way she could kill a person.”
そんな理不尽な事をする筈がない。: She would never do something so irrational.
久遠「――私はただ可能性を論じただけです」: Kuon: “—I was merely discussing a possibility.”
奏介「……そんな可能性は認めねえ」: Sousuke: “...I won't accept such a possibility.”
久遠の言い種に声を荒げる。: I raised my voice at Qwon’s remark.
久遠「ええ、そうです。自分以外の真犯人を見つけると云う事は、則ち他者に罪を被せる: Kuon: “Yes, exactly. Finding a true culprit other than yourself means, in effect, pinning the crime on another.”
久遠「知人が犯人だと云う可能性を排除してしまうのは、身勝手で非合理的、真実から遠: Kuon: “Excluding the possibility that an acquaintance is the culprit is a selfish, irrational, and foolish move that may distance you from the truth.”
蕩々と語る久遠を睨み付けるが、彼女はしっかりとした目線で見つめ返してきた。: I glared at Qwon as she spoke at length, but she met my gaze with a firm look of her own.
迷いのない真っ直ぐな視線を俺は睨み返す事が出来なかった。: I couldn't stare back at those straight, unwavering eyes.
久遠「知人を含む他者に罪を擦り付けるのが厭ならば、このまま自らが罪を被るべきです: Kuon: “If you are loath to pin the crime on others, including acquaintances, then you should simply take the blame yourself.”
奏介「……俺はそこまで殊勝な人間じゃねえよ」: Sousuke: “...I’m not that noble of a person.”
――そうは云いつつも、やはり身内に罪を着せるのは抵抗がある。: —Even as I said that, I still felt a resistance to pinning the crime on someone close to me.
久遠の云う通り、もしも犯人が知っている人間だとしたら――俺はどう振る舞えるだろう: As Qwon said, if the culprit was someone I knew—how would I be able to act?
久遠「それと、もうひとつ――奏介は二度、事件に巻き込まれています」: Kuon: “And one more thing—you have been involved in two incidents.”
久遠「彼女が殺された件だけでなく、その前――全ての切欠となった事件――芸妓殺しの: Kuon: “Not just the murder of that girl, but the incident before that—the one that started everything—the murder of the geisha. You must consider that as well.”
忘れていたと云ってしまえば聞こえは悪いが、正直綾音の件に囚われすぎていて気が回ら: It sounds bad to say I’d forgotten, but honestly, I was so preoccupied with Ayane’s case that I hadn't given it much thought.
その事件だって人が死んでいるのに、優先順位を下げてしまっていた。: Even though a person died in that incident, too, I’d lowered its priority.
奏介「……やっぱり身勝手か」: Sousuke: “...I guess I am selfish after all.”
久遠「――自分本位で物事を考えるのが悪いとは云いません。むしろそれが普通です。問: Kuon: “—I do not say that thinking of things from a self-centered perspective is bad. In fact, it is only normal. The problem is when you think in a way that involves others.”
久遠「私も――そのような考え方をする時が多々あります」: Kuon: “I, too—often think in such a way.”
久遠はそう小声で呟くと腰を浮かせた。: Qwon muttered in a small voice and stood up.
久遠「眠りましょう。答えの出ない問いを繰り返すことに意味はありません」: Kuon: “Let us sleep. There is no meaning in repeating questions with no answers.”
それでも――必ず答えを出さなくてはならないのだ。: Even so—I must find the answer.
まだ答えは見つからない。: I still haven't found the answer.
朝陽が貌に当たり、微睡みから目覚める。: The morning sun hit my face, and I woke from my slumber.
夏にしては涼しげな朝の空気に、もう少し惰眠を貪る事にする。: The morning air was cool for summer, so I decided to indulge in a bit more sleep.
まあ――俺はまだ眠ろう。久遠が起きていようといまいと関係ない。: Well—I’m going to keep sleeping. It doesn't matter if Qwon is awake or not.
たとえすぐ隣に美華夏が居ようと――: Even if Mikage were right there next to me—
……どうもまだ脳が活性化されていないようだ。喋っている事の半分も理解出来ていない: ...My brain didn't seem to be active yet. I couldn't even understand half of what they were saying.
久遠「貴女は物事を複雑に捕らえ過ぎています。事象を単純化する事で見えてくるものも: Kuon: “You are making things far too complicated. There are things that become visible by simplifying the events.”
なにやらふたりで話し込んでいるようだ。: It seemed the two of them were deep in conversation.
久遠「例えば――そこで奏介は狸寝入りをしています。それは単純に起きるのが面倒臭い: Kuon: “For example—right there, Sousuke-san is pretending to be asleep. Is it simply because he is too lazy to get up, or is he eavesdropping on our conversation—?”
久遠「それとももっと複雑な――男性特有の朝の生理現象をもよおしているのか――」: Kuon: “Or perhaps it is more complex—he is experiencing the morning physiological phenomenon unique to males—”
耐え切れなくなり、俺は声を発した。: Unable to take it any longer, I spoke up.
奏介「黙って聞いてりゃ勝手な事抜かしやがって――」: Sousuke: “If you’re just going to sit there and talk nonsense while I’m trying to listen—”
身体を起こし、薄い掛け毛布を腰に巻き付ける。: I sat up and wrapped a thin duvet around my waist.
久遠「恥ずかしがる事はありません。朝勃ちは健康の証ですから」: Kuon: “There is no need to be embarrassed. A morning erection is a sign of good health.”
奏介「……淡々と解説される事が恥ずかしいんだよ」: Sousuke: “...Being explained to indifferently like that is what’s embarrassing.”
久遠「……朝から元気ですね。おはようございます」: Kuon: “...You are quite energetic this morning. Good morning.”
久遠は俺の下半身に向かって挨拶をしやがった。: Qwon actually addressed her greeting to my lower body.
美華夏の低い声。貌は真っ赤に染まっているが、少し怖い。: Mikage’s voice was low. Her face was bright red, but she looked a little scary.
久遠「――私が居てはひとりで処理するのも大変でしょうが、夢精だけはしないでくださ: Kuon: “—It must be difficult to handle it yourself while I am here, but please do not have a nocturnal emission. Washing the sheets is a chore.”
奏介「……しねえよ。何の話をしてたんだ、いったい……」: Sousuke: “...I won't. What the hell were you talking about, anyway...?”
久遠「奏介の性欲の処理方法を話していました」: Kuon: “We were discussing how to handle your sexual desire, Sousuke-san.”
美華夏「違うわよっ！ 少しだけ現場の状況が掴めたからそれを伝えにっ！！」: Mikana: “No, we weren't! I found out a bit about the situation at the scene, so I came to tell you!!”
耳たぶまで赤くなりながら美華夏は怒鳴った。: Mikage shouted, her ears turning red.
美華夏「……ええ。あの日の朝、住人からの通報によって後楽園近くの憲兵詰所から憲兵: Mikana: “...Yes. That morning, it seems the Military Police were dispatched from the MP station near Korakuen following a report from a resident.”
後楽園近く――なるほど、あの日の朝に電車通りを歩いていた時にすれ違ったトラックは: Near Korakuen—I see. The truck I passed while walking on the tram street that morning must have been them rushing to the scene.
美華夏「でもね、アパートの人に話を聞いてみたら、誰も通報なんてしていないのよ。そ: Mikana: “But, when I talked to the people at the apartment, nobody had made a report. In fact, they didn't even seem to know you were living in that room.”
久遠「――真犯人が通報したという事ですか」: Kuon: “—Does that mean the true culprit made the report?”
美華夏「その可能性は充分にあるわ。奏介に――罪を被せるために」: Mikana: “The possibility is high. To—to pin the crime on Sousuke.”
奏介「しかし……もしも俺が捕まっていたとしたら、おかしな処が出てくるんじゃないの: Sousuke: “But... if I’d been caught, wouldn't there be something strange?”
あの朝、俺が起きるのが遅れ、憲兵隊が部屋に踏み込んできて逮捕されていたら、それこ: If I’d been late getting up that morning and the Military Police had raided the room and arrested me, then nobody would have been able to make the report.
その場合、現場は密室で、俺が犯人である事は明白になってしまうのだが――: In that case, the room would be a locked room, and it would be clear that I was the culprit—but...
久遠「多少の齟齬があったとしても、今の官憲ではそれを黙殺してしまうでしょうね」: Kuon: “Even if there were some discrepancies, the authorities would likely just ignore them.”
久遠「事件があり、犯人を確保できた以上、目撃者の通報の信憑性などは取るに足らない: Kuon: “Once there is an incident and a culprit has been secured, the credibility of the witness’s report is a trivial matter.”
美華夏「あと……部屋の中にあった鍵とナイフから指紋が検出されて……先日の芸妓殺し: Mikana: “And... fingerprints were detected on the key and the knife that were in the room... and apparently they matched the fingerprints left on the weapon in the geisha murder the other day...”
久遠「奏介が犯人であることは目撃者の情報で明らかになっていたはずです。指紋が一致: Kuon: “The fact that Sousuke-san was the culprit should have been clear from the witness information. Saying he is the culprit because the fingerprints matched is getting the order wrong.”
美華夏「前回の事件と同じ指紋だったからこそ、次の事件も奏介の犯行だと云えたんじゃ: Mikana: “Wouldn't the fact that they were the same fingerprints as the previous case be why they could say the next case was also Sousuke’s doing?”
久遠「それにしては、奏介を犯人と断定するのは早すぎました。部屋の主が奏介だと判り: Kuon: “If that was the case, they concluded that Sousuke-san was the culprit far too quickly. They must have found out the occupant of the room was Sousuke-san and issued an emergency alert.”
美華夏「そっか……確かに、あの日のお昼前には指名手配はされていた……」: Mikana: “I see... it’s true, the alert was out before noon that day...”
腕を組み、頤に指をあて考え込む美華夏。: Mikage crossed her arms and leaned her chin on her finger, deep in thought.
奏介「指紋云々は後付の理由――か」: Sousuke: “So the fingerprints were an afterthought—huh.”
そう考えると、さらに憲兵隊――軍による陰謀説が有力になってくるが――: If you think about it that way, the theory of a conspiracy by the Military Police—the military—becomes even more likely, but—
美華夏が今何か重要なことを言わなかったか？: Didn't Mikage just say something important?
指紋が検出されたのは、ナイフと――: The fingerprints were detected on the knife and—
奏介「美華夏……鍵もあったと言ったな？」: Sousuke: “Mikage... you said there was a key, too?”
慌ててポケットを探る。幾許かの小銭に混じって、真鍮製の鍵が出てきた。: I hurriedly searched my pocket. Mixed in with a few coins, a brass key came out.
奏介「いや……こいつは……バーの鍵だ」: Sousuke: “No... this is... the key to the bar.”
千花から預かったバーの合鍵だ。: It was the spare key to the bar I’d received from Chika.
アパートの鍵は――何処に行った？: Where did the key to the apartment go?
奏介「……あの部屋の錠は、内側からは鍵を使わなくても開閉できる」: Sousuke: “...The lock to that room can be opened and closed from the inside without a key.”
俺が部屋を出るときも、鍵は使わなかった。: When I left the room, I didn't use a key.
戸締まりが出来るほど余裕もなかった。: I didn't have the composure to lock up.
奏介「鍵を部屋に忘れていた――？」: Sousuke: “Did I leave the key in the room—?”
久遠「奏介が鍵を所持していなかったのなら、話は簡単です。真犯人は被害者の殺害後、: Kuon: “If you did not have the key, Sousuke-san, the story is simple. After killing the victim, the true culprit took the key, left the room and locked it, then returned the key while pretending to be the one who discovered the body.”
美華夏「でもそれだと、侵入方法に無理があるんじゃないかしら？そのときはまだ鍵を持: Mikana: “But wouldn't that make the method of entry impossible? They wouldn't have had the key at that time...”
久遠「……奏介がまめに戸締まりをするとは思えません」: Kuon: “...I do not believe Sousuke-san is the type to diligently lock his doors.”
美華夏「そういえば……私が最初に部屋へ行った時も鍵は開いていたわね……」: Mikana: “Now that you mention it... the door was unlocked when I first went to his room, too...”
美華夏「……だとしたら単なる物盗りの可能性だって……」: Mikana: “...If that’s the case, there’s even the possibility of a simple robbery...”
奏介「いや――それはねえな。物盗りだとしたら、俺を生かしておく意味がない。野郎は: Sousuke: “No—not a chance. If it were a robbery, there would have been no reason to leave me alive. He would have killed me quickly—and taken the girl.”
久遠「奏介を陥れると云う目的でもない限り、確かに不可解な状況ですからね」: Kuon: “Unless the goal was to frame Sousuke-san, it is certainly an inexplicable situation.”
美華夏「じゃあ、鍵が開いているのを知って……なおかつ、そこに綾音さんも居ると判っ: Mikana: “In that case, someone knew the door was unlocked... and furthermore, they knew Ayane-san was there...?”
久遠「――そして、側に居た奏介に気付かれることなく人を殺す事の出来る人物――」: Kuon: “—And someone capable of killing a person without being noticed by Sousuke-san, who was right there beside them—”
あまりにも怪しすぎる人物が知っている範囲にひとりいるじゃないか――: Isn't there one person who fits that description perfectly among the people I know—?
俺があのアパートに居た事も知っている。: He knows I was in that apartment.
俺を見張っていただろうから、綾音の存在も知られている可能性が高い。: He must have been watching me, so there’s a high probability he knew about Ayane’s existence, too.
そしてなにより――軍との関わりも深い。: And above all—he has deep ties to the military.
奏介「……俺が芸妓殺しの容疑を掛けられた時に匿ってくれた人だ。借金取りの元締めみ: Sousuke: “...The person who hid me when I was suspected of killing the geisha. He does something like working as a debt collector—and he hinted at ties with the military.”
芸妓殺しの時に匿ったのも、今では怪しく感じられる。: Even him hiding me during the geisha murder feels suspicious now.
美華夏「戻ったら調べてみるわ」: Mikana: “I’ll look into it when I get back.”
奏介「ああ……《清鳳会》って組織の事も頼めるか？」: Sousuke: “Yeah... can you also check on an organization called Seihoukai?”
夜も更けて、表の人通りも少なくなってきた。: The night had deepened, and the foot traffic outside had grown sparse.
ひとりごち、腰を上げる。: Muttering to myself, I pushed myself up.
奏介「――ちょっと出かけてくる」: Sousuke: “—I’m heading out for a bit.”
相変わらず本を読んでいる久遠に告げる。: I directed the announcement toward Qwon, who was, as usual, buried in a book.
奏介「何処に行くのか聞かないのか？」: Sousuke: “Aren't you going to ask where I’m going?”
久遠「聞かずとも判ります。――確かめに行くのですね」: Kuon: “I do not need to ask to know. —You are going to confirm it, are you not?”
向かうは《清鳳会》の事務所。: My destination was the Seihoukai office.
連中――相馬葵が綾音の殺害に関わっているのか確かめなくてはならない。: I had to find out if those bastards—if Souma Aoi—were involved in Ayane’s murder.
そう簡単に口を割る事はないだろう。多少――強引にでも詰め寄らねばならない。: He wouldn't spill the truth easily. I’d have to corner him—even if things got a little rough.
手荒な方法――暴力を使うのも想定の範囲内だ。: Force—violence—was well within the realm of my expectations.
何時も懐に忍ばせていた折りたたみナイフは、もうないのだが――: Though the folding knife I usually kept tucked away was no longer with me—
まるで何でもない事――散歩に行くのを送り出すような気安さで久遠は声を掛けてきた。: Qwon sent me off with a casualness that made it sound like I was merely going for a stroll.
実際彼女には何ら関係ない事ではあるから、その気軽さも判る事なのだが。: In reality, it had nothing to do with her, so I suppose her lack of concern made sense.
人目に付かないよう、電車通りを避けて神楽坂までやってきた。: To avoid being seen, I stayed off the main tram lines and made my way toward Kagurazaka.
酔客は歩いているが、あたりに警察や憲兵の姿はない。: A few drunks were stumbling around, but there was no sign of the police or the Military Police.
バーへ行く事も考えたが、あまり寄り道をする訳にもいかない。: I considered stopping by the bar, but I couldn't afford any detours.
足早に細い路地へ向かう。: I quickened my pace as I ducked into a narrow alley.
《清鳳会》の事務所近くに人影は全く無かった。: There was not a soul to be seen near the Seihoukai office.
好都合――ではあるが、不安にもなる。何かあった場合、目撃される事はないが、助けて: It was convenient—yet it filled me with unease. If something happened, there would be no witnesses, but there would be no hope of rescue either.
武器になる物を持ってきていないのが非常に悔やまれる。: I deeply regretted not bringing anything that could serve as a weapon.
道端に転がる石でもポケットに忍ばせていくべきだろうか――: Perhaps I should have tucked a stone from the roadside into my pocket—
そこまで考えたが、やめておく事にする。感づかれたとき申し開きが出来ない。: The thought crossed my mind, but I dismissed it. I wouldn't be able to talk my way out of it if I were searched.
随分と臆病になったモンだ――情けねえ。: I’d become quite the coward—how pathetic.
自らを鼓舞するように一声呟き、《清鳳会》の事務所が入っているビルに入っていった。: Muttering a single phrase to bolster my courage, I stepped into the building that housed the Seihoukai office.
最初、部屋を間違えたのかと思った。: At first, I thought I’d walked into the wrong room.
だが――俺の記憶の中にある《清鳳会》の事務所は此処で間違いなかった。: But—the Seihoukai office in my memory was right here.
何もない部屋。いや――部屋と云うよりも単なるビルの中に出来た空間。: The room was empty. No—it was more like a hollow space within the building.
何時の間に引き払ったのか――: When the hell had they vacated—?
何かが残されてはいないだろうかと、ゆっくりと部屋を見渡す。: I slowly scanned the room, searching for any scrap they might have left behind.
期待に反して、紙屑ひとつ転がってやしなかった。: Contrary to my hopes, not even a single piece of waste paper was rolling across the floor.
完璧なまでに痕跡を消している。: They had erased every trace with clinical perfection.
怪しげな組織だったから、何かへまでもしでかして身を隠したのだろうか。: They were a suspicious bunch; perhaps they’d botched something and gone into hiding.
それはある意味俺には好都合だ。: In a sense, it was convenient for me.
俺が隠れなくてはならない理由のうちひとつが消える。: One of the reasons I had to hide had vanished.
まあそれでも、もうひとつの方が大きすぎるので、隠れている事にはかわりないのだが。: Well, the other reason was still far too massive, so it didn't change the fact that I had to remain a ghost.
奏介「……もう此処に居ても仕様が無えな……」: Sousuke: “...No point in staying here any longer...”
何も手懸かりとなりそうな物は見つからないだろう。: I wasn't going to find anything that could serve as a lead.
建物の入口近くに煙草の吸殻が落ちていた。: A cigarette butt lay near the entrance of the building.
結局何も判らなかった。: In the end, I found out nothing.
連中が何処へ行ったのか、何をしていたのか。: Where they went, what they were doing.
面倒が無くなったと素直に喜べればいいのだが、更なる面倒を抱え込みそうな予感がして: I wanted to be happy that one of my headaches was gone, but I had a premonition that I was about to stumble into an even bigger mess.
結局《清鳳会》の事務所が入っていた建物では何も見つからず、俺は軽い失望と疑念を抱: In the end, I found nothing in the building that housed the Seihoukai office, and I returned to the street nursing a mixture of slight disappointment and suspicion.
夜も更けている為、人通りはほとんど無い。俺にとっては好都合なのだが――: Since the night had grown so late, there were almost no pedestrians. It was convenient for me, but—
この時間になっても営業している屋台――あからさまに怪しい。: A food stall still operating at this hour—it was blatantly suspicious.
親父はじっとこちらを見ている。: The old man was staring intently at me.
付近に、他に客になりそうな人の姿はない。: There wasn't another soul around who looked like a potential customer.
まあ――俺には関係のない事だ。: Well—it had nothing to do with me.
親父がこちらを見ていようが、付き合ってやる義理もない。: It didn't matter if the old man was staring; I was under no obligation to entertain him.
……やや眉尻が下がったような気がする。: ...I felt like his brow twitched slightly.
気にしてはならない。: I shouldn't let it bother me.
こんな場面が官憲に見つかれば厄介だ。例え俺が清廉潔白な一市民だとしても捕縛されて: It would be trouble if the authorities caught me in a place like this. Even if I were a clean-handed, law-abiding citizen, they’d still haul me in.
俺は親父に背を向け、その場を足早に立ち去った。: I turned my back on the old man and hurried away.
親父を構う理由も義理も味も無いのだが――: I had no reason, duty, or taste for dealing with that old man—
一瞬、親父の千枚通しを操る腕の動きが止まった。: For a split second, the old man’s hands—expertly wielding his awls—stopped moving.
口の端を歪ませる親父。絶対に笑ったようには見えない。: The old man’s lips twisted. It didn't look like a smile in the slightest.
親父「……お前には関係ねえよ」: Old Man: “...None of your damn business, kid.”
儲かっているのなら、こんな時間まで店を開いていまい。: If it were good, he wouldn't be keeping his stall open until this ungodly hour.
親父「これだから若造は……年配者を労ろうって気がねえのかよ……」: Old Man: “This is what’s wrong with you youngsters... no respect for your elders, huh...?”
……俺にどうしろと云うのだ、この親父は。: ...What the hell does this old man want from me?
親父「……同情は要らねえよ……」: Old Man: “...I don't need your pity...”
そういいつつも、親父は程良い焼き加減のタコ焼きを包み始めた。: Even as he said it, the pops began to wrap up a serving of takoyaki that had been grilled to perfection.
明らかに大きく膨らんだ包みを持たされる。: He thrust a package into my hands that was visibly bulging.
十個どころの騒ぎじゃない。: This was a lot more than ten pieces.
親父「余っていたからな……おまけだよ」: Old Man: “I had some left over... it’s a bonus.”
奏介「……まあ、余るのも解る気がするがな……」: Sousuke: “...Well, I can see why you’d have leftovers...”
大人しく十銭支払い、包みを受け取る。: I quietly paid my ten sen and took the package.
陰気な親父の声を背中で聞き、俺はさっさと立ち去ることにした。: Leaving the gloomy old man’s voice behind me, I decided to make a quick exit.
寝間着姿の久遠が出迎える。: Qwon, dressed in her nightclothes, greeted me.
久遠はじっと俺の手に視線を注いでいる。: Qwon’s gaze was fixed intently on my hand.
買ってきた――むしろ買わされた――タコ焼きの包みを久遠に渡す。: I handed the package of takoyaki I’d bought—or rather, been forced to buy—to Qwon.
包みを開き、久遠が呟く。: Qwon muttered as she opened the wrapper.
奏介「人参は入ってねえぞ」: Sousuke: “There aren't any carrots in there.”
奏介「俺はいらねえから、全部食ってもいいぞ」: Sousuke: “I don’t want any, so you can eat the whole thing.”
久遠「そうですか。では――いただきます」: Kuon: “Is that so? Then—thank you for the meal.”
ぺこり、と頭を下げ、久遠はタコ焼きを摘んだ。: With a polite bow, Qwon plucked a piece of takoyaki.
久遠「なかなか……変わった感じですね。タコの弾力がアクセントとなっています」: Kuon: “It is... quite a curious sensation. The elasticity of the octopus provides a distinct accent.”
久遠「決して至高の美味と云う訳ではありませんけれど、吐き出してしまうような味では: Kuon: “I would not call it a supreme delicacy, but it is certainly not a flavor one would be compelled to spit out.”
……今回のはうまく出来たのだろうか。: ...I wonder if this batch was actually cooked right.
試しに、久遠が持つ包みからタコ焼きをひとつ摘んで口へ放り込んだ。: Just to test it, I plucked a piece from the package in Qwon’s hand and popped it into my mouth.
持ち帰るときに冷めてしまったのだろう、熱くはないが――: It must have cooled down on the way home; it wasn't hot, but—
奏介「……中まで焼けてないじゃねえか……」: Sousuke: “...The inside isn't even cooked through...”
焼けているのは皮一枚だけで、それが破れるとどろどろとした生地が口の中に広がってき: Only the outer skin was grilled; once that broke, the gooey, half-raw batter coated my mouth.
久遠「こういう食べ物ではなかったのですね」: Kuon: “So this is not how it is supposed to be.”
久遠は納得しながらも、またタコ焼きを口に入れた。: Qwon nodded as if satisfied, yet she popped another piece into her mouth anyway.
奏介「……腹壊すからやめておけ」: Sousuke: “...Stop it, you’ll get an upset stomach.”
そう言いはしたものの、暫くの間久遠はタコ焼きらしき物をずっと食べていた。: Despite my warning, Qwon continued to eat those things—which only vaguely resembled takoyaki—for some time.
そろそろ日付も変わりそうな時間にさしかかっていた。: The time was nearing the turn of the day.
眠るでもなく、ただ呆として寝転んでいた。: I wasn't sleeping; I was just sprawling there, staring vacantly.
相変わらず久遠は本を読んでいる。まあ――この家にそれ以外の暇の潰し方が無いので仕: Qwon was still buried in her book. Well—there was no other way to kill time in this house, so I couldn't blame her.
久遠「――そろそろ眠りましょうか」: Kuon: “—Perhaps it is time to sleep.”
起きている理由もない。: There was no reason to stay awake.
彼女が本を読み終えたのなら寝てしまっても良いだろう。: If she was finished reading, I might as well call it a night.
電灯を消そうと手を伸ばす。: I reached out to turn off the light.
時間からして美華夏や柚芭とは考えにくい。: Given the hour, it was unlikely to be Mikage or Yuzuha.
こそ泥という可能性も高いのだが――: There was a high probability it was a petty thief, but—
久遠「その方がいいでしょうね」: Kuon: “That would be for the best.”
階段を下り、古書店の跡へ足を踏み入れる。: I headed down the stairs and stepped into the remains of the bookstore.
本棚に囲まれるようにして、璃宝がぽつねんと突っ立っていた。: Riho was standing there, all alone, surrounded by the towering bookshelves.
目に生気がない。: There was no life in her eyes.
虚ろ気な、何も捉えていない眼差しで、璃宝はこちらを眺めている。: Her gaze was hollow, unfocused, as she stared in my direction.
解りきっている事を訊ねる。: I asked a question I already knew the answer to.
先日の出来事以来、精神状態をまともに保てなくなっているのだろう。人を殺してしまっ: Ever since the incident the other day, she must have been unable to maintain her sanity. She exuded the same aura as when she first came here, claiming she’d killed someone.
俺の姿を認めるなり、璃宝は床にへたり込んでしまった。: The moment she recognized me, Riho collapsed onto the floor.
駆け寄って抱え起こす。近くで見ると頬は痩け、一層窶れている印象を受けた。: I ran to her and pulled her up into my arms. Seeing her up close, her cheeks were sunken; she looked even more haggard than before.
肩を貸し、半ば抱え上げるようにして璃宝を連れて行く。: I let her lean on my shoulder and half-carried her up.
何も食べていないのか、やけに彼女の身体は軽く感じた。: Her body felt incredibly light, as if she hadn't eaten a thing.
階段を上がり、様子を窺っていたらしい久遠に声を掛ける。: I called out to Qwon, who had been watching the scene from the top of the stairs.
璃宝はゆっくりと俺から離れ――床に座り込む。: Riho slowly pulled away from me—and slumped onto the floor.
座るというよりは、崩れ落ちたという方がしっくり来る。: Rather than 'sitting,' it felt more like she had simply crumbled.
背中に手を添え、軽く撫でる。: I placed a hand on her back and stroked it gently.
目の下に浮かんだ隈を見て訊ねる。: I asked, looking at the dark circles under her eyes.
掠れた声で璃宝は洩らした。: Riho let the words out in a raspy voice.
璃宝「眠ったら……また、アタシがアタシじゃなくなるような気がして……怖いの……」: Riho: “If I sleep... I feel like I won’t be myself when I wake up... I’m scared...”
その恐怖は――俺と同じだ。: That fear—it was exactly like mine.
綾音が死に、暫くの間眠れなかった。: After Ayane died, I couldn't sleep for a while.
下手をするとまた誰かを殺してしまうかもしれない、其の不安が常に付き纏っていた。: The terror that I might end up killing someone else if I let my guard down was always looming over me.
カタカタと震える手で湯飲みを掴む。水が零れそうになり、手を添えて飲ませる。: She grabbed the cup with hands that shook uncontrollably. The water threatened to spill, so I steadied her hands as she drank.
璃宝の喉が動く。口角から溢れた水が伝っていく。: Riho’s throat worked. Water spilled from the corners of her mouth and trailed down.
空になった湯飲みを床に置く。: I set the empty cup on the floor.
璃宝「あんな目に遭っても……ちゃんと自分をしっかりと持っているもの」: Riho: “Even after going through something like that... you’ve kept a firm hold on yourself.”
俺だって、あの直後はボロボロだった。: I’d been a total wreck right after it happened.
それこそ先だっての璃宝と同じ――いや、さらに酷い状態だった。: I was in the same state Riho was in just now—no, probably even worse.
時間が経てば忘れられる――そんな事もなく、未だ綾音の死は俺を苛み続けていた。: It wasn't as if time made me forget; Ayane’s death still gnawed at me every single day.
僅かながらも俺が殺していないと云う可能性が出てきたからこそ、何とか我を取り戻せた: The only reason I’d managed to regain any sense of self was because of the slim possibility that I wasn't the one who killed her—
奏介「――お前が遭遇した事件だって……お前が殺したと決まった訳じゃねえだろ？」: Sousuke: “—Even the incident you were caught up in... it hasn't been decided that you were the killer, has it?”
俺と同じ事ならば。: If her situation was like mine.
璃宝の場合だって、濡衣――或いは思い込みと云う可能性も残されている筈だ。: In Riho’s case, there was surely a possibility that it was a frame-up—or a delusion.
璃宝「違う、そうじゃないの……アタシは、人を殺した事よりも、アタシが自分の意に沿: Riho: “No, it’s not that... It’s not about having killed someone. It’s that I’m terrified by the fact that I did something against my own will...”
璃宝「自分が自分じゃなくなる感覚……アンタならわかるでしょう？」: Riho: “The sensation of losing yourself... you understand, don’t you?”
自分が何をしたのかわからなくなる感覚。: The sensation of losing track of what you’ve done.
以前、借金取りの仕事中に豹変した男を思い出す。『俺は何もやっちゃいない』そう叫ん: I remembered the man who had suddenly changed during my job as a debt collector. *“I didn't do anything!”* he had screamed.
やはり――皆、同じ事に巻き込まれているのか。: Just as I thought—everyone is being caught up in the same thing.
璃宝「……アタシは、自由を奪われるのが何よりも厭なの」: Riho: “...More than anything, I loathe having my freedom taken away.”
自由を求めて東京――日本へ来た上海生まれの璃宝。: Riho was born in Shanghai and had come to Tokyo—to Japan—in search of freedom.
果たしてこの街に自由はあったのだろうか。: But had she truly found freedom in this city?
璃宝「アタシと……何処か遠くへ行かないかい？」: Riho: “Won’t you go... somewhere far away with me?”
璃宝「そう――遠く。此処から離れて、何処か遠くへ……逃げてくれたら――アタシ……: Riho: “Yes—far away. Away from here, somewhere distant... if you ran away with me... I...”
切れ長の目に涙を浮かべ、璃宝はそう懇願してくる。: Riho pleaded, her almond-shaped eyes brimming with tears.
俺は彼女の言葉に頷いていた。: I found myself nodding at her words.
このまま東京に残った処で、犯罪者同様の隠遁生活が続くのだ。: If I stayed here in Tokyo, I’d just be living the life of a hermit, no different from a criminal.
それなら何処か別の、俺たちの事を誰も知らないような土地へ行った方がいい。: It would be better to go somewhere else—some land where no one knew us.
それ以上に――この弱り果てている璃宝を見捨ててはおけなかった。: More than that—I couldn't abandon this broken, fragile Riho.
璃宝が数度瞬きした。その拍子に堪っていた涙が零れ落ちる。: Riho blinked a few times. With each blink, her accumulated tears spilled over and rolled down her cheeks.
彼女とはずっと曖昧な関係を築いてきたが、もういいだろう。: Our relationship had been vague for so long, but I supposed that was fine now.
逃げることは悪ではないし、そして何よりこの街は――綾音の影が濃すぎて、否応なしに: Running away wasn't a sin. And above all, this city—Ayane’s shadow was too thick here; it forced me to remember what I’d done.
俺を助けてくれた連中には申し訳ないが、これ以上迷惑を掛ける事はないと云うことで勘: I felt bad for the people who had helped me, but I hoped they’d forgive me for not wanting to cause them any more trouble.
奏介「――悪ぃな、久遠。勝手に決めちまって」: Sousuke: “—Sorry, Qwon. I’ve decided this on my own.”
久遠「いいえ。私は元々、単なる居候でしたから」: Kuon: “No. I was a mere guest from the start.”
奏介「……何処か行く宛はあるのか？ ないのなら――」: Sousuke: “...Do you have anywhere to go? If not—”
久遠「ご心配には及びません。宛など無くとも、生き延びる術は心得ていますから」: Kuon: “There is no need for concern. Even without a destination, I know how to survive.”
何時も通りの自信に満ちた口調ではあるのだが――: Her tone was full of its usual confidence—
俺には、ほんの少しだけ彼女が強がっているようにも聞こえた。: Yet, to my ears, she sounded like she was putting up a front.
奏介「――悪ぃ、璃宝。済まねえが今俺が逃げる訳にもいかねえ」: Sousuke: “—I’m sorry, Riho. I can't run away right now.”
ここで逃げてしまえば、これまで力を貸してくれた連中に申し訳が立たない。: If I ran now, I wouldn't be able to face the people who have helped me so far.
美華夏もマスターも、俺が無実だと信じていてくれている。: Mikage and the Master both believe I’m innocent.
ここで逃げるのは、彼らに対する裏切り行為だ。: Fleeing now would be an act of betrayal against them.
璃宝は笑う。今にも泣き出しそうな、痛々しい笑み。: Riho smiled. It was a painful, fragile smile that looked like it would shatter into tears at any moment.
だがその原因を与えたのは、他ならぬ俺なのだ。: But I was the one who had given her cause to smile that way.
璃宝「アンタは強いから。今までだってちゃんとしてこられたから……」: Riho: “Because you’re strong. You’ve always carried yourself well...”
璃宝「それなら――アタシはアタシで何とかするしかないわね」: Riho: “In that case—I’ll just have to figure something out on my own.”
璃宝「――いいのよ。それがアンタの決断なんだし」: Riho: “—It’s fine. That was your decision.”
奏介「――送っていくか？」: Sousuke: “—Want me to walk you back?”
璃宝が部屋の外へ出ようとしたので近づいて声を掛ける。: As Riho made a move to leave the room, I stepped toward her.
手を伸ばした一瞬――: In the moment I reached out—
余韻も残さず、璃宝はするりと俺の手を逃れて階段を下りていった。: Without leaving a trace of lingering warmth, Riho slipped from my hand and headed down the stairs.
表面だけの淡い接吻。: It was a shallow, surface-level kiss.
そういえば――璃宝と口付けを交わすのはこれが始めてではないだろうか。: Come to think of it—was that the first time I’d ever kissed Riho?
幾度となく肌を重ねてきているのに。: We’d shared our bodies countless times.
恋人と呼べるような関係ではなかったからだろうか。: Was it because our relationship was never something you’d call 'lovers'?
何か――胸騒ぎがする。: I felt... a sense of foreboding.
目隠ししていた手を退ける久遠。: Qwon lowered the hands she’d used to cover her eyes.
奏介「そうじゃねえよ。ちょっと――璃宝を送っていく事にするわ」: Sousuke: “It’s not that. I’m—I’m going to walk Riho home.”
久遠がいても問題はない。: It wouldn't be a problem if Qwon was there.
俺はただ、璃宝を自宅まで送り届けるだけだから。: I was simply going to see Riho to her front door.
そんな――色艶のある話もしようがない。: It wasn't as if anything romantic was going to happen.
久遠「――着替えますので、下で待っていてください。すぐに行きます」: Kuon: “—I shall go change. Please wait for me downstairs. I will be right there.”
本当は早く追いかけたいところではあるが――: I really wanted to chase after her as quickly as possible, but—
それだけ云うと、俺は階段を下りていった。: With that, I headed down the stairs.
夜道を歩いて来た璃宝は、神田川の橋まで来て欄干に軽く腰を下ろした。: Walking through the night, Riho reached the bridge over the Kanda River and sat lightly on the railing.
ここ数日の自身を取り巻く環境の変化に、身も心も疲れ果てていた。: The sudden changes in her environment over the last few days had left her body and soul completely spent.
特に心は――荒れ果て、擦り切れんばかりに弱りきっていた。: Her heart, in particular—it was ravaged, worn thin to the point of breaking.
ただ人を殺したかもしれないだけなのに――: To think that just because she might have killed someone—
璃宝は自分を人の死程度の事で動じる人間ではないと思いこんでいた。: Riho had convinced herself that she wasn't the type of person to be rattled by a single death.
物心付いた頃には父は居らず、母の手ひとつで育てられていた。: Her father was gone by the time she was old enough to remember, and she was raised by her mother’s hand alone.
父の記憶は全くない。ただ母からいつも父は日本人だったと言い聞かせられたのを覚え: She had no memory of her father at all. She only remembered her mother always telling her that he was Japanese.
それが真実だったのかは結局判らない。↓母はもう死んでしまったからだ。: In the end, she never found out if that was the truth. Because her mother was already dead.
四年前の正月、突然街が戦火に包まれた。: Four years ago, on New Year’s Day, the city was suddenly engulfed in the flames of war.
上海事変と呼ばれる抗争の始まりだった。: It was the beginning of the conflict known as the Shanghai Incident.
戦争の経緯など璃宝にとってはどうでもよかった。: The circumstances of the war didn't matter to Riho.
母が死に、頼れる者も居なくなった璃宝は、教えられた父方の名字――韮崎を頼りに、: With her mother dead and no one left to rely on, Riho followed the only lead she had—her paternal surname, Nirasaki—and crossed to Japan alone.
故郷が戦火に包まれ、自らの行動が制限されてしまうのを嫌ったのだ。: She loathed how her hometown had become a battlefield and how her own actions were being restricted.
横浜へ向かう貨物船に乗り込み、璃宝は日本へ密航した。: She boarded a cargo ship bound for Yokohama and smuggled herself into Japan.
はじめは散々だった。生きていく為には何でもやった。↓金を稼ぎ日本語を学ぶ為と、: At first, things were miserable. She did anything to survive. To earn money and learn Japanese, she shared her body with countless men.
悪い男に騙された事もあるが、それでもへこたれる事はなかった。↓自分が自分らしく: She was cheated by wicked men at times, but she never gave up. Riho always told herself that as long as she remained true to herself, everything would be fine.
＄ ――璃宝は何よりも自由である事を望んでいた。: Riho wanted to be free above all else.
璃宝は空を仰ぎ見た。昏い空に灰色の雲が流れている。↓雨が降りそうだ。: Riho looked up at the sky. Gray clouds were drifting across the dark expanse. It looked like it was about to rain.
確か――奏介と初めて逢った時も雨が降りしきる夜だった。: Come to think of it—the night she first met Sousuke was a night of pouring rain, too.
ひょんな事から知り合った男――当然身体を売った相手ではあったが――に誘われ、璃: Invited by a man she’d met by chance—a man she’d sold her body to, of course—Riho had started singing at a bar in Ginza.
歌う事は昔から好きだった。↓とりわけ決められた曲目ではなく、適当にリズムを取っ: She’d always loved to sing. Especially not the set pieces, but taking a rhythm and singing however she felt.
音を外すわけでもなく歌唱力は折紙付きだった璃宝だが、当然バー専属のピアノ弾きに: Her singing ability was guaranteed, never missing a note, but naturally, the bar’s exclusive pianist found her distasteful.
そんな時、ピアノを弾かせてくれと店に若い男がやってきた。: That was when a young man came to the shop, asking to play the piano.
最初璃宝は、彼がピアノ弾きとはとても思えなかった。↓ぼろぼろの服装に荒んだ目、: At first, Riho couldn't believe he was a pianist. His clothes were ragged, his eyes were hollow, and he was soaked to the bone from the passing rain.
バーの店長は彼を物乞いか何かだと思いこんで追い返そうとした。↓それを止め、何か: The bar manager assumed he was a beggar and tried to drive him away. It was Riho who stopped him and suggested letting the man play something.
彼はピアノの前に座るなり、譜面も何も見ずに演奏を始めた。: The moment he sat at the piano, he began to play without looking at a single sheet of music.
＄ ――透き通った音色の優しい曲だった。: It was a gentle song with a crystal-clear melody.
演奏を終えた彼に璃宝は何と云う曲なのか尋ねた。: When he finished, Riho asked him the name of the piece.
彼の返答は――適当に呼べばいい。: His response was—'Call it whatever you like.'
そこで初めて彼が作曲したのだと璃宝は気付いた。: It was then that Riho realized he had composed it himself.
璃宝は店長に彼を雇うよう提案した。↓同情などではなく、純粋に彼のピアノが素晴ら: Riho suggested to the manager that he hire him. It wasn't out of pity, but because she truly felt his piano was wonderful.
最後まで渋っていた店長だったが、雇わないのなら店を辞めるとまで言った璃宝の熱意: The manager hesitated until the very end, but pressed by Riho’s passion—she’d even threatened to quit if he didn't hire the man—he agreed to let him work at the bar.
奏介はよく璃宝の深酒に付き合ってくれた。↓あまり――いや、全然優しいとは云えな: Sousuke often accompanied Riho during her heavy drinking sessions. His attitude could hardly be called 'kind'—in fact, not at all—but he stayed by her side until dawn.
璃宝は何人もの男と寝てきたと云う事を話した。↓自慢にもならないが、隠しておく事: Riho told him about how she’d slept with many men. It wasn't something to be proud of, but it wasn't something to hide, either. It was just what she needed to do to live.
奏介は何時もと変わらない仕草――まるで鍵盤に触れる時のような柔らかい動きで璃宝: Sousuke pulled Riho’s body close with a familiar gesture—a movement as soft as when he touched the keys.
＄ そう――まるで、極めて弱い旋律のように――: Yes—as if he were playing an extremely faint melody.
服を脱いだ奏介の肌は傷だらけだった。↓喧嘩ばかりしていたのだろう。↓それはそれ: When he undressed, Sousuke’s skin was covered in scars. He must have been fighting constantly. Riho thought that was fine in its own way.
ただ赦せなかったのは、拳も傷だらけだった事だった。↓璃宝はピアノ弾きが指を労ら: The only thing she couldn't forgive was that his fists were also covered in scars. Riho scolded him, asking how a pianist could fail to look after his fingers.
彼は困ったように頬を掻きながら、気をつけるようにすると素っ気なく呟いた。: He scratched his cheek as if troubled and muttered curtly that he’d be careful.
あの時の奏介の貌を思い出し、璃宝は笑みを洩らした。: Remembering Sousuke’s face from back then, Riho let out a smile.
その後、演奏と歌を聴いた今のバーのマスターに誘われ、奏介共々璃宝は店を変えたの: After that, the Master of the current bar heard their performance and singing and invited them both to change shops.
後でマスターは笑いながら奏介はおまけでしかなかったと言っていたけれど、それでも: Later, the Master laughed and said Sousuke was merely an 'extra,' but Riho felt that he truly valued Sousuke.
だからこそ、奏介が殺人容疑を掛けられたときも心配して気に掛けていたのだろう。: That was why he’d been so worried and looked after Sousuke when he was suspected of murder.
璃宝も奏介が人を殺すとは思えなかった。: Riho, too, couldn't believe Sousuke would kill a person.
＄ 自分がいざその立場に置かれてしまうと、その信頼も揺らいでしまう。: Now that she found herself in that same position, her trust had begun to waver.
マスターが失踪してから、璃宝は千花に店の留守番を任せ、昔働いていたバーで歌って: After the Master disappeared, Riho left Chika to look after the shop and sang at the bar where she used to work.
生活があったし、歌うのが好きだったからだ。↓奏介が店に来なくなってから歌ってい: She had a life to live, and she loved to sing. Part of it was also because she hadn't sung since Sousuke stopped coming to the shop.
新橋駅近くにある外国人が集まるバーで歌っていた時だった。↓璃宝は突然眩暈に襲わ: She was singing at a bar near Shimbashi Station where foreigners gathered. Suddenly, Riho was hit by a wave of dizziness. She’d only had a tiny bit to drink, yet her consciousness suddenly cut out.
そして数瞬の後――璃宝は右手に包丁を握り締めていた。: And a few moments later—Riho was clutching a kitchen knife in her right hand.
店の中は阿鼻叫喚の地獄絵図と化していた。: The interior of the shop had transformed into a scene of carnage from hell.
一面に鮮血が飛び散り、切り刻まれた人間が床をのたうち回っている。: Fresh blood splattered everywhere, and mangled people were writhing on the floor.
目の前に肉塊が転がっている。↓見覚えのある衣装。↓ここへ来た時に身の回りの世話: A lump of flesh lay before her eyes. An outfit she recognized. It was the body of the girl who had looked after her since she’d come here.
ひくひくと痙攣し、少女の目が璃宝を捉えた。↓何も映っていないだろう瞳に、璃宝は: The girl’s eyes caught Riho’s as she convulsed. In those eyes, which surely reflected nothing, Riho saw her own blood-stained reflection.
包丁を取り落とし、璃宝はその場から逃げ出した。: She dropped the knife and fled the scene.
――人を殺したのは初めてだった。: —It was the first time she’d killed a person.
ただ、それだけではここまで落ちなかっただろう。: However, she wouldn't have fallen this far just from that.
生き延びる為には仕方の無かった事だろう。: It was likely something she had no choice but to do to survive.
＄ ――赦せないのは、自分がやったのにも拘わらず、全くその間の記憶がない事だった: What she couldn't forgive was the fact that despite having done it, she had absolutely no memory of the interval.
夢遊病者のように包丁を振るったのだろうか。: Had she swung the knife like a sleepwalker?
薬物中毒者のように暴れたのだろうか。: Had she gone on a rampage like a drug addict?
――どちらにせよ、璃宝の意に沿った行動ではなかった。: —Either way, it was not an action that accorded with Riho’s will.
自由を奪われ、意に沿わぬ事を強制されたと感じた。: She felt as though her freedom had been stolen and she had been forced to do something against her will.
操られた事。: The fact that she was manipulated.
そんな事、認めたくはないのに。: She didn't want to admit such a thing.
奏介と同じような境遇に置かれて解ったことがあった。: Having been placed in the same situation as Sousuke, there was something she now understood.
奏介はずっと否定し続けていたし、そう璃宝も信じていたが――彼は本当に人を殺した: Sousuke had continued to deny it, and Riho had believed him—but had he really killed someone?
同じような状態ならば――充分に有り得る事だ。: If he were in the same state—it was entirely possible.
璃宝は振り返り、川の方を眺めた。: Riho turned and looked toward the river.
川岸に見えるのは鉄道のお茶の水駅。↓すでに夜も遅く、電車も走っていないのか暗く: On the riverbank, she could see Ochanomizu Station. It was already late, and the trains weren't running; it was sunken in darkness.
外へ出て暫くすると、久遠が二階から降りてきた。: A short while after I went outside, Qwon came down from the second floor.
久遠「――お待たせしました」: Kuon: “—I am sorry to have kept you waiting.”
目の前の道――千駄木の団子坂は東西に延びている。: The road in front of us—Dangozaka in Sendagi—stretched east to west.
西に坂を上れば賑わっている駒込肴町から帝大前に出る。東はすぐに根津の電車通りで、: If you head west up the slope, you come out at Teidai-mae from the bustling Komagome Sakanacho. To the east is the Nezu tram street, which leads to Ueno’s Shinobazu Pond.
奏介「璃宝がどっちに行ったか、だな……」: Sousuke: “The question is which way Riho went...”
久遠「あまり深く考える必要はないと思います。彼女も官憲に追われている以上、市電を: Kuon: “I do not believe there is a need to think too deeply. Since she is also being pursued by the authorities, it is unlikely she would use the city tram.”
久遠「後は彼女が帰る場所さえ判ればいいのですが――」: Kuon: “If only we knew where she was returning to—”
奏介「……青山の同潤会アパートだろう」: Sousuke: “...It’s likely the Doujunka Apartments in Aoyama.”
確かそこに璃宝は住んでいた筈だ。: I was certain that was where Riho lived.
だが――今も其処に居られているかは判らない。: However—I didn't know if she could still stay there.
奏介「……まずは帝大の前を通って湯島――神田川まで出るか」: Sousuke: “...First, we’ll pass by Teidai and head toward Yushima—the Kanda River.”
久遠の確認に無言で頷く。: I nodded silently at Qwon’s confirmation.
出来ればあそこには近寄りたくない。: If possible, I didn't want to go near that place.
湯島――お茶の水駅の近くには、綾音が死んだ文化アパートが建っているのだ。: Yushima—near Ochanomizu Station stood the Bunka Apartments, where Ayane had died.
終始早足で、俺達はようやく神田川までたどり着いた。: Keeping a brisk pace throughout, we finally reached the Kanda River.
息を切らしている久遠を気遣う。: I asked, concerned for the out-of-breath Qwon.
身体を動かしているようには見えないから、さすがに此処まで早足で来るのは辛かったろ: She didn't look like the type to get much exercise, so coming this far at such a fast pace must have been grueling.
久遠「いえ……大丈夫です。足手纏いになってしまい申し訳ありませんでした」: Kuon: “No... I am fine. I apologize for being a burden.”
此処に来るまでに璃宝の姿は見えなかった。: We hadn't seen a sign of Riho on the way here.
辺りに人影はない。御茶ノ水の駅も照明を落としている。: There wasn't a soul around. The lights at Ochanomizu Station were dimmed.
もう先に行ってしまったのか、それとも――: Had she already passed through, or—
久遠が俺の袖を引き、川の方を指した。: Qwon tugged on my sleeve and pointed toward the river.
欄干の上に璃宝の姿があった。: Riho was there, perched on the railing.
その声を聞いてしまい、俺は駆け寄ろうとした脚を止めてしまった。: Hearing her voice, I stopped in my tracks just as I was about to run to her.
これ以上近づいたら――危険だ。: If I went any closer—it would be dangerous.
奏介「……降りてこっちに来いよ」: Sousuke: “...Get down and come over here.”
何かあればすぐに飛びつける距離を意識しつつ、璃宝に呼びかける。: I called out to Riho, mindful of a distance from which I could spring at a moment’s notice.
璃宝「悩まないで――此処に来てすぐ落ちれば……アンタにこんな無様な姿見せずに済ん: Riho: “If I hadn't hesitated—if I’d just fallen the moment I got here... I wouldn't have had to show you such a pathetic sight.”
璃宝は微笑む。全てを諦めきった空虚な笑み。: Riho smiled. It was a hollow smile of total resignation.
声がうまく出せない。: My voice wouldn't come out right.
璃宝「アンタは強いから……そう言えるのよ」: Riho: “You can say that... because you’re strong.”
とん、と璃宝の足が欄干から離れ、軽くステップを踏む。: With a soft thud, Riho’s feet left the railing as she performed a light step.
璃宝「――自由であり続ける事を、唯一の誇りにしていたから」: Riho: “—Because my only pride was continuing to be free.”
璃宝「アタシがアタシじゃ無くなったから……アタシは――消える」: Riho: “Because I’m no longer myself... I—will vanish.”
璃宝の動きが止まり、こちらを向く。: Riho’s movements stopped, and she turned toward me.
彼女は、極上の笑みを浮かべて。: She wore a supreme, exquisite smile.
指先が触れるか触れないか――: My fingertips may or may not have brushed her—
璃宝の身体が欄干の向こうに消えた。: Riho’s body vanished over the railing.
欄干へ駆け寄り、身を乗り出して下を覗き込む。: I rushed to the railing and leaned over, peering down.
川は暗く、何も見えない。: The river was dark; I couldn't see a thing.
川縁に降りようとしたが、此処からでは降りられない。: I tried to find a way down to the riverbank, but there was no way from here.
降りられる処は――と辺りを見回し、向こうにあるアーチ橋に人だかりが出来ている事に: Searching for a place to descend, I noticed a crowd gathering on the arch bridge further down.
しきりに川面を指差している。微かにだが、警察を――と云う声も風に乗って聞こえてき: They were pointing frantically at the river's surface. Faintly, the cry for "the police!" drifted to me on the wind.
もう一度川を――璃宝が落ちたと思わしき箇所を見る。川縁の積石が崩れ、何か染みが―: I looked at the river again—at the spot where Riho had likely fallen. The stones along the bank were crumbled, and there was a stain—what looked like splattered blood.
もし頭を打っていたとしたら――いくら高さのない橋の上から落ちたとしても助からない: If she’d hit her head—she wouldn't survive, no matter how low the bridge was. And if she’d been swept away by the river—
こちらの橋も人が増えてきた。ちらほらと派出所から来たと思しき警官の姿も見える。: The crowd on this bridge was growing, too. I could see the figures of policemen who had likely come from a nearby post.
引き上げ時――だろうか。璃宝を助けに行くには衆人環視がありすぎる。: Time to leave. There were too many eyes watching for me to try to rescue Riho.
最悪だ――彼女の無事よりも自らの身の安全を優先しようとしている。: It was the worst—I was prioritizing my own safety over her life.
先程から黙ったままの久遠を呼ぶ。: I called out to Qwon, who had been silent for some time.
俯き加減で久遠は呆然としていた。明らかに普段の彼女とは様子が違う。: Qwon was staring blankly, head bowed. She was clearly not her usual self.
奏介「チッ――とにかく、ずらかるぞ」: Sousuke: “Tch—anyway, let’s get out of here.”
無反応の久遠の手を取り、この場所から離れる事にした。: I took the hand of the unresponsive Qwon and decided to leave this place.
呆然としたままの久遠を抱えるようにして、俺は古書店まで引き返してきた。: Supporting the dazed Qwon, I made my way back to the bookstore.
目の前に居て、助けられる筈だったのに、璃宝は落ちてしまった。: She was right there in front of me, I should have been able to save her, yet Riho fell.
あの高さなら死ぬことはないと考えるのは都合の良い思い込みだ。: Convincing myself she wouldn't die from that height was just a convenient delusion.
川岸の石垣にだって血のようなものが見えたではないか。: I saw what looked like blood on the stone walls of the riverbank.
また――俺の周りで人が死ぬ。: Once again—someone near me has died.
呆としたまま佇んでいる久遠に声を掛ける。: I spoke to Qwon, who was standing there in a daze.
ぴくん、と久遠の身体が動く。: Qwon’s body gave a sharp twitch.
明らかにいつもとは違い、様子がおかしい。: She was clearly different from usual; something was wrong.
やはり、目の前で衝撃的な事を見てしまったからだろうか。: It must be because she’d seen something so shocking right before her eyes.
奏介「……まだ死んだと決まったわけじゃねえ」: Sousuke: “...It hasn't been decided that she’s dead yet.”
久遠の言葉を否定し、自分にも言い聞かせる。: I denied Qwon’s words, trying to convince myself as well.
奏介「それに……お前は何も関係ない」: Sousuke: “Besides... you had nothing to do with it.”
奏介「人を狂わせる機械を作ったとか……そういうのはお前の落ち度じゃない」: Sousuke: “Making a machine to drive people mad... that wasn't your fault.”
目の前で見てしまったからか、久遠は頑なに言い続ける。: Perhaps because she’d seen it happen, Qwon remained stubbornly insistent.
奏介「巻き込んだのは……俺だ」: Sousuke: “I’m the one... who dragged you into this.”
久遠「原因を作ったのは私です」: Kuon: “I am the one who created the cause.”
これでは平行線のままだ。: We were just talking in circles.
久遠「今は……彼女の無事を祈る事、それ以外には何も出来ません」: Kuon: “Right now... we can only pray for her safety. There is nothing else we can do.”
彼女が無力だとしたら、俺には何も残されなくなってしまう。: If she was powerless, then I had nothing left.
久遠の手が俺の服の襟を握り締める。: Qwon’s hands gripped the collar of my clothes.
久遠「私を――抱いてくださいませんか？」: Kuon: “Will you—will you not hold me?”
――それは、俺が聞いた中で初めて彼女が発した懇願だった。: —It was the first time I had ever heard a plea escape her lips.
久遠の肩に手を掛け、引き離そうとする。: I placed my hands on Qwon’s shoulders, trying to pull away.
とてもじゃないが、そんな気分にはなれない。: There was no way I was in the mood for that.
久遠は俺の手を取り、自らの胸に添えさせる。: Qwon took my hand and pressed it against her chest.
熱い体温と、早い鼓動が伝わってくる。: A hot body temperature and a rapid heartbeat traveled through to me.
久遠「抱いてくだされば――私は戻れますから」: Kuon: “If you hold me—I believe I can return.”
体重が感じられないほど軽い久遠の身体を抱き上げ、布団の上に横たえる。: I scooped up Qwon’s body, which felt so light it was as if she had no weight at all, and laid her down on the futon.
これからする事の意味すら解っていないような、あどけなさで見つめてくる。: She gazed at me with an innocence that made it seem as if she didn't even understand the meaning of what was about to happen.
だが誘ったのは――彼女からだ。: But she was the one who had invited this.
頬に手を添える。: I placed my hand against her cheek.
その仕草が新鮮で、俺は擽るように久遠の首筋を愛撫する。: That gesture felt fresh to me, so I began to caress the nape of her neck, teasing her.
奏介「俺はただ触っているだけだぞ？」: Sousuke: “I’m only touching you, you know?”
久遠「撫で方が……いやらしいです」: Kuon: “The way you stroke me... is lewd.”
奏介「……これからそう云う事をするんだからな」: Sousuke: “...Well, that’s exactly what we’re about to do.”
構わず撫で回す。: I continued to caress her, ignoring her protest.
顎に指を沿わせ、耳朶も軽く弄る。: I trailed my fingers along her jawline, lightly playing with her earlobe.
耳の産毛が乾いた肌の上で毛羽立つ。: The fine hairs on her ear stood up against her dry skin.
耳の穴も、耳朶の裏にも、髪の生え際の辺りにも指を這わせ、久遠の感じる処を探ろうと: I let my fingers wander into her ear, behind the lobe, and along her hairline, searching for her sensitive spots.
久遠「んふ……っ、奏介……弄り過ぎ、です……」: Kuon: “Mmm... Sousuke-san... you are playing with me too much...”
普段とは全く違う久遠の様子に、俺はこれ幸いとささやかな復讐がてら彼女を責め立てる: Seeing Qwon so different from her usual self, I took the opportunity to tease her further, a small act of revenge for her usual attitude.
顎の骨に沿って指を下げ、喉を擽りながら胸元のリボンをほどく。: I slid my fingers down the line of her jaw, tickling her throat as I untied the ribbon at her chest.
久遠が声を発する度に、喉がひくひくと蠢く。: Every time Qwon let out a sound, her throat twitched and pulsed.
ブラウスの釦を外していくと、久遠が少し非難がましく俺を見つめているのに気が付いた: As I undid the buttons of her blouse, I noticed her looking at me with a slightly reproachful gaze.
久遠「見ても……詰まらないと思いますけど」: Kuon: “I believe you will find it... quite boring to look at.”
奏介「……詰まるとか詰まらんと云う問題じゃねえよ」: Sousuke: “...It’s not a matter of it being boring or not.”
剥き出しになった首筋に指を這わせる。: I ran my fingers over her exposed neck.
久遠が小さく首を振る。: Qwon gave a tiny shake of her head.
奏介「……ま、そのうち解るようになるだろ」: Sousuke: “...Well, I’m sure you’ll find out soon enough.”
首筋から首の根本、鎖骨の付け根に指を動かす。: I moved my fingers from her neck down to the base of her throat, tracing her collarbone.
肉の薄い細い身体は、鎖骨の窪みもはっきりと現れている。: Her slender, thin frame made the hollows of her collarbone stand out sharply.
そしてそれは、胸の隆起にも云えた。: And the same could be said for the swell of her breasts.
