奏介「……お前、まさか……不法侵入でもしでかすつもりか？」: Sousuke: “...Are you seriously... planning on committing breaking and entering?”
琢磨「……正式な捜査許可が下りる筈ねーだろ」: Takuma: “...You think they'd ever give me an official search warrant for this?”
声を潜め、奴が耳打ちしてきた。: He lowered his voice and whispered into my ear.
琢磨「心配すんなよ。相馬葵を捕まえて自供させちまえばこっちのモンだ」: Takuma: “Don't sweat it. Once we snatch Aoi Souma and make her squawk, the game's ours.”
奏介「――刑事の言葉とも思えんな」: Sousuke: “—Hardly the words of a detective.”
琢磨「うるせーよ。……ま、御巫邸に相馬葵が確実に居るという保証は無いから、居なけ: Takuma: “Shut up. ...Besides, there’s no guarantee Aoi Souma is actually at the Mikanagi estate. If she’s out, we'll just scavenge for anything that looks like a lead.”
ますます警官とは思えない。: He sounded less and less like a cop by the second.
家捜しまでするとなれば、不法侵入に窃盗か。: Ransacking a house? That’s trespassing and theft.
いつの間にか隣に来るまで歩みを遅めていた琢磨が背中を叩いてくる。: Takuma, who had slowed his pace until he was walking beside me, gave me a heavy pat on the back.
琢磨「心配すんな。なるようになるさ」: Takuma: “Don't worry. Things have a way of working out.”
奏介「誰が手前ェの心配なんざするか」: Sousuke: “Who said I was worried about *you*?”
琢磨「――ま、何かあったとしても問題はねーよ。手は打ってあるからな」: Takuma: “—Well, even if something goes south, it won't be an issue. I’ve already made arrangements.”
琢磨「――ああ、そういや御巫邸の場所をまだ伝えていなかったな」: Takuma: “—Oh, right, I haven't actually told you where the Mikanagi estate is yet, have I?”
俺の声を無視して、琢磨は話を続ける。: Ignoring my question, Takuma kept right on talking.
琢磨「麻布にある氷川神社の近くだ。此処からだと赤坂に出て――いや、青山の方を少し: Takuma: “It’s near Hikawa Shrine in Azabu. From here, we'll head toward Akasaka—actually, taking the long way around through Aoyama is better.”
琢磨「その方が警邏の連中にも遭う事は少ないだろうし、時間も潰せる」: Takuma: “Fewer chances of bumping into patrols that way, and it kills some time.”
まあ――東京市の中心部を避けるのは得策だろうとは思うのだが。: Well—I supposed avoiding the heart of Tokyo City was a sound enough strategy.
奏介「……警官がお尋ね者と一緒に居るのはまずいか」: Sousuke: “...I guess it’s a bad look for a cop to be seen with a wanted man.”
琢磨「当たり前だ。もし気付かれた場合、お前を連行していると云う事にするか」: Takuma: “No shit. If anyone notices, I'll just say I'm hauling you in.”
奏介「ハッ――犯人に逃げられた間抜けな警官の方がしっくり来るだろうさ」: Sousuke: “Hah—the 'bumbling cop who let his suspect escape' narrative suits you a lot better.”
大都市にそぐわない鬱蒼とした森の中に、白い外壁の洋館がひっそりと佇んでいた。: Tucked away inside a forest so dense it felt out of place in the great metropolis, a Western-style mansion with white exterior walls stood in eerie silence.
奏介「ここが――久遠の家だった処か」: Sousuke: “So this is—where Qwon used to live.”
思ったよりも――いや、思った以上に大きな建物だ。美華夏の家もでかかったが、此処の: It was larger than I’d imagined—no, significantly larger. Mikage’s place was big, but it didn't even compare to this.
荒れ果てている――と云う事もなく、静かに昔日の姿を晒している。: It didn't look particularly dilapidated; it just sat there, exposing its former glory to the elements.
久遠が此処に住んでいた当時と変わらぬ姿なのだろうか。: Was this exactly how it looked when Qwon lived here?
窓から光は洩れてきていない。無人だからか、それともカーテンを閉めているのか。: No light spilled from the windows. Was it empty, or were the curtains just drawn tight?
頷き、足を踏み出した。: I nodded and took a step forward.
奏介「どこから侵入るつもりだ？」: Sousuke: “Where do you plan on breaking in?”
テラスまで来て、琢磨に問いかける。: We reached the terrace, and I threw the question at Takuma.
でかい扉の取っ手を慎重に捻り、琢磨は呟く。: Takuma murmured as he cautiously tried the handle of the massive door.
琢磨「――玖藤、鍵開けはできねーのか？」: Takuma: “—Kudou, you don't happen to know how to pick a lock, do you?”
奏介「……出来る訳ねえだろ」: Sousuke: “...How the hell would I know how to do that?”
奏介「そもそも警官の前でンな真似出来るか」: Sousuke: “Like I’d ever pull a stunt like that in front of a cop anyway.”
琢磨「警官が嗾けるのも問題だな」: Takuma: “And a cop instigating it is its own brand of trouble.”
琢磨「真性犯罪者に言われたくはねーな」: Takuma: “I don't want to hear that from a genuine criminal.”
琢磨「窓を破るしかねーか……」: Takuma: “Guess we've gotta smash a window...”
暗く内部の見えない窓を軽く叩く。硝子全体が揺れ、破るのはさほど難しくないように思: He lightly tapped on a pane that looked into the dark interior. The whole glass rattled; it didn't seem like it would be too hard to break.
ここを割れば――どう言い繕ったところで不法侵入罪確定だ。: If we shattered this—no matter how we tried to spin it, we’d be guilty of illegal entry.
奏介「やれやれ――犯罪の片棒を担がされる羽目になるとはね」: Sousuke: “Good grief—I never thought I'd end up as an accomplice to a crime.”
琢磨「……担がされているのは俺だ」: Takuma: “...*I’m* the one being made into an accomplice here.”
奏介「――俺は手前ェの指図に従ってるだけだ」: Sousuke: “—I’m just following your orders, Officer.”
その仕草に腹は立つが、今は啀み合っている場合でもない。: His attitude was irritating, but now wasn't the time for bickering.
奏介「――この辺の窓なら、割れば侵入れそうだな」: Sousuke: “—This window looks like a good spot to break in.”
大きめの窓――尚かつ表から目立たない――を選び、脚を振り上げる。: I picked a large window—one that was hidden from the street—and drew back my leg.
？？？「――おいコラ、他人の家で何してんだよ」: ???: “—Hey now, what do you think you’re doing to someone else’s house?”
暗闇から掛けられた声に振り返る。: I whirled around at the voices calling out from the darkness.
ついさっき俺等が来た方角から連中は現れた。: They had appeared from the same direction we’d just come from.
ハチ「――久し振り、だな」: Hachi: “—It’s been a while, hasn't it?”
ハチ「殺すんじゃねーよっ！！」: Hachi: “Don't go killing me off already!!”
いきなり気障な仮面をかなぐり捨てて叫ぶハチ。: Hachi screamed, suddenly ripping off his pretentious mask.
ハチ「ちょっと刃先が掠ったくれーで死ぬかよっ！」: Hachi: “You think I'd die just because the tip of your blade grazed me?!”
奏介「その割には死にそうなくらい痛がっていたがな」: Sousuke: “You certainly acted like you were dying from the pain, though.”
貌を紅潮させハチが怒鳴り散らす。: Hachi’s face turned bright red as he began to rant and rave.
クマ「ハチ、熱くなるな」: Kuma: “Hachi, don't get so worked up.”
ハチ「此奴等……なまらむかつく……」: Hachi: “These guys... they really piss me off...”
琢磨「なるほど。そいつは見過ごせないな」: Takuma: “I see. In that case, I can't just let them walk.”
ハチ「おいコラ玖藤。そいつ……誰だ？」: Hachi: “Hey, Kudou. Who... who is this guy?”
ハチ「デ――ッ！？ そ、そんなハッタリで僕等がびびるとでも――」: Hachi: “A de—?! Y-You think a bluff like that is gonna scare—?”
ハチ「後退りしながら言うんじゃねーよテメーはよおっ！！」: Hachi: “Don't you go saying that while you're backing away yourself, dammit!!”
奏介「……漫才はそのくらいにしてくれ。……葵は此処にいるのか？」: Sousuke: “...Can we end the comedy routine? ...Is Aoi inside?”
ハチ「ヘッ……教えられるとでも思っているのか？」: Hachi: “Heh... you really think we’d just tell you?”
奏介「……喋らないのなら吐かせるまでだ」: Sousuke: “...If you won't talk, I'll just have to beat it out of you.”
胸の前で指の関節を鳴らす。: I cracked my knuckles in front of my chest.
ハチ「お、おいっ！！ 刑事が見てんのに暴力を振るってもいいのかよ！？」: Hachi: “H-Hey!! Is it really okay for him to use violence right in front of a detective?!”
ハチが琢磨に同意を求める。: Hachi turned to Takuma, looking for agreement.
琢磨「俺は玖藤の意見に賛成だ」: Takuma: “I happen to agree with Kudou’s methods.”
ネクタイを弛め、身構える琢磨。: Takuma loosened his tie and squared his shoulders.
ハチ「こっ、公僕がそれでいいのかよ！？」: Hachi: “Th-That's how a public servant acts?!”
クマ「無駄だハチ。男なら――覚悟を決めろ」: Kuma: “It’s no use, Hachi. If you’re a man—steel yourself.”
琢磨「――そっちのデカブツは手応えがありそうだな」: Takuma: “—That big one looks like he might actually give me a decent workout.”
琢磨がクマの方へ向き直る。: Takuma turned his attention toward Kuma.
クマ「――男とは無駄な闘いをしないものだ」: Kuma: “—A real man does not engage in pointless fighting.”
何をしに来たのだ。: What did they even come here for?
ハチ「チッ――やるしか……ねーようだな」: Hachi: “Tch—looks like we've... got no choice.”
ふたりが身構える。: The two of them braced themselves.
琢磨「――やっとやる気になったか」: Takuma: “—Finally decided to grow a spine?”
奏介「――ま、肩肘張らずに軽く揉んでやるか」: Sousuke: “—Well, I might as well give you a light thrashing to loosen you up.”
拳を振り上げ、ハチがかかってくる。: Hachi swung his fist and charged.
あっさりと琢磨に殴り返され、ハチの身体が吹っ飛ぶ。: Takuma countered with a simple blow that sent Hachi flying.
奏介「――お前もやるか？」: Sousuke: “—You want a piece of this too?”
クマ「……ハチの手当が先だ」: Kuma: “...Tending to Hachi is my priority.”
ほぼ予想通り、クマは引き下がって白目をむいているハチを抱え起こした。: Almost exactly as I expected, Kuma backed off and scooped up the unconscious, white-eyed Hachi.
ハチ「へっ――姐さん相手だったらこうはいかねえぞ畜生……」: Hachi: “Heh—it wouldn't go like this if it were the Boss Lady you were fighting, dammit...”
奏介「……だから手前等は何をしに来たんだよ」: Sousuke: “...Seriously, what the hell did you two even come here for?”
息を吹き返したハチに琢磨が近づいていく。: Takuma approached Hachi as he regained his breath.
琢磨「お前等……ここの鍵は持っているのか？」: Takuma: “Do you punks... have the keys to this place?”
ハチ「ヘッ……持ってたってテメー等に渡す筈が――」: Hachi: “Heh... even if we did, why would we ever give them to—”
琢磨がハチの胸ぐらを掴む。: Takuma grabbed Hachi by the collar.
ハチがポケットから何かを取り出し、地面に放り投げた。: Hachi pulled something from his pocket and threw it to the ground.
ハチ「……鍵はどっかに落としちまった」: Hachi: “...I must have dropped the keys somewhere.”
俺は土の上に落ちた物――鍵を拾い上げた。: I picked up the object that had fallen onto the soil—the keys.
ハチ「……言っておくが、姐さんは僕等の比じゃねーぞ」: Hachi: “...Just so you know, the Boss Lady is on a whole other level compared to us.”
――まあ、此奴等が段違いで弱いのではあるが。: —Though, it was mostly that these two were exceptionally weak.
ハチ「糞ッ……覚えてやがれっ！！」: Hachi: “Damn it... you better remember this!!”
クマ「――精々足掻くがいい」: Kuma: “—Struggle all you wish. It will be for naught.”
ハチとクマは蹌踉めきながら森の闇へ消えていった。: Hachi and Kuma stumbled away and vanished into the darkness of the forest.
奏介「――これで不法侵入では無くなったな」: Sousuke: “—Well, I guess it’s not breaking and entering anymore.”
ハチが放り投げた鍵を琢磨に渡す。: I handed the keys Hachi had discarded to Takuma.
琢磨「何も変わらんさ。違法であるのは間違いない」: Takuma: “Doesn't change a thing. It’s still illegal as hell.”
そう言いながらも、琢磨は鍵穴に鍵を挿し、扉を開けた。: Even as he said that, Takuma inserted the key into the lock and swung the door open.
建物の内部も闇が広がっている。: Darkness filled the interior of the building as well.
奏介「さて――鬼が出るか蛇が出るか」: Sousuke: “Well then—let’s see what kind of monsters are waiting for us.”
ふたり頷きを交わし、中へ足を踏み入れた。: We shared a nod and stepped inside.
緋色の絨毯を踏みしめ、無人の屋内を探索する。: We moved through the empty house, our footsteps muffled by the crimson carpet.
広い洋館をふたりだけで調べるのはさすがに辛い。一階の台所や居間、洋室を見て回るだ: Searching a massive Western mansion with just the two of us was grueling work. By the time we’d finished checking the kitchen, the parlor, and the other rooms on the first floor, a significant amount of time had passed.
階段で反対側を調べていた琢磨に声を掛ける。: I called out to Takuma, who had been checking the other side of the staircase.
琢磨「ほとんど人が住んでいると云う印象は受けないな」: Takuma: “It doesn't feel like anyone’s actually living here.”
奏介「こっちもそうだ。台所なんざ綺麗なモンだった」: Sousuke: “Same here. The kitchen was spotless.”
もう何年も使われてないようだった。: It looked like it hadn't been used in years.
奏介「此処に何かがあるとすれば――な」: Sousuke: “If there’s anything to find—it’ll be up there.”
綾音殺害に関する直接的な証拠じゃなくても構わない。: It didn't have to be direct evidence of Ayane’s murder.
連中が悪事に関わっている何らかの手懸かりさえあればいいのだが――: As long as it was some kind of lead linking those bastards to their crimes—
琢磨がそう俺を急かしたその時――: Just as Takuma hurried me along—
銃声の元――階段の上の方に向き直る。: I whirled toward the source of the gunshot—the top of the stairs.
葵「鼠が二匹……いや、一匹は犬かしら？」: Aoi: “Two little mice... or wait, is one of them a dog?”
踊り場に現れた葵を睨み付ける。: I glared up at Aoi as she appeared on the landing.
葵「あたしはただ押し込み強盗を撃退しようとしただけよ。文句を云われる筋合いはない: Aoi: “I was simply repelling a couple of burglars. I don't see why anyone should complain.”
銃口から棚引く硝煙を散らし、葵は拳銃を弄ぶ。: Aoi toyed with her pistol, the muzzle trailing a wisp of acrid smoke.
葵「――まだ殺してないんだからなおさらよ」: Aoi: “—Especially since I haven't even killed you yet.”
倒れたままの琢磨に銃口を向けた。: She pointed the gun at Takuma as he lay on the floor.
葵「――ホント、貴方の周りではよく人が死ぬわね」: Aoi: “—Honestly, people seem to die around you quite often, don't they?”
薄笑いを浮かべる葵。: Aoi let out a thin, cruel smirk.
葵「――あの娘も、貴方と関わり合いにならなければ死ぬ事も無かったのに……」: Aoi: “—That girl, too. She never would have had to die if she hadn't gotten involved with you...”
葵「フフ……可哀相。すぐ側に人が居るのに助けても貰えなかったんですもの。貴方にも: Aoi: “Fufu... how tragic. To have someone right beside her and yet receive no help at all. I really wanted you to see it too—how her face twisted in despair as—”
葵の声を遮り、階段を駆け上がる。: I cut her off and charged up the stairs.
銃口を向けられ、僅かながら残っていた理性で足を止める。: Staring down the barrel of her gun, the last shred of my reason forced my feet to a halt.
葵「ウフッ……大人しくしていた方が身の為よ？」: Aoi: “Ufufu... it’s for your own good to stay quiet, you know?”
葵は銃を構えたまま、ゆっくりと階段を下りてきた。: Aoi kept the gun leveled at me as she slowly descended the stairs.
奏介「何故……綾音を殺した……？」: Sousuke: “Why... why did you kill Ayane...?”
ぼそり、と葵が洩らした。: Aoi whispered the words under her breath.
葵「そう――貴方があたしのモノにならなかったから。あたしが用意してあげた部屋に他: Aoi: “That’s right—because you wouldn't become mine. Because you brought another woman into the room I prepared just for you!”
――貌のすぐ横を風が通り過ぎていった。: —A rush of air whipped past my face.
葵「フフ――殺さないから安心なさい。こんなちゃちなピストルであっさり殺しちゃ勿体: Aoi: “Fufu—don't worry, I won't kill you. It would be such a waste to end things so quickly with a cheap little pistol like this.”
頬に軽い痛み。以前、あの狂った芸妓に簪で斬られた処と同じだ。: A stinging pain flared on my cheek. It was in the exact same spot where that crazed geiko had sliced me with her hairpin before.
葵「――予めお酒に睡眠薬を混ぜておいたのよ。念の為直前にも直接嗅がせたわ。そして: Aoi: “—I’d already laced your sake with sleeping pills. I even let you have a direct whiff right before, just to be sure. And then, while you were dead to the world right next to her, I took that girl and—”
葵「フフッ――怖ぁい……。目覚めて助けられなかった貴方の落度でしょう？」: Aoi: “Fufufu—how scary... It was your own failing that you woke up too late to save her, wasn't it?”
低い声で呻く。: I growled, my voice low and dangerous.
これ以上此奴に喋らせると、俺がどうにかなってしまいそうだった。: If I let her keep talking, I felt like I was going to snap.
葵「あら……？ 貴方達は此処へそれを聞きに来たんじゃないのかしら？」: Aoi: “Oh...? Isn't this exactly what you two came here to hear?”
葵「貴方に命令する権利があるとでも思っているのかしら？」: Aoi: “Do you really think you’re in any position to be giving me orders?”
拳銃をちらつかせる葵に、俺は黙るしか無かった。: With Aoi flaunting her pistol, I had no choice but to remain silent.
葵「何処まで話したかしら……そうそう、貴方が持っていたナイフであの娘の心臓をひと: Aoi: “Now, where was I... oh yes, a single plunge into that girl's heart with the knife you were carrying—honestly, I wanted to hack her to pieces—”
葵「人の物をかっ攫うような泥棒猫にするのは当然でしょう？でも目覚められると厄介だ: Aoi: “It’s only natural for a thieving cat who tries to snatch what belongs to others, isn't it? But it would have been a nuisance if she’d woken up, so I let her go quickly.”
葵「一度だけ刃先を抜いて血を噴き出させたわ……演出は派手にしたかったんだもの」: Aoi: “I pulled the blade out just once to let the blood spray... I wanted the performance to be dramatic, after all.”
葵「後は簡単。子飼いの憲兵隊を呼び寄せて貴方を捕縛させ、逃れられない状態にして飼: Aoi: “The rest was easy. I was going to have my pet Kenpeitai arrest you and keep you as a prisoner who could never escape... but...”
葵「もういいわ。従順じゃないペットは要らないの」: Aoi: “I’ve had enough. I don't need a pet that won't obey me.”
銃口を俺の頭に向ける。: She pointed the gun directly at my head.
銃口が逸れ、壁を撃ち抜く。: The muzzle wavered, and the shot blasted into the wall.
倒れていた琢磨が飛びついた。: Takuma, who had been lying on the floor, lunged at her.
琢磨「やらせる……かよっ！」: Takuma: “I won't let you... do it!”
琢磨の身体が数度震え、葵から離れる。: Takuma’s body convulsed several times before he fell away from Aoi.
葵「ハッ……驚かせやがって……」: Aoi: “Hah... you actually managed to surprise me...”
葵が嘗て無い口調で吐き捨て、床に崩れ落ちた琢磨を蹴り付けた。: Aoi spat out the words with a vulgarity I’d never heard from her before, then kicked Takuma as he lay crumpled on the floor.
銃をこちらに向けようとする葵だが、遅い。: Aoi tried to turn the gun back toward me, but she was too slow.
強引に銃を奪い、床に押し倒す。: I forcibly wrened the gun away and pinned her to the floor.
葵の上に馬乗りになる。: I straddled Aoi, pinning her down.
――手には奪い取った拳銃。: —The stolen pistol was in my hand.
――こいつは、綾音を殺して俺に罪を擦り付けた奴。: —This was the woman who killed Ayane and pinned the crime on me.
銃を握り直す。: I adjusted my grip on the gun.
弾丸も恐らく薬室に装填されている。: There was almost certainly a round in the chamber.
此奴だけは、赦す訳にはいかない。: This woman was the one person I could never forgive.
葵の眉間に銃口を押しつける。: I pressed the muzzle against the center of Aoi’s forehead.
奏介「……すぐ楽にしてやる」: Sousuke: “...I'll make it quick for you.”
葵が睨み付けてくるが、構うモノか。: Aoi glared up at me, but I didn't care.
この指に軽く力を篭めるだけで、此奴は――: With just a little pressure from my finger, this woman would—
硝子の割れる音――窓か？: The sound of shattering glass—a window?
銃口を密着させているのだ、外すわけが――: The muzzle was pressed right against her; there was no way I could miss—
腕を弾かれ、弾道が逸れる。: My arm was knocked aside, and the shot went wide.
ほぼ同時、真下からの強い力で俺の身体が浮く。: Almost simultaneously, a powerful force from directly below me sent my body flying.
床に叩きつけられた拍子に拳銃を手放してしまう。: I lost my grip on the pistol as I slammed into the floor.
闇の中、葵の姿は見えない。声だけが少し離れた処から聞こえてきた。: In the darkness, I couldn't see Aoi at all. Only her voice reached me from a short distance away.
葵「――裏切りの代償は高く付くわよ」: Aoi: “—The price of betrayal will be steep.”
暫くじっと息を潜め、本当に居なくなったのかを確認する。: I stayed perfectly still for a moment, holding my breath, confirming that she was truly gone.
目が慣れてきて、倒れ伏したままの琢磨の元へ駆け寄る。: My eyes began to adjust, and I rushed over to where Takuma lay.
暗い為奴の顔色を窺い知る事は出来ないが、決して良くはないだろう。: It was too dark to see his face, but I knew his condition couldn't be good.
あの距離で葵が射撃を外すとは思えない。: I didn't believe for a second that Aoi would miss from that distance.
琢磨「……それよりも、二階を調べるんだろ？ さっさと……行くぞ」: Takuma: “...More importantly, weren't we going to search the second floor? Let’s... get moving.”
俺の手を振り払い、琢磨は一段ずつ階段を上っていく。: Takuma shook off my hand and began to climb the stairs, one agonizing step at a time.
琢磨「誰が……てめーなんかの為に無理するかよ」: Takuma: “Who the hell... would push themselves for a guy like you?”
――結局琢磨は、俺の手を借りることなく二階へたどり着いた。: —In the end, Takuma reached the second floor without any help from me.
再び二手に分かれ、探索を開始する。: We split up again and began our search.
琢磨の奴は、俺が休んでいろと言ったのも聞かず、別の部屋を家捜ししている。: That stubborn bastard Takuma wouldn't listen when I told him to rest, and he was already ransacking another room.
無理やりにでも休ませた方がいいに決まっているはずなのだが――今は人手が足りない。: I knew I should have forced him to rest—but we were short on hands.
人が居た形跡のある部屋を調べる。: I searched a room that showed signs of recent use.
葵が使っていたのだろうか、大きなベッドはシーツが乱れ、女物の洋服が何着か並べられ: Aoi must have been using it; the sheets on the massive bed were ruffled, and several pieces of women's clothing were laid out.
テーブルには書類のような物が散乱している。: Documents were scattered across the table.
何が書かれているのだろうか――: I wondered what was written on them—
数枚手に取って眺める。様々な数字――金額だろうか、万の桁がずらりと並んでいる。: I picked up a few pages and scanned them. Columns of numbers—likely monetary amounts—with figures in the tens of thousands lined up row after row.
何処其処の憲兵隊に流れた金か――: Was this money being funneled to various Kenpeitai units—?
他にも何やら機械部品を調達したらしき痕跡もある。: There were also records of what looked like mechanical parts being procured.
まともな帳簿とは思えなかった。: It was clearly an illicit ledger.
《清鳳会》の裏帳簿か――: A secret account book for the Seifuu-kai—
葵の自供を琢磨も聞いてはいるが、警察の上層部が握りつぶしてしまうかもしれない。: Even though Takuma had heard Aoi’s confession, the police brass might just bury it.
――憲兵隊からの圧力等があるだろう。: —The pressure from the Kenpeitai would see to that.
そうなれば、もっと文字等で残された客観的な書類――証拠が必要だ。: If we wanted to take them down, we needed more objective documentation—physical evidence.
裏帳簿らしき書類を脇に退け、その他の書類を漁る。: I pushed the secret ledger aside and dug through the rest of the papers.
《逢禅寺商会》という会社名が目立つ。《清鳳会》の取引先か何かだろうか。: The name “Ouzenji Trading” stood out. Was it a business partner of the Seifuu-kai or something?
紙束の間から、何かが床に滑り落ちた。: Something slid from between a stack of papers and fell to the floor.
鎖の付いた、銀色のロケットだ。: It was a silver locket on a chain.
何の気なしに蓋を開けてみる。: Without thinking, I clicked the lid open.
中には親子と思われる写真が嵌められていた。: Inside was a photograph of what looked like a mother and daughter.
母と幼い娘。: A mother and her young child.
古く、小さな写真なので、面影があるかどうかも解り難い。: The photo was old and small, making it hard to tell if there was a resemblance.
ただ――誰かの手が端から伸びてきている。: But—someone’s hand was reaching into the frame from the edge.
不自然に端が切り取られているようだ。: It looked like the edge had been unnaturally cut off.
ロケットの留め金を外し、中の写真を取り出す。: I undid the clasp of the locket and pulled the photo out.
元は少し大きめの写真だったらしく、端は折り返されていた。: It seemed it had originally been a slightly larger photo, and the edge had been folded over.
ロケットに入れる為に折ったのだろうか――: Had it been folded just to fit inside the locket—?
軍服に身を包んだマスター――護堂が佇んでいた。: The Master—Godou—stood there, dressed in military uniform.
――若い。十年ではきかない、大正――震災前の写真だろうか。: —He was young. This wasn't just ten years ago; it looked like it was from the Taisho era—before the Great Earthquake.
何故マスターが写っているのか。これはまるで――家族の写真ではないか。: Why was the Master in this photo? It looked exactly like—a family portrait.
訳が判らねえ。: I can't make sense of this.
マスターと葵が親娘だからってどうだと云うのだ。: What does it matter if the Master and Aoi are father and daughter?
確認する術だって今はない。: There was no way to confirm it now anyway.
マスターが行方不明にさえなっていなければ、バーに乗り込んで確かめてやれるのに。: If only the Master hadn't disappeared, I could have marched into the bar and demanded the truth from him.
物音に振り向く。: I whirled around at a sound.
部屋の扉の側、琢磨が蹲っていた。: Takuma was slumped against the doorframe.
駆け寄って抱え起こす。: I rushed over and scooped him up.
――抵抗する体力すら残されていないようだった。: —It seemed he didn't even have the strength left to try.
腹を押さえている手を退かす。それを遮る力もない。: I tried to pull his hand away from his stomach. He didn't even have the strength to stop me.
上着どころか、ズボンにまで血の染みが広がっていた。: The bloodstain had spread not just through his jacket, but all the way down his trousers.
奏介「――さっさと手当さえしていれば……ッ！！」: Sousuke: “—If only I’d treated this sooner...!!”
琢磨「……手当ならしたさ、自分でな……っ！ その時に気付いちまった……どのみちこ: Takuma: “...I treated it myself... ugh! That’s when I realized... a wound like this... I wasn't gonna make it... anyway...”
奏介「喋るなと言ってるだろ……！！」: Sousuke: “I told you to shut up...!!”
シャツを脱ぎ、琢磨の疵口に押しつける。: I pulled off my shirt and pressed it against his wound.
琢磨「無駄だ……もう、出て行く血も残っちゃいないさ……」: Takuma: “It’s no use... I don't have enough blood left to keep going...”
琢磨「それよりも……何か見つかったのか……？」: Takuma: “More importantly... did you find anything...?”
《清鳳会》の裏帳簿如きで事態が好転するとは思えないが、今の琢磨にはそう言うべきだ: I didn't think a secret ledger for the Seifuu-kai would actually change much, but that was what Takuma needed to hear right now.
琢磨「……そうか……だったら、そいつを俺に渡してくれ」: Takuma: “...I see... then, give it to me.”
琢磨「今日仕事が終わった時に……署の俺の机に、書き置きを残しておいた……。俺が朝: Takuma: “When I finished work today... I left a note on my desk at the station... Telling them that if I wasn't back by morning, they should... raid this place...”
奏介「なんだって……そんな事を……」: Sousuke: “Why... why would you do that...”
琢磨「……犯人の潜伏先に突入するんだ、予感は……していたさ。……相馬葵の名義の建: Takuma: “...I was raiding the suspect's hideout. I had a feeling... something might happen. ...A detective dying in a building owned by Aoi Souma, carrying evidence of her crimes...”
琢磨「……如何に動きの鈍い警察でも……動かざるを得ないだろうさ……」: Takuma: “...Even the slowest-moving police force... would be forced to act...”
琢磨「……五月蠅えよ、耳元で怒鳴るな……ったく……相馬葵を白河綾音殺害の重要参考: Takuma: “...You’re so loud, don't shout in my ear... honestly... I also wrote that Aoi Souma should be wanted as a key witness in Ayane Shirakawa’s murder... so things should work out.”
いよいよ呼吸が途切れ途切れになってきた。: His breathing was becoming increasingly shallow and ragged.
琢磨「だから……っ、おめーは証拠を置いて……帰れ。俺は置いていけ……どうせお前は: Takuma: “So... leave the evidence and... go. Leave me here... you’re still a suspect anyway. You couldn't even take me to a hospital, right...?”
握り締め、皺くちゃになった《清鳳会》の書類を琢磨に握らせる。: I pressed the crumpled Seifuu-kai documents into Takuma’s hand.
琢磨「へっ……そうだ、それでいい……ああ、柚芭の事は済まなかったな……」: Takuma: “Heh... yeah, that’s it... Oh, and I’m sorry about Yuzuha...”
奏介「……そんな事は……今はどうでもいい……」: Sousuke: “...That... it doesn't matter right now...”
琢磨「……なんだよ、黙りかよ……ま、当然か……」: Takuma: “...What’s with the silence... well, I guess that’s expected...”
琢磨「……心配するなよ、柚芭に手は出しちゃいねえさ。俺だってそんくらいの……分別: Takuma: “...Don't worry, I never laid a finger on Yuzuha. Even I have... some decency... *cough*, *cough*!”
ポケットから煙草の包みを取り出し、残っていた最後の一本を琢磨に持たせる。: I pulled a pack of cigarettes from my pocket and placed the final one between Takuma’s fingers.
奏介「待ってろ。今、火を――」: Sousuke: “Wait here. I'll get a light—”
琢磨の手から煙草が滑り落ちる。: The cigarette slipped from Takuma’s hand.
手が、床に落ちる。: His hand fell to the floor.
覚悟していたとは云え――: Even though I’d been prepared for this—
友の死に立ち会うのは、堪らなく――: Watching a friend die was almost too much to bear—
床に拳を振り落とす。: I slammed my fist into the floor.
奏介「糞ッ、糞ッ、こん畜生がッ……！！」: Sousuke: “Damn it, damn it, you bastard...!!”
繰り返し、拳で床を叩く。: I slammed my fist against the floor repeatedly.
暫くの間自分の莫迦さ加減を呪い、夜が明ける直前になって、漸くこの御巫邸を立ち去っ: I spent a long time cursing my own stupidity, and finally, just before dawn, I left the Mikanagi estate.
――琢磨の遺体はそのままに、喫えなかった煙草を奴の指に挟ませたままで――: —Leaving Takuma’s body behind, with that unlit cigarette still held between his fingers—
美華夏は庇の下で雨を凌ぎながら、葬儀場へ入っていく人の列を眺めていた。: Mikage stood under the eaves, shielding herself from the rain, as she watched the line of people filing into the funeral hall.
やはり、警察の関係者が多いようだ。↓胸元に大仰な勲章を煌めかせながら、粛々と進: As expected, there were many police officers. They filed past solemnly, their chests adorned with pretentious medals.
彼らが琢磨のことをどれだけ知っているのだろうか。↓美華夏はそう思わずに居られな: I wonder how much they actually knew about Takuma. Mikage couldn't help but wonder.
＄ 何人が彼のやろうとした事を理解していただろう。: How many of them understood what he was trying to achieve?
だけど……こうして関係者が参列するだけいいのかもしれない。↓全くの独断専行であ: But... perhaps it was enough that his colleagues were here to pay their respects. Despite having acted entirely on his own, Takuma had been posthumously promoted to Assistant Inspector for dying in the line of duty.
死んだ後の階級など、何の意味も成さない。↓美華夏にとって重要なのは、綾音殺害に: A rank given after death means absolutely nothing. For Mikage, the only thing that mattered was that the suspicion surrounding Sousuke’s involvement in Ayane’s murder had finally begun to fade.
＄ 琢磨はその命と引き替えに、友の無実を証明しようとした。: Takuma had traded his life to prove his friend's innocence.
美華夏「……死んじゃったらどうしようもないじゃない、莫迦……」: Mikana: “...You idiot, what’s the point if you go and die...”
誰にも聞こえないよう、小声で呟く。: She whispered the words so quietly no one could hear.
泣きたい人は他に居る。: There is someone else who needs to do the crying.
＄ だから私は、彼女を慰める為に我慢しなくてはならない。: So I must endure, so that I can comfort her.
柚芭は近くに居ない。↓今頃は家の中の控え室に居るだろう。↓外は寒くて辛いからと: Yuzuha was nowhere to be seen. She was likely in the waiting room inside the house. Mikage had almost forced her inside, insisting it was too cold and miserable out here.
だから今は――ほんの少しなら泣いてもいいのかもしれない。: So for now—perhaps it’s okay to cry just a little.
＄ 昔馴染みの友人の為に、少しだけ涙を流そう。: For the sake of an old friend, let me shed just a few tears.
一歩前に踏み出す。↓すぐさま雨が降りかかってくるが、構わない。↓傘も差さず、曇: She took a step forward. The rain immediately began to pelt her, but she didn't care. Without an umbrella, she looked up at the overcast sky.
誰も、私が泣いてるとは思わないから――: No one will think I’m crying—
＄ 貌も、髪も、身体も濡らし、美華夏は再び庇の下へ戻る。↓貌を拭い、瞼から零れ落: Wetting her face, her hair, and her body, Mikage stepped back under the eaves. She wiped her face, hiding the droplets that spilled from her eyelids.
こんな事したって自分自身すら騙せていないのに。: Doing this doesn't even fool myself.
哀しいのならば素直に泣けばいい。: If I’m sad, I should just cry.
大切な友達が死んだのだから。: Because a dear friend has died.
美華夏が琢磨と初めて出会ったのは、確か六年程前の事だ。: It was about six years ago that Mikage first met Takuma.
奏介を捜しに出た美華夏は、街の不良連中に絡まれた。↓その時窮地を救ってくれたの: Mikage, who had gone out looking for Sousuke, had been cornered by some local thugs. The one who had saved her from that predicament was a man who had also been playing at being a delinquent.
震災後、両親を亡くし鬱ぎ込んでいた奏介を外へ連れ出したのも琢磨だった。: After the earthquake, it was Takuma who had managed to pull Sousuke out of the depression he’d slumped into after losing his parents.
いくら美華夏が言い聞かせても無駄だった事をあっさりとやってのけた琢磨に、当時は: Takuma had easily achieved what Mikage had failed to do no matter how much she’d tried to reason with him, and she’d felt a sliver of jealousy toward him back then.
ふたりは復興していく街で悪さを働いていたが、やがて奏介が音楽の道に進むこととな: The two of them had spent their time acting out in the city as it rebuilt itself, but eventually, Sousuke had decided to pursue a path in music.
それが面白くなかったのか、琢磨はそれからもごろつき紛いの狼藉を繰り返していた。: Perhaps he hadn't found that amusing, as Takuma had continued his delinquent-like behavior for some time after.
それを諭したのは美華夏だった。: It was Mikage who had finally reasoned with him.
奏介がやっとやりたい事を見つけたのに、それを邪魔しないで欲しい――と。: She told him that Sousuke had finally found something he wanted to do, and she didn't want him to get in the way—
今にして思えば、それは単純に奏介を案じての事だったのだが――: Thinking back, she’d simply been worried about Sousuke—
そうして、琢磨は性根を入れ替え、警察官となった。↓元々曲がった事が嫌いな性分だ: And so, Takuma had changed his ways and become a police officer. Because he’d always hated unfairness, his sudden transformation hadn't felt particularly out of place.
だがそれが、彼の運命を決定づける事になるとは、その時の美華夏には全く予想が付か: But at the time, Mikage could never have guessed that this decision would eventually seal his fate.
美華夏＄「……何も、死ぬ事はないじゃない」: Mikana: <...There was no need for you to die.>
琢磨が警察官となって暫く経ったある日の事だ。↓非番だった彼が珍しく美華夏を訪ね: It was one day, a while after Takuma had become a cop. He’d visited Mikage while he was off duty, which was rare for him.
＄ 美華夏は驚いたが、その申し出を受ける訳にはいかなかった。↓既に思いを抱いてい: Mikage was surprised, but she couldn't accept his offer. Because she already had feelings for someone else.
琢磨にはすぐに勘付かれた。↓……いや、知っていたのだろう。↓判っていて敢えて告: Takuma had caught on immediately. ...No, he must have known all along. He had a certain straightforwardness to him, as if he’d confessed knowing exactly what the answer would be.
奏介を放って置く訳にはいかないから。↓そう美華夏は答えた。: Because I can't just leave Sousuke alone. That was how Mikage had answered.
＄ ずっと彼の事を見ていたから。: Because I’ve always been looking at him.
喜びも悲しみも、ずっと見てきたから。: Because I’ve watched him through all his joys and sorrows.
その想いを共有する事は出来なかったけど、少しでも彼の為に何かしてあげたいと思っ: I couldn't share those feelings with him, but I wanted to do whatever I could for his sake.
それがふたりの仲違いの原因となったとは思いたくない。↓相手を疎ましがっているの: I don't want to believe that was the reason for the rift between the two of them. Because no matter how you looked at it, Sousuke was the one who seemed to resent the other.
琢磨の事が羨ましかったのだろうか。↓奏介の考えている事は美華夏にはよく解らない: I wonder if he was jealous of Takuma. Mikage didn't really understand what was going through Sousuke’s head.
解らないのは琢磨も同じだ。↓わざわざ奏介と同列まで降りてきて啀み合っている。: She didn't understand Takuma either. He’d gone out of his way to lower himself to Sousuke’s level just to bicker with him.
ふたりは仲直り出来たのだろうか。↓美華夏はそれが気懸かりだった。: I wonder if they ever managed to make up. That was Mikage’s concern.
琢磨の遺体が発見されたのは、奏介の元に身を寄せている少女――御巫久遠の生家であ: Takuma’s body had been found in the childhood home of the girl Sousuke was sheltering—Qwon Mikanagi—which was also the residence of the woman who was now the prime suspect in Ayane Shirakawa’s murder.
そこに奏介も居ただろうと、美華夏はほぼ確信していた。: Mikage was almost certain that Sousuke had been there as well.
彼から詳しい話は聞いていない。↓ただ、家に掛かってきた電話で、疲れ果てた声で琢: She hadn't heard the details from him. All she knew was what he’d told her over the phone in an exhausted voice: that Takuma was dead.
柚芭にも美華夏が伝えた。↓奏介にその重責を担わせたくなかった。↓辛い事は分担し: Mikage had also told Yuzuha. She didn't want Sousuke to have to carry that burden alone. It’s always better to share painful things.
柚芭と琢磨付き合っていると聞かされたのは、今年に入ってからだった。↓驚きはした: It was only earlier this year that she’d been told Yuzuha and Takuma were dating. She’d been surprised, but it had made a strange kind of sense.
＄ 奏介と琢磨は似ているから。: Because Sousuke and Takuma are so similar.
……美華夏が少々嫉妬心を覚えてしまう程に。: ...To the point that Mikage sometimes felt a little jealous.
柚芭が戻ってきた。↓美華夏は振り向き、何ともやるせない気分になった。: Yuzuha had returned. Mikage turned around, feeling a sense of utter helplessness.
柚芭は目を真っ赤に腫らし、ハンカチを握り締めていた。↓それでも涙は見せていない: Yuzuha’s eyes were red and swollen, and she was clutching a handkerchief. But she wasn't letting any tears fall. She was likely enduring it, thinking that crying was shameful.
彼女はそんな子だ。: That’s just the kind of girl she is.
柚芭が美華夏の服の裾を握り締めてくる。: Yuzuha gripped the hem of Mikage’s clothes.
これ以上此処に居ても何も出来やしない。↓友人であったとは云え、琢磨の家族にはあ: There was nothing more she could do by staying here. Even though she’d been a friend, she likely wasn't well-regarded by Takuma’s family.
過去にやんちゃをしていた時の仲間と思われているだろうから。: They likely saw her as one of his old delinquent companions from his younger days.
美華夏は傘を広げようとしていた手を止めた。: Mikage stopped as she was about to open her umbrella.
柚芭「菱谷さんは……お兄ちゃんの所為で死んじゃったの……？」: Yuzuha: “Did Hishiya-san... die because of my brother...?”
美華夏「――所為だなんて言っちゃだめ」: Mikana: “—Don't ever say it was because of him.”
いつになく強い調子で美華夏は諭した。: Mikage reasoned with her in a tone that was unusually firm.
美華夏「それは奏介にも、琢磨にも失礼だから。琢磨は……警察の職務で亡くなったの。: Mikana: “That’s disrespectful to both Sousuke and Takuma. Takuma... he died in the line of duty. It has nothing to do with Sousuke.”
柚芭に言いながら、美華夏は自分自身にも言い聞かせていた。: As she spoke to Yuzuha, Mikage was also trying to convince herself.
……狡い言い種だ。: ...What a cowardly way to put it.
そうやって逃げられる場所を作り出す大人に――自分に腹が立つ。: She felt a surge of anger toward herself—at the adult who sought out such an escape.
美華夏「それに……奏介は何もしてないのよ。奏介も琢磨も……諍い事に巻き込まれただ: Mikana: “And besides... Sousuke didn't do anything. Both Sousuke and Takuma... they just got caught up in a dispute—”
柚芭は力なく頷くと、美華夏に寄り掛かってきた。↓胸に貌を埋める彼女を優しく抱き: Yuzuha gave a weak nod and leaned against Mikage. Mikage gently caught her as she buried her face in her chest.
柚芭「少しだけ……こうしていてもいい？」: Yuzuha: “Can I stay... like this for just a little while?”
断る事など出来やしない。美華夏は柚芭を慰める為に此処に居るのだから。: There was no way she could refuse. Mikage was here specifically to comfort Yuzuha, after all.
美華夏の服にしがみつき、声を押し殺して柚芭が嗚咽する。: Yuzuha clung to Mikage’s clothes, her sobs muffled as she tried to suppress them.
我慢に我慢を重ねていたのだろう、柚芭の泣き声は徐々に大きくなっていく。: She must have been holding it in for so long; Yuzuha’s crying gradually grew louder.
こうなる事は予想できていた筈なのに。: She should have known it would end up like this.
＄ ――美華夏には、柚芭へ掛ける慰めの言葉を見つけられなかった。: Mikage couldn't find any words of comfort to offer Yuzuha.
＄ 美華夏には、ここに来られなかった奏介の涙に思えてならなかった。: To Mikage, it felt like the tears Sousuke, who couldn't be here, was unable to shed.
グラスの氷が爆ぜる。: The ice in my glass cracked.
俺は何杯目かのウィスキーをグラスに注ぐと、軽く杯を合わせてから喉に流し込んだ。: I poured another glass of whiskey, gave it a light toast, and let it burn down my throat.
朝から降り続いている雨は、止む素振りを見せない。: The rain that had been falling since morning showed no signs of stopping.
こんな日は部屋に閉じこもっていても気が滅入るばかりだ。: On a day like this, staying cooped up in my room would only make me more depressed.
それなら――無人の酒場で杯を重ねている方が幾らかましだった。: In that case—drinking away the hours in an empty bar felt like the better option.
カウンタに近づいてきた千花が、俺の隣の席に置かれたままになっていたグラスを指す。: Chika approached the counter and pointed to the glass that had been sitting in the seat next to me.
奏介「いや……謝られるような事じゃない」: Sousuke: “No... you've got nothing to apologize for.”
大分氷が溶けたグラスを手元に引き寄せ、一気に飲み干す。口腔の粘膜が灼けるように痛: I pulled the glass, its ice mostly melted, toward me and downed it in one go. The alcohol stung my mouth like a burn.
空になったふたつのグラスを並べ、さらにウィスキーを注ぎ入れる。: I lined up the two empty glasses and poured more whiskey into both.
今度は千花も何も言わない。: This time, Chika didn't say anything.
奏介「――結局、彼奴とサシで飲むことはなかったな」: Sousuke: “—In the end, I never actually got to sit down and have a drink with that bastard.”
グラスを傾ける。先程からひっきりなしに飲んでいるにも拘わらず、全く酔えなかった。: I tilted my glass. Even though I’d been drinking non-stop for a while now, I couldn't get drunk at all.
俺なんかの為に死んでしまった琢磨に対して、申し訳が立たない。: I felt a crushing sense of guilt toward Takuma, who had died for someone like me.
琢磨に託した裏帳簿だって、どれほどの証拠になるのか。: And even the secret ledger I’d entrusted to him—how much of an impact would it really have?
俺の無実が証明される事があるのだろうか――: Would my innocence ever actually be proven—?
奏介「マスターの事で訊きたいことがある」: Sousuke: “There’s something I want to ask about the Master.”
千花ならば、もしかしたらマスターの家族の事なども知っているかもしれない。: If it was Chika, she might know something about the Master’s family.
葵との関係が判るかもしれない。: I might be able to find a connection to Aoi.
千花「全然っ、何もマスターの事は知りませんっ！ 判らないんですっ！ 何も……本当: Chika: “I don't know anything at all about the Master! I don't understand! I really... don't know anything...”
何も其処まで否定する事もないだろうに。: There was no reason for her to be *that* defensive.
千花「わ……わたしっ、何も……何も見てませんっ！！」: Chika: “I... I didn't see anything... anything at all!!”
今にも千花は泣き出しそうだが――: Chika looked like she was about to burst into tears—
静かな声で千花を諭す。: I tried to reason with Chika in a quiet voice.
こうも狼狽えられてしまうと、話を聞くこともできやしない。: I couldn't get any information out of her while she was this distraught.
千花は俯き――ぽろぽろと涙を零し出してしまった。: Chika looked down—and tears began to spill from her eyes.
貌を覆って泣き出してしまった千花。: Chika covered her face and began to wail.
奏介「いや……話せないのなら無理には訊かないが……」: Sousuke: “No... if you can't talk about it, I won't force you...”
千花「ちっ……違うんですっ！ は、話しますっ！ だ、だからっ……怒らないで……く: Chika: “N-No, that’s not it! I’ll... I’ll tell you! S-So... please don't... don't be angry...!”
――こういう時、自分の無愛想さが恨めしい。言葉通りに千花は受け取らないだろう。: —At times like this, I cursed my own bluntness. Chika likely wouldn't take my words at face value.
千花「……わたしっ……あの夜にっ……その……見てしまったんです……」: Chika: “...I... on that night... um... I saw it...”
千花「あの……玖藤さんが最初に事件に巻き込まれた夜……玖藤さんがお店を出て行った: Chika: “On the night Kudou-san first got caught up in the incident... right after Kudou-san left the shop, the Master went somewhere too...”
千花「……はい。わたしはマスターに用事があったので後を追いました。そうしたら……: Chika: “...Yes. I had some business with the Master, so I followed him. And then...”
千花は厭々をするように頭を振る。: Chika shook her head as if trying to block out the memory.
千花「マ……マスターがっ……！ 女の人をナイフで刺してっ……！！」: Chika: “The... the Master...! He stabbed a woman with a knife...!!”
奏介「……その、刺された女ってのは……」: Sousuke: “...That woman who was stabbed, she was...”
千花「たぶん……芸者さんでした」: Chika: “I think... she was a geisha.”
マスターが――殺したのか……？: The Master—he was the one who killed her...?
それで、俺を犯人に仕立て上げた……？: And then, he framed me for the crime...?
余程俺は厳しい貌をしていたのだろう、脅えた千花がまた謝ってくる。: I must have been wearing an incredibly harsh expression, as a terrified Chika began to apologize again.
千花「わたしが悪いんですっ！ わたしがっ……ちゃんとすぐにこの事を話していれば…: Chika: “It’s my fault! If only... if only I’d spoken up about this right away... none of this would have happened...!!”
千花「……怖かったんです……隠れて見てたのに、マスターがこっちを見て……何時もの: Chika: “...I was scared... I was watching from hiding, but the Master looked right at me... with that same look he always has... and I was just so scared...!”
千花「そのあとも……マスターは何時も通りで……わたしが見たのは幻だったんじゃない: Chika: “And even after that... the Master acted just like he always did... I started to think maybe what I’d seen was just an illusion... I just didn't understand anything...”
千花が泣き腫らした貌を上げる。: Chika looked up, her eyes puffy from crying.
奏介「話してくれて感謝している」: Sousuke: “I’m grateful to you for telling me.”
千花「あっ……でもっ……わたしっ……言うのが遅すぎたからっ……」: Chika: “Ah... but... I... I waited too long to say anything...”
千花「でもっ……もし、わたしがちゃんと言っていたら……こんな事にはならなかったん: Chika: “But... I wonder if things would have been different if I’d just said something...”
慰めでも何でもなく、考えていた事を言葉にする。: It wasn't just a comfort; I was simply stating what I was thinking.
奏介「もし事件後すぐにそれを聞かされていたって、俺は信じられなかっただろうし、周: Sousuke: “Even if I’d heard that right after the incident, I probably wouldn't have believed it, and no one else would have either. ...But now, there’s other evidence too.”
千花「それじゃ……やっぱり、マスターがっ……」: Chika: “Then... it really was... the Master...”
マスターと葵がもし親娘であるならば、ふたりが結託して俺を嵌めた可能性は高い。: If the Master and Aoi really were father and daughter, there was a high probability they’d conspired to frame me.
やはり、俺を逃れられなくする為――？: Was it really to make it so I couldn't escape—?
俺を嵌めて逃れられなくした処で、何の意味があるのだろうか。: What point was there in framing me and making it so I couldn't run?
連中は一体何を考えているのか――: What on earth were those people thinking—?
直接問いただす以外に、俺が知る方法はないようだった。: It seemed there was no way for me to know without demanding the truth from them directly.
扉が開き、疲れた様子の美華夏が店に入ってきた。: The door opened, and a weary-looking Mikage entered the shop.
先程まで泣いていた千花だったが、女給としての職務を思い出したのか美華夏に声を掛け: Chika, who had been crying until a moment ago, called out to Mikage, seemingly remembering her duties as a waitress.
美華夏は無言のまま軽く会釈し、俺の隣のスツールに腰を下ろした。: Mikage gave a silent, slight nod and took a seat on the stool next to mine.
美華夏「……厭なものね。友人の葬儀と云うのは」: Mikana: “...It’s a dreadful thing. A friend's funeral.”
奏介「……親の葬式よりはましだ」: Sousuke: “...It's better than a parent's funeral.”
美華夏「――っ、そうかもしれないけど……」: Mikana: “—! That might be true, but...”
軽く美華夏が睨んでくる。: Mikage shot me a light glare.
氷の溶けたグラスにウィスキーをほんの少し注ぎ、美華夏の前へ。: I poured a small amount of whiskey into the glass with the melted ice and slid it in front of her.
自分のグラスにも酒を注いで口を付ける。: I poured some into my own glass and took a sip.
まだ元号が大正と呼ばれていた、十年以上前の話だ。: A story from more than ten years ago, back when the era was still called Taisho.
グラスの中の液体を舐めるように口に付け、美華夏は呟く。: Mikage muttered, her lips touching the liquid in the glass as if tasting it.
横目で俺を見ていた美華夏が瞼を閉じる。: Looking at me out of the corner of her eye, Mikage closed her eyelids.
美華夏「昔は……奏介ひどかったもの」: Mikana: “Back then... you were terrible, Sousuke.”
奏介「……非道い、はないだろ」: Sousuke: “...Terrible' is a bit much, don't you think?”
美華夏「酷かったわよ、あの頃は。他人を寄せ付けないと云うか――自分自身すらどうで: Mikana: “You *were* terrible in those days. It was like you wouldn't let anyone near you—like you didn't even care about yourself—you were in a wretched state.”
引き取られた美華夏の実家に帰らず、街を彷徨っていた時代。: The days when I didn't go back to Mikage’s family home where I’d been taken in, but wandered the streets instead.
ただあるがままに、喧嘩をし、物を奪い、何にでも噛み付かんばかりだったあの頃。: Just existing, fighting, stealing, ready to bite at anything and everything.
側に居て俺に発破を掛けていたのは琢磨だった。: The one who stayed by my side and spurred me on was Takuma.
側に居て俺の身を案じてくれたのは美華夏だった。: The one who stayed by my side and worried for my safety was Mikage.
当時は疎ましかった。: At the time, I found them burdensome.
俺の事は放っておいて欲しかった。: I wanted to be left alone.
自堕落な生活を改善してくれたのは柚芭だった。: It was Yuzuha who fixed my self-destructive lifestyle.
音楽への道を示してくれたのは美華夏だった。: It was Mikage who showed me the path to music.
そして俺の前に突然現れ、導いてくれたのは――: And the one who suddenly appeared before me and guided me was—
彼女が居なければ、俺はここまで戻ってこれなかっただろう。: If she hadn't been there, I wouldn't have made it back this far.
本当に裏社会に身を投じて、《清鳳会》の――葵の言うが侭に動く兵隊となっていたかも: I might have truly thrown myself into the underworld and become a soldier for the Seifuu-kai—moving exactly as Aoi dictated.
奏介「彼奴が居なければ――俺は取り返しの付かないことになっていただろうな」: Sousuke: “If she hadn't been there—I would've ended up in an irreversible state.”
美華夏「綾音さんは……奏介の事を大切に想っていたからこそ、ずっと信じ続けていたの: Mikana: “Ayane-san... precisely because she cherished you, Sousuke, she never stopped believing in you...”
奏介「……綾音の考えていた事は、今になっちゃ誰にも解らねえがな」: Sousuke: “...No one will ever truly know what was going through Ayane’s head now.”
美華夏「何となくなら……私にも解る気がする」: Mikana: “Somehow... I feel like I understand.”
美華夏「だって奏介は……放っておけないから」: Mikana: “Because you... you just can't be left alone, Sousuke.”
美華夏「ずっと奏介の事見てきたから……」: Mikana: “Because I've been watching you all this time...”
奏介「……飲みすぎだ、お前」: Sousuke: “...You've had too much to drink.”
俺の肩に寄り掛かってきそうになる美華夏に、軽く釘を刺す。: I gave Mikage a light warning as she leaned toward my shoulder.
――気恥ずかしさも多分にあったのだが。: —Though, a large part of it was my own embarrassment.
気分を害したのか、美華夏は頬を膨らませるとグラスのウィスキーを口に運んだ。: Offended, Mikage puffed out her cheeks and brought the whiskey to her lips.
美華夏「これくらいで酔う筈がないじゃない」: Mikana: “There’s no way I’d get drunk off just this much.”
奏介「あまり無理するなよ。……俺が面倒を負う羽目になるんだからな」: Sousuke: “Don't overdo it. ...I'm the one who'll end up having to take care of you.”
美華夏「今まで面倒掛けられて来たんだもの、少しくらい……私が面倒を見られてもいい: Mikana: “I've been looking after you all this time; surely it's okay... if I'm the one being taken care of for once?”
そう言うと、美華夏はグラスに酒を注いだ。: With that, Mikage poured more alcohol into her glass.
俺の言う事を美華夏がまともに訊くはずがないのだが――: There was no way Mikage would actually listen to me, but—
果たして美華夏は、俺の言葉を無視してグラスの酒をあおるように飲み干してしまった。: True to form, Mikage ignored my words and drained the glass in a single gulp.
深々と溜息を吐く。: I let out a deep sigh.
隣にはカウンタに俯せになった美華夏。: Beside me, Mikage was slumped face-down on the counter.
奏介「だから飲み過ぎるなと言っただろうが」: Sousuke: “That’s why I told you not to overdo it.”
むくり、と美華夏が身体を起こして睨んでくる。: Mikage sat up abruptly and glared at me.
美華夏「今日くらい……いいじゃない」: Mikana: “Just for today... it’s fine, isn't it?”
奏介「ったく……面倒を増やしやがって。――千花」: Sousuke: “Tch... just adding more to my plate. —Chika.”
離れた処で待機していた千花が近づいてくる。: Chika, who had been waiting nearby, approached.
微笑みを浮かべ、千花は首を振る。: With a smile, Chika shook her head.
千花「あっ……お代は結構です。営業している訳ではありませんから」: Chika: “Ah... no charge is necessary. We aren't actually open for business, after all.”
千花「……それよりも、橘さんをお家まで送ってあげた方がよさそうですよ？」: Chika: “...More importantly, it looks like you should probably take Tachibana-san home?”
千花に言われ美華夏を見ると、俺の服の裾を掴んだまま突っ伏している。: Following Chika’s suggestion, I looked at Mikage; she was slumped over again, still clutching the hem of my clothes.
深く溜息を吐き、美華夏の脇から腕を通して身体を抱え上げる。: Sighing deeply, I slid my arm under Mikage’s and hoisted her up.
聞いているのか聞いていないのかよく解らない声を返してくる美華夏。: Mikage replied with a sound that made it unclear if she was actually listening.
元よりそのつもりだ。: I had intended to from the start.
美華夏はそれだけを耳元で囁くと、俺の肩に身体を預けてきた。: Mikage whispered that much into my ear and leaned her weight against my shoulder.
さすがにこの状態で神楽坂から赤坂までを歩くのはきつい。: Walking all the way from Kagurazaka to Akasaka in this state was going to be brutal.
美華夏「――大丈夫よ、完全に眠ったりはしないから」: Mikana: “—It's okay, I won't fall completely asleep.”
目を閉じたままで美華夏は言う。: Mikage spoke with her eyes still closed.
美華夏「……ちゃんと送って行って頂戴ね」: Mikana: “...Make sure you take me home properly.”
街を横切り、赤坂にある美華夏の家までやって来た頃には、既に陽は沈み、辺りは闇に沈: By the time we crossed the city and reached Mikage’s house in Akasaka, the sun had already set and the world was bathed in darkness.
此処に来るまで、機械のように脚を動かしているだけだった美華夏を揺り起こす。: I shook Mikage awake; she had been moving her legs like a machine the whole way here.
薄目を開け、美華夏は俺の瞳を覗き込んでくる。: Opening her eyes a sliver, Mikage peered into mine.
美華夏「もう少し……呑みたい気分だし……呑んで忘れたい事もあるし……」: Mikana: “I still feel like... drinking a bit more... and there are things I want to drink away...”
琢磨の葬儀などで気が滅入っているのだろう。: She was likely depressed from Takuma’s funeral and everything else.
俺に出来るのは、精々その酒席に付き合ってやることくらいだ。: The least I could do was keep her company while she drank.
本当に解っているのか非常に疑わしい曖昧な返事で、美華夏は頷いた。: Mikage nodded with an ambiguous reply that left me very doubtful she actually understood.
美華夏の部屋まで彼女を運び、椅子に座らせる。: I carried Mikage to her room and sat her down in a chair.
テーブルの水差しから水をコップに注ぎ、美華夏に持たせる。: I poured water from a pitcher on the table and handed it to her.
美華夏「お酒なら……そこの戸棚の下にあるわよ」: Mikana: “If you want alcohol... it's in the bottom of that cabinet.”
美華夏の指差す方向には壁しかない。: Mikage was pointing toward a plain wall.
黒い洋服が衣紋掛けに吊されている。: A black dress hung on a rack nearby.
ぼやきながらその近くの戸棚を開ける。: Grumbling, I opened the nearby cabinet.
……絹の下着がぎっしりと入っていた。: ...It was stuffed full of silk lingerie.
別の扉を開けて、漸く洋酒の壜を見つける。: I opened another door and finally found a bottle of Western liquor.
コルク栓を抜いて中身を確認する。封を切ってそれほど時間が経っていないのか、まだア: I pulled the cork and checked the contents. It didn't seem to have been open long; the alcohol hadn't evaporated.
奏介「――これでカシス・リキュールがあればキールも作れるんだがな」: Sousuke: “—If I had some cassis liqueur, I could make a Kir.”
ないものは仕方ない。ワイングラスは見あたらないので、タンブラー風のグラスにワイン: Can't be helped. I couldn't find any wine glasses, so I poured the wine into tumbler-style glasses.
奏介「……だからお前は呑み過ぎだって」: Sousuke: “...I told you, you've had too much.”
伸びてくる美華夏の手を払い、自分で飲む。: I brushed away Mikage’s reaching hand and took a drink myself.
美華夏にワインを渡す。: I handed the wine to Mikage.
みるみるワインが減っていく。: The wine vanished before my eyes.
奏介「……お前、酔うと性格変わるのな……」: Sousuke: “...Your personality really changes when you're drunk, doesn't it...?”
美華夏「……変わらないわよ、私は。変わったように見えるのは、それは奏介が……私の: Mikana: “...I haven't changed. If it looks that way to you, isn't it just because... you haven't been looking at me, Sousuke?”
美華夏「私は……酔っぱらっちゃったら、いつもこんなだよ？」: Mikana: “I’m... always like this when I'm drunk, you know?”
美華夏「――もうっ！ 何で気付かないのよっ！！」: Mikana: “—Geez! Why don't you ever notice?!”
美華夏「いつも奏介はそうっ！ 私の事なんか気にも留めないで、自分勝手でふらふらし: Mikana: “You're always like that, Sousuke! Never giving me a second thought, just being selfish and wandering off on your own!!”
美華夏「こんなだったら……ずっと待っていた私が……莫迦みたいじゃない……っ！！」: Mikana: “If it was going to be like this... all that time I spent waiting... I feel like a fool...!!”
美華夏の頬を涙が伝う。: Tears began to stream down Mikage’s cheeks.
美華夏「私の方が……綾音さんよりもずっと――ずっと前から――っ！？」: Mikana: “I've been... much longer than Ayane-san—way before her—?!”
そこまで捲し立て、美華夏は失言に気付いたのだろう、急に口を抑えて黙り込んだ。: She cut herself off there, seemingly realizing she’d said too much, and suddenly fell silent, covering her mouth.
美華夏「ごっ、ごめん……私、綾音さんの事は言わないって決めていたのに……」: Mikana: “S—Sorry... I promised myself I wouldn't bring up Ayane-san...”
もう――気にしても仕方のない事だ。: It's—it's not like I can do anything about it now.

柚芭「――だからあたしも、お兄ちゃんの事が好きになった。ほとんど一緒に居なかった: Yuzuha: “—That’s why I fell in love with him too. We were hardly ever together, but even so, I always wanted to be with him.”
柚芭「だからっ――絶対に、美華夏ちゃんにお兄ちゃんは渡さない」: Yuzuha: “So—I’m absolutely not giving him to you.”
美華夏の眼に涙が浮かんでいる。: Tears were visible in Mikage’s eyes.
俺は柚芭に組み敷かれながら、何も言葉を発することが出来ない。: Pinned beneath Yuzuha, I couldn't find a single thing to say.
美華夏が身体を翻し、俺たちに背中を向ける。涙が頬を伝ってこぼれ落ちるのが微かに見: Mikage turned on her heel, her back to us. I could see the tears trailing down her cheeks as she left.
無言で美華夏は部屋を出て行く。: She walked out of the room without another word.
そんな事――この状況で出来る筈もなかった。: In this situation—there was no way I could have.
柚芭「……お兄ちゃん、これでふたりきりだよ……」: Yuzuha: “...Onii-chan, now it’s just the two of us...”

柚芭「んっ……ふ、あっ……ん、ふぁ……ちゅ、んふっ……」: Yuzuha: “Nn... fu, ah... nn, fua... chup, nfu...”

柚芭「あっ……ふぁっ……、あ、あたしはっ……お兄ちゃんと一緒なら……どうなっても: Yuzuha: “Ah... fuah... I... I don't care what happens to me... as long as I’m with you, Onii-chan...”
俺は黙って彼女の身体を抱き締めた。: I silently pulled her body into a tight embrace.
ポケットにはナイフを忍ばせている。: I had the knife tucked away in my pocket.
麻布にあるという久遠の生家の場所も、大体は把握している。: I had a rough idea of where Qwon’s family home in Azabu was.
今から市電や乗合を使わずに向かえば、着く頃には日は沈んでいるだろう。: If I headed out now without using the city trams or buses, the sun would be down by the time I arrived.
葵の住処を探るには丁度良い頃だ。: The perfect time to scout out Aoi’s residence.
綾音「――何処へ行くつもり？」: Ayane: “—Where do you think you’re going?”
背後から掛けられた声に、振り返らずに答える。: I answered the voice behind me without turning around.
決意を秘めた固い声。: Her voice was firm, filled with resolve.
綾音が俺の前へ回り込む。: Ayane moved in front of me.
綾音「だからこそ、奏介をひとりになんて出来ない」: Ayane: “That’s exactly why I can't let you go alone, Sousuke.”

綾音「だったらちゃんと護ってね？」: Ayane: “Then you’d better protect me, okay?”
――どうやら、退くつもりはないらしい。: —Apparently, she had no intention of backing down.

――俺は手を差し出す事で答えた。: —I answered by reaching out my hand.

それが今は――怪しげな連中の住処となっているとは。: And now—it had become a hideout for a suspicious gang.
闇に沈んだ建造物は、近づくにつれ不気味さを増している。: The building, shrouded in darkness, felt increasingly ominous as we drew closer.
綾音「――人の出入りはあるみたいね」: Ayane: “—Looks like people have been coming and going.”
玄関前の板張りの床に、靴の跡がいくつも残っていた。: There were numerous boot prints left on the wooden floor before the entrance.
無骨な大きい足跡が目立つものの、いくつか小さな女物の靴跡もある。: While large, rough prints stood out, there were also several smaller, feminine ones.
奏介「さて――どうやって入るか」: Sousuke: “Now then—how do we get in?”

人差し指を口唇にあて、綾音は静かにドアのノブを捻った。: Placing her index finger to her lips, Ayane quietly turned the doorknob.

屋内を見渡し、俺は感想を洩らす。: I murmured as I looked around the interior.
一階だけでいくつも部屋がある。: There were several rooms on the first floor alone.

積もった埃の上に靴跡がいくつか残されているだけで、煙草の吸殻や空瓶などが転がって: Aside from a few footprints in the dust, there were only cigarette butts and empty bottles scattered about.
今のところ、人の気配はない。: So far, there was no sign of anyone.
綾音が反対側から戻って来た。: Ayane returned from the other side.

奏介「――綾音、ちょっと来てくれ」: Sousuke: “—Ayane, come here for a second.”

階下に居たような気配は無かった。: There had been no sign of her downstairs.

溜息ひとつ、美華夏は階段を下りていった。: With a sigh, Mikage headed back down the stairs.
畳の上に座り込み、部屋の中を見回す。: I sat down on the tatami and scanned the room.
布団が畳まれている以外に変わった処はない。久遠の事だから、何かあった場合にそれと: Aside from the futon being folded, nothing was out of place. Knowing Qwon, if something had happened, she probably would have left a subtle lead—
奏介「……俺が見つけられるようなモンは残さねえか」: Sousuke: “...But nothing I’d be able to find.”

広げた布団の上に大の字に寝転がる。: I sprawled out on the spread-out futon.
天井の沁みを眺めていた処で、答えなど見つからない。: Staring at the stains on the ceiling wouldn't provide any answers.
もしかしたら、ただ単にふらりと散歩に出ているだけなのかもしれない。: Maybe she’d just gone for a walk on a whim.

美華夏「色眼鏡を掛けていて人相は判らなかったらしいけど――背の高い、顎に傷痕のあ: Mikana: “He was wearing tinted glasses, so they couldn't see his face—but he was tall, with a scar on his chin—”

正義の味方を気取るつもりはない。: I had no intention of playing hero.

昇降機に乗り、美華夏は地上二十階の廊下に出た。↓これほど高い建物に足を踏み入れ: Taking the lift, Mikage stepped out into the hallway on the twentieth floor. It was her first time setting foot in a building of such dizzying height.
此処は九段下、《逢禅寺商会》が所有するビルディングだ。↓すぐ隣には二・二六事件: This was Kudanshita, a skyscraper owned by Ouzenji Trading. Standing right next to it was the Army Hall, where the Martial Law Headquarters had been stationed during the February 26 Incident.
商会の会長である逢禅寺清正氏に記者として取材を申し込んだものの、正直承けて貰え: She had requested an interview with the company’s chairman, Kiyomasa Ouzenji, under the guise of a reporter, but in all honesty, she hadn't expected him to actually grant it.
廊下には誰も居ない。↓受付で二十階へ行って欲しいと言われ、この昇降機に案内され: The hallway was deserted. At the reception, she'd simply been told to head to the twentieth floor and was guided to this elevator. She couldn't even be certain if this was actually the twentieth floor.
何せ昇降機には階数表示もなく、この廊下にも窓ひとつないのだ。↓実は地下だったと: After all, the lift lacked a floor indicator, and this corridor didn't have a single window. If they were actually deep underground, she wouldn't have been able to tell the difference.
まあ――昇降機は明らかに昇っていたから心配はないけれど。: Well—the elevator had clearly been ascending, so she wasn't too worried about that.
美華夏は辺りを見回しながらゆっくりと廊下を進んだ。: Mikage proceeded slowly down the hallway, her eyes scanning the surroundings.
廊下を歩きながら美華夏は独りごちる。: Mikage muttered to herself as she walked.
窓がないのはともかく――扉すら見あたらない。扉のように見えるのも嵌め殺しの装飾: The lack of windows was one thing—but she couldn't even see any doors. What looked like doorways were merely fixed, ornamental reliefs.
何処へ行けばいいのかも判らない。: She had no idea where she was supposed to go.
敵かもしれない相手の本拠地に乗り込んでいるという感覚が、美華夏の心に僅かばかり: The sensation of having marched straight into the heart of what might be enemy territory planted a seed of primal fear in Mikage's heart.
厭な予感に身震いし、美華夏は脚を進める。: Shuddering at a dark premonition, she pressed onward.
やっとの思いで見つけた扉を開けてみるも、薄暗い階段が下階へ向かって伸びているだ: She managed to find a door and pulled it open, but it only revealed a dimly lit staircase stretching down to the lower floors. It seemed she truly was on the top floor.
――そんな事は何の気休めにもならないけれど。: —Not that such a realization provided any actual comfort.
暫く無人の廊下を美華夏が進んでいると。: After wandering the empty corridor for a while...
逢禅寺「――何かお探しかな、お嬢さん」: Houzenji: “—Are you looking for something, young lady?”
突然背後から声を掛けられ、美華夏は恐る恐る振り返った。: Suddenly addressed from behind, Mikage whirled around, her heart hammering against her ribs.
逢禅寺「驚かせてしまったようだな」: Houzenji: “I seem to have startled you.”
天井に頭が届きそうな程の偉丈夫だった。: He was a man of immense stature, his head nearly brushing the ceiling.
彼は大仰に頷くと、美華夏に向かって右手を差し出してきた。: He gave a grand, theatrical nod and extended his right hand toward Mikage.
逢禅寺「儂が此処の会長である、逢禅寺清正だ」: Houzenji: “I am the chairman of this establishment, Kiyomasa Ouzenji.”
美華夏「よ……宜しくお願いします」: Mikana: “I... It's a pleasure to meet you.”
差し出された手を握り返す。↓ごつごつとした無骨な掌は、その容姿と相まって経営者: She took the offered hand. His palm was rough and calloused; combined with his imposing physique, he looked more like a battlefield commander than a corporate executive.
逢禅寺「お嬢さんが、今日来ると云う記者さんかな？」: Houzenji: “Are you the reporter I was told would be visiting today?”
美華夏「え……ええ、《夢譚堂》の橘といいます」: Mikana: “Y... Yes, I am Tachibana, from Mutandou.”

皇居すら見下ろせそうなのは、ともすれば不敬になってしまいそうではあるが、それが: Looking down upon the Imperial Palace itself felt almost sacrilegious, but perhaps such a privilege was permitted because of his distinguished service.

――これ以上は訊けそうにない雰囲気が逢禅寺の身体から感じられた。: —The atmosphere radiating from the man made it clear that she wouldn't be getting anything more out of him.

美華夏は心臓を鷲掴みにされたような悪寒に身体を身震いさせた。: Mikage felt a sudden, icy chill, as if an invisible hand had clenched around her heart.
それほどの気魄があの一瞬で逢禅寺から発せられていた。: Such was the sheer force of the killing intent Ouzenji had radiated in that single instant.

九段下の路上を、周囲に気を配りながら歩く。: I walked the streets of Kudanshita, keeping a sharp eye on my surroundings.
警官や憲兵に呼び止められないよう、出来る限りさりげなく貌を隠し、目立たぬよう目的: I kept my face hidden as naturally as I could to avoid being stopped by the police or the Kenpeitai, making my way toward the destination while staying as inconspicuous as possible.
今頃美華夏は例のビルに潜入し、情報を集めているだろう。: By now, Mikage should be inside that building, gathering information.
その努力を無にしない為にも、俺は周りに怪しまれてはならないのだ。: I couldn't afford to arouse any suspicion and let her efforts go to waste.
こんな昼間から、こんな繁華街の広い通りにぽつんと屋台を出している妙なタコ焼き屋が: Right in the middle of this busy commercial district in broad daylight, there was a single, bizarre takoyaki stand sitting all by itself on the wide street.
仕方なしに声を掛ける。: I called out to him, having no other choice.

奏介「まあ――時間が来るまで暇だからな」: Sousuke: “Well—I'm just killing time until my appointment.”

……変な親父に言われちゃおしまいだ。: ...Coming from a strange old man like him, that’s rich.

湯気があがり旨そうではある。: Steam rose from them, and they looked delicious.
……いや、金がねえから要らないって訳ではない。: ...It’s not like I don't have the money.
だが親父は強引に皿を俺の手に押しつけてくる。: But the old man forcibly pressed the plate into my hand anyway.

いやそれ処の騒ぎではなく歯を立てた途端に中で固まっていない熱されたどろどろの小麦: No, "hot" didn't even begin to cover it. The moment I bit down, a torrent of scalding, uncooked, gooey flour-paste flooded my mouth, searing my tongue and numbing my entire oral cavity.
吐き出す訳にもいかずかと言って飲み込める筈もなく俺は苦痛にのたうち回って居たのだ: I wanted to vomit so badly I thought I might die, but I couldn't exactly spit it out in front of him, yet there was no way in hell I could swallow it either.
文句のひとつも言ってやりたいのだが口は開けず奇妙な呻き声を出していればこのタコ焼: I was practically writhing in agony, but the old man just watched me with a satisfied nod, which was so infuriating I wanted to deck him or at least scream every curse word I knew, but I couldn't even open my mouth, so I just let out a series of strangled moans that should have communicated exactly how dangerous this takoyaki was, but the old man seemed completely oblivious—
――思わず言ってしまった。: —The words burst out before I could stop them.
親父の貌が歪む。いや、元から歪んでいるのかもしれないが。: The old man’s face twisted. Or maybe it was just naturally like that.

俺――此処で殺されるかもしれない。: I—I might actually get killed right here.

親父「……これだから他人は信じられねえんだ……顔馴染みだからおまけもつけてやった: Old Man: “...This is why I can't trust anyone... I even gave you an extra one because I recognized your face, and here you are calling it disgusting...”
奏介「……親父、自分で焼いたモン喰った事あるのか？」: Sousuke: “...Old man, have you actually tasted your own cooking?”

親父の前に皿を突き出す。: I thrust the plate back at him.

親父は呟きながら屋台を片付け始める。: The old man muttered as he began to pack up his stand.

親父「……悪かったな兄さん、こんな不味いモン喰わせてよ……」: Old Man: “...Sorry about that, kid. Feeding you such trash...”
親父「上海の本社でみっちり研修を受けるとするよ――それじゃな」: Old Man: “I’ll head to the headquarters in Shanghai and undergo some rigorous training—see you around.”
それだけ言うと、親父は呆然と佇む俺を置き去りにして屋台を引いて何処かへ行ってしま: With that, the old man dragged his stand away, leaving me standing there in a daze.

奏介「タコ焼きって……確か大阪発祥じゃなかったのか……？」: Sousuke: “Takoyaki... wasn't that supposed to have originated in Osaka...?”
何故上海へ行く。: Why the hell is he going to Shanghai?

――本当に関係ない事を心から祈る。: —I truly, desperately hope it has nothing to do with me.
こんな親父の屋台がひとつ潰れた処で、俺の知ったこっちゃない。: Whether this old man’s stand goes under or not is none of my concern.
俺は親父の前を通り過ぎ、美華夏が待ち合わせに指定したカフェの方へ歩を進めた。: I brushed past where he’d been and headed toward the cafe Mikage had chosen for our meeting.
いい年した親父の哀しげな表情なんざ俺の目には全く入らなかった――事にした。: I decided that the old man's lonely, pathetic expression didn't register in my vision at all.
表が見える窓際の席に腰を下ろし、外を眺める。: I took a seat by the window and stared outside.
道路を挟んだ向こう、皇居がある方角に高いビルディングが聳え立っている。: Across the road, in the direction of the Imperial Palace, a tall skyscraper loomed.
其処こそ、《逢禅寺商会》が所有している建物だ。: That was the building owned by Ouzenji Trading.
すぐ隣には先の《二・二六事件》の際に戒厳司令部が置かれた軍人会館がある。: Right next to it was the Army Hall, the former site of the Martial Law Headquarters during the February 26 Incident.
美華夏の調査によると、《逢禅寺商会》の建物にも多くの陸軍関係の事務所が軒を連ねて: According to Mikage’s investigation, the Ouzenji building also housed numerous offices related to the Imperial Army.
その美華夏は今――《逢禅寺商会》へ乗り込んでいる。: And right now, Mikage was inside that very building.
当然の事ながら、記者として、商会の会長――逢禅寺清正氏への取材と称してはいるが。: Of course, she was there under the guise of a reporter interviewing the chairman, Kiyomasa Ouzenji.
舌打ちし、冷やし珈琲を啜る。酸味も苦みも全くない、水とさほど変わらないような味だ: I clicked my tongue and sipped my iced coffee. It had no acidity or bitterness whatsoever; it tasted like slightly tainted water.
千花の淹れた珈琲の方が遥かに旨い。: The coffee Chika brewed was leagues ahead of this swill.
カフェの扉が開き、疲れた様子の美華夏が入ってきた。: The cafe door opened, and a weary-looking Mikage walked in.
軽く手を挙げて場所を知らせる。: I raised a hand to catch her eye.
盛大に溜息を吐き、美華夏は向かいの椅子に腰を下ろすと、俺が飲んでいた冷やし珈琲を: Letting out a massive sigh, Mikage slumped into the chair opposite me and drained my iced coffee in a single gulp.
肩を竦めて美華夏はぼやく。: Mikage shrugged and began to complain.
美華夏「殺されるかと思ったわ……」: Mikana: “I thought I was going to be killed...”
奏介「……だから危険だと言っただろ」: Sousuke: “...That’s why I told you it was dangerous.”
美華夏「そうじゃないのよ。会長の逢禅寺さんの――気魄と云うか、そう云うのが……凄: Mikana: “It wasn't that. It was the chairman, Mr. Ouzenji—his presence, his aura... it was overwhelming.”
美華夏「仕方ないでしょ、私だって何と言っていいのか判らないんだから。とにかく、彼: Mikana: “I can't help it, I don't know how else to describe it. Regardless, he definitely knows something.”
美華夏「久遠ちゃんじゃなくて……彼、マスターの名前を出したら一瞬反応したわ。知ら: Mikana: “Not Qwon-chan... but when I brought up the Master’s name, he reacted for a split second. He claimed he didn't know him—but I'm certain he does. I just feel it.”
関係はあると思っていたが――: I figured there was a connection—
美華夏「それは……訊けなかったわ。でも仕方ないでしょう？ 訊ける雰囲気じゃなかっ: Mikana: “I... I couldn't ask about that. But can you blame me? The atmosphere just wouldn't allow it.”
奏介「まあ……危険な事にならなくてなによりだ」: Sousuke: “Well... I'm just glad you made it back in one piece.”
美華夏はもう一度溜息を吐くと、再度グラスの珈琲に口を付けた。: Mikage sighed again and brought the empty glass to her lips once more.
奏介「その逢禅寺って奴がマスターの事を知っているならば、久遠の居場所も知っている: Sousuke: “If this Ouzenji guy knows the Master, there’s a chance he knows where Qwon is too. If we can just find a way to make him talk—”
――と、俺はひとつの可能性に思い至った。: —And then, a possibility occurred to me.
マスターだけでなく、葵とも関わりのある人物――: A man connected to both the Master and Aoi—
奏介「なあ美華夏、その逢禅寺ってのはどう云う奴だった？」: Sousuke: “Hey, Mikage. What was this Ouzenji guy like?”
美華夏「如何にも元軍人さんって感じだったわよ。あ、あと……眼帯をしていたわ」: Mikana: “He felt like a career military man through and through. Oh, and... he was wearing an eyepatch.”
《清鳳会》のボスと同一人物か――: He was the same person as the boss of the Seifuu-kai—
やれやれ――でかい会社の社長のくせに、ちんけな組織のボスまでやってるとは。: Good grief—to think the president of a major corporation was also moonlighting as the boss of a small-time criminal outfit.
どちらが本当の姿なのか――: I wondered which one was his true face—
奏介「……いよいよ怪しくなってきたな」: Sousuke: “...Things are finally starting to look suspicious.”
美華夏「これからどうするの？」: Mikana: “What are you going to do now?”
奏介「……そうだな、やるしかねえか」: Sousuke: “...Well, I guess I've got no choice.”
今の俺には、それ以外手は残されていない。: It was the only lead I had left.
奏介「今の処手懸かりが掴めそうなのはその逢禅寺って奴が居る建物だけだ。何とか侵入: Sousuke: “The only place I'm likely to find a lead is that building where Ouzenji is. I've got to find a way inside.”
奏介「――そう心配そうな面するんじゃねえよ」: Sousuke: “—Don't give me that worried look.”
幾分声の調子を落とし、美華夏は表情を曇らせる。: Mikage’s voice dropped, her expression clouding over.
美華夏「それにどうやって中に入るつもりなのよ？」: Mikana: “And how exactly do you plan on getting inside?”
美華夏「……どうせ何も考えてないんでしょ？」: Mikana: “...You don't have a plan at all, do you?”
美華夏「いっつも奏介はそう。行き当たりばったりで後先考えないで……そんなので上手: Mikana: “You’re always like this, Sousuke. Just rushing in without a second thought... don't think for a second that things will just magically work out.”
奏介「……何か手はあるのか？」: Sousuke: “...Do you have a better idea?”
美華夏「あるわよ。何の為にあのビルの中を見回ったと思ってるのよ」: Mikana: “I do. Why do you think I spent all that time looking around the building?”
奏介「……迷ってたんじゃねえのか？」: Sousuke: “...I thought you were just lost?”
美華夏「そっ――そんな事ある筈ないじゃない、もぉ……」: Mikana: “O—Of course I wasn't lost, honestly...!”
しどろもどろになって美華夏は勢いよく珈琲を啜る。: Flustered, Mikage took a large gulp of the non-existent coffee.
美華夏「――っ！？ こほ、けほっ……へっ、変な事言うからっ……」: Mikana: “*Cough*, *hack*... th-that’s because you said something stupid...!”
奏介「……俺は悪くねえぞ」: Sousuke: “...I'm not the one who tried to drink from an empty glass.”
ハンカチを手渡しながら弁解する。: I offered her a handkerchief while making my excuse.
口元を拭い、美華夏は居住まいを正す。: Wiping her mouth, Mikage straightened her posture.
美華夏「あの建物……大きさの割にあまり人がいないようなの。見つからないように入る: Mikana: “That building... for its size, there weren't many people around. Slipping inside without being seen wouldn't be that difficult. But—”
美華夏「人が少ない分、見られてしまえばすぐに不審者だと気付かれてしまうわ」: Mikana: “Precisely because there are so few people, if you *are* seen, you’ll be marked as a suspicious intruder immediately.”
見つからなければいいだけの話なのだが、そう簡単に巧く行く事はないだろう。: The trick was simply not to be found, but easier said than done.
奏介「……で、お前は何か誤魔化せるような手段を持っていると云う事か」: Sousuke: “...So, are you saying you have a way to avoid detection?”
美華夏「誤魔化すと云うか――見つからないような道を見つけただけなんだけれどね」: Mikana: “It’s not so much a trick as it is that I found a path that shouldn't be guarded.”
美華夏「あのビルは二十階もあるわ。エレベータもいくつかあるようで、最上階直通のも: Mikana: “That building has twenty floors. There are several elevators; some go straight to the top, while others only stop at the middle floors.”
美華夏「最上階まで行くのは一基だけ。それにさえ乗る事が出来れば――社長室まで見つ: Mikana: “Only one goes all the way to the top. If we can just get on that one—we can reach the chairman's office undetected.”
美華夏「でも――行くしかないでしょう？」: Mikana: “But—we don't have any other choice, do we?”
奏介「先に言っておくが――中に侵入するのは俺だけだぞ」: Sousuke: “Just so we're clear—I'm the only one going inside.”
美華夏の機先を制してそう告げる。: I cut her off before she could even start.
案の定、美華夏は口を半開きにして固まってしまった。: As expected, Mikage froze, her mouth agape.
奏介「当たり前だろ。ンな処にお前を連れて行けるか」: Sousuke: “Of course. There’s no way in hell I'm taking you with me.”
美華夏「でっ、でもっ！ 奏介ひとりだけでどうやって中に入ろうって云うのよ！？」: Mikana: “B-But! How are you supposed to find your way around in there all by yourself?!”
何とか隙を見つけて行くつもりだが――: I’d planned to find an opening, but—
そんな危険な場所に、美華夏を連れて行くわけにはいかない。: I couldn't bring Mikage into a place that dangerous.
美華夏「……奏介ひとりにすると、無茶をしそうで厭なのよ」: Mikana: “...I hate the idea of leaving you alone, Sousuke. You’d just do something reckless.”
美華夏「そもそも――あんなビルに侵入するって事が無茶な事なんだけれどね」: Mikana: “Infiltrating a building like that is the very definition of reckless, you know.”
ほんの少しだけ美華夏は苦笑混じりの笑みを浮かべた。: Mikage let out a tiny, wry smile.
奏介「ま――それもそうだな」: Sousuke: “Well—I guess you've got me there.”
笑える事なんざ何ひとつない筈なのに。: There wasn't a single thing to laugh about, and yet here we were.
美華夏「――絶対に、私は行くからね」: Mikana: “—I’m coming with you, no matter what.”
俺の目を睨むように見据え、美華夏は言い切った。: She stared me down, her gaze fixed and unyielding.
こう云う態度を取った場合の美華夏は、梃子でも動かなくなる。: When Mikage got like this, no amount of arguing would move her an inch.
奏介「……足手まといになるんじゃねえぞ」: Sousuke: “...Just make sure you don't slow me down.”
決意を秘めた表情で、美華夏はしっかりと頷いた。: With an expression of grim resolve, Mikage gave a firm nod.
俺たちは、夜が更けるのを待って行動を開始した。: We waited for night to fall before making our move.
闇の中でビルディングが不気味に佇んでいる。: The building stood in the darkness, a looming, ominous presence.
靖国通りの街路灯が射し込まない暗がりを選びながらビルへ近づく。: We approached the building, sticking to the shadows where the streetlights of Yasukuni-dori couldn't reach.
軍人会館や靖国神社の方に憲兵らしき警護がいるのが遠目にも判った。: Even from a distance, I could see guards who looked like Kenpeitai stationed around the Army Hall and Yasukuni Shrine.
目的のビルに、警備のようなものはついていないようだ。: Fortunately, there didn't seem to be any security around our target building.
明かりの点いている窓もない。: Not a single window showed any light.
果たして――こんなでかい建物が無人である事など有り得るのだろうか。: Was it even possible for a massive building like this to be completely empty?
それだけを言うと、美華夏は物陰に隠れながらビルの入口――正確にはその隣にある通用: With only those words, Mikage crept toward the building’s entrance—more specifically, a small service door tucked away next to it.
俺は辺りを窺いながら、誰かが来る事がないか見張っていた。: I kept a lookout, scanning the area for any sign of movement.
月が、雲の切れ間から覗いていた。: The moon peered out from behind a break in the clouds.
美華夏が遠くで手招きをしている。: Mikage beckoned to me from the distance.
俺も出来るだけ目立たないように闇を縫って近づいていく。: I wove through the shadows, keeping as low as possible as I approached her.
美華夏は不思議そうに――訝しげに首を傾げていた。: Mikage was tilting her head, a look of pure confusion on her face.
美華夏「誰もいないのに――鍵が掛かっていないのよ」: Mikana: “No one’s here—and the door isn't even locked.”
扉の取っ手に手をかけながら美華夏は呟く。: Mikage murmured as she rested her hand on the handle.
美華夏「……そうね。でも……罠だとも云えないわ。だって――私たちが今晩此処へ来る: Mikana: “...Probably not. But... I don't think it's a trap either. After all—no one should have known we were coming tonight.”
奏介「……中に誰か居る可能性は高いな」: Sousuke: “...There’s a high chance someone *is* inside, though.”
美華夏「……そうね。気を付けて行かないと」: Mikana: “...True. We’ll have to be careful.”
僅かに扉を開き、中の様子を窺いながら美華夏は言う。: Mikage spoke as she nudged the door open a crack and peered inside.
――退くつもりは全くないようだ。: —She clearly had no intention of backing down.
俺には建物の構造など全く判らない。: I had no idea how the building was laid out.
美華夏が俺の手を引いて前を歩いている。: Mikage took my hand and led the way.
闇に目が慣れてきて、朧気ながら今居る処の様子が見えるようになってきた。: As my eyes adjusted to the gloom, I began to get a vague sense of our surroundings.
美華夏が口元に指をあて、進行方向を指し示す。: Mikage pressed a finger to her lips and pointed ahead.
エレベータホールだろうか、廊下が広がっている空間に照明が灯っている。: Up ahead, in what looked like an elevator hall, the lights were on.
美華夏が無言で後退するよう促してくる。: Mikage silently gestured for us to retreat.
ホールから離れ、ふたりで貌を突き合わせる。: Once we were back in the dark, we put our heads together.
美華夏「危険ではあるだろうけど、ね……」: Mikana: “That’s going to be dangerous, though...”
階段では隠れる場所がない。昇る際には足音も響くだろうし、他の階から階段を使おうと: The stairs offered nowhere to hide. Our footsteps would echo as we climbed, and there was always the risk of running into someone using the stairs from another floor.
奏介「……他に手はねえだろ」: Sousuke: “...Do we have any other choice?”
短く美華夏は言うと、再び俺の手を引いて歩き出した。: Mikage spoke briefly, then took my hand and started walking again.
暫く廊下を進むと、階段室の扉があった。: We followed the corridor for a while until we reached the stairwell door.
中は廊下とは比べものにならないほど暗い。: Inside, it was even darker than the hallway.
美華夏「たぶん……二十階まで続いているはずよ」: Mikana: “It should lead all the way to the twentieth floor.”
奏介「何にせよ行くしかねえだろ」: Sousuke: “Well, I guess we've just gotta keep going.”
目配せし合い、俺たちは階段を昇り始めた。: We shared a nod and began the long climb.
闇の中で、美華夏の吐息だけが微かに聞こえる。: In the silence, I could hear only the faint sound of Mikage's breathing.
足音も立てないようにゆっくりと、一段ずつ昇っていく。: We climbed slowly, one step at a time, making sure not to let our footsteps ring out.
十数階までは来ている筈だ。: We must have reached at least the mid-teens by now.
頭上に拡がる闇も、心なしか薄く晴れてきたような気がする。: The darkness above seemed to be thinning, just a little.
そして――階段は無くなり、目の前に扉が現れた。: And then—the stairs ended, and a door appeared before us.
声を潜め、美華夏が呟く。: Mikage whispered, her voice barely audible.
他の階と同じような廊下が延びている。: A corridor similar to the ones on the other floors stretched out before us.
他の階と同じように人影は無かった。: And like the others, there was no sign of life.
その所為か、小声で囁いても辺り一面に響きそうで、俺たちはほとんど言葉を交わさずに: Perhaps because of that, even the slightest whisper felt like it would echo through the entire building; we barely spoke at all, communicating through nods and gestures as we moved down the hallway.
ここでも美華夏は俺の手を引き、先頭で歩いていく。: Mikage kept her lead, her hand still gripping mine.
目の前にある大きな扉を見据え、俺は鼻を鳴らした。: I stared at the massive door before us and snorted.
美華夏が少しだけ扉を開く。: Mikage nudged the door open just a crack.
美華夏は中を覗き込むと、するりと身体を扉の奥へ滑り込ませた。: Mikage peered inside, then slipped through the opening.
だだっ広い空間が広がっている。: A vast, open space lay before us.
ビルの一室とは思えないほど、広大な空間だ。: It was so large it was hard to believe it was just a room in a building.
奥の壁は硝子張りになっているらしく、外の街灯がぽつぽつと灯っているのが見える。: The far wall was entirely glass; outside, the city lights dotted the darkness.
近づくと、眼下は皇居の北の丸のようだ。麹町あたりの街並みが微かに判別できた。: As I approached the window, I realized we were looking down at the Kitanomaru park by the Imperial Palace. I could just make out the streets around Kojimachi.
奏介「――十二階を思い出すな」: Sousuke: “—Reminds me of the Twelve Stories.”
美華夏「浅草にあった凌雲閣の事？」: Mikana: “You mean the Ryounkaku in Asakusa?”
外を眺めていた美華夏が振り返る。: Mikage turned around from the window.
奏介「餓鬼の頃に親父に連れられて行ったきりだけどな。浅草の街並みが見渡せたんだが: Sousuke: “My old man took me there once when I was a kid. You could see all of Asakusa from there—but I bet this is even higher.”
美華夏「……あまりいい趣味とは思えないけど。御所を見下ろすなんて不遜だとは思わな: Mikana: “...I don't think much of his taste. Doesn't he think it's sacrilegious to look down on the Palace?”
奏介「元軍人だとか言ってたが――案外大それた事でも考えているのかもな」: Sousuke: “He said he was an ex-military man—maybe he’s got grander ambitions than we thought.”
美華夏「主義主張ははっきりしているみたいだったけど……人は解らないわね」: Mikana: “His convictions seemed clear enough... but you never truly know what's in a person's heart.”
奏介「ま――今の俺たちには関係ねえさ」: Sousuke: “Well—it’s none of our business right now.”
窓際から離れ、部屋の中程にある机へ歩み寄る。: I moved away from the window and approached the desk in the center of the room.
小さい机だと思ったのだが、それはどうやら部屋が広すぎて比較する物がなかったからの: I’d thought it was small at first, but that was only because the room was so massive there was nothing to compare it to.
近づくと天板だけで畳二枚分はあるような程でかくて重厚な机だった。: Up close, the desktop alone looked like it was the size of two tatami mats—it was a heavy, imposing piece of furniture.
奏介「――良いモノを喫ってやがる」: Sousuke: “—Smoking the good stuff, I see.”
机の上に置かれていた木箱を覗き込み、中の葉巻を手に取る。: I peered into the wooden box on the desk and picked up one of the cigars inside.
美華夏「それは関係ないでしょ」: Mikana: “That has nothing to do with this.”
すぐさま美華夏に葉巻を取り上げられる。: Mikage immediately snatched the cigar from my hand.
美華夏「ほら、何か少しでも久遠ちゃんの手懸かりを見つけないと――」: Mikana: “Come on, we have to find some kind of lead on Qwon-chan—”
そう言いながら美華夏は机の引き出しを開け、中の書類を一枚ずつ調べ始めた。: Mikage began pulling open drawers and leafing through the documents inside.
美華夏「そんなにのんびり出来ないんだから」: Mikana: “We don't have time to waste.”
俺は机の上に視線を向けた。: I turned my attention to the desktop.
葉巻が入っていた木箱の他に、いくつか書類の束がある。捲ってみると何かの帳簿らしく: Besides the cigar box, there were several stacks of papers. I leafed through them; they seemed to be ledgers, filled with nothing but columns of numbers.
内容は解らない。どうせ解った処で、久遠の居場所には繋がらないだろう。: I couldn't make sense of them. Even if I could, I doubted they’d lead me to Qwon.
他には――机の周りには何もない。: Aside from that—there was nothing else around the desk.
部屋の中央から離れ、壁際を調べる。: I moved away from the center of the room and began checking the walls.
窓のない壁には本棚が並んでいた。ここから何かを探すと云うのは難しい。: The windowless walls were lined with bookshelves. Finding anything useful here seemed like a hopeless task.
ふと、棚の一箇所に写真立てが置かれているのが目に入った。: Suddenly, my eye caught a photograph standing on one of the shelves.
軍人だろうか、数人の男たちが並んでいる写真だ。: It was a photo of several men in what looked like military uniforms.
『大正三年、青島』と小さく書かれている。: 'Taisho 3, Tsingtao' was written in small characters at the bottom.
中央に写っている長身の男――《清鳳会》のボスだ。: The tall man in the center—the boss of the Seifuu-kai.
逢禅寺と共に写っている痩躯の男――: The thin man standing next to Ouzenji—
認めたくないものだ。: I didn't want to believe it.
