見たことのない服装だ。: She’s wearing clothes I’ve never seen.
葵「今更貌を出して、何の用があるのかしら？」: Aoi: “Showing your face after all this time, what business could you possibly have?”
葵は笑みを崩そうともせず、床に叩きつけられたままの俺の身体を靴先で突く。: Without losing her smile, Aoi pokes at my body with the toe of her boot as I lie there on the floor.
踵で手の甲を踏みつけられる。: She stomps on the back of my hand with her heel.
葵「……お友達には可哀想な事をしたわね」: Aoi: “……I feel quite sorry for your little friend.”
誰を指しているのかは明白だ。: It’s obvious who she’s talking about.
奏介「やっぱり手前ェが……綾音をっ！！」: Sousuke: “It was you after all…… Ayane was!!”
踏まれた手がぎしぎしと痛む。: My trampled hand creaks with pain.
葵「――だとしたらどうするのかしら？」: Aoi: “――And if I did, what are you going to do about it?”
渾身の力をこめて葵の脚を振り払う。: With every ounce of my strength, I shake off Aoi’s leg.
葵に掴み掛かろうとした、その時。: Just as I move to lung at her.
踏まれていた手の甲で拭う。: I wipe it with the back of the hand that was just stepped on.
目にも汗が流れ込んできた。: The sweat flows into my eyes.
頭の右側に鈍い衝撃。: A dull impact hits the right side of my head.
視界が半分奪われた。: Half of my vision is snatched away.
受け身すらとれず、顔面から床に落ちる。: Unable to even brace myself, I fall face-first onto the floor.
歪んだ視界の先に、誰かの靴。: In my distorted vision, I see someone’s boots.
真っ暗な世界に、一瞬綾音の姿が見えたような気がしたが、そんな筈はない。: In a world of total darkness, for a moment, I thought I saw Ayane’s figure, but that’s impossible.
唐突に、死を意識した。: Abruptly, I become conscious of death.
奏介「――そうだな、寂しい事はねえだろな」: Sousuke: “――I guess so, it won’t be lonely.”
俺は地獄へ堕ちるかもしれないが。: Though I might be falling straight to hell.
残した者に向け、呟く。: I mutter to those I’ve left behind.
惜しむらくは――綾音と俺を殺した奴を道連れに出来なかった事か。: The only regret――is that I couldn’t take the one who killed Ayane and me down with me.
葵「さようなら玖藤奏介。なかなか――愉しませてもらったわよ」: Aoi: “Goodbye, Kudou Sousuke. You certainly――entertained me.”
朧気に残っていた触覚が、額に押しつけられる物を感じた。: My fading sense of touch feels something pressed against my forehead.
部屋の隅、中に入ったばかりの俺からは死角になっていた柱の陰に、その男は佇んでいた: In the corner of the room, in a blind spot from where I had just entered, a man stood in the shadow of a pillar.
マスター――護堂は、俺の問いに答えずに煙草の煙を吐き出した。: Master――Godou, exhaled cigarette smoke without answering my question.
護堂「……厭な世の中になったもんだ」: Godou: “……The world’s become a nasty place.”
奏介「マスター……店を放って何してやがった……？」: Sousuke: “Master…… what were you doing, abandoning the shop……?”
護堂「――いろいろさ。やらねばならん事が多過ぎてな」: Godou: “――Various things. There were too many matters that needed attending to.”
煙草の灰が床に落ちる。: Cigarette ash falls to the floor.
バーで煙草を喫おうとすると、マスターは厭な貌をするのだが――: Whenever I tried to smoke in the bar, Master would make a face――
護堂「――もう喫わないと決めていたんだが」: Godou: “――I had decided not to smoke anymore.”
バットの包みを弄んでいたマスターが貌を上げる。: Fiddling with a pack of Bat cigarettes, Master looks up.
何時もと同じ、バーのカウンタに居るマスターだ。: It’s the same Master as always, the one behind the bar counter.
煙草を投げて寄越され――俺は手を伸ばした。: He tosses a cigarette my way――and I reach out for it.
刀を鞘に収め、マスターは苦み走った表情で呻いた。: Sheathing his sword, Master groans with a look of bitter resentment.
急激に身体から力が抜け、膝から床に落ちる。: The strength suddenly drains from my body, and I fall to my knees on the floor.
違和感に腹に手を当てる。: Feeling an odd sensation, I press my hand to my stomach.
床に赤い水溜まりが広がっていく。: A red puddle spreads across the floor.
上体が床に崩れる。: My upper body collapses onto the floor.
真夏のはずなのに――寒い。: It should be midsummer――but it’s so cold.
目の前に、火の点いた煙草が落ちてきた。: In front of me, the lit cigarette falls.
暖まれるだろうか？: I wonder if I can get warm?
革靴が吸殻を踏みつぶす。: A leather shoe crushes the cigarette butt.
護堂「……人を殺すってのはいつでもそうだ。苦いモンだよ」: Godou: “……Killing a person is always like this. It’s a bitter thing.”
遥か、遠くから、聞こえて――: Sounds like it’s coming from somewhere far, far away――
千花に別れを告げ、通りへ出る。: After saying goodbye to Chika, I head out into the street.
今日は酔客の姿も少ないようだ。: There don’t seem to be many drunken customers today.
のんびりと道の中程を歩く。: I walk down the middle of the road at a leisurely pace.
屋台がいくつか出ているようだが、冷やかすつもりもなく素通りする。: A few stalls are set up, but I pass them by without any intent to browse.
ふらついている間に、狭い路地裏に入り込んでしまった。: While wandering, I find myself in a narrow back alley.
厭な処へ来てしまった。: I’ve come to a nasty place.
あの雨の夜、俺は此処で狂った女に襲われた。: That rainy night, I was attacked by a crazed woman right here.
あれさえなければ、俺は今も普段通り過ごせていただろう。: If it hadn't been for that, I’d still be living my life as usual.
振り返り――一瞬、息を止めた。: I turn around――and for a moment, my breath catches.
路地を塞ぐように琢磨が立っていた。: Takuma stands there, blocking the alley.
琢磨「今までは泳がせて様子を窺ってはいたが――もう終わりだ」: Takuma: “I’ve let you run and watched how things played out until now――but it’s over.”
苦虫を噛み潰したような表情で言い放つ琢磨。: Takuma says with an expression like he’s chewing on a bitter bug.
琢磨の背後、俺の後ろにも背広姿の私服警官と思しき連中が現れる。: Behind Takuma, and behind me as well, men who appear to be plainclothes police officers in suits appear.
狭い道で逃げ場もない。: In this narrow path, there’s no way to escape.
琢磨「……犯罪者を捕まえる事が、俺の――警察の仕事だ」: Takuma: “……Catching criminals is my――the police’s job.”
だったら――そんな苦しげな面をするんじゃねえよ。: If that’s the case――don’t make such a pained face.
琢磨が数歩近づく。: Takuma takes a few steps closer.
ここが年貢の納め時か――: I guess this is the end of the line――
私服刑事の向こうに――奴の姿を見てしまった。: Beyond the plainclothes detectives――I see her.
琢磨のこの態度――《清鳳会》からの圧力か――: Takuma’s attitude――is this pressure from the Seihoukai?――
奏介「巫山戯ンなよ……畜生が……」: Sousuke: “Don’t screw with me…… you bastards……”
肩に掛かった琢磨の手を振り払う。: I shake off Takuma’s hand on my shoulder.
鳩尾に拳を叩き込む。: I drive my fist into his solar plexus.
膝を付く琢磨の横をすり抜け、私服刑事達の間を抜けようとする。: I slip past the kneeling Takuma and try to push through the detectives.
それを避けつつ、葵を捕まえようと――: Dodging them, I try to catch Aoi――
腕を取られ、地面に叩きつけられる。: My arm is grabbed, and I’m slammed to the ground.
幾重にも刑事達がのし掛かってくる。: The detectives pile onto me one after another.
葵をぶちのめさないと、気が済まねえ――: If I don’t beat the hell out of that bitch Aoi, I won’t be satisfied――
挑発的な葵の嘲笑が耳に届く。: Aoi’s provocative, mocking laughter reaches my ears.
この先どんな運命が俺を待ち構えていようと、此奴だけは決して赦す訳にはいかないと心: I swore in my heart that no matter what fate awaited me, I would never forgive this woman.
「私が生きている事に意味はありません」: “There is no meaning in my being alive.”
「私が生きる意味は、貴女を超える事」: “The meaning of my existence is to surpass you.”
「いつも姉様は私の数歩先を行く」: “You are always several steps ahead of me.”
「それは貴女が私の数歩後を歩いているからです」: “That is because you are walking several steps behind me.”
「この世界で一番罪深い事とは何でしょうか」: “What is the most sinful thing in this world?”
「世界は功罪を識りません。罪悪を認識するのは人間のみ」: “The world knows no merit or sin. Only humans recognize transgression.”
「ならば――人間が認識し得る最大の罪悪は」: “Then――what is the greatest transgression a human can recognize?”
「――死ぬ事は功績でもなんでもありません。＄単なる停止状態、それだけです」: “――Dying is not an achievement or anything of the sort. $It is merely a state of cessation, nothing more.”
「私は何も選びません。強いて選ぶとしたら――＄貴女が選んだもう一方を選択するでし: “I choose nothing. If I were forced to choose――$I suppose I would select the opposite of whichever you picked.”
「私が生きる事を選べば、姉様は死ぬのですね」: “If I choose to live, then you will die, Sister.”
「意地悪ですね、貴女は。ふふ……さすがは私の妹です」: “You are quite malicious, aren't you. Fufu…… you truly are my sister.”
「……私は姉様ほど意地悪くありません」: “……I am not as malicious as you, Sister.”
ほんの少しだけ早く母の胎内から出て行った姉、永遠。: My sister, Towa, who left our mother’s womb just a moment before me.
数瞬遅れて空気を吸った私は産声も上げなかった。↓姉が泣いているのを見て、人前で: I, who inhaled the air a few seconds later, did not even cry out. I felt that shedding tears in front of others was a shameful thing after seeing my sister weeping.
後に姉は言った。↓私が泣いていたのはこの世に生を受けたのを嘆いたから。↓生まれ: Later, my sister said. That the reason she was crying was because she lamented being brought into this world. That she regretted ever being born.
私と姉は瓜二つだった。↓少なくとも自分たち以外、両親ですらも私たちの判別が出来: My sister and I were two peas in a pod. At least to anyone other than ourselves, even our parents could not tell us apart.
でも私と姉には決定的に違っている処があった。↓才能と云うべきなのだろうか、彼女: But there was a critical difference between my sister and me. Perhaps it should be called talent, but she was born with “something” that I did not possess.
私は姉を超える事は出来ないと、生まれた時既に悟ってしまっていた。: I realized even as I was born that I could never surpass my sister.
私たちは幼い頃より暇があれば父の書斎に篭もり、古今東西の文献を読みあさっていた: From the time we were young, whenever we had free time, we would shut ourselves in our father’s study and pore over literature from all times and places.
科学者だった父は、私たちの才能を悟ったのか、新聞社等に私たちを売り込んだ。: Perhaps our father, a scientist, realized our talent, as he marketed us to newspapers and the like.
天才姉妹現る――そんなくだらない文章で私たちは表世界へ引き摺り出された。: The Genius Sisters Appear――with such trivial sentences, we were dragged out into the public eye.
結局父は私たちの事を何も解っていなかったのだ。↓私たちは名誉も何も必要としない: In the end, our father understood nothing about us. We didn't need fame or anything of the sort. We just wanted to be able to do what we loved.
私はいつも姉の背中を見ていた。↓全く同じようでありながら絶対に追いつけない存在: I was always looking at my sister’s back. An existence that seemed identical yet was absolutely impossible to catch.
姉は私に優しかった。↓数時間の差で姉になった彼女は、それでも姉貴風を吹かせる事: My sister was kind to me. Even though she became the elder by a few hours, she naturally acted as the older sister without ever putting on airs.
それが私にはたまらなく嬉しく――たまらなく悔しかった。: That made me incredibly happy――and incredibly frustrated.
周囲の人間にはふたりの区別など付きやしないのに、私たちの間には明確なる隔たりが: While the people around us couldn't distinguish between the two of us, there was a clear divide between us.
――私が彼女に対して殺意を抱くのは、至極当然の流れだった。: ――It was only natural that I would come to harbor murderous intent toward her.
ある夜、私と姉は話し合いの場を屋上に設けた。: One night, my sister and I met on the rooftop to talk.
姉はさして疑いもせずに私についてきた。: My sister followed me without the slightest suspicion.
私と同じ貌の姉を前に、私は話を切り出す。: Facing the sister who had the same face as mine, I broached the subject.
久遠「――私はふたりも要りません」: Kuon: “――I don't need two of us.”
姉も頷く。↓やはり――私と同じ考えだった。: My sister nods. As I thought――she had the same idea as me.
永遠「――ですが、私と貴女は別の人間です。遺伝子的に同一の存在であるかもしれませ: Towa: “――However, you and I are different people. We may be genetically identical, but I am me, and you are you. We are separate personalities.”
彼女は微笑を浮かべる。↓私が嗤っている。: She wears a smile. I am mocking her.
彼女が私と同じ考えを持っているのなら、彼女は私を殺そうとするだろう。↓私はふた: If she has the same thoughts as me, then she will try to kill me. Because I don't need two of us.
優しく、寧ろ愉しげに。↓彼女は私に寄り添う。: Gently, even playfully. She draws close to me.
永遠「さあ――貴女は私をどうしてくれるのですか？」: Towa: “Now――what do you intend to do with me?”
僅かに腕の力が緩む。: The strength in my arm wavers slightly.
彼女は私の手から抜け出し、屋上の縁に立つ。: She slips from my grasp and stands on the edge of the roof.
永遠「私はふたりも要らない。だから私は――」: Towa: “I don't need two of us. So I――”
姉――御巫永遠は私には真似の出来ない極上の笑顔を浮かべる。: My sister――Mikanagi Towa wears a sublime smile that I could never emulate.
姉の葬儀はしめやかに行われた。: My sister’s funeral was held quietly.
多くの人々が姉の死を惜しむ涙を流していた。: Many people shed tears, lamenting my sister’s death.
何を悲しんでいるのだろうか。↓まだ私は生きていると云うのに。: What are they grieving for? Even though I am still alive.
名も知らぬ人が泣いている。↓偽善者ばかりだ。↓貴方たちは姉様の何を識っていたと: People whose names I don't even know are crying. They are all hypocrites. What could you possibly have known about my sister?
――両親が流している涙だけは赦そうと思う。↓私たちを売り出した《天才双児姉妹》: ――I think I can forgive only the tears shed by my parents. Because they can no longer use the catchphrase of the “Genius Twin Sisters” they marketed us as.
姉が屋上から落下した後、私は正直に両親に話した。: After my sister fell from the rooftop, I told my parents the truth.
両親が信じる事はなかった。↓賢い久遠がそんな事をする筈がない、仲が良かったのだ: My parents did not believe it. They said the clever Qwon could never do such a thing, that it was impossible for me to kill her since we got along so well.
何故仲良しなら殺さないと言い切れるのだろう。: Why are they so certain that people won't kill each other if they get along?
仲が良すぎたからこそ――殺す事だってあるのに。: It’s precisely because we got along so well――that killing becomes a possibility.
私はその日を境に、更に知識を吸収するようになった。: From that day on, I began to absorb even more knowledge.
今までは姉と共に知識の蒐集にあたっていた為、それをひとりで行うのは中々に骨の折: Until now, I had worked with my sister to collect knowledge, so doing it alone was quite a laborious task.
全く同等だと自負していたのだが、やはり姉は偉大な存在だった。: I had prided myself on being completely equal to her, but my sister was a great existence after all.
私が殺してしまった所為で、もう追いつけない存在になってしまったのだけれど。: Because I killed her, she became an existence I can never catch up to.
姉が居なくなってからも、私はしばしば新聞に取り上げられた。↓類い希なる才能と持: Even after my sister was gone, I was often featured in the newspapers. I was praised as having rare talent, but I could harbor no interest in such things.
どれだけ周囲の評価が高まっても、私は一生姉を追い越す事など出来はしない。: No matter how much the evaluation around me rose, I will never be able to surpass my sister for the rest of my life.
この頃から私は鏡を見なくなった。: From around this time, I stopped looking in the mirror.
光を跳ね返す硝子の向こうに、私と同じ貌の姉が嗤っている気がした。: I felt as if my sister, with the same face as mine, was mocking me from the other side of the light-reflecting glass.
まだ追いつけないのと言っているような気がして。: As if she were saying that I still haven't caught up.
まだ生きているのと言っているような気がして。: As if she were saying that I’m still alive.
＄ 私は私の貌を生まれて初めて憎んだ。: For the first time in my life, I hated my own face.
姉が死んでから一年が過ぎた。: A year has passed since my sister died.
私は相変わらず、偏執的に知識をかき集めていた。: As always, I was obsessively gathering knowledge.
此程までに続けていたのは愉しかったからだろう。↓姉にはもう出来ない事をしている: The reason I continued to this extent must have been because it was fun. With only the pride of doing something my sister could no longer do, I pored over books mechanically.
＄ 酷い自己満足――ただの自慰行為でしかなかった。: A terrible self-satisfaction――it was nothing more than an act of masturbation.
私の知識は既に家の蔵書量を遥かに超え、食指を海外にまで伸ばすようになっていた。: My knowledge already far exceeded the amount of books in the house, and I had begun to reach out as far as overseas.
論文を読む為だけに英語、独語、仏語を覚え、趣味娯楽として伊語、蘭語、露語、中国: I learned English, German, and French just to read academic papers, and I even came to read and write Italian, Dutch, Russian, and Chinese as a hobby.
そんな私を両親は蝶よ花よと愛でるように接してきた。↓姉が居なくなった分の愛情が: My parents treated me as if they were cherishing a flower or a butterfly. The affection that would have gone to my sister came flooding toward me unilaterally.
＄ 正直、とても煩わしかった。: To be honest, it was very troublesome.
そんなある夜の事だ。: It was on a certain night.
私は外から聞こえてきた僅かな物音で目を醒ました。: I woke up at a slight noise coming from outside.
窓か扉が開いたのだろう、微かに空気の揺れも感じ、私はベッドから起きあがった。: A window or a door must have opened; I felt a slight vibration in the air and rose from my bed.
何者かがこの建物に侵入したようだ。↓押し込み強盗か説教強盗か――: It seems someone has intruded into this building. A burglar, perhaps, or a serial intruder――
この家には多くの資産がある。↓強盗がそれを狙って来てもおかしくはないのだが――: There are many assets in this house. It wouldn't be strange for a robber to come aiming for them――
どうやら物盗りではないようだ。↓歩き方に迷いがない。: Apparently, he’s not a common thief. There is no hesitation in his stride.
真っ直ぐに――この部屋へ向かってきている。: He is heading straight――for this room.
強盗ではないのなら出迎えをしなくてはならない。: If he isn't a burglar, then I must welcome him.
私は身だしなみを整え、部屋の扉の正面に立った。: I made myself presentable and stood in front of the door to the room.
扉が開き、背の高い男が姿を見せた。: The door opened, and a tall man appeared.
久遠「――お待ちしておりました」: Kuon: “――I have been waiting for you.”
機先を制し、私は男に声を掛けた。: Seizing the initiative, I called out to the man.
男は驚いたのだろう、色眼鏡の奥の目を見開き、私を凝視していた。: He must have been surprised; he widened his eyes behind his tinted glasses and stared at me.
久遠「わざわざ確認せずとも判ることではないでしょうか」: Kuon: “Is that not something you could tell without going through the trouble of confirming?”
私は少々の失望を篭めて声を発する。: I speak with a hint of disappointment.
久遠「貴方の目的は私ですね」: Kuon: “Your objective is me, isn't it?”
久遠「貴方は真っ直ぐ此処へ来ました。強盗ならばまず書斎などの金が仕舞われていそう: Kuon: “You came straight here. If you were a burglar, you would have first headed to the study or somewhere else where money was likely to be kept.”
久遠「普通の強盗でしたら私の名前を確認したりはしません」: Kuon: “An ordinary burglar would not confirm my name.”
護堂「なるほど……噂通りの才媛だ」: Godou: “I see…… a talented young lady, just as the rumors say.”
久遠「これしきの事――誰にでも予想は付きます」: Kuon: “Something of this level――anyone could have predicted it.”
私はゆっくりと男へ近づいた。↓世間では悪人と呼ばれる類の人間なのかもしれないが: I slowly approached the man. He might be the type of person society calls a villain, but such an evaluation is not a problem for me.
利用できる人間か、否か。: Whether he is a person I can utilize or not.
彼は愚直に口を噤む。↓当然だろう。↓だが私は猶も彼の目を真っ直ぐに見据えた。: He stubbornly holds his tongue. Naturally. But I still looked him straight in the eye.
久遠「そうですか。……では護堂、案内してください」: Kuon: “I see. ……Then Godou, please guide me.”
久遠「貴方は私を攫いに来たのでしょう」: Kuon: “You came here to kidnap me, didn't you?”
彼――護堂は予想通り戸惑っている。: He――Godou, is perplexed, as I expected.
私は壁際に飾られていた大きな花瓶に手を伸ばした。: I reached for a large vase decorated against the wall.
花瓶が床に落ち、ただの塵と化す。: The vase falls to the floor and turns into mere dust.
久遠「さあ――どうするのですか。今の音は屋敷中に響き――皆目覚めてしまったようで: Kuon: “Now――what will you do? That sound echoed throughout the mansion――it seems everyone has woken up.”
彼がこの先どうするのか私には判っていた。↓私の身柄を確保するまで逃げ出したりは: I knew what he would do from here on out. He would not flee until he had secured my person. Furthermore, he is not soft enough to overlook any witnesses.
護堂「……来て貰うぞ、御巫久遠」: Godou: “……You’re coming with me, Mikanagi Qwon.”
後顧の憂いを立つ必要がある。: I need to cut off any lingering worries.
＄ 二人分の足音が聞こえてくる。: The sound of two sets of footsteps can be heard.
久遠「さあ――護堂。私を失望させないでください」: Kuon: “Now――Godou. Please do not disappoint me.”
扉が開き、両親の貌が見えた。: The door opens, and I see my parents’ faces.
間近で銃声を聞いたのは初めてだった。: It was the first time I had heard a gunshot from so close.
私は護堂に腕を掴まれたままだったので、耳に直接音の波が飛びこんできた。: Because Godou was still holding my arm, the wave of sound jumped directly into my ears.
酷い耳鳴りで、両親が苦悶の声をあげて死んだのかすら判らない。: My ears are ringing terribly; I can't even tell if my parents died with a cry of agony.
護堂「……何をさせるつもりだった」: Godou: “……What did you intend for me to do?”
護堂の声は遠くから聞こえてくるようだった。: Godou’s voice seemed to come from far away.
久遠「――貴方は私の意図した通りの結果を示してくれました。それになんの不満があり: Kuon: “――You showed me the result exactly as I intended. What dissatisfaction could I possibly have with that?”
久遠「いつまでも籠の小鳥ではつまらないですから。それに、此処にはもう私が識る事の: Kuon: “Because being a caged bird forever is boring. Besides, there is nothing left here for me to know.”
護堂「……これから行く処も籠の中のような処だぞ」: Godou: “……The place we are going is also like a cage, you know.”
久遠「それでもここに残るよりは有意義です」: Kuon: “Even so, it is more meaningful than remaining here.”
私は護堂に向き直り、ネグリジェの裾を軽く摘んだ。: I turned toward Godou and lightly pinched the hem of my negligee.
久遠「私を攫いに来てくれて、ありがとうございます――おじさま」: Kuon: “Thank you for coming to kidnap me――Uncle.”
両親の血の臭いを嗅ぎながら、私は優雅に一礼した。: While smelling the scent of my parents’ blood, I gave an elegant bow.
護堂に連れられてやってきたのは、九段下にある大きなビルディングだった。: The place Godou brought me to was a large building in Kudanshita.
私はすぐ地下にある資材置き場のような倉庫に幽閉された。: I was immediately confined in a warehouse that looked like a materials storage area in the basement.
カーブしたコンクリの天井は、ここがトンネルのような地下施設の一部だと云う事を物: The curved concrete ceiling told of the fact that this was part of an underground facility like a tunnel.
住処とすれば良い待遇とは云えない。↓だが私にとって、周囲の人間との煩わしさから: It could not be called good treatment as a dwelling. However, for me, this warehouse, which freed me from the troubles of dealing with people, was an irreplaceable home.
護堂はよく私の元を訪れた。↓来る度に私と同じ年代の少女ならば喜びそうな人形や小: Godou often visited me. Each time he came, he brought dolls or trinkets that a girl of my age would likely enjoy, but I was never interested in such things.
それよりも滅多に手に入らない書物が読みたいと言い、護堂を困惑させた。: Instead, I told him I wanted to read books that were rarely obtainable, which perplexed him.
だが彼は私の要求を満たすべく立ち回り、海外の論文雑誌や軍の機密情報までも私の元: But he acted to fulfill my requests, bringing overseas academic journals and even classified military information to me.
＄ そんな日が暫く続き、私は久し振りに地下の倉庫を出る事となった。: After those days continued for a while, I came to leave the underground warehouse for the first time in a long while.
一言で云えば悪趣味な部屋だった。: In a word, it was a room in poor taste.
この部屋の主は自己顕示欲が強いのだろう、ただ広い室内に机がひとつ。↓広々と開い: The master of this room must have a strong desire for self-display; there was only one desk in the vast room. Below the wide-open window was the Kitanomaru of the Imperial Palace.
尊大な老爺――逢禅寺清正は鋭い視線で私を値踏みしてきた。: The arrogant old man――Ouzenji Kiyomasa, sized me up with a sharp gaze.
特に感情を篭めるでもなく、見つめ返す。: Without harboring any particular emotion, I looked back at him.
気に入られようとも思っていない。: I have no intention of being liked.
逢禅寺「護堂よ――この小娘の監視は貴様に任せる。例の件――必ずや成功させるように: Houzenji: “Godou――I leave the surveillance of this girl to you. That matter――make sure it succeeds.”
護堂は私を誘い、この悪趣味な部屋から連れ出した。: Godou invited me and led me out of this room in poor taste.
姓で呼ばれるのは好きではない。↓私と姉どちらが呼ばれているのか判らないからだ。: I don't like being called by my family name. Because I can't tell whether my sister or I am being called.
護堂「そ、そうか。……では久遠、今後は別の処に移ってある物の開発をして貰う事にな: Godou: “I, I see. ……Then Qwon, from now on, you will be moved to another place and have you develop a certain thing.”
私は即座に頷く。↓此処の地下は静かで快適なのだが、偶には外の光も浴びたくもなる: I nod immediately. The basement here is quiet and comfortable, but occasionally I do want to bathe in the outside light.
私が連れて来られた処は、普段は護堂が暮らしていると云う一軒家だった。: The place I was brought to was a detached house where Godou usually lived.
護堂は頷きながら珈琲を淹れている。: Godou is making coffee while nodding.
護堂「……君には此処で人の心を操る装置の開発をして貰う事になる」: Godou: “……I’m going to have you develop a device to manipulate people’s hearts here.”
護堂は話しながら珈琲を湯飲みに注ぐ。↓せめて洋風のカップにして貰いたい処だ。: Godou pours the coffee into a teacup while talking. I would prefer it if he used a Western-style cup at least.
久遠「人の心を操る装置、ですか――」: Kuon: “A device to manipulate people’s hearts, you say――”
瞬時に頭の中でいくつかの方法が思いついた。: Several methods immediately came to mind.
護堂「……そうだ。この国を良くする為には必要な物なのだ」: Godou: “……That’s right. It is something necessary to make this country better.”
自分でもそう感じていたのだろう、護堂は抗弁する事もなく押し黙る。: Perhaps he felt that way himself, as Godou fell silent without arguing back.
湯飲みの珈琲に口を付ける。↓酸味ばかりが目立っていてあまりおいしくなかった。: I take a sip of the coffee in the teacup. It wasn't very tasty, with only the acidity standing out.
久遠「――人の心を操る機械など必要ありません」: Kuon: “――There is no need for a machine to manipulate people’s hearts.”
護堂「……君ならそう云うだろうな」: Godou: “……I suppose someone like you would say that.”
護堂は怒るでもなく、苦笑すら浮かべる。: Godou does not get angry, and even wears a wry smile.
護堂「誰もが君のように優れた人心掌握術を心得ている訳ではない」: Godou: “Not everyone is as skilled in the art of psychological manipulation as you are.”
少々の驚きと共に呟く。: I mutter with a bit of surprise.
護堂「命令される事に慣れていたからな、指示を出してくれるのは正直助かっている」: Godou: “I was used to being ordered around, so it’s honestly a help to have someone give instructions.”
護堂「それが人を動かす秘訣なのかもしれんな」: Godou: “Perhaps that is the secret to moving people.”
彼はひとり合点し、旨そうに珈琲を飲んだ。: He convinced himself and drank the coffee as if it were delicious.
美味しい珈琲を淹れられるよう命令すべきなのかもしれない。: Perhaps I should order him to be able to make delicious coffee.
それから私は人の心を操る装置の研究を始めた。↓命ぜられたから仕方なく、ではない: After that, I began researching the device to manipulate people’s hearts. It wasn't that I was doing it because I was ordered to. I simply wanted to explore whether such a machine was feasible.
護堂はよく家を空ける。↓私は監視されている筈なのだが、信用されているのだろうか: Godou often leaves the house. I’m supposed to be under surveillance, but am I being trusted?
いや――逃げ出す事はないと判っているのだろう。↓確かに此処を出るつもりはない。: No――he likely knows that I have no intention of running away. Indeed, I have no intention of leaving this place.
既に私は、姉の呪縛から逃げ出したのだから。: Because I have already escaped from my sister’s curse.
人の心を操る装置を作るには問題があった。↓幾ら対象を催眠状態に持ち込んでも、厭: There was a problem in creating a device to manipulate people’s hearts. No matter how much I brought the subject into a state of hypnosis, I couldn't force them to do things they disliked. That is the limit of suggestion.
出来ない事は出来ない。↓それを実行させるには強い暗示を与えなくてはならない。↓: What cannot be done cannot be done. To make them execute it, I had to give a strong suggestion. To do that, I had to create a mentally unstable state.
人間の聴覚では認識できない低周波音を用い、人の精神を不安定にさせる。↓そうする: Using low-frequency sounds that human hearing cannot perceive, I make the person’s mind unstable. By doing so, I make them more susceptible to suggestion.
後は人の意識に影響を与える音を探す必要があった。↓クラシックの名曲などは心を震: After that, I had to find sounds that influence human consciousness. Masterpieces of classical music stir the heart, but I don't quite understand that sensation.
感受性が鈍いのかもしれない。↓自覚はしているけれど。: Perhaps my sensitivity is dull. Though I am aware of it.
目を通していた本から視線を上げず簡潔に答える。: I answer concisely without raising my gaze from the book I was reading.
護堂「今度、神楽坂に店を出す事になった」: Godou: “I’ve decided to open a shop in Kagurazaka soon.”
任務で必要なのだろう。: It must be necessary for the mission.
この時既に《清鳳会》、そして背後にある《逢禅寺商会》の事は把握していた。↓これ: By this time, I had already grasped the existence of the Seihoukai, and the Ouzenji Trading Company behind it. I could investigate things of this level without asking Godou.
護堂「――ジャズバーをやろうと思ってな」: Godou: “――I’m thinking of doing a jazz bar.”
一瞬耳を疑った。↓思わず本から貌を上げる。: For a moment, I doubted my ears. I involuntarily looked up from my book.
護堂「……そんな貌をするのは珍しいな。そんなに妙な事を言った覚えはないのだが」: Godou: “……It’s rare to see you make a face like that. I don't recall saying anything that strange.”
表情を切り替える。↓考えた事が貌に現れるとは、まだ精進が足りないようだ。: I switch my expression. It seems I still have a way to go if my thoughts are appearing on my face.
護堂「昔からの夢だ。小さな店を構えてバーテンダーをやってみたかった」: Godou: “It’s an old dream of mine. I wanted to try being a bartender in a small shop.”
私が口を挟む問題ではない。: It is not a problem for me to intervene in.
護堂「銀座で良い声の歌い手を見つけてな。こちらでも歌って貰おうと思ったんだが、そ: Godou: “I found a singer with a good voice in Ginza. I thought I’d have her sing here as well, but she told me that the condition was for me to hire a budding piano player as well.”
私には関係のない事だ。: It has nothing to do with me.
新しい店内を見渡し、私は素直な感想を洩らした。: Looking around the new interior of the shop, I let out my honest impression.
護堂は上機嫌で洋酒のボトルを棚に並べている。: Godou is in high spirits as he lines up bottles of Western liquor on the shelves.
護堂「元は蕎麦屋だったのだが、内装から全て直したからな」: Godou: “It was originally a soba shop, but I redid everything from the interior.”
カウンタのスツールに腰掛ける。: I sit on a stool at the counter.
護堂「注文を承りたい処だが――生憎まだグラスが揃っていないな」: Godou: “I’d like to take your order, but unfortunately, I don't have all the glasses yet.”
久遠「……楽しそうですね」: Kuon: “……You seem to be having fun.”
護堂「楽しいさ。好きでやっているんだからな」: Godou: “I am. Because I’m doing what I love.”
私も好きで知識を蓄えている――筈だ。: I, too, am accumulating knowledge because I love it――I should be.
そうでなければ――こんな事をしていない。: If not――I wouldn't be doing this.
護堂は腕時計を見て呟いた。: Godou muttered while looking at his wristwatch.
荷物の配送が来るのだろうか。: Perhaps a delivery of goods is coming.
護堂「久遠、済まないが先に家へ戻っていてくれないか」: Godou: “Qwon, sorry, but could you go back home first?”
護堂「此処で働く予定の女給がそろそろ来る筈だ。君は姿を現さない方がいいだろう」: Godou: “The waitress who’s scheduled to work here should be arriving soon. It’s better if you don't show yourself.”
両親が殺され、行方知れずになった筈の娘が此処に居てはおかしいだろう。: It would be strange for the daughter who was supposed to have gone missing after her parents were killed to be here.
久遠「――では護堂、また後ほど」: Kuon: “――Well then, Godou, I shall see you later.”
私が外へ出ようとしたとき、扉の方から勝手に開いた。: Just as I was about to go outside, the door opened on its own.
眼鏡を掛けた少女が息を切らせていた。↓此処で働くと云う女給だろう。: A girl wearing glasses was out of breath. She must be the waitress who’s going to work here.
護堂「……三十分ほど早いが」: Godou: “……You’re about thirty minutes early, though.”
護堂は少々表情が強張っている。↓――まあ、私と鉢合わせしないよう考えたのがあっ: Godou’s expression is a bit stiff. ――Well, it can't be helped if his plan to avoid having her run into me was easily overturned.
叱られた訳でもないのに、彼女の目尻に涙が浮かんでいる。: Even though she hasn't been scolded, tears are appearing in the corners of her eyes.
護堂「いや――構わないさ。どうせ人手が足りなかった処だ」: Godou: “No――I don't mind. I was short-handed anyway.”
そう言いつつ護堂は私に目配せをする。↓今のうちに帰れと云う事か。: While saying so, Godou gives me a look. Telling me to go home while I can.
私が横を通り過ぎようとすると、彼女はすっ、と私の肩に手を掛けてきた。: As I tried to pass by her, she reached out and placed her hand on my shoulder.
千花「ところで……マスター。この子は……？」: Chika: “By the way…… Master. This girl is……?”
頼むから其処で言い淀まないで欲しい。↓怪しまれるのは明白だ。: Please don't hesitate there. It’s obvious that she’ll be suspicious.
久遠「――久遠と申します。伯父がいつもお世話になっているようで」: Kuon: “――I am called Qwon. It seems my uncle is always in your debt.”
彼女に対して一礼する。: I give a bow to her.
千花「あ……そ、そうだったんですか……てっきり娘さんだと思ってました」: Chika: “Ah…… i, is that so…… I thought for sure you were his daughter.”
私は無言で護堂を見た。: I looked at Godou in silence.
千花「久遠ちゃんね。わたしは高梨千花、よろしくお願いしますね」: Chika: “Qwon-chan, right. I’m Takanashi Chika, it’s nice to meet you.”
……何をよろしくされればよいのだろう。: ……What am I supposed to be nice to?
バー《ＣＡＤＥＮＺＡ》はこうして開店を迎えた。: The bar CADENZA thus welcomed its opening.
護堂は夜になると家を完全に空けるようになった。↓全く――監視されている気がしな: When night fell, Godou began to leave the house entirely. I don't feel like I’m being monitored at all.
護堂自身も監視をしているつもりはないのかもしれない。: Perhaps Godou himself has no intention of monitoring me.
家で研究を続けていても良かったのだが、私は息抜きと考察を兼ねて良くバーを訪れた: I could have continued my research at home, but I often visited the bar for both a change of pace and for consideration. Of course, I sit on the back stairs so as not to be seen by other customers.
そんな折り――私はピアノの音色を耳にした。: It was on one of those occasions――I heard the sound of a piano.
レコードから流れるノイズ混じりではない、生の演奏だ。: It wasn't a record with mixed-in noise, but a live performance.
護堂は歌手のおまけでピアノ弾きも雇ったと言っていた。その演奏だろうか。: Godou had said he hired a piano player as a bonus for the singer. Is that his performance?
私はそっと立ち上がり、目立たぬよう、そっと店内を窺った。: I stood up quietly and peeked into the shop, being careful not to stand out.
若い男がピアノを弾いていた。: A young man was playing the piano.
こちらに背を向けている。↓白いシャツの広い背中がゆっくりと揺れていた。: His back was turned toward me. His broad back in a white shirt swayed slowly.
荒々しさの中に安らぎがある、不思議な演奏だった。↓曲調が問題なのではない。↓恐: It was a mysterious performance, with a sense of peace within its roughness. It wasn't a matter of the melody. Perhaps it was precisely because he was performing it that I was seized by such a deep emotion.
＄ それが――私が玖藤奏介を識った最初だった。: That was――the first time I came to know Kudou Sousuke.
私は護堂に完成したばかりの装置を見せた。: I showed Godou the device I had just finished.
少し大振りな拳銃程の大きさしかないそれを手に取り、護堂は率直な感想を洩らす。: Picking up the object, which was no larger than a bulky handgun, Godou offered his blunt impression.
久遠「大きさは問題にはなりません。バッテリの駆動時間は限られますが、手軽に持ち運: Kuon: “Size is not an issue. While the battery life is limited, I prioritized ease of transport.”
久遠「信じたくないのでしたら信じなくても結構です」: Kuon: “If you do not wish to believe it, you don't have to.”
むしろその方がこちらも楽だ。: In fact, that would make things easier for me.
護堂「いや……済まん。久遠が作ったのだから間違いはないと思うが」: Godou: “No…… forgive me. Since you made it, Qwon, I’m sure it works.”
私は嘘を吐く。↓これが完成する事など未来永劫有り得ない。: I tell a lie. This will never be ‘complete’ for as long as eternity lasts.
久遠「繰り返し苛立ちを与えるものと云う《Ｒｉｆｆ・Ｒｕｆｆｌｅ》を略した言葉です: Kuon: “It is an abbreviation of ‘Riff-Ruffle,’ meaning something that repeatedly causes irritation.”
久遠「実験をする必要があります」: Kuon: “We need to conduct an experiment.”
久遠「はい。これはまだ未完成。人を操ると云う効果はなく――ただ人を狂わせるだけの: Kuon: “Yes. As I said, it is incomplete. It has no effect for controlling people――it is merely a device that drives them mad.”
久遠「参照できるデータを集めなくてはなりません。実際に人体に対して使用した際、ど: Kuon: “We must gather reference data. Please investigate what kind of effects manifest when it is actually used on a human body.”
護堂は実直な性格上、このような鬼道の物を使おうとはしないだろう。: Given Godou’s upright nature, he would likely hesitate to use such a sinister tool.
久遠「《清鳳会》の方々に使わせれば良いかと」: Kuon: “You should simply have the members of the Seihoukai use it.”
久遠「逢禅寺殿も成果を欲しているでしょうから、彼らに任せましょう」: Kuon: “Lord Ouzenji will be wanting results as well, so let us leave it to them.”
《清鳳会》には護堂の実の娘がいる。↓それは護堂自身の口から聞いた事だ。: Godou’s biological daughter is in the Seihoukai. That is something I heard from Godou’s own mouth.
そして、これは彼も洩らした事はないが、護堂は逢禅寺清正のやろうとしている事に不: And, though he has never let it slip directly, Godou harbors a sense of distrust toward what Ouzenji Kiyomasa is trying to do.
愛国をうたうのならばこのような悪魔の機械を使う必要などない。↓国民あっての国家: If one claims to love their country, there is no need to use such a demonic machine. Godou would occasionally vent his frustrations indirectly, saying that a nation exists only because of its people.
私は国の事などどうでもいい。↓今はこの《リフ＝ラフ》がどのような動作をするかが: I couldn't care less about the country. I simply wanted to know how this ‘Riff-Raff’ would operate.
護堂は《リフ＝ラフ》を持って立ち上がる。: Godou stood up, holding the Riff-Raff.
久遠「こちらが取り扱い説明書です。使われる人には充分に気をつけるよう申しつけてお: Kuon: “Here is the instruction manual. Please tell those using it to be extremely careful.”
紛失などされては、こちらの興が削がれてしまう。: If it were lost, it would spoil my fun.
《清鳳会》は《リフ＝ラフ》の実験を有楽町で行った。: The Seihoukai conducted an experiment with the Riff-Raff in Yurakucho.
結果、使用方法を間違えた男が突然暴れだし、周囲の通行人に突然襲いかかって殺傷す: As a result, a man who misused it suddenly went berserk, attacking and killing passersby.
その哀れな男はすぐさま憲兵隊――《清鳳会》の息が掛かっている――に射殺された。: That pitiful man was immediately shot dead by the Military Police――who are under the thumb of the Seihoukai.
＄ これが《連続狂人殺傷事件》の幕開けだった。: This was the beginning of the ‘Serial Madman Slasher Incidents’.
廊下の向こうから歩いて来た女が私の前で足を止める。: A woman walking from the other end of the hallway stopped in front of me.
彼女――相馬葵は私に侮蔑の視線を投げかけると、乱暴に《リフ＝ラフ》を放って寄越: She――Souma Aoi, threw a look of contempt at me and roughly tossed the Riff-Raff back.
久遠「遣い手によってはどんな銘刀であれなまくらになってしまいます」: Kuon: “In the hands of the wrong wielder, even the finest blade becomes a dull hunk of iron.”
葵「あたしの部下が無能とでも云うつもり？」: Aoi: “Are you trying to say my subordinates are incompetent?”
久遠「無能とは言っていません」: Kuon: “I did not say they were incompetent.”
葵「それの所為であたしの部下が何人やられたと思ってんだい！ 五人だよ、五人！ 三: Aoi: “Do you have any idea how many of my men were taken down because of that thing! Five! Five men! Three are dead, and two are severely injured!”
葵＄「あまり舐めた事言ってんじゃないわよ！」: Aoi: <Don't you dare talk down to me!>
彼女は肩を怒らせて立ち去った。: She stormed off, her shoulders squared in anger.
子供じゃないのだから、癇癪を起こして人に当たるのは止して欲しい。: She’s not a child, so I wish she would stop throwing tantrums and taking them out on others.
《リフ＝ラフ》は修正が施され、再度街頭で実験が行われた。: The Riff-Raff was modified, and experiments were conducted on the streets again.
実行する相馬葵はずっと渋っていたらしい。↓唯々諾々と任務を遂行する父とは大違い: Apparently, Souma Aoi, who was to execute them, had been hesitant for a long time. She is quite different from her father, who carries out missions with blind obedience.
品川の路上で行われた試験はある程度の成功を収めた。↓被験体は突然暴れだし、周囲: The test conducted on the streets of Shinagawa achieved a certain level of success. The subject suddenly went berserk, attacking people nearby and leaving several victims.
《リフ＝ラフ》の使用を中止すると、それまでの記憶に混乱が現れ、まるで夢遊病者の: When use of the Riff-Raff was discontinued, confusion appeared in the subject's memory, and they fell into a state resembling somnambulism.
それは本来の『人を操る』と云う趣旨からは外れたものだったが、それでも逢禅寺清正: It drifted from the original purpose of ‘manipulating people,’ but apparently Ouzenji Kiyomasa was pleased nonetheless.
自由主義者や共産主義者のアジトでこれを使えば彼らを一掃できるなどと云っていた。: He was saying things like how he could wipe out the hideouts of liberals and communists if he used this.
そう旨く行くはずがないのは作った私が一番良く解っている。↓信念に裏打ちされた自: As the one who made it, I know best that things won't go that smoothly. Those with confidence backed by conviction are rarely manipulated by others.
だが私はそれを黙っていた。↓言う必要もない事だった。: But I kept quiet about it. There was no need to say anything.
ある日私は護堂に《リフ＝ラフ》を改良するために必要なものがあると打ち明けた。: One day, I confessed to Godou that there was something I needed to improve the Riff-Raff.
予想通り、護堂は困惑の目を私に向けてきた。: As expected, Godou looked at me with eyes full of perplexity.
仕方あるまい。↓人を操る音を作るには、人を魅了する音が必要なのだ。: It cannot be helped. To create a sound that manipulates people, one needs a sound that captivates them.
久遠「――幸い、未完成ながら《リフ＝ラフ》はある程度の効果を発揮します。これで彼: Kuon: “――Fortunately, though incomplete, the Riff-Raff displays a certain level of effect. It is possible to use this to corner him and lead him here.”
この人は、基本的に善人なのだ。: This man is, at his core, a good person.
おまけのピアノ弾きと言いながら、自らの庇護下に入った者には情が移ってしまう。: Despite calling him a ‘bonus piano player,’ he has grown attached to the one who entered his protection.
まあ――それは護堂に保護されている私も同様なのだろうけれど。: Well――I suppose the same could be said for me, being protected by Godou.
店の方から彼の声が聞こえてくる。↓私はいつものように階段に腰掛け、時が来るのを: His voice carries from the shop. I sat on the stairs as usual, waiting for the time to come.
……確かに護堂は少々過保護の処がある。: ……Godou certainly does have an overprotective streak.
そろそろ動く時間のようだ。: It seems it’s about time to move.
私は《リフ＝ラフ》を携え、裏口から外へ出る。: I take the Riff-Raff in hand and exit through the back door.
雨傘をさし、道を急ぐ。↓彼の帰り道はおおよそ把握している。: I hurry down the road, holding an umbrella. I have a general grasp of his route home.
途中で道端に放置されていた荷車を動かし、道を塞ぐ。↓泥水が撥ねて服が少し汚れて: On the way, I move a cart that had been left by the roadside to block the path. Muddy water splashed and stained my clothes a little.
暗く細い道へ入り、建物の間の細い隙間に身体を滑らせる。↓よくよく注意して視なく: Entering a dark, narrow path, I slide my body into a thin gap between buildings. Unless someone looks very carefully, they shouldn't be able to see me from the alley.
じっと息を潜めて待つ。: I hold my breath and wait.
護堂が前もって呼び出した色街の芸妓だ。: It was the geisha from the red-light district whom Godou had called out in advance.
近づいてくる前に、一瞬《リフ＝ラフ》を作動させた。: Before she drew close, I activated the Riff-Raff for an instant.
芸妓の身体が頽れる。↓まだ暴れさせる訳にはいかない。: The geisha’s body slumped. I cannot let her go wild just yet.
苦しむ芸妓の元へ、彼がやってきた。: He arrived at the side of the suffering geisha.
予想通り、彼は蹲る芸妓の介抱を始めた。下心もあるのだろうか。↓酔っている所為だ: As expected, he began to tend to the crouching geisha. I wonder if he has ulterior motives. I suspect it’s just because he’s drunk, though――
互いに背中を向ける。↓――今だ。: They turn their backs to each other. ――Now.
――私は《リフ＝ラフ》をふたりに向けた。: ――I aimed the Riff-Raff at both of them.
――凄惨な殺し合いだった。: ――It was a gruesome struggle to the death.
芸妓は完全に我を喪い、彼へ襲いかかっていった。↓仕事柄鬱屈した精神状態だったの: The geisha completely lost herself and lunged at him. Her mental state was likely suppressed due to her profession. The Riff-Raff works well on such people.
対して彼にはあまり効いていないようだった。↓設定を強めるべきなのかもしれないが: In contrast, it didn't seem to have much effect on him. Perhaps I should increase the setting, but there’s no point if I break him.
だが――効きが悪いのに彼は凶暴性を増していた。: But――despite the poor effectiveness, his aggression was increasing.
《リフ＝ラフ》が効いている間に喋る事が出来るのは、彼が操られていない証拠だった: The fact that he can speak while the Riff-Raff is active was proof that he was not being controlled.
何処かで笛が鳴っている。↓《清鳳会》が抱えている憲兵の一団だろう。↓彼を捕縛出: A whistle is blowing somewhere. It must be the group of Military Police the Seihoukai keeps. It would be perfect if they could capture him, but――
彼は身体を引き摺りながらも移動を始めてしまった。↓私が出て行って捕まえる訳にも: He began to move, dragging his body. I couldn't exactly go out and catch him myself, so I watched him pass by before my eyes.
彼が居なくなり、路地の窪みから出る。↓随分と雨で濡れてしまった。↓早く戻って熱: Once he was gone, I emerged from the hollow in the alley. I’ve become quite wet from the rain. I want to go back quickly and take a hot bath.
名も知らぬ芸妓は微かに息をしている。↓彼は殺しきれなかったようだ。: The nameless geisha is faintly breathing. It seems he couldn't finish her off.
これでは――彼を堕とせない。: At this rate――I cannot make him fall.
護堂「済まない。少々時間を喰ってしまった」: Godou: “Forgive me. It took a little more time than expected.”
護堂は地面に倒れ伏したままの芸妓を見た。: Godou looked at the geisha who was still lying prostrate on the ground.
久遠「彼なら既に帰りました。まだ効きが甘かったようです」: Kuon: “He has already gone home. It seems the effect was still too weak.”
芸妓が呻き声を発し、起きあがろうとする。: The geisha let out a groan and tried to push herself up.
彼を堕とす為には、人を殺してしまったと云う自責の念を植え付ける必要があった。: In order to make him fall, it was necessary to plant the seeds of self-reproach for having killed someone.
社会的に抹殺し、精神的にも追いつめれば、必ず彼は私の言葉に従う操り人形となるだ: If I destroy him socially and corner him mentally, he will surely become a puppet that follows my words.
その為には、彼女に犠牲となってもらう必要があった。: For that, she had to become a sacrifice.
この雨の中、返り血を浴びる事もなく、陰惨に、凄惨に、華美に、優雅に、哀れな女を: In this rain, without even being showered in spray, he offered the pitiful woman up in a blood sacrifice that was somber, gruesome, ornate, and elegant.
久遠「――ご苦労様です」: Kuon: “――Thank you for your hard work.”
護堂は雨の中、煙草に火を付ける。: In the rain, Godou lit a cigarette.
護堂「――こんな仕事はあまりさせないでくれ」: Godou: “――Please don't make me do this kind of work too often.”
私は返事をしなかったが、一応心に留めておく事にした。: I didn't give an answer, but I decided to keep it in mind.
護堂「さすがに見られていると決心も鈍る」: Godou: “As I thought, having you watch makes my resolve waver.”
路地の入口に転がっている傘に視線を向け、護堂は煙草を水溜まりに落とした。: Aiming his gaze at the umbrella lying at the entrance of the alley, Godou dropped his cigarette into a puddle.
――思惑通り、彼は尋ね人となり、《清鳳会》の庇護を受けるようになった。: ――As planned, he became a wanted man and came under the protection of the Seihoukai.
ただ問題なのは、相馬葵がいたく彼の事を気に入ってしまった事だ。↓独占欲と嫉妬心: The only problem is that Souma Aoi has taken quite a liking to him. Knowing her strong possessiveness and jealousy, she won't let go of him easily.
先手を打つ必要があるかもしれない。: I might need to make a preemptive move.
私は録音機材を持ち、バーの奥、ピアノの裏側の階段に腰を下ろしていた。: Carrying recording equipment, I sat on the stairs behind the piano at the back of the bar.
先程千花が会釈をして二階へ上がっていった。↓私が持っているこの機械の事も気付か: Earlier, Chika had nodded and gone upstairs. She didn't seem to notice this machine I have, either.
先程から流れているピアノの音色を録音していた。↓波形を解析して《リフ＝ラフ》に: I was recording the sound of the piano that had been playing since a while ago. I will analyze the waveform and see if it can be used for the Riff-Raff.
しかし――いい音だと思う。↓稀代の名演奏家たちとは比べるべくもないが、私にとっ: Still――I think it’s a good sound. While it cannot be compared to rare, famous performers, for me it permeates the heart far more than a performance heard on a record.
――哀愁を感じる旋律だ。: ――It is a melody that carries a sense of sorrow.
――気付けばひとりでに立ち上がり、ピアノへと近づいていた。: ――Before I knew it, I had stood up and approached the piano.
名前を呼びそうになり、口を噤み一礼する。: I almost called his name, but held my tongue and gave a bow.
私は暫し彼と歓談した。: I chatted with him for a short while.
下らない事を話していたと思う。: I think we talked about trivial things.
こんな事は普段有り得ない事だ。: Something like this would normally be impossible.
彼のピアノの音色がそうさせたのだろうか。: I wonder if the sound of his piano made it so.
＄ 彼は私の名前を聴いても何の反応も見せなかった。: He showed no reaction even when he heard my name.
新聞は読まないようだ。↓確か彼の知り合いが雑誌社に勤めていた。↓そちらで調べら: It seems he doesn't read the newspapers. I believe an acquaintance of his worked for a magazine company. It might be a little troublesome if they investigated there.
まあ――気付かれればそれでもいい。: Well――if I am found out, so be it.
バーで採取した彼の演奏のデータは、あまり役に立ちそうになかった。: The data of his performance gathered at the bar didn't seem like it would be of much use.
根本的に録音機材の性能が低すぎるのだ。↓ノイズが激しく、そのまま使える素材では: Fundamentally, the performance of the recording equipment is too low. The noise was intense, and it wasn't material that could be used as-is.
それでも多少の修正を施し、《リフ＝ラフ》に組み込んだ。: Even so, I applied some corrections and incorporated it into the Riff-Raff.
彼の音楽はどの様な効果を現すだろうか――: I wonder what kind of effect his music will manifest――
本番の前に六本木で行った試験は上々の結果をもたらした。↓これまでよりも遥かに効: The test conducted in Roppongi before the real thing yielded excellent results. It manifested its performance on the subjects far more effectively than before.
やはり彼の旋律は人を狂わせる――: As I thought, his melody drives people mad――
久遠＄「――玖藤奏介に《リフ＝ラフ》を使ってください」: Kuon: <――Please use the Riff-Raff on Kudou Sousuke.>
彼女は苛立ちを隠そうともせず、爪先で床を叩きながら訊ねてくる。: She asked, tapping her toe against the floor without even trying to hide her irritation.
久遠「――彼を完全にこちらへと引き込む為です」: Kuon: “――It is to draw him completely over to this side.”
葵「はっ――あんたにゃ渡すつもりはないわよ」: Aoi: “Hah――I have no intention of handing him over to you.”
久遠「結構です。私は彼に興味はありませんので」: Kuon: “That is fine. I have no interest in him.”
私が興味を抱くのは、彼の旋律だ。: What I harbor interest in is his melody.
彼の旋律に惹かれると云う事は――私も狂っているのだろうか。: To be captivated by his melody――does that mean I am also mad?
恐らく効かないだろう《リフ＝ラフ》を使わせようと考えているのだから。: Because I’m thinking of having her use the Riff-Raff, which likely won't work.
予想通り――彼に《リフ＝ラフ》は効かなかった。: As expected――the Riff-Raff had no effect on him.
河豚の毒と同じだ。↓彼は自らが紡ぐ旋律に酔いしれるような自己陶酔者ではない。: It’s the same as pufferfish poison. He is not a narcissist who becomes intoxicated by the melody he spins himself.
無人の店内は静か過ぎて、物音を立ててはいけないような感覚に囚われる。: The empty shop is too quiet, and I am seized by the sensation that I must not make a sound.
私はただじっとピアノの椅子に腰掛けていた。: I just sat still on the piano stool.
護堂は居ない。↓店内は好きに使ってもいいと言われている。↓突然此処を鮨屋に改装: Godou isn't here. I’ve been told I can use the shop however I like. I wonder if he would say nothing even if I suddenly remodeled this place into a sushi shop.
＄ 立ち上がり、厨房へ水を取りに行く。: Standing up, I go to the kitchen to get some water.
店の扉が音を立てた。: The door of the shop made a sound.
――誰かが入ってきた。↓外の照明は消灯しているから客が来る事はないと思うのだが: ――Someone has entered. Since the outside lights are off, I didn't think any customers would come.
そっと店内をのぞき見る。: I quietly peek into the shop.
――女性だった。↓スツールに腰を下ろし、俯いていた。: ――It was a woman. She sat on a stool, her head bowed.
＄ 突然素っ頓狂な声をあげ、勢いよく立ち上がった。↓妙な人だ。: Suddenly letting out a startled voice, she stood up vigorously. What a strange person.
所在なげに彼女は辺りを見回す。↓どうしようも何も、帰ればいいのにと思う。: She looks around restlessly. Rather than wondering what to do, I think she should just go home.
彼女が不思議そうな声をあげ、周囲を見回した。: She let out a voice of wonder and looked around.
彼女は一瞬、椅子の座面に手を触れていた。↓先程まで座っていた温もりを感じたのだ: For an instant, her hand touched the surface of the stool. She must have felt the warmth from where I had been sitting until just now.
私は店の飾り付けに使われていた小さな硝子玉を床に落とした。: I dropped a small glass ball that had been used for decoration in the shop onto the floor.
挨拶をしながら暗がりより現れる。: Greeting her, I emerge from the darkness.
彼女は訝しげに再度訊ねてきた。↓店の関係者ではないと感じたのだろう。: She asked again, looking suspicious. She must have felt that I was not involved with the shop.
素直に名乗ったのは、ひとつの賭だった。: Naming myself honestly was a gamble.
彼女は私の事を訝る様子は見せなかった。↓人の噂も七十五日と云う事だろうか。: She showed no sign of being suspicious of me. I wonder if it’s a case of people’s rumors only lasting seventy-five days.
下らない事を話した。↓話しすぎたかもしれない。: We talked about trivial things. Perhaps we talked too much.
綾音「……うん。大切な人たちとね。まぁ……喧嘩というよりもわたしが勝手に拗ねちゃ: Ayane: “……Yeah. With people important to me. Well…… rather than a fight, it’s just that I went and got sulky on my own, ahaha……”
乾いた笑いを彼女は洩らす。: She lets out a dry laugh.
私の周りには喧嘩ができる相手が存在しない。↓私を過小評価するか過大評価するかの: There is no one around me whom I can fight with. People either underestimate or overestimate me, and I have never been able to build an equal relationship.
言葉を付け加える。↓姉はもう居ない。↓それに血が繋がっていては友人とは呼べまい: I added those words. My sister is no longer here. And if one is related by blood, they cannot be called a friend.
何をむきになっているのだろう、私は。: What am I getting worked up for?
本当に不可思議だ。↓こんな事、初対面の人相手に話すなんてどうかしている。: It truly is mysterious. To talk about such things with someone I’ve met for the first time is insane.
それでも私は、彼女と話しすぎたと感じていた。: Even so, I felt as though I had talked too much with her.
その後は《連続狂人殺傷事件》について軽く話をした。↓話し過ぎないように注意をし: After that, we talked lightly about the ‘Serial Madman Slasher Incidents.’ I was careful not to talk too much, but it seems I inevitably say too much when talking with her.
はじめは唯の好奇心かと思ったのだが、彼女は事件に巻き込まれた友人の話を始めた。: At first, I thought it was just idle curiosity, but she began to talk about a friend who was caught up in the incident.
すぐにそれが彼の事だと判った。: I immediately realized it was him.
彼女はいい勘をしていると思う。↓少ない知識の中から的確に近い予測を立ててくる。: I think she has good intuition. She makes remarkably close predictions from a small amount of knowledge.
私は彼女に別れを告げ、店を出た。: I said goodbye to her and left the shop.
これ以上彼女の側で話をしてぼろを出す訳にもいかない。↓幾ら自らを制御できてると: I cannot risk talking by her side anymore and letting something slip. No matter how much I can control myself, I felt as though she possessed some inexplicable power.
＄ 店を出ると、すぐに彼の姿を見つけた。: Leaving the shop, I immediately found him.
そう告げ、私はその場を後にする。: With that, I left the area.
彼は彼女を守る事ができるのだろうか。: I wonder if he can protect her.
守るよう導かねば――喪った時の衝撃は弱くなってしまう。: I must lead him to protect her――otherwise, the impact when he loses her will be weakened.
この先の予定を考えつつ、私は家に戻った。: While thinking about the plans ahead, I returned home.
不機嫌さを隠そうともせず、彼女は私を見下ろしてくる。: She looks down at me, without trying to hide her foul mood.
葵「……あんたに言う必要なんてないでしょう？」: Aoi: “……There’s no need for me to tell you, is there?”
彼女はあくまで尊大に答えてくる。: She answers with her usual arrogance.
久遠「彼は私の実験に必要なのです」: Kuon: “He is necessary for my experiments.”
葵「はっ……だったらさっさと結果を出してちょうだい」: Aoi: “Hah…… then hurry up and produce results.”
久遠「結果を出すには彼が必要です」: Kuon: “To produce results, I need him.”
それがこの人には解らないのだろうか。: Does this woman not understand that?
やれやれ――これだから大人の女は面倒なのだ。↓蛇蝎のように捕らえた獲物を放そう: Good grief――this is why adult women are so troublesome. They won't let go of the prey they’ve captured, like a serpent or a scorpion.
そんなに構っている暇もない。: I don't have the time to deal with this.
私は彼女に背を向ける。: I turn my back to her.
彼女の方に向き直る事なく言葉を続ける。: Without turning back toward her, I continue speaking.
久遠「最近彼の元に足繁く通っている女性が居るようです」: Kuon: “It seems there is a woman who has been visiting him frequently of late.”
久遠「こちらの事が知られる前に、何らかの対策をうった方がいいかもしれませんね」: Kuon: “It might be best to take some sort of countermeasure before our affairs are discovered.”
後の言葉は聞いていなかったかもしれない。: She might not have heard the words that followed.
彼女なら――私が意図した通りに動いてくれるだろう。: She will――likely move exactly as I intended.
これしきならば、《リフ＝ラフ》を使わずとも簡単な事だった。: Something of this level was simple even without using the Riff-Raff.
＄ 人を操るなど、機械に頼るまでもない。: There is no need to rely on machines to manipulate people.
計画は恙なく進んだ。↓相馬葵は此方の意図した通り動いてくれた。: The plan proceeded without a hitch. Souma Aoi moved exactly as I intended.
護堂「……奏介は団子坂にある古書店跡に隠れているらしい」: Godou: “……It seems Sousuke is hiding in the ruins of an old bookstore in Dangozaka.”
店から戻ってきた護堂が報告してくる。: Godou reports upon returning from the shop.
それはまた面白い処に隠れたものだ。↓明智小五郎に出て来られては、私の計画も見抜: He’s hidden in an interesting place. If Akechi Kogoro were to appear, my plan might be seen through.
護堂「これからどうするつもりだ？」: Godou: “What do you intend to do now?”
久遠「――不測の事態に備えるべきでしょうね」: Kuon: “――I suppose we should prepare for unforeseen circumstances.”
少しだけ護堂の貌を見る。: I look at Godou’s face for just a moment.
久遠「護堂――貴方は私と組織、どちらを優先させるか教えてください」: Kuon: “Godou――please tell me which you would prioritize: me, or the organization?”
護堂は苦笑し、顎の傷痕を掻く。: Godou gave a wry smile and scratched the scar on his jaw.
護堂「……優先させるべきなのは君の事だろうな」: Godou: “……The one I should prioritize is likely you.”
護堂「……今の大佐殿は私服を肥やす事に夢中になりすぎている」: Godou: “……The current Colonel is too obsessed with lining his own pockets.”
護堂はカップの珈琲を飲みながら呟く。↓もう湯飲みには淹れていない。↓味は相変わ: Godou mutters while drinking coffee from a cup. He no longer pours it into a teacup. The taste is still as weak as ever, though.
久遠「ならば――有事の際には私情を挟む事はありませんね？」: Kuon: “Then――in the event of an emergency, you won't let personal feelings interfere?”
護堂の目を見つめて訊ねる。: I ask while staring into Godou’s eyes.
一瞬護堂は目を泳がせた。: Godou’s eyes flickered for an instant.
信じてはいない。↓これはただ釘を刺しただけだ。: I don't believe him. This was just a warning.
突然護堂が私の名を呼び、手招きする。: Suddenly Godou calls my name and beckons me over.
護堂「……其処の押入から外に抜け出せる」: Godou: “……You can escape through that closet.”
護堂「……そのようだ。此処を知っていると云う事は――葵か」: Godou: “……It seems so. To know this place――it must be Aoi.”
私は押入の襖を開けながら声を掛ける。: I call out to him while opening the sliding door of the closet.
久遠「先程言った事――私情を挟まぬようお願いします」: Kuon: “What I said earlier――please do not let personal feelings interfere.”
護堂「……こちらが挟んだとて、向こうは赦さんだろうさ」: Godou: “……Even if I don't let them interfere, they won't show any mercy.”
覚悟を決めたのか、護堂は刀を手に立ち上がる。: Having made his resolve, Godou stands up with his sword in hand.
戦士の貌になった護堂を背に、私は押入の奥の通路へ潜り込んだ。: With Godou’s warrior face at my back, I slipped into the passage at the back of the closet.
狭い抜け道を通り抜け、家の裏手に出る。↓随分と使っていなかったのだろう、埃まみ: Passing through the narrow escape route, I emerge at the rear of the house. It must not have been used for a long time, as I’ve become covered in dust. I seem to have scraped my knees, too.
さて――団子坂か。↓夜中のひとり歩きは危険だから気をつけなくては。↓狼に襲われ: Now then――Dangozaka. I must be careful, as walking alone at night is dangerous. I couldn't bear to be attacked by a wolf.
下らない事を考えつつ、私は道を急いだ。: While thinking of trivial things, I hurried down the road.
ハチ＄「――待ちな、嬢ちゃん」: Hachi: <――Hold it right there, little lady.>
＄ ――妙な風体の男が前に立ち塞がった。: A man in strange attire stood in my path.
何処かで見た事がある。↓確か相馬葵の部下だったろうか。: I’ve seen him somewhere. I believe he was one of Souma Aoi’s subordinates.
ハチ「さすがは姐さん。こっちで張っといて正解だったよ。さあ……こっちへおいで」: Hachi: “Just as Big Sis thought. I was right to stake out this spot. Come on…… come here.”
猫撫で声で手招きをする男。: The man beckons me with a coaxing voice.
久遠「私は帰らないといけません。あまりしつこいようですと……警察を呼びますよ」: Kuon: “I have to go home. If you continue to be so persistent…… I shall call the police.”
ハチ「そいつは勘弁して欲しいな……お互い困るだろ？」: Hachi: “I’d rather you didn't do that…… it’d be a problem for both of us, wouldn't it?”
久遠「私は別に困りませんけれど」: Kuon: “It wouldn't particularly be a problem for me.”
私が警察へ赴いた処で、誘拐された可哀想な少女が自力で逃げ出して来たと思われるだ: Even if I went to the police, it would only be thought that a pitiful kidnapped girl had escaped on her own.
久遠「《清鳳会》は極力波風を立てないよう行動するのが原則だった筈です」: Kuon: “The principle of the Seihoukai was supposed to be to act without causing a stir as much as possible.”
ハチ「ぐっ……めんこくねぇ……」: Hachi: “Guh…… you’re not cute at all……”
クマ「男として彼女のような清純な乙女は、恭しく扱い、何としてでも手厚く保護しなく: Kuma: “As a man, one must treat a pure maiden like her with respect and protect her at all costs.”
ハチ「僕を名前で呼ぶなと言ってるだろクマ」: Hachi: “I told you not to call me by my name, Kuma!”
何やら訳の解らないふたりを前に、私はこの場を乗り切る算段を考える。↓背の高い男: Facing these two who were talking nonsense, I thought of a plan to get through this. The tall one seems easier to handle.
久遠「お願いです、此処を通してください。おじ……おにいさん」: Kuon: “Please, let me through. Uncle…… I mean, Mister.”
クマ「もちろんだ可愛いお嬢さん。↓なんなら行きたい処まで送ってさしあげましょう」: Kuma: “Of course, lovely young lady. In fact, let me escort you to wherever you want to go.”
ハチ「おいクマっ！ 勝手な事すんじゃねえよ！ 姐さんにどやされるだろっ！」: Hachi: “Hey, Kuma! Don't go doing things on your own! Big Sis will give you an earful!”
久遠「それでは、私はこれで」: Kuon: “Well then, I shall be going.”
ハチと呼ばれていた男が前に立ち塞がる。: The man called Hachi stood in my way.
久遠「……暴力を振るうつもりですか」: Kuon: “……Do you intend to use violence?”
クマ「ならんぞハチっ！ このような可憐な少女に暴力を振るうなどとはっ！」: Kuma: “You mustn't, Hachi! To use violence against such a delicate girl!”
予想よりも早くクマと呼ばれた男がハチとの間に割って入ってくる。: Faster than expected, the man called Kuma stepped in between me and Hachi.
ハチ「だっ、誰が暴力なんか……僕は女に手を挙げたりはしねえってば……ったくっ！: Hachi: “W-Who’s using violence…… I’m telling you, I’d never lay a hand on a woman…… seriously! This is so uncomfortable!”
北の方の方言だろう、ハチは癇癪を起こしたように地団駄を踏む。: It must be a northern dialect; Hachi stomps his feet as if throwing a tantrum.
ハチ「……いいよ、行けよ。これ以上みったくない真似したくねえわ」: Hachi: “……Fine, go. I don't want to do anything else unsightly.”
ハチ「仕方ねえべや。僕らは何も見つけてねえって姐さんに言うしかねえべ？」: Hachi: “It can't be helped. We’ll just have to tell Big Sis we didn't find anyone.”
一応例を言っておいた方がいいだろう。: I should offer my thanks, for now.
クマ「なに、構いませんよ美しいお嬢さん」: Kuma: “Don't mention it, beautiful young lady.”
確か別れの挨拶はこう言う筈だ。: I believe this is how the parting greeting goes.
私は足早に団子坂を目指した。↓お陰で日が昇る前にたどり着くことは出来たが、完全: I hurried toward Dangozaka. Thanks to that, I managed to arrive before sunrise, but I was completely out of breath.
こんなに歩くのは久し振りの事だ。↓机に向かってばかりではいけないと云う事か。: It’s been a long time since I’ve walked this much. I guess it’s a sign I shouldn't just stay at my desk.
見たところこの付近に古書店は一軒しかないようだ。↓深夜なので古書店跡なのか営業: From what I can see, there is only one bookstore in this vicinity. Since it’s late at night, it’s unclear whether it’s an old bookstore ruin or just outside business hours.
がたがたときしむ戸を開け、中に入る。↓黴と埃の臭い、それに懐かしい紙の匂いがし: Opening the rattling door, I go inside. There was the smell of mold and dust, and the nostalgic scent of paper.
さすがに格子戸などはついていないようだ。: Apparently, it doesn't have a lattice door or anything like that.
疲れているのにそんな事を考えながら屋内を見回す。↓一階は店舗と台所に浴室がある: Even though I was tired, I looked around the interior while thinking of such things. The first floor has a shop, a kitchen, and a bathroom. I wonder if it’s the second floor?
襖を開け、部屋の中に入った。: Opening the sliding door, I entered the room.
彼女が死んだからだろう。↓私の来訪に驚いたのも一瞬、すぐに起こしかけていた身体: It was likely because she died. The surprise at my visit lasted only an instant, and he immediately let his half-raised body fall back.
私がした事は、彼を予想以上に打ちのめしたようだ。↓こんな奏介では使い物にならな: What I did seems to have crushed him more than expected. A Sousuke like this might be useless.
私は擦り剥いた膝を洗うため、階下へ降りていった。: I went downstairs to wash my scraped knees.
酷い有様だった奏介は、徐々に気力を回復しているようだった。: Sousuke, who had been in a terrible state, seemed to be gradually recovering his energy.
悪夢に魘されて飛び起きるような事も減った。: The instances of him jumping awake from nightmares decreased as well.
能動的になるのは構わないが、今のような状態は勘弁して欲しい。: Becoming active is fine, but I wish he’d stop his current behavior.
階下から女性の声が聞こえてくる。↓護堂のバーで歌っていた人だ。↓寝たふりをして: A woman’s voice carries from downstairs. It was the woman who was singing at Godou’s bar. I tried to be considerate by pretending to sleep, but apparently it was a failure.
男女の関係と云うのはよく解らない。↓行為自体は理解しているつもりなのだけど。: I don't quite understand relationships between men and women. Though I believe I understand the act itself.
人はひとりでは生きていけない。↓それは真理だ。↓だが相手との接し方は千差万別だ: Humans cannot live alone. That is the truth. But the way one interacts with another must vary in a thousand ways.
＄ 普通の人は相手を愛して生きていく。: Ordinary people live by loving others.
私の場合は相手を利用して生きている。: In my case, I live by utilizing others.
愛と云うのも相手を利用しているのは同じだ。↓相手が居なくては愛する事すらできな: Love is the same in that it utilizes the other. One cannot even love if the other is not there.
私が利用している人間を愛している事はない。↓そんな感情は永遠が居なくなった時に: I do not love the people I utilize. Such emotions vanished when Towa was gone.
私は永遠を憎んでいた。↓一生超える事の出来ない相手として。: I hated Towa. As a rival I could never surpass for the rest of my life.
＄ そこに愛など――ある筈がない。: There could be no love――in such a place.
私がここに居るようになってから暫く経った。: Some time has passed since I came to stay here.
彼は自らが犯した殺人を疑うようになっていた。: He was beginning to doubt the murder he had committed.
《リフ＝ラフ》は彼に効果を及ぼし難い。↓幾ら隣で彼女が死んでいたとしても、それ: The Riff-Raff is difficult to exert an effect on him. No matter how much she was dead beside him, it is only natural for him to harbor suspicion toward it.
他殺の証拠を残すとは、あの人に任せたのは失敗だったかもしれない。↓最近私の計画: To leave evidence of a third-party murder, leaving it to that man might have been a mistake. I feel as though my plans――my schemes, have not been moving well of late.
わざわざ手懸かりを与える事はない。: There is no need to give him clues.
暗にまだ証拠が足りないと云う事を匂わせる。: I hint that the evidence is still lacking.
久遠「例えば――そうですね、可能性のひとつとして考えられる事をお話ししてみましょ: Kuon: “For example――well, let us talk about one possibility.”
私は非道い事を話しているなと自覚する。: I realize I am saying something terrible.
案の定、彼は静かに怒った。: As expected, he became quietly angry.
可能性だけならば幾らでも適当な事を言える。: If it’s just possibilities, I can say anything I like.
彼の意識を分散させてやる。↓ひとつの事ばかりを考えさせて、もし真実に辿り着くの: I’ll disperse his focus. If he thinks of only one thing and reaches the truth too quickly, it will be a problem for me as well.
もう少しだけ、彼を堕とす為の時間が欲しかった。: I wanted a little more time to make him fall.
久遠「まだ眠っているようです」: Kuon: “He seems to be still sleeping.”
朝早くに訪問してきた彼女に答える。: I answer her, who visited early in the morning.
久遠「用事があるのでしたらどうぞ中でお待ち下さい」: Kuon: “If you have business, please wait inside.”
中に案内しようとすると、彼女は急に逡巡しだした。: When I tried to lead her inside, she suddenly began to hesitate.
未通女いなと思う。奥ゆかしいと云うか、奥手と云うか。: How innocent. I wonder if she’s modest, or just late to the game.
朴念仁にはお似合いかもしれないけれど。: Perhaps she’s a good match for a blockhead.
下着一枚で横になっている彼を、彼女はちらりと横目で見ただけでやり過ごした。: She managed to pass by him while he was lying there in only his underwear, with only a sidelong glance.
久遠「何か判った事があるのですね」: Kuon: “You’ve found something out, haven't you.”
美華夏「現場の事が少しだけ……ね」: Mikana: “Just a little about the scene…… you know.”
あの人がどんな失態をしでかしたのか把握しておいた方がいいだろう。: I should grasp what kind of blunder that man committed.
美華夏「奏介が居なくなってすぐに憲兵隊がやってきたらしいわ。それも……住人が誰も: Mikana: “Apparently the Military Police arrived right after Sousuke was gone. And that’s despite…… none of the residents reporting it.”
全く――これだから使えない人間は困る。↓彼を飼うにしても、逃げられては元も子も: Truly――this is why incompetent people are a problem. Even if they were to keep him, it would all be for naught if he escaped.
美華夏「誰も通報していないのだから、真犯人が自分で通報したんじゃないのかしら？」: Mikana: “Since no one reported it, perhaps the true culprit reported it themselves?”
久遠「それは有り得そうな話ですが、面倒を避ける為に住人が黙秘している可能性もあり: Kuon: “That is a likely story, but there is also the possibility that the residents are keeping quiet to avoid trouble.”
言葉を交わしながら思う。↓こんな杜撰な犯罪、官憲がよほど無能か丸め込みでもして: I think while exchanging words. For such a sloppy crime, even an amateur could solve it, unless the authorities were incredibly incompetent or in on it.
奏介が目覚め、殺害現場の状況についての話が続く。: Sousuke woke up, and the talk about the situation at the murder scene continued.
私は解決して貰いたいのだろうか、して欲しくないのだろうか。: I wonder if I want it solved, or if I don't.
――仕方ない。彼女には生贄の羊となってもらおう。: ――It cannot be helped. She shall become the sacrificial lamb.
真犯人――実行犯の名前を、彼は口にした。: He spoke the name of the true culprit――the executor.
――歌唄いの彼女が、人を殺してしまったとやってきた。↓話を訊いてすぐ、《リフ＝: ――The singer came to me, saying she had killed someone. As soon as I heard the story, I realized it was the effect of the Riff-Raff.
私は実験が続けられているのを知らなかった。↓私が居なくて、誰が実験の結果を解析: I didn't know the experiments were being continued. Without me, who was analyzing the results?
悩んだ末、彼はそう結論を出した。: After agonizing, he reached that conclusion.
彼女は笑う。↓今にも泣き出しそうな、痛々しい笑みを浮かべ。: She smiles. A painful smile, as if she were about to burst into tears.
彼女は彼に惚れ込んでいたのだろう。↓それ故彼の演奏が組み込まれた《リフ＝ラフ》: She must have been deeply in love with him. Therefore, she was undoubtedly susceptible to the influence of the Riff-Raff, which had his performance incorporated into it.
奏介「ちょっと――璃宝を送っていく事にするわ」: Sousuke: “I’m going to go see Riho off for a bit.”
確認したい事があった。: There was something I wanted to confirm.
私は寝間着を着替えながら考えを纏める。: I gather my thoughts while changing out of my nightwear.
彼女が打ち拉がれている原因は何処にあるのかが気になっていた。: I was curious as to where the cause of her being crushed lay.
人間を殺しただけではそこまで壊れる事はないだろう。↓私はそう思う。: One wouldn't break that much just by killing a human. That is what I think.
彼女の言葉の端々からは操られる事への恐怖感が窺えた。↓つまりはそう云う事なのだ: From her every word, I could see a fear of being controlled. Does that mean that is what it was?
彼女を堕とすのに意味はない。: There is no meaning in making her fall.
＄ どうやら――私は厭な物を作ってしまったようだ。: It seems――I have made something unpleasant.
彼と共に彼女を捜し、神田川沿いまで出てきた。: Searching for her with him, we came to the banks of the Kanda River.
さすがに早足の彼に付いていくのは疲れる。: As expected, it is tiring to keep up with his fast pace.
息を整え、辺りを見回し――見つけてしまった。: Catching my breath, I look around――and I found her.
彼の袖を引き、欄干を指差す。: I pull his sleeve and point to the railing.
彼女は哀しげな、絶望的な声を発した。: She let out a sad, despairing voice.
璃宝「――自由であり続ける事を、唯一の誇りにしていたから。アタシがアタシじゃ無く: Riho: “――Because continuing to be free was the only thing I took pride in. Because I stopped being myself…… I shall――vanish.”
……私は、姉の姿を視てしまった。: ……I saw the figure of my sister.
彼の声が遠くから聞こえる。: His voice sounds like it’s coming from far away.
私は、また――姉を殺してしまった。: I have killed my sister again.
自らが作った機械の所為で。: Because of the machine I made.
自らが犯した過ちの所為で。: Because of the mistake I committed.
――どうやって帰ってきたのかは覚えていない。: ――I don't remember how I got back.
彼が私を抱きかかえて連れてきたのだろうか。: I wonder if he carried me back.
いや、姉はもう死んでしまった。: No, my sister has already died.
私が殺したのだ。↓関係ない筈がない。: I killed her. There is no way I have nothing to do with it.
姉が死んだのも、彼女たちが死んだのも。: My sister’s death, and their deaths.
やはりまだ私は混乱している。: As I thought, I am still confused.
歌唄いの彼女が死んだのと、姉が死んだのとの間には何も因果関係はない。: There is no causal relationship between the singer’s death and my sister’s death.
ただその最期が――私の中で重なっただけだ。: It is just that their final moments――overlapped within me.
こんな事を頼むだなんて、どうかしている。: To ask for such a thing, I must be insane.
彼はただの研究対象で、それ以上の何者でもない筈だ。: He is merely a research subject, and should be nothing more.
だけど――この混乱を止めるには、何か別の方法を用いるしかない。: But――to stop this confusion, I have no choice but to use some other method.
彼は相馬葵の根城――元々は私は住んでいた家に向かった。: He headed for Souma Aoi’s stronghold――the house I originally lived in.
良い思い出がない家だから何の感慨もないのだが、どうせなら取り壊してしまえばよか: Since it’s a house with no good memories, I have no sentimentality, but I wish they had just torn it down.
今の所有者は誰なのだろうか。↓まあ――どうでもいい事ではあるけれど。: I wonder who the current owner is. Well――it doesn't matter.
建物全体が見渡せる近くの建物の屋根に登り、私はじっと時が来るのを待ち続けていた: Climbing onto the roof of a nearby building from which the entire building could be seen, I waited still for the time to come.
護堂は長銃を構え、微動だにしない。↓銃口はずっと光が洩れる窓に向けられている。: Godou held his long gun, motionless. The muzzle was aimed constantly at the window from which light leaked.
相馬葵は余計な事を喋る前に始末しなければならない。: Souma Aoi must be eliminated before she says anything unnecessary.
それを実の父親である護堂に命ずるのは残酷であろうか――: I wonder if it’s cruel to order her own father, Godou, to do it――
家を相馬葵に襲撃されて以来、護堂は行方を眩ませていた。↓何処へ居たのかは知らな: Ever since the house was attacked by Souma Aoi, Godou had vanished. I don't know where he was.
ただ古書店の二階にひとりで居るとき、たまに外から手紙を投げ込まれる事があったの: However, when I was alone on the second floor of the bookstore, letters were occasionally thrown in from outside, so I could guess he was hiding in the city.
手紙には《清鳳会》の事務所が引き払われた事などが記されていた。: The letters stated things like the Seihoukai office being vacated.
元々《逢禅寺商会》の裏の仕事を受け持つ部署だったので名称を変えただけだろう。: Since it was originally a department that handled the back-end work of the Ouzenji Trading Company, they likely just changed the name.
――今は目の前の事だ。: ――For now, it is the matter at hand.
護堂「……此処からでは狙いにくい」: Godou: “……It is hard to aim from here.”
護堂「……当たるかどうか判らん」: Godou: “……I don't know if I can hit her.”
暫く、何の音も聞こえない。: For a while, I could hear nothing.
耳鳴りと煙で、護堂の表情も窺えなかった。: Amidst the ringing in my ears and the smoke, I couldn't even tell Godou’s expression.
耳鳴りの中、どうにか護堂の声が聞こえた。: Amidst the ringing, I somehow managed to hear Godou’s voice.
平静を保ち答える。↓『外れた』ではなく『外した』ではないのか。: I answer while maintaining my composure. Was it not ‘missed,’ but ‘missed on purpose’?
今は詰問するつもりもない。: I have no intention of interrogating him now.
久遠「戻りましょう。まだ次にやらなくてはならない事があります」: Kuon: “Let us return. There is still the next thing we must do.”
貴方が止めを刺さなかった所為で。: Because you didn't deliver the finishing blow.
わざわざ私が言わなくとも、護堂はそれを解っているだろう。: Even if I don't go out of my way to say it, Godou likely understands that.
熱く灼けた薬莢を握り、護堂は呟いた。: Gripping the hot, scorched shell casing, Godou muttered.
戸山ヶ原の近衛騎兵連隊から皇居北の丸の近衛師団まで続く地下通路を使い、私と護堂: Using the underground passage that leads from the Imperial Guard Cavalry Regiment in Toyamahara to the Imperial Guard Division in Kitanomaru, Godou and I infiltrated the basement of the Ouzenji Trading Company.
懐かしい処だったが、感傷に浸る暇はなかった。: It was a nostalgic place, but I had no time to indulge in sentimentality.
普段よりも更に感情を押し殺し、護堂に指示する。: Suppressing my emotions even more than usual, I instruct Godou.
――そこから先は、単なる一方的な殺戮の現場でしかなかった。: ――From that point on, it was nothing more than a scene of one-sided slaughter.
下半身を晒したままの男共は次々に斬り伏せられ、醜い骸と化していく。: The men, who were still exposed from the waist down, were cut down one after another, turning into ugly corpses.
白濁した汚液まみれとなった彼女に護堂が外套を掛ける。: Godou tossed a coat over her, who was covered in cloudy, foul liquid.
要らぬ犠牲をまた出してしまった自分自身が。: I couldn't forgive myself for making unnecessary sacrifices again.
今度こそ、護堂はその役割を果たした。: This time, Godou fulfilled his role.
彼が護堂に銃を向けた。: He aimed a gun at Godou.
私は彼の背後へ回り込み、自分の拳銃を確認する。: I moved behind him and checked my own handgun.
レミントン社製の二連装銃だ。↓銃身は短いが、至近距離であれば何の問題もなく当て: It’s a double-barreled gun made by Remington. The barrel is short, but at point-blank range, I can hit anything without a problem.
彼に銃を向け、声を掛ける。: Aiming the gun at him, I call out.
彼が発砲した処で、護堂には当たるまい。↓しかし護堂が避ける際、彼を斬ってしまう: Even if he fired, it wouldn't hit Godou. However, there was a slight possibility that Godou would cut him down while evading.
それだけは避けたかった。↓必要な人間をこれ以上喪うのは厭だ。: That was the one thing I wanted to avoid. I don't want to lose any more necessary people.
彼と護堂の間に入る。: I step in between him and Godou.
私が居なくなれば、全てが落ち着くのかもしれない。: If I were gone, perhaps everything would settle down.
彼には教えなくてはならないだろう。: I suppose I must tell him.
これまで多くの人を巻き込んだ私の罪を。: Of my sins that have involved so many people until now.
真実を全て話していないだけだ。: I am simply not telling the whole truth.
最上階の趣味の悪い社長室。: The distasteful President's office on the top floor.
私は彼と思想を共にした事はない。: I have never shared his ideology.
それ故、裏切りと呼ばれるのは心外だった。: Therefore, being called a traitor felt entirely misplaced.
＄ 彼の思想には賛同できない。: I cannot agree with his ideals.
私は、あの死んでしまった歌唄いの彼女と同じく、自由が欲しいのかもしれない。: Perhaps, like that songstress who died, I too simply want freedom.
気儘に研究を続けられる自由が。: The freedom to continue my research as I please.
彼が彼女の身体を抱き留める。: He catches her body in his arms.
おかしい。↓あの逢禅寺がこんなに潔い筈がない。: Something is wrong. There is no way that Ouzenji would give up this gracefully.
今の彼女では、《リフ＝ラフ》に抗う力が残っていない――: With her current state, she doesn't have the strength left to resist the "Riff-Raff"—
猛烈な吐き気が襲ってくる。: An intense wave of nausea washes over me.
《リフ＝ラフ》は発動すると人体を不定愁訴の状態に陥らせる低周波音を発する。: When activated, the "Riff-Raff" emits low-frequency sounds that plunge the human body into a state of general malaise.
これだけならば体調が悪くなるだけで済むのだが、同時に暗示も掛ける働きがある。: If it were only that, it would just end with feeling ill, but it also functions by planting suggestions.
それが難しいのは知っている。↓元々彼には効き難く出来ているのだが――: I know how difficult that is. It was originally designed to be less effective against him, but—
《リフ＝ラフ》の出力が上がった。↓これは……私にも影響を及ぼしかねない。: The output of the "Riff-Raff" increased. This... it might even affect me.
だが、リミッタを超えた出力は《リフ＝ラフ》の回路を灼き付かせる――筈だ。: However, output exceeding the limiter should burn out the "Riff-Raff's" circuits—or so it should.
それまで持つだろうか――彼女が。: Will she hold out until then—will she?
彼女は声にならない声を荒げ、彼に掴み掛かっていく。: She lets out a wordless scream and lunges at him.
奏介の掌から鮮血が迸る。: Fresh blood spatters from Sousuke's palm.
彼女が彼に馬乗りとなり、彼の首を絞める――: She mounts him, wrapping her hands around his throat to strangle him—
私には、どうする事もできない――: There is nothing I can do—
姉様の声が聞こえる筈もない。: There’s no way I should be hearing Sister's voice.
永遠『私の存在を自らの内に残して神格化する事で、自らの至らなさを誤魔化しいている: Towa: “You are merely an existence deceiving yourself of your own inadequacies by keeping my presence within you and deifying it.”
永遠『貴女は既に私を超えている。――私は神じゃない』: Towa: “You have already surpassed me. —I am no God.”
私を――堕とすつもりですか？: Are you... planning to let me fall?
逢禅寺の高笑いが響くとほぼ同時、護堂が動いた。↓隙を窺っていたのだろう、彼なら: Almost at the same time Ouzenji's loud laughter echoes, Godou moves. He must have been watching for an opening; a man like him should have had that much composure even while the "Riff-Raff" was active.
護堂も刀を構え、静かに声を発する。: Godou also readies his blade, speaking quietly.
私の護堂が負ける筈はない。: There is no way my Godou would lose.
たとえ――左腕を負傷していようとも。: Even if—his left arm is wounded.
幾合か斬り結び、倒れた燭台から炎が床に燃え広がる。: After several clashes of their blades, the fire from a fallen candlestick spreads across the floor.
護堂が窓際に追いつめられた。: Godou is cornered by the window.
逢禅寺の斬撃に護堂の動きは鈍く――: Godou's movements are slow against Ouzenji's slash—
護堂の左腕が跳んだ。: Godou's left arm flies through the air.
――こんな時にでも、冷静に戦況を判断してしまう自分が恨めしい。: —Even at a time like this, I hate myself for calmly judging the state of the battle.
逢禅寺は大きく刀を振りかぶり、隙が大きくなる。: Ouzenji swings his sword back wide, leaving a massive opening.
一瞬だけ、護堂は私を見た。: For just an instant, Godou looked at me.
ふたりの姿が、夜空の向こうへ消えた。: The two figures vanish beyond the night sky.
一瞬見せた護堂の微笑みが、姉の姿と重なった。: The smile Godou showed for an instant overlapped with my sister's image.
すぐに私は窓際へ駆け寄った。: I immediately rushed to the window.
遥か下の内堀の水面が揺れている。: Far below, the surface of the inner moat's water is rippling.
久遠「――奏介は先に行ってください。私にはまだやらねばならない事があります」: Kuon: “—Sousuke-san, please go on ahead. I still have things I must do.”
全ての幕引きをしなくてはなるまい。: I must bring the curtain down on everything.
私は炎から逃れるように窓際ぎりぎりに立つ。: I stand at the very edge of the window to escape the flames.
此処なら、大丈夫だ。: If it's here, I'll be fine.
彼の絶叫を耳に残し、私の両脚は床を蹴った。: Leaving his scream in my ears, my legs kicked off from the floor.
浜風に護堂の左袖がはためいている。: Godou's left sleeve flutters in the sea breeze.
＄ あの夜、窓際に駆け寄った私が見たものは、右手一本で窓の縁にぶら下がっている護: That night, what I saw when I rushed to the window was the sight of Godou hanging from the edge with one hand.
彼の目を視て、私はその身体に飛びついた。: Seeing his eyes, I threw myself toward him.
護堂は私を喪われた左腕で支え、階下の窓を蹴破って中に転がり込み、事なきを得た。: Godou supported me with his lost left arm, kicked through the window on the floor below, and we tumbled inside, safe.
逢禅寺はそのまま壕へ落下したらしい。↓この高さでは生きてはいまいと護堂は言った: Ouzenji apparently fell straight into the moat. Godou said he wouldn't be alive after falling from that height.
私たちは地下に降り、地下道路を通って燃えるビルから脱出した。: We went down to the basement and escaped the burning building through the underground road.
彼らを謀った以上、その前に姿を見せる事はもう出来なかった。: Since we had deceived them, we could no longer show ourselves before them.
久遠「護堂こそよろしいのですか？」: Kuon: “Are you certain you are alright with this, Godou-san?”
楽しみにしていたジャズバーを放り出してまで、私に付いてくる必要はない筈だ。: There should be no need for him to follow me, even throwing away the jazz bar he had been looking forward to.
護堂「……珍しいな、君が俺の意志を確認するとは」: Godou: “...That's rare, for you to confirm my will.”
意志はあれど、愛はない――お互いに。: There is will, but no love—between us.
私は私の力を活かせる場を求める。: I seek a place where I can utilize my power.
その為には――この国は小さ過ぎる。: For that—this country is too small.
腹の前で組んだ指をそっと押し当てる。: I softly press the fingers I've crossed over my stomach.
久遠「たった一度の契り――それで充分です」: Kuon: “A single night's vow—that is enough.”
海の向こう、東京の街の方を仰ぎ見る。: I look out across the sea, toward the city of Tokyo.
彼に伝えていない事がある。: There is something I haven't told him.
結局言えなかった、最後の真実。: The final truth I could never say.
久遠＄「全ての真実を明かしても、彼は私の事を赦してくれるでしょうか――」: Kuon: <Even if I revealed the whole truth, would he ever be able to forgive me—>
貴方の大切な人を死に至らしめた私を。: Me, who led your precious person to her death.
＄ 願わくば、あの人が居る世界の夢を、貴方が観られますように――: I pray that you may dream of a world where that person still exists—
